CHAPTER 3
(S. B. 7)
AN ACT relating to national and state defense ; authorizing
and creating a State Defense Council to assist in coordinat-
ing state and local activities related to national and state
defense ; providing for the organization of such council and
for the appointment of its members by the Governor ; pro-
viding for their qualifications and term of office ; authoriz-
ing the Council to employ an executive director and other
assistants and make other expenditures to carry out the
purposes of this Act ; prescribing the powers and duties of
the Council ; directing the Council and local and regional
defense councils to utilize existing services and facilities ;
providing for the creation of local and regional defense
councils and for the powers and duties of such councils ;
and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. This Act may be cited as the " State
Defense Council Act . "
Section 2. There is hereby created a board and
public body- corporate to be known as the State

CHAPTER 3 63
Defense Council , hereinafter designated as the
"Council " , for the general purpose of assisting in
the coordination of the state and local activities
with the activities of the nation and other states in
matters related to national and state defense .
Section 3. (a ) The Council shall consist of
five members to be appointed by the Governor and
to hold office during the pleasure of the Governor
for a period not to exceed four years from the time
of the appointment . The Governor shall appoint
the five members from state officials . The Governor
shall serve as ex-officio Chairman of the Council .
He shall designate one of the members of the Coun-
cil as Vice -Chairman and State Director of Civil
Defense . No state officer shall be ineligible as a
member of the Council by reason of holding any
such other office , any existing law to the contrary
notwithstanding . Three members of the Council
shall constitute a quorum. The Governor may ap-
point deputy directors of the Council who need not
be members of the Council .
(b) The Council may employ an executive sec-
retary and such technical , clerical , stenographic ,
and other personnel , and fix their compensation ,
and may make such expenditure within the appro-
priation therefor , or other funds made available to
it , as are necessary to carry out the purposes of this
Act .
(c) The members of the Council shall serve
without compensation , but shall be reimbursed for
all their actual and necessary hotel and traveling
expenses within or without the State , including
traveling from their respective homes to Frankfort
and return , and hotel and living expenses while in
Frankfort solely on the official business of the
Council , and other expenses incurred in the per-
formance of their duties under this act . No agree-

64 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ment for compensation to any employee who may
be engaged under the provisions of subsection (b )
above shall be valid unless authorized by a quorum
of the Council and approved by the vice -chairman
and the Governor ; nor shall any voucher for ex-
penses be valid or honored unless so approved .
(d ) The Council is authorized and empowered
to purchase , maintain and operate such automobile ,
or other motor transportation , as it may deem nec-
essary for facilitating the carrying out of the pur-
poses of this act .
Section 4. Unless called by the chairman to
meet elsewhere , the members shall hold all meet-
ings in Frankfort , Kentucky , and said Council shall
have the right to expend such reasonable sums as
may be necessary to establish and maintain offices
in that city . The Governor or the vice - chairman
and State Director of Civil Defense shall have a
right to call a meeting of the Council at any other
place in the State of Kentucky .
Section 5. The Council shall have the following
powers and duties :
( a ) To adopt , amend and repeal rules , regula-
tions , and by -laws governing its procedure and
activities .
(b ) To initiate and carry on within the state
measures to co -ordinate emergency defense activi-
ties of the state , including those of all voluntary
organizations and committees engaged in patriotic
service , and supervise the solicitation of funds by all
such organizations and committees ; and to cooperate
with the United States Director of Civilian Defense ,
or with any other similar federal agency or agencies
hereafter created and with any departments or
agencies of the United States or other states en-
gaged in defense activities .
(c ) The Council is authorized to receive appro-

CHAPTER 3 65
priations from the State of Kentucky or any sub-
division thereof , and donations from organizations
or private individuals , and to use and account for
the same for the purposes of said Council . All
monies received by said Council shall be credited
to a revolving fund account in the general fund of
the Commonwealth and expended only for the pur-
poses set forth in this act . If any fund shall re-
main in its hands after the expiration of its duties ,
the same shall be covered into the Treasury of the
State of Kentucky for the general purposes of the
state unless otherwise directed to be disposed of
under the terms of the particular appropriation or
donation .
(d) To supervise and direct investigation , and
report to the Governor with recommendations for
legislation or other appropriate action as it may
deem necessary with respect to any matter relating
to emergency defense activities , including mainte-
nance of law and order , measures to guard against
sabotage and subversive activities , police mobiliza-
tion , coordination for fire protection, and disaster
prevention and relief , and any other type of activity
directly or indirectly related to acts or measures of
emergency defense .
(e ) To create committees , either within or with-
out its membership , to aid it in the discharge of its
powers and duties .
(f) To require and direct the cooperation and
assistance of state and local governmental agencies
and officials within their respective official terri-
tories .
(g) To do all acts and things , not inconsistent
with law , for the furtherance of emergency defense
activities .
Section 6. In order to avoid duplication of serv-
ices and facilities the Council and the local and
Acts-3

66 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
regional defense councils established under the
authority of this Act are :
(a) Directed to utilize the services and facili-
ties of state , county and municipal officers , offices ,
departments , commissions , boards , bureaus , insti-
tutions and other agencies of the state and of the
political subdivisions thereof , and
(b ) All such offices and agencies shall cooperate
with and extend their services and facilities to the
Council and to local and regional defense councils
as the State Council may direct or require .
Section 7. The Council is authorized to estab-
lish local defense councils in areas within each
county , and to dissolve and reorganize such local
councils at its pleasure . Local defense councils , if
and when established , shall cooperate with and as-
sist the Council , and shall report to it when and as
required by it and shall act under its direction .
Such local councils shall in any event terminate and
cease activity whenever the Council itself is dis-
solved or suspended .
Section 8. The Council is authorized to estab
lish regional defense councils containing two or
more counties of especial importance in emergency
defense activities . Such regional councils shall act
as coordinating agencies under the supervision and
direction of the Council , and shall report to it when
and as required by it .
Section 9. The Council shall render to the Gov-
ernor on the first day of January in each year and
through the Governor to the Legislature at each
session a full account of its activities during the
preceding year and an itemized statement of all
monies received and paid out by it of all funds
drawn upon the State Treasury .
Section 10. Because of existing world , national ,
state and local conditions and the necessity for the

CHAPTER 4 167
complete coordination of all activities in connection
with national defense , it is hereby declared that an
emergency exists within the meaning of the Con-
stitution of Kentucky , therefore , this Act shall be
in full force and effect from and after its passage
and approval .
Approved by the Governor January 26 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 4
( S. B. 1)
AN ACT relating to the Militia of this State .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. KENTUCKY ACTIVE MILITIA AUTHORIZED :
Sections 2711a - 145 to 2711a - 150 , inclusive , being
Chapter 51 of the Acts of 1934 , and being sections
inclusive, of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , are hereby repealed and
reenacted and when reenacted shall read :
to
That whenever the President of the United
States shall call any part of the National Guard of
this State into active Federal service , the Governor
is hereby authorized to organize the Kentucky
Active Militia under such regulations as the Sec-
retary of War may prescribe for discipline and
training .
2. GOVERNOR SHALL BE COMMANDER - IN - CHIEF :
The Governor shall be Commander -in -Chief of
the Kentucky Active Militia , and the Adjutant
General shall be the Executive Officer and shall be
responsible to the Governor for the proper func-
tioning of the Kentucky Active Militia , and he is
hereby authorized and empowered to take neces-
sary action to perfect and maintain an efficient or-

68 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ganization for the purposes herein set out . He
shall have charge of all matters of administration
and organization , which shall be in all respects ,
insofar as necessary and applicable , the same as
that of the National Guard .
3. EXECUTIVE COUNCIL :
For the purpose of assisting the Executive Of-
ficer in the organization and maintenance of the
Kentucky Active Militia an Executive Council of
three ( 3 ) members is hereby created . They shall
be apponted by the Governor and they are hereby
empowered to make and promulgate , through the
Adjutant General , rules and regulations for the
organization and government of the Kentucky
Active Militia ; to prescribe the uniforms and
badges or insignia to be worn ; to prescribe the
character of arms to be used ; and to have general
supervision of the Kentucky Active Militia as re-
gards to all matters not definitely fixed by law .
Said Executive Council shall hold meetings at the
State Capitol at Frankfort , Kentucky , whenever
called by the Adjutant General after due notice to
that effect . The members of the Executive Council
shall serve without compensation when attending
meetings of the Council but shall be allowed their
reasonable expenses so incurred .
4. GOVERNOR TO ORGANIZE KENTUCKY ACTIVE
MILITIA :
The Governor is hereby authorized to enlist ,
organize , maintain , equip , discipline and pay when
called into active field service a volunteer defense
unit other than the National Guard , to be known
as the Kentucky Active Militia , which shall consist
of able bodied males who are residents of the State
of Kentucky and who are between the ages of

CHAPTER 4 69
eighteen ( 18 ) and sixty -four ( 64 ) . At no time shall
the number of enlisted men and officers in the Ken-
tucky Active Militia exceed two thousand ( 2,000 ) .
5. GOVERNOR AUTHORIZED TO COMMISSION
NECESSARY OFFICERS :
The Governor of Kentucky is hereby authorized
to appoint and commission the necessary officers for
the Kentucky Active Militia . The Adjutant General
of the State with the approval of the Governor
shall prescribe rules and regulations governing the
enlistment , organization , administration , pay, equip-
ment , discipline and discharge of the personnel of
the Kentucky Active Militia ; obtain by requisition
or purchase from the Secretary of War such neces-
sary arms and equipment as may be secured from
the War Department , or procure from some other
source the necessary arms and equipment to main-
tain and equip the Kentucky Active Militia ; and
provide the necessary space for the housing of
property and for the purpose of drills and instruc-
tion .
6. COMMISSIONS FOR OFFICERS AND ENLISTMENTS
AND DISCHARGES FOR ENLISTED MEN :
All officers shall be appointed and commissioned
by the Governor . All men shall be enlisted by the
unit commanders and shall be discharged upon
order of the Governor for the same reasons as men
are discharged from the National Guard , when such
discharge is requested in writing by the unit com-
manders .
7. OATH OF COMMISSIONED OFFICERS AND ENLISTED
MEN :
All officers and enlisted men of the Kentucky
Active Militia shall take an oath similar to that re-

70 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
quired to be taken by commissioned officers and en-
listed men of the National Guard . Said oath when
duly subscribed to shall be filed with and preserved
by the Adjutant General of Kentucky . The oath
shall be administered to all officers by any person
authorized to administer an oath . The Command-
ing Officers of the units are authorized and em-
powered to administer the oath to all enlisted men
in their units .
8. THE GOVERNOR MAY DISBAND AND REORGANIZE
THE KENTUCKY ACTIVE MILITIA :
The Governor shall have the power to alter ,
divide , annex , disband or reorganize any organiza-
tion of the Kentucky Active Militia whenever in his
judgment the efficiency of the State forces will
thereby be increased , and he shall have power to
change the organization so as to conform to the
regulations now or hereafter prescribed by the laws
of the United States for the organization of the
National Guard .
9. GOVERNOR AUTHORIZED TO CALL KENTUCKY
ACTIVE MILITIA INTO ACTIVE SERVICE :
The Governor is hereby authorized to call and to
assign any part of the Kentucky Active Militia to
active service for the purpose of resisting invasion ,
suppressing rebellions , insurrections , riots or
threats thereof ; to suppress any active , unlawful
or threatened violence to persons or property in
this State ; to authorize the arrest of all persons
engaged in aiding or abetting therein ; to investigate
any acts of treason , sabotage or attempted sabotage
and to cause the arrest of such persons engaged
therein for aiding and abetting ; to protect life and
property in the event of any emergency or disaster .
The Governor may by his order prescribe the duty

CHAPTER 4 71
to be performed by the troops thus called into active
field service . The Governor may direct the com-
manding officer of such military forces thus ordered
into service to report to any of the following named
civil officers of the district , county , city or town :
Circuit judge, sheriff county judge or mayor ; and
such civil officers may direct the specific object to be
accomplished by such military force , but the tactical
direction and disposition of the troops and the
particular means to be employed to accomplish the
object shall be left solely to the officers of the Ken-
tucky Active Militia . Troops shall not be relieved
from active field service except by order of the
Governor
10. CODE OF REGULATIONS :
The system of organization , armament , discipline
and government shall be prescribed from time to
time in a " Code of Regulations " to be promulgated
by the Adjutant General after being authorized by
the Executive Council .
11. DRILLS AND ARMS AUTHORIZED FOR THE
KENTUCKY ACTIVE MILITIA :
Companies and other units of the Kentucky
Active Militia are hereby authorized to drill , to
parade and to have all other ceremonies , privileges
and rights in regard to such matters , within the
State , as is authorized by law for the National
Guard .
12. OFFICER OR SOLDIER REFUSING TO OBEY :
Any officer or soldier of the Kentucky Active
Militia who shall refuse or neglect to obey the
orders of the commanding officer or of any officer
or non -commissioned officer legally appointed as his
superior , or who shall wilfully fail to report for or
attend any duly prescribed drill or formation or

72 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
who shall wilfully breach the discipline or infringe
any of the rules and regulations prescribed for said
Kentucky Active Militia shall be guilty of a mis-
demeanor and shall be punishable in courts of
criminal jurisdiction by a fine of not less than $ 1.00
or more than $ 25.00 for each offense , and cost .
13. OFFICE IN ACTIVE MILITIA NOT INCOMPATIBLE
WITH ANY OTHER CIVIL OFFICE :
No office in the Kentucky Active Militia shall be
incompatible with any civil office in the Common-
wealth , either State , county , district or city .
14. NOT LIABLE FOR ACTS DONE IN PURSUANCE OF
DUTY :
Officers and enlisted men of the Kentucky Active
Militia ordered into active service by proper author
ity shall not be liable civilly or criminally for any
act or acts legally done in the pursuance of their
duty in such service .
15. COMMANDING OFFICERS ' VISITS OF
INSTRUCTION AND INSPECTION :
Commanding and staff officers of regiments and
battalions are hereby authorized to make such
visits of instruction and inspection , when author-
ized by the Adjutant General to do so , as in his dis-
cretion may be necessary . When making said visits
they shall serve without compensation but shall be
allowed reasonable expenses .
16. PAY FOR MEMBERS OF KENTUCKY ACTIVE MILITIA
WHEN ON ACTIVE DUTY :
Officers and enlisted men when called into active
field service of the State as provided and defined
in this Act , beginning with the day they assemble
at their armories or other designated places until

CHAPTER 4 73
the day they return thereto and have been properly
relieved , inclusive , fractional parts of a day count-
ing as a full day , shall receive pay at the following
daily rates :
Major General
Brigadier General
Colonel
$ 12.00
10.00
8.00
Lieutenant Colonel 7.00
Major 6.00
Captain 5.00
First Lieutenant
Second Lieutenant
4.50
4.00
Warrant Officer 3.50
Master Sergeant 3.00
Technical Sergeant 2.75
First Sergeant 2.50
Staff Sergeant 2.25
Sergeant 2.00
Corporal 1.75
Private 1.50
and in addition thereto actual and necessary cost,
of transportation , when such transportation is not
furnished in kind . Officers shall be allowed not to
exceed three dollars ( $3.00 ) per day in lieu of
quarters and subsistence , when such quarters and
subsistence are not furnished in kind . Enlisted men
shall receive subsistence in addition to the pay and
transportation provided above .
17. EXPENSE TO BE PAID OUT OF ANY MILITARY
DEPARTMENT BUDGET APPROPRIATION :
All necessary cost and expense of perfecting and
maintaining the organization of the Kentucky
Active Militia , the payment of salaries , expenses of
administration , purchase of necessary equipment ,
supplies , printing , postage and all other necessary
expenditures shall be paid out of any budget ap-

74 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
propriation made for the present or that may be
made for any future fiscal year, for use of the
Military Department , and the Adjutant General is
hereby authorized and empowered to use any funds
so appropriated interchangeably for the Kentucky
Active Militia or the National Guard , except as to
the Armory Fund . In the event there in not suf-
ficient funds in the budget allotted to the Adjutant
General , the Governor is authorized to expend from
his Emergency Fund or any other fund appro-
priated by the Legislature a sufficient amount to
take care of any deficiency that may arise .
18. OFFICERS AND ENLISTED MEN RIGHT TO CARRY
ARMS :
Officers and enlisted men of the Kentucky Active
Militia when ordered on active duty as prescribed
herein shall have the right to carry arms . All
officers and enlisted men shall have the right to
carry arms at any drill or ceremony .
19. IMPERSONATING A MEMBER OF THE ACTIVE
MILITIA ; PENALTY :
Any person who shall wear any uniform or in-
signia or badge duly authorized as a designation of
an officer or an enlisted man of the Kentucky Active
Militia , shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor
and upon conviction shall be punished by a fine of
not less than $ 1.00 nor more than $ 10.00 at the
direction of the Court or Jury .
20. REPEALS :
All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith are
to the extent of such conflict hereby repealed .
21. EMERGENCY CLAUSE :
WHEREAS , the United States of America is now
at war with several foreign nations and all of the

CHAPTER 4 75
Kentucky National Guard , which heretofore was
used by the State of Kentucky to protect and safe-
guard life , liberty and property and to prevent law-
lessness and in any other emergency , has been called
into Federal service , the Kentucky Active Militia
is badly needed to protect life and property ; and
WHEREAS , under the present law of the State
of Kentucky the State is not permitted to equip
them in any manner , and they are badly in need of
uniforms and other equipment in order that they
may be called into active service which may be
needed at any time
An emergency is hereby declared to exist and this
Act shall take effect immediately upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
22. PARTIAL INVALIDITY OF THIS ACT :
If any section , sub - section , sentence , clause or
phrase of this Act shall be adjudged by any court
of competent jurisdiction to be invalid , such judg-
ment shall not affect , impair or invalidate the re-
mainder of this Act, as it is the intention of the
General Assembly in the enactment of this Act to
enact each and every section separately and in-
dependently of each other.
23. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , this Act shall be treated as repealing KRS
36.160 , 36.210 , all of KRS Chapter 37 , and subsec-
tion ( 2 ) of KRS 38.030 , regardless of the date of
enactment or taking effect of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
24. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session , KRS 36.170 shall, upon
the taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
stand amended to read as follows :
"The system of organization , armanent , dis-

76 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cipline and government of the National Guard shall
be prescribed from time to time in a code of regula-
tions promulgated by the Governor , subject to the
laws and regulations of the United States and to
general exceptions authorized by the Secretary of
War ."
and paragraph (j ) of KRS 29.030 shall stand
amended to read :
" Officers and enlisted menmen of the National
Guard . "
Approved by the Governor February 14 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 5
(H. B. 70 )
AN ACT relating to motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 2739g -82 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as
follows :
No person shall operate on any highway any
motor truck or semi -trailer truck whose gross
weight , including load exceeds eighteen thousand
( 18,000 ) pounds ; provided , however , that said
limitation of eighteen thousand ( 18,000 ) pounds is
increased to twenty -eight thousand ( 28,000 ) pounds
in the casecase of such operation over National
Emergency Highways or portions thereof , as de-
scribed in Section 3 of this Act, during the re-
mainder of the period of National Emergency which
was declared to exist by proclamation of the Presi-
dent of the United States dated May 27 , 1941 , and
provided , however , in no event or circumstance shall
the gross weight including load of any truck or semi-
trailer truck , exceed six hundred ( 600 ) pounds per
inch of the combined width of the tires upon which

CHAPTER 5 77
such truck may be propelled , or shall the gross
weight including load of any such vehicle exceed
sixteen thousand ( 16,000 ) pounds per each axle of
such vehicle .
§ 2. That 2739g -85 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as
follows :
No person shall operate on any highway any
motor truck whose length , including any part of
body or load , exceeds twenty-six and one -half
(262 ) feet , nor any semi -trailer truck whose length
including any part of body or load shall exceed
thirty ( 30 ) feet , provided however , that said limita-
tion of thirty ( 30 ) feet for a semi -trailer truck is in-
creased to thirty-three (33 ) feet in the case of such
operation over a National Emergency Highway or
portions thereof as hereinafter described in Sec-
tion 3 hereof.
§ 3. ( 1 ) The Commissioner of Highways may,
by orders duly issued in accordance with the pro-
visions of subsection ( 2 ) of this section , designate
from time to time particular highways or portions.
thereof in this state as National Emergency High-
ways , and from time to time thereafter may by like
order change any such designation in part or
cancel it entirely .
( 2 ) The Commissioner of Highways shall make
such designations as in his judgment will best pro-
mote the public interest , after considering the
following :
(a) The need for the operation of motor vehicles
weighing more than eighteen thousand ( 18,000 )
pounds , or of a length in excess of thirty ( 30 ) feet ,
on account of National Defense and the preparation
therefor ;
(b) The physical characteristics of each such
highway and the bridges thereon , and the extent to

78 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
which they would be damaged by operation of such
heavier motor vehicles ;
(c) The effect upon the safety of the public in
the use of such highways caused by the operation
thereon of heavier or longer motor vehicles than
are now permitted by law .
4. At the expiration of the National
Emergency which was declared to exist by procla-
mation of the President of the United States , dated
May 27 , 1941 , section one of this Act shall stand re-
pealed and thereafter § 2739g -82 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , shall read as follows :
No person shall operate on a highway any motor
truck or semi -trailer truck whose gross weight, in-
cluding the load , exceeds eighteen thousand pounds .
$ 5 . At the expiration of the National
Emergency which was declared to exist by proclama-
tion of the President of the United States , dated
May 27 , 1941 , section two of this Act shall stand
repealed and thereafter § 2739g - 85 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , shall read as
follows :
No person shall operate on any highway any
motor truck whose length , including any part of the
body or load , exceeds twenty - six and one-half feet ,
nor any semi -trailer truck whose length , including
any part of the body or load , exceed thirty feet .
§ 6. At the expiration of the National
Emergency which was declared to exist by proclama-
tion of the President of the United States , dated
May 27 , 1941 , section three of this Act shall stand
repealed .
$ 7.$ 7. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, sections one and four of this Act shall , upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of KRS 189.220 , and shall

CHAPTER 6 79
thereafter be treated as an amendment to KRS
189.220 rather than an amendment to $ 2739g - 82 of
Carroll's Statutes , and the number " KRS 189.220 "
shall be substituted for " § 2739g -82 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , " in section four
of this Act .
66
§ 8. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , sections two and five of this Act shall , upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
189.180 , and shall thereafter be treated as an
amendment to subsection ( 3 ) of KRS 189.180 rather
than an amendment to § 2739g -85 of Carroll's
Statutes , and the number " KRS 189.180 ( 3 ) " shall
be substituted for "$ 2739g -85 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , " in section five of this
Act .
9. Because the Nation is now engaged in
preparation to defend this Country and its people
from the aggression of foreign dictators , an
emergency is declared to exist , and this Act shall
become effective for all purposes when approved
by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor February 14 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 6
(H. B. 8)
AN ACT relating to the periods within which civil actions
shall be commenced .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1 . A cause of action for payment of any
warrant of the Commonwealth shall expire and be-
come noneffective unless brought within two years
after the Commonwealth has in good faith offered

80 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to redeem the warrant by payment in full of all
interest and principal due , or within two years
from the effective date of this Act , whichever is the
longer period .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be num-
bered and considered to be subsection ( 2 ) of KRS
413.110 .
Approved by the Governor February 14 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 7
(H. B. 7)
AN ACT relating to obligations of Rural Electric Cooperative
Corporations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Sections 883j - 17 , 883j - 18 and 883j -19 of
Baldwin's 1939 Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes are consolidated into one section and
amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Any corporation formed under §§ 883j -1 to
883j -30 may issue its obligations and pledge its
future revenues for the payment thereof . The
obligations may be in the form of bonds , notes ,
debentures , interim certificates or other evidences
of indebtedness . The obligations shall be author-
ized by resolution of the board of directors , after
a resolution is first passed by a majority of the
common stockholders giving the board that power .
The resolution of the board shall fix the dates of
issuance and maturity , the rate and time of pay-
ment of interest , the denominations , the form
(either coupon or registered ) , the registration
privileges , the manner of execution , the place and

CHAPTER 7 81
medium of payment , and the terms of redemption ,
but the period of maturity shall not exceed twenty-
five years , or the rate of interest six percent , and
the obligations shall not be made redeemable at
more than par and accrued interest .
(2 ) The obligations may be issued for money or
property , at public or private sale , for such price as
the board determines , but the interest cost to
maturity of the money or property received for any
issue of the obligations shall not exceed six percent
per annum , payable semiannually , the value of the
property for this purpose to be determined con-
clusively by the board .
(3 ) Pending the preparation or execution of
definitive obligations , interim receipts or certificates
or temporary obligations may be delivered to the
purchaser of the obligations . All obligations and
interest coupons issued pursuant to §§ 883j -1 to
883j - 30 shall be negotiable instruments .
(4 ) Any corporation formed under §§ 883j - 1 to
883j -30 may make such covenants or agreements
and do such other things as may be necessary , con-
venient or desirable in order to secure its obliga-
tions , or that the board believes will tend to make
the obligations more marketable , notwithstanding
that such covenants , agreements or other things
may constitute a limitation on the exercise of the
powers granted by §§ 883j - 1 to 883j -30 .
(5 ) Any corporation organized under §§ 883j - 1
to 883j -30 may , out of any funds available therefor ,
purchase or redeem any obligations issued by it at
a price not exceeding the principal amount and ac-
crued interest . All obligations so purchased shall
be canceled and retired .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking

82 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 279.130 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 279.130
rather than an amendment to §§ 883j - 17 , 883j - 18 and
883j - 19 of Carroll's Statutes and the words "this
chapter " shall be substituted for the numbers
§§ 883j -1 to 883j - 30 wherever they appear in section
one of this Act
Approved by the Governor February 14 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 8
(H. B. 89 )
AN ACT relating to appropriations for the Division of
Banking .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection (7) of section 1 , of Chapter 30
of the 1940 Acts of the General Assembly is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) All moneys collected by the Division of
Banking , from any source , during any fiscal year , in
excess of the amount appropriated out of the
General Expenditure Fund to the Division of Bank-
ing for that fiscal year , shall be placed in a revolving
fund in the State Treasury to be expended for the
ordinary recurring expenses of operation of the
Division of Banking .
( 2 ) All moneys collected by the Division of
Banking during the fiscal year from July 1 , 1941
to June 30 , 1942 , in excess of the moneys appro-
priated to the Division of Banking out of the
General Expenditure Fund for that fiscal year shall
be placed in the revolving fund created by this
section .
$ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-

CHAPTER 9 83
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 47.070 , and thereafter be
treated as an amendment to KRS 47.070 rather than
an amendment to 1940 , c . 30 , § 1 , subsection 7 .
WHEREAS , the money made available under this
act is needed immediately by the Division of Bank-
ing , an emergency is hereby declared to exist and
this act shall take effect immediately upon its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor February 18 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 9
( H. B. 104 )
AN ACT amending charters of cities of the fourth class may
provide for the creation of civil service commission , pre-
scribing its duties and declaring the qualifications of its
members ; providing for examinations of all applicants for
positions as members of the police and fire department in
such cities ; providing for the appointment and dismissal
of policemen and firemen and providing for appeal to the
Circuit Court and Court of Appeals ; providing for the crea-
tion of a Board of Trustees of a pension fund for policemen
and firemen and prescribing the duties of such boards ; pro-
viding for the establishment and maintenance of a pension
fund ; and providing for appropriations and stipulating a
limitation therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section I -Definitions as used in this Act , the
following words and terms shall have the following
meaning :
(A) Regular Police Department . For the pur-
pose of this Act , a regular police department is de-
fined as one having a fixed headquarters , where
police equipment is maintained and where police-
man or policemen are in constant and uninterrupted

84 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
attendance to receive and answer police calls , and
execute regular police patrol duties .
(B) Regular Fire Department. For the pur-
pose of this Act , a regular fire department is defined
as one having a fixed headquarters where fire fight-
ing apparatus and equipment is maintained , and
where a fireman or firemen are in constant and unin-
terrupted attendance to receive and answer fire
alarms .
(C) Legislative Body . Wherever in this Act
the term " Body " or " Legislative Body " is em-
ployed , it shall be construed to mean the legislative
branch of the city government .
(D ) Commissions . The word " Commission " ,
shall mean the board of Civil Service Commis-
sioners , as established under the terms of this Act .
(E ) Trustees . The word " Trustees " shall
mean the " Board of Pension Fund Trustees " , as
established under the terms of this Act .
(F ) Pension Fund . The term " Pension Fund " ,
shall mean the monies derived from the policeman
or policemen and fireman or firemen's salary or
" salaries" , and appropriations by the " Legislative
Body ", or any other sums derived from whatever
source by gifts or otherwise to be used for the re-
tirement of Policeman or Policemen and Fireman
or Firemen after the prescribed number of years of
service and for the benefit of disabled policeman
or policemen and fireman or firemen , and for the
benefit of widows and dependent children or de-
pendent fathers or mothers in the case of death of
a policeman or fireman , within the scope of his em-
ployment , according to the terms of this Act .
Section II . Creation of the Civil Service Com-
mission .
That any city of the fourth class which has now ,
or in which there may be hereafter established a

CHAPTER 9 85
regular police or fire department , in the future , may
by ordinance create a Civil Service Commission ,
whose duties shall be to hold examinations as to the
qualifications of applicants for employment within
the police or fire departments .
Section III . Qualifications of applicants for
Policemen and Firemen .
The Commission shall require all applicants for
appointments as members of the police or fire de-
partments to be examined as to their qualifications
to fill the office of policeman or fireman , and as to
their knowledge of the English language , and as
to the law and rules governing the duties of police-
men and firemen . Every member of the police or
fire department shall be able to read and write and
understand the English language , and to have such
other general qualifications as may be prescribed .
No person shall be appointed a member of the
police or fire departments , unless he is well known
to be a man of sobriety and integrity , and has been
and is an orderly , law-abiding citizen , nor shall any
person be appointed as a member of said police or
fire department on account of any political , partisan
service rendered by him or on account of political
sentiments or affiliations , or who is under 21 years
of age or over forty, unless the applicant has had
as much as five years ' experience as a regular
policeman or fireman and is not over fifty five ( 55 )
years of age . No member of the police or fire de
partments shall be removed or discharged or re-
duced in grade or pay for any political partisan
opinion . The appointment and continuance upon
the police and fire departments shall depend solely
upon the ability and willingness of a person to com-
ply with the rules of the said departments and to
perform the duties of said departments . No ap-
pointment to or continuance as a member of a police

86 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or fire department shall be as a reward for political
activity nor be obtained by political services or con-
tributions to campaign funds , nor shall any member
of said department pay or collect any assessments
made by political organizations , or contribute to
political campaign funds , or be unduly active in
politics or work for the election of candidates for
office . The examination and qualifications provided
for in this Act shall not apply to the members of
the regular police and fire departments at this time ,
who have been continuously in the service for a
period of three ( 3 ) years . Members of police and
fire departments otherwise qualified under this law
shall hold their positions during good behavior , pro-
vided , however , that the provisions of this Act shall
not prevent the said city Legislative Body from in-
creasing or decreasing the number of policemen or
firemen , as may be deemed proper from time to
time , and providing further that in the event the
said city Legislative Body decreases the number of
policemen or firemen , the youngest member in point
of service shall be the first to be reduced and re-
turned to the eligible list and to advance according
to the rules and regulations of said department .
Section IV . Personnel of the Commission
The Mayor , by and with the approval of the
Legislative Body , shall appoint three citizens , who
have been taxpayers and voters of the city for five
(5 ) years previous to their appointment , and who
shall not be less than thirty ( 30 ) years of age, who
shall constitute the Civil Service Commission of said
city . Such appointees shall originally be appointed
for one year , two years , and three years , respec-
tively , and the successors of such appointees shall
be appointed in like manner , each for a period of
three years . Any member of the Civil Service Com-
mission shall be eligible for reappointment .

CHAPTER 9 87
Vacancies shall be filled in the same manner as ap-
pointments of said commissioners are made , pro-
viding that any person appointed to fill a vacancy
shall serve only to the end of the term of the com-
missioner whose office was vacated . Said commis-
sioner shall-elect one of their members as Chairman
and one as Secretary of the Civil Service Commis-
sion . Each of said commissioners shall qualify by
taking the oath as prescribed by the Constitution of
Kentucky .
Section V. Powers and duties of the Civil
Service Commission .
Said Civil Service Commission shall conduct an
examination of persons for appointment as mem-
bers of the police and fire departments each time a
vacancy shall occur in said departments . The
Mayor shall notify the commissioners each time a
vacancy occurs in either of said departments and
said commissioners shall within the two weeks fol-
lowing the receipt of such notice , hold an examina-
tion for persons seeking appointment to such
vacancy after giving notice at least five days prior
to the holding of such examination in some news-
paper of general circulation in the city. Within one
week following the holding of said examination , said
commissioners shall certify to the Legislative Body
the names of the three persons receiving the high-
est grades. The Legislative Body may appoint any
one of said three persons to said department .
Providing , however , that in the event a vacancy
thereafter occurs in said department , or additions
thereto of regular or substitute policemen or fire-
men are made , within one year following the certi-
fication of said names , the Legislative Body shall
fill said vacancy or make such additions from the
list of names certified . The commissioners shall
make all rules and regulations for the proper con-

88 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
duct of their office . The Civil Service Commis-
sioners shall receive a salary each of one dollar
( $1.00 ) per annum , paid out of the city's General
Fund , for their services and the expense of holding
any one examination by the commissioners after due
notice by the Mayor has been given to the Chairman
of the said Commission that a vacancy occurs in
either of said departments and after the eligible
list has been exhausted , shall be paid by the Legisla-
tive Body out of the city's General Fund , not to
exceed ten dollars ($ 10.00 ) .
Section VI . Removal of Policemen or Firemen ,
No member of the police or fire departments shall
be removed from the department or reduced in
grade upon any reason except inefficiency , miscon-
duct , insubordination or violation of law , or viola-
tion of the rules adopted for the departments . Any
person may prefer charges against a member of
the police or fire departments , which must be filed
in the office of the Mayor , who shall , thereupon com-
municate said charge without delay to the Legisla-
tive Body . Said charges must be written , signed
by the person making such charges and must set out
with clearness and distinctness each and every
charge . It shall be the duty of the Mayor and each
member of the Legislative Body , whenever prob-
able cause appears , to prefer charges against any
member of the police or fire departments , whom , he
or she believes to have been guilty of any conduct
justifying his removal or punishment in the interest
of public order. The charges thus filed shall be
written and shall set out with distinctness and clear-
ness the charges made , and upon the hearing of any
charges , as hereinafter provided , all said charges
shall be considered traversed , and put in issue , and
the trial shall be confined to matters related to the
issue so presented . All charges against members
5734

CHAPTER 9 89
of the police or fire departments shall be filed with
the Clerk of the Legislative Body , and within three
days after said filing , the Legislative Body shall
proceed to hear and examine said charges ; provided
two days before said hearing the member of the
police or fire departments , accused , has been served
with a copy of said charges , and a statement of the
day , place and hour at which and when the hearing
of said charges shall begin . The person accused
may , however , in writing , waive the services of said
charges , and demand trial within three days after
said charges are filed with the Clerk of said Legisla-
tive Body . The Legislative Body will have the
power to summon and compel the attendance of
witnesses at all hearings or sittings , by said body ,
upon subpoena issued by the Clerk of said body , and
served upon said witnesses by any officer authorized
to serve subpoenas from any court of justice in the
county , and said body shall have the power of a
Justice of the Peace , to punish for contempt . The
member of the police or fire department , the ac-
cused , shall have the right to have subpoenaed , in
his behalf , any witness he may desire , upon furnish-
ing their names to the clerk of said body , and the
action and decision of said body on said charges
shall be reduced to writing and shall be entered in a
book to be kept for that purpose by the clerk of said
Legislative Body , and the written charges filed in
this matter shall be preserved and securely attached
to the book containing the Legislative Body's
decisions .
In cases where the Mayor or Chief has probable
cause to believe that a member of the police or fire
department has been guilty of any conduct justify-
ing removal or punishment , he may suspend said
member from duty, or from both pay and duty,
pending said trial , and said member shall not be

90 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
placed on duty or allowed pay thereafter until the
charges are heard by the Legislative Body . The
said Body shall fix punishment against a member
of the police or fire departments found guilty of any
charge under this Act , by reprimand or suspension
for any length of time in their judgment, not to ex-
ceed six months or by reducing the grade , if the ac-
cused be Chief or other officer , or by combining any
two or more of said punishments , or by removal or
dismissal from the service of any such member of
the police or fire department . No member of the
police or fire department except as provided in this
Act shall be reprimanded , removed , suspended , or
dismissed from the department until written
charges have been made , or preferred against him,
and a trial had as herein provided .
Section VII. Appeal to Circuit Court and Court
of Appeals .
Any member of the police or fire departments
who shall be found guilty by the legislative body of
any charge as hereinbefore provided , shall have the
right to appeal to the Circuit Court of the County
in which said city may be located , provided the
punishment be a suspension of more than thirty
days , or a reduction in grade or a removal or dis-
missal from the police or fire department , but the
enforcement of the judgment of said body under
such charges shall not be suspended pending said
appeal . Upon the request of the party accused , the
Clerk of said body shall file a certified copy of the
charges made and the judgment or the findings of
the said body in the Circuit Court ; which transcript ,
having been filed , the cause shall be docketed in the
Circuit Court and tried de novo , by the Judge of
said court . Should the Clerk of said body fail to
certify and transmit the transcript aforesaid to the
Circuit Court within five days after the request is

CHAPTER 9 91
made for same , then the party aggrieved may file
an affidavit in the said Circuit Court , setting out as
fully as possible the charges made , the time of trial
by said court and the judgment of said body , to-
gether with the statements of the facts that the de-
mand for transcript had been made upon the clerk
of said body more than five days before the filing
of said affidavit .
Upon the filing of said affidavit in the Circuit
Court , said cause shall be docketed in said court and
the Circuit Court is hereby authorized to compel
the filing of said transcript by said clerk by enter-
ing the proper mandatory orders , and by fine and
imprisonment , as in cases of contempt . Such ap-
peal shall have precedence over other business and
be taken up and determined speedily . An appeal
will lie from the judgment of the Circuit Court to
the Court of Appeals , as in other cases , provided
the punishment fixed by the body be suspension for
more than sixty days , reduction in grade , or re-
moval or dismissal from the department .
Section VIII . Board of Trustees of Pension
Fund .
There shall be organized in connection with said
police and fire departments a board to be known as
the Board of Trustees of the Policemen's and Fire-
men's Pension Fund , which shall be composed of the
Mayor of the City , City Treasurer , Police Chief and
Fire Chief , and one member of the City Council or
City Commissioner appointed by the Mayor of said
city, and said Trustees shall select from their num-
ber a president and secretary . The City Treasurer
shall be ex-officio Treasurer of said board and cus-
todian of its funds .
Section IX. Appropriations , Service Records ,
Gifts of Monies , Fees , Rewards and Investment of
Funds .

92 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The Legislative Body may appropriate out of the
General Fund of the said city , each month , an
amount equal to the monthly amount paid into said
fund by the Policemen's and Firemen's salaries as a
fund for the pensioning of any Policeman or Fire-
man who has served in the police or fire depart-
ments for at least a period of twenty years or more ,
providing the applicant has reached his 51st birth-
day , and all members of the police or fire depart-
ments shall be entitled to be credited with the
service rendered continuously prior to the adoption
ordinances of this Act , by said city , to the eligibility
of the twenty year , or more , period for pension , not
less than three years nor to exceed fifteen years of
previous service , and for the further purpose of
pensioning any member of the police or fire depart-
ment who may become permanently crippled while
in the service and on duty , and for the further pur-
pose of pensioning the widow or dependent children
under the age of fourteen ( 14 ) years of age , or
either of them , of any member of the said depart-
ments who may lose his life while in the service
and on active duty , and all rewards , fees , proceeds
of gifts and emoluments that may be paid or given
on account of extraordinary service of any member
of either of said departments shall be paid into the
treasury to the credit of the policemen's and fire-
men's pension fund . The payment so made , to-
gether with the appropriation shall constitute and
be kept as a fund to be called the Policemen's and
Fireman's Pension Fund ; and the said Board of
Trustees of the Policemen's and Firemen's Pension
Fund is hereby declared to be the Trustee of said
fund , and they shall have power , and it shall be their
duty, from time to time , to invest the same in whole
or in part , as they shall deem most advantageous
for the objects of said fund ; and they are em-

CHAPTER 9 93
powered to make all the necessary contracts and to
pursue all the necessary remedies in the premises .
Section X. Powers of the Board of Trustees-
Assessment of Policemen and Firemen
Said Board of Trustees of the Policemen's and
Firemen's Pension Fund shall have the exclusive
control and management of the said fund and all
monies donated , paid or assessed for the relief or
for pensioning retired , crippled or disabled mem-
bers of the police or fire departments or the widows
and dependent children under the age of fourteen
(14 ) years or dependent fathers or mothers of any
members of the police of the departments killed in
the service , while in line of duty , and shall assess
each member of the police and fire departments not
exceeding three and one -half ( 31 ) per centum of
the salary of such member , to be deducted and
withheld from the monthly pay of each member so
assessed , the same to be placed by the Treasurer of
each city to the credit of such fund , subject to the
order of such Board .
Section XI. Board of Trustees ; Rules and Regu-
lations .
The said Board of Trustees of the Policemen's
and Firemen's Pension Fund shall have all needful
rules and regulations for its government in the dis-
charge of its duties , and shall hear and decide all
applications for relief or pensions under this Act ,
and its decision of such applications shall be final
and conclusive , and not subject to revisions or re-
versal , except by said Board , and a record shall be
kept of all meetings and the proceedings of said
Board .
Section XII . Character of Investments of Pen-
sion Fund .
The said Board of Trustees of Policemen's and
Firemen's Pension Fund shall have the power to

94 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
draw such pension fund from the Treasury and
may invest the same , or any part thereof , in the
name of the Board of Trustees of the Policemen's
and Firemen's Pension Fund , in interest -bearing
bonds of the United States or the state of Kentucky ,
or any county or city of the first , second , third or
fourth class in the state of Kentucky , or in any
securities in which trustees or guardians are per-
mitted to invest , trust or guardianship funds under
the laws of this state , and all such securities shall be
deposited with the Treasurer of said city , as ex-
officio Treasurer of said Board and shall be subject
to the order of said Board .
Section XIII. Both Principal and Interest Apply .
Both the principal and interest of said pension
fund shall be applicable to the payment of pensions
under this Act .
Section XIV . Pension for Disabled Policemen
and Firemen ; Dependents ; Amounts .
anyIf member of the police or Fire Departments ,
while in the performance of his duty , becomes
temporarily , totally disabled , physically or mentally
for services by reason of service in such depart-
ments , the Board of Trustees shall order the pay-
ment to such disabled member , monthly during such
disability , not to exceed one year , from such pen-
sion fund not exceeding sixty dollars ( $ 60.00 ) per
month , the said amount to be determined by the
Board of Trustees , provided such member , during
the same period , is paid no salary as such member .
If any member of the Police or Fire Departments ,
while in the performance of his duty , becomes
mentally or physically permanently disabled by
reason of service in either of said departments so as
to render necessary his retirement from service in
either of said departments , said Board of Trustees
shall retire such disabled member from service in

CHAPTER 9 95
either of said departments , provided no such retire-
ment on account of disability shall occur , unless
said member has contracted said disability while
on duty in the service of said police or fire depart-
ment , and upon such retirement, the Board of
Trustees shall order the payment to such disabled
member of such police or fire department , monthly ,
from such Pension Fund , a sum equal to one -half of
the monthly salary such member was receiving at
the date of his retirement, providing such member
has had twelve ( 12 ) or more years ' constant service
in either of said departments . Any member of the
said police or fire departments who has less than
twelve ( 12 ) years of constant service in the said
department and becomes disabled for service and
eligible for retirement under this Act shall receive
monthly such sum from such Pension Fund fixed by
the Board of said Trustees any amount not to ex-
ceed one -half of the amount which such said mem-
ber of police or fire department was receiving in
monthly salary at the date of his retirement . If any
member of said police or fire department shall ,
while in the performance of his duty , be killed or
die as a result of an injury received in the line of
his duty , or of any disease contracted by reason of
his occupation , or shall die from any cause what-
soever as a result of his services in either of said
departments , and while in said service , or after
having served continuously for twelve ( 12 ) years ,
shall die while in the service or on the retired list
from any cause , and shall leave a widow or child or
children under the age of fourteen ( 14 ) years , said
Board of Trustees shall direct the payment from
said Pension Fund , monthly , to such widow , while
unmarried , the sum of thirty dollars ( $ 30.00 ) and
for each child until it reaches the age of fourteen
( 14) years , six dollars ( 6.00 ) , and to the dependent

96 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
father and mother to be paid as follows : If the fa-
ther be dead , the mother shall receive the entire
thirty dollars ( $ 30.00 ) , and if the mother be dead ,
the father shall receive the entire thirty dollars
($30.00 ) ; and if both be living , each shall receive
fifteen dollars ( $15.00 ) .
Section XV . Payments when Funds Insufficient .
If at any time there shall not be sufficient money
in such Pension Fund to pay each person entitled
to the benefit thereof , the amount per month as
herein provided , then an equal percentage of each
monthly payments shall be made to each beneficiary
until the said fund shall be replenished to warrant
the payment in full to each of said beneficiaries .
Section XVI . Amount of Pension After Twenty
Years Service .
Any member of the Police and Fire Departments
of such cities having served twenty ( 20 ) years or
more and having reached his 51st birthday in such
police or fire departments , and if his application to
be relieved from such police or fire departments is
granted, the said Board of Trustees shall order and
direct that such person be paid monthly pension
equal to one-half of the amount of the salary said
person is or was in receipt of as a member of said
department at the time of granting application .
Section XVII . Funeral Expense Benefit .
Whenever an active or retired policeman or fire-
man dies from any cause , the Board of Trustees.
shall appropriate from the Pension Fund , a sum
not exceeding one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) , made
payable to the widow or family for funeral ex-
penses .
Section XVIII . Pension Limitations .
No person shall be entitled to receive any pension
from the said Pension Fund under this Act , except

CHAPTER 9 97
a regularly retired member or a regular member in
the said Police or Fire Department, his widow or
children under the age of fourteen ( 14 ) years , or
dependent mother or father .
Section XIX . Treasurer to Furnish Bond .
The Treasurer of the Board of Trustees of the
Policemen's and Firemen's Pension Fund shall be
the custodian of said Pension Fund and shall se-
curely and safely keep the same , subject to the con-
trol and direction of said Board , and shall keep his
or her books and accounts concerning such fund in
such manner as may be prescribed by the Board
and the said books and accounts shall always be
subject to the inspection of the Board , or any mem-
ber thereof . The Treasurer shall , within ten days
after his appointment or election , execute a bond to
the city with good and sufficient surety , in such
penal sum as the Legislative Body shall direct , to
be approved by the Legislative Body of said city ,
conditioned for the faithful performance of the
duties of his or her office , and that he or she will
safely keep and will and truthfully account for all
money and properties which may come into his or
her hands as such Treasurer ; and that , upon the
expiration of his or her term of office , he or she will
surrender and deliver to his or her successor all
bonds , securities , and all unexpended monies or
other properties which may have come into his or
her hands as Treasurer of said fund . Said bond
shall be filed in the office of the City Clerk , as other
bonds , and may be sued on in the name of the said
city , for the use of said Board of Trustees , or in
the name of the Board of Trustees , or any person
or persons injured by a breach thereof . The pre-
mium on said bond shall be paid out of said pension
fund in the manner under this Act for expending
sums .
Acts-4

98 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section XX . Warrants to issue for Sums Due
Pension Fund .
It shall be the duty of such officer or officers of
the said city as are designated by law to draw war-
rants on the treasurer of said city, on request in
writing by said Board of Trustees , payable to the
Treasurer of said Board of Trustees for all funds
belonging to said Pension Fund as aforesaid .
Section XXI . Warrants ; How Signed .
All monies ordered to be paid from said pension
fund to any person or persons , shall be paid by the
Treasurer of the Board of Trustees only upon war-
rants signed by the President of said Board and
countersigned by the Secretary thereof , and no
warrant shall be drawn except by order of the
Board , after having been duly entered on the
records of the proceedings of the Board of Trus-
tees.
Section XXII . Report of Board of Trustees .
The Board of Trustees of the Policemen's and
Firemen's Pension Fund , shall , make a report in
writing , signed by the President and Secretary of
said Board , to the Legislative Body of said city of
the condition of said Pension Fund on the first
meeting in September of the Legislative Body of
said city in each and every year .
Section XXIII . Pension Not Subject to
Attachment.
No portion of said Pension Fund shall, before or
after its order for distribution by the Board of
Trustees to the persons entitled thereto , be held ,
seized , taken , subjected to or detained or levied
upon by virtue of any attachments , execution , in-
junction , writ , interlocutory , or other order , or de-
cree , or any process or proceeding whatever issued
out of or by nay court of this state for the payment
or satisfaction , in whole or in part , of any debt ,

CHAPTER 9 99
damage , claim , demand or judgment against the
beneficiary of said fund , but said fund shall be held
and distributed for the purpose of this Act , and for
no other purpose whatever .
Section XXIV . City Attorneys ; Duties .
It shall be the duty of the attorneys for the said
cities , to give advice to the Board of Trustees of
the Policemen's and Firemen's Pension Fund in
all matters pertaining to their duties and manage-
ment of said fund whenever requested , and he shall
represent and defend said Board as its attorney in
all suits or actions at law , or in equity that may be
brought against it , and bring all suits and actions
in its behalf that may be required or determined
upon by said Board .
Section XXV . Adoption Ordinance ; How Repealed ,
And Dissolvement of Pension Fund in the
Treasury at time of Adoption Ordinance Re-
peal Under this Act.
In the event that the provisions of this Act are
accepted and adopted by the Legislative Body of a
city of the fourth class by ordinance , as herein pro-
vided , the repeal of such ordinance shall not become
effective unless adopted by the unanimous vote of
the duly elected members of such Legislative Body .
If , in the event a repeal ordinance is adopted by
such Legislative Body , all monies or property
belonging to the Policemen's and Firemen's Pen-
sion Fund at the time of the repeal of the said
Adoption Ordinance , shall be dissolved or liqui-
dated by the Board of Trustees of said Policemen's
and Firemen's Pension Fund and distributed by
said Board in the following manner :
Within sixty ( 60 ) days of adoption by said Legis-
lative Body of said repeal ordinance , the said
Board of Trustees shall proceed with the liquida-
tion of said Pension Fund as follows : All unex-

100 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pended monies appropriated to said Pension Fund
out of the said City's General Fund to the Police-
men's and Firemen's Pension Fund by the said
Legislative Body of such city and at the time of
adoption of a repeal ordinance shall revert back to
the City's General Fund . All other unexpended
monies or property which has come into the said
Pension Fund's hands shall be liquidated by said
Board of Trustees in the following manner : All
unexpended monies in the said Pension Fund which
accumulated thereto by assessments from Police-
men's and Firemen's salaries and gifts as accumu-
lated thereto in any manner , except appropriations
from the said City's General Fund shall revert back
to the active or retired members of the said Police-
men and Firemen and dependents who have quali-
fied under this Act in such city . In the division to
the beneficiaries , the Board of said Trustees shall
use in the division of said fund the percentum of
the present salaries of such members . After all
disbursements have been made of said fund by the
Board of Trustees , the said Board of Trustees shall
file , as their last act , a complete report of same with
said Legislative Body within thirty days , and such
report shall be kept in the office of the City Clerk as
other city records .
Section SSVI . Voluntary Retirement .
No policeman or fireman shall be entitled to the
benefits herein proided incidental to voluntary re-
tirement from either of said departments on ac-
count of service , unless he shall have been em-
ployed continuously for a period of at least five ( 5 )
years from and after the date of the passage of the
adoption ordinance by the Legislative Body and
the amount of such sum paid to such member , shall
be fixed by the Board of Trustees payable monthly ,
not to exceed one -half of salary such member was

CHAPTER 10 101
in receipt of at the time of voluntary retirement.
The payments fixed by said Trustee to such mem-
ber , shall not exceed one ( 1 ) year .
Section XXVII .
When the provisions of this Act have been ac-
cepted and adopted by the legislative body of any
city of the fourth class , §§ 3493 and 3494 of Car-
roll's Statutes shall not apply to that city .
Section XXVIII .
If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are enacted at
the present session of the General Assembly , the
numbers " KRS 95.700 ( 2 ) , 95.710 and 95.76 " shall
be substituted for "§§ 3493 and 3494 " in section
XXVII of this Act .
66
Approved by the Governor February 18 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 10
(H. B. 93 )
AN ACT providing for airports for cities and counties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. As used in this Act , unless the context
otherwise requires :
66
(1 ) Airport " includes landing field and avi-
ation field ; and
( 2 ) "Airport board " or " board " means a
board appointed as provided in section four , but
this board may be known by whatever title the city
or county or city and county may designate .
(3) " City " means any city of the second , third ,
fourth , fifth or sixth class , and " city and county "
means a city and county jointly acquiring an air-
port under section two .
§ 2. ( 1 ) Any city of the second , third , fourth ,
fifth or sixth class , or any county or any such city

102 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
jointly with the county in which it is located may
acquire , either by lease , purchase , gift or con-
demnation , in the same manner as park property is
condemned , any land necessary or suitable for es-
tablishing airports within or without the limits of
such city or county . Such cities and counties may
erect , equip , operate and maintain , on the land ,
buildings , equipment , runways and other things
necessary or proper for the establishment and
maintenance of airports .
(2 ) All airports already acquired by cities or
counties or cities and counties mentioned in sub-
section ( 1 ) shall be subject to the provisions of this
Act, but this Act shall not impair existing con-
tracts as to the acquisition and use of such air-
ports .
§ 3. The city or county or city and county may
lease the airport to one or more persons upon such
terms as may be provided by ordinance or resolu-
tion, or may place the management and control of
the airport under an airport board , or may operate
the airport without the appointment of a board .
§ 4. ( 1 ) The city or county , or city and county
may by ordinance or resolution provide for the ap-
pointment of an airport board , composed of six
members , to operate , manage and control the air-
port . The legislative body of the city or county ,
or city and county shall , by the adoption of such
ordinance or resolution , prescribe the rules and
regulations by which the board shall be governed .
( 2) The members of the airport board shall be
citizens , taxpayers and legal voters of the county ,
and no member of the board shall be interested
directly or indirectly in any contract for the fur-
nishing of supplies or of service of any kind to the
airport .
(3 ) The first airport board appointed shall

CHAPTER 10 103
serve for terms as follows : Two for one year , two
for two years , and two for three years . Upon the
expiration of the first terms , successors shall be
appointed for a term of three years . In the case of
an airport jointly owned by a city and county the
members shall be appointed so that the terms of
one city member and one county shall expire each
year .
(4 ) If at any time during his term of office any
member of the airport board becomes a candidate
for nomination or is elected or appointed to any
public office , he shall automatically vacate his mem-
bership and another person shall be appointed to
serve for the remainder of his term, or until a suc-
cessor has been appointed .
§ 5. ( 1 ) The airport board shall be a body-
politic and corporate, and shall have the usual cor-
porate powers .
( 2 ) The airport board may make and adopt its
own bylaws , rules and regulations , not inconsistent
with those of the legislative body or bodies creating
it , for the orderly , efficient and economic operation
of the airport .
§ 6. Members of the airport board shall serve
without compensation unless by ordinance or reso-
lution a salary is fixed , in which event the salary of
each member of said board shall not exceed three
hundred dollars per year .
$ 7. (1 ) The airport board shall choose a
member of the board as president and shall also
elect a secretary -treasurer who may or may not be
a member of the board .
( 2) The legislative body or bodies shall by ordi-
nance or resolution fix the salary of the secretary-
treasurer at not exceeding five hundred dollars per
year . The secretary-treasurer shall execute an
official bond , to be approved by the board and the

104 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cost thereof shall be paid by the legislative body or
bodies which created the board .
$ 8. ( 1 ) The secretary -treasurer shall keep
the minutes of all meetings of the board and shall
also keep a set of books showing the receipts and
expenditures of the board . He shall preserve on
file duplicate vouchers for all expenditures . Such
books and vouchers shall at all times be subject to
the examination of the legislative body by whom
the board is created .
( 2 ) The secretary -treasurer shall annually in
January transmit to the legislative body a detailed
report of all acts and doings of the board. The re-
port shall show a complete accounting of the
board's financial obligations for the preceding year ,
an itemized account of all receipts and disburse-
ments of money , and an itemized estimate of the
money required for operation of the airport during
the next year .
9. The city or county or city and county may
make rules and regulations for the use and safety
of airports and may deny to anyone not a regular
licensed pilot or student under a licensed pilot the
right to use the airport . They may establish such
rates , charges or fees for the use of the airports as
they deem best . The airports shall be policed in
the manner determined by the city or county or city
and county .
10. The city or county or city and county may
contract with any person or governmental agency
for the use of the airport . Such contract shall not
prevent the general use of the airport by the citi-
zens of the city or county .
§ 11. All moneys derived from the use of the
airport shall be first applied to the maintenance of
the airport , and the city or county or city and
county may expend out of their general funds or

CHAPTER 10 105
any other funds available for the maintenance of
the airport .
§ 12. The city or county or city and county may
keep gasoline and oil on the airport for the fueling
and refueling of aircraft and may sell it at prevail-
ing prices to persons using the airport .
§ 13. The title to all property acquired under
the provisions of this Act shall vest in the city or
county or in both . All properties acquired for the
establishment and maintenance of an airport shall
be exempt from taxation to the same extent as other
property used for public purposes . All fees charged
or collected for the admission to or for the use of
the airport shall also be exempt from taxation .
§ 14. Sections 2741q to 2741q -5 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition, and Chapter 169
of the Acts of 1938 are repealed .
$ 15 . If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section fifteen of this Act shall be treated
as repealing KRS 183.300 to 183.360 , regardless of
the date of enactment or taking effect of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes .
§ 16. There being a state of war existing
between the United States and the governments of
Germany and Japan , it is necessary that the full
power of the Commonwealth of Kentucky be ex-
ercised at once to enact all legislation necessary to
most effectively carry into effect the immediate
establishment of air ports and aviation fields for
military and civilian use . Therefore , an emergency
is declared to exist and this act shall take effect
from and after its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Approved by the Governor February 18 , 1942 .
