CHAPTER 11
(H. B. 19 )
AN ACT relating to Cooperative Marketing Associations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Section 883f - 10 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , being
Section 10 of Chapter 1 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of 1922 , be , and the same hereby is ,
amended and reenacted so that said section as
amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
"By-laws ."-Each association must , within
thirty ( 30 ) days after its incorporation , adopt for
its government and management a code of by- laws
not inconsistent with the powers granted by this
Act . A majority vote of the members or stock-
holders , or their written assent , is necessary to
adopt such by -laws . Each association , under its
by-laws , may provide for any or all of the following
matters :
( a ) The time , place and manner of calling and
conducting its meetings .
(b) The number of stockholders or members
constituting a quorum .
( c ) The right of members or stockholders to
vote by proxy or by mail or both ; and the condi-
tions , manner , form , and effects of such votes .
(d ) The number of directors constituting a
quorum .
(e ) The qualifications , compensation and duties
and term of office of directors and officers ; time of
their election and the mode and manner of giving
notice thereof .
(f) Penalties for violations of the by -laws .
(g ) The amount of entrance , organization and
membership fees , if any ; the manner and method of

CHAPTER 11 107
collection of the same ; and the purposes for which
they may be used .
(h ) The amount which each member or stock-
holder shall be required to pay annually or from
time to time , if at all , to carry on the business of
the association ; the charge , if any , to be paid by
each member or stockholder for services rendered
by the association to him and the time of payment
and the manner of collection ; and the marketing
contract between the association and its members
or stockholders which every member or stock-
holder may be required to sign.
(i ) The number and qualification of members
or stockholders of the association and the condi-
tions precedent to membership or ownership of
common stock ; the method , time and manner of
permitting members to withdraw or the holders of
common stock to transfer their stock ; the manner
of assignment and transfer of the interest of mem-
bers and of the shares of common stock ; the condi-
tions upon which and time when membership of
any member shall cease ; the automatic suspension
of the rights of a member when he ceases to be
eligible to membership in the association ; and the
mode , manner and effect of the expulsion of a mem-
ber ; the manner of determining the value of a mem-
ber's interest and provision for its purchase by the
association upon the death or withdrawal of a mem-
ber or stockholder , or upon the expulsion of a
member or forfeiture of his membership , or , at the
option of the association , the purchase at a price
fixed by conclusive appraisal by the board of di-
rectors . In case of the withdrawal or expulsion of
a member , the board of directors shall equitably
and conclusively appraise his property interests in
the association and shall fix the amount thereof in

108 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
money , which shall be paid to him within one year
after such expulsion or withdrawal .
Such by -laws may be amended , changed , or al-
tered at any time thereafter by the affirmative vote
of a majority of the entire number of directors of
the association at any director's meeting duly
called and held . "
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly , Section 1 of this Act shall, upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of KRS 272.150 , and shall
thereafter be treated as an amendment to KRS
272.150 rather than an amendment to Section
883f -10 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
Approved by the Governor February 18 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 12
(H. B. 156 )
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation providing for the
exemption of alcohol and distilled spirits for the national
defense and industrial purposes from the import and pro-
duction taxes imposed by sections 4214a - 13 and 4214a - 17 ,
respectively , of Baldwin's 1936 edition of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Alcohol or distilled spirits manufac-
tured in or imported into the Commonwealth of
Kentucky upon the order of or at the direction of
the War Production Board or any other agency
created under any national defense act shall, during
the period of the unlimited national emergency
proclaimed by the President of the United States
on May 27 , 1941 ( F. R. Doc . 21-3808 ; filed May 28 ,
1941 ; 9:45 a. m . ) , be exempt from the manufactur-
ing and import taxes imposed by sections 4214a -13

CHAPTER 13 109
and 4214a -17 of Baldwin's 1936 edition of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , subject to such regulations as
the Department of Revenue may prescribe .
Section 2. Alcohol or distilled spirits manufac-
tured in or imported into the Commonwealth of
Kentucky for industrial purposes as distinguished
from beverage purposes , and under circumstances
such as to render the same free from the payment
of internal revenue taxes under the laws of the
United States , shall be exempt from the manufac
turing and import taxes imposed by sections
4214a -13 and 4214a -17 of Baldwin's 1936 edition of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , subject to such regu-
lations as the Department of Revenue may pre-
scribe .
Section 3. In view of the fact that the United
States is now at war and there is an urgent need
that the production facilities for the manufacture
of alcohol and distilled spirits in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky be devoted to the production of ma-
terials instant to the prosecution of the war and
other defense needs at the earliest possible moment ,
an emergency is hereby declared to exist and this
Act shall become effective on and after its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Section 4. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly, the numbers " subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 )
( a ) of KRS 243.680 " shall be substituted for " sec-
tions 4214a -13 and 4214a - 17 of Baldwin's 1936 edi-
tion of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes " in sections 1
and 2 of this Act .
Approved by the Governor February 20 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 13
(H. B. 5)
AN ACT providing for special police during the National
Emergency .

110 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
"
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
(1 ) Any person in charge of any premises ,
which he believes to be essential to National De-
fense , may apply to the Governor to appoint and
commission such persons as the person in charge
of such premises designates to act as special police-
men . Upon such application the Governor may , if
he believes it is to the interest of National Defense
and the person recommended possesses the neces-
sary qualifications , appoint the persons designated ,
or as many thereof as he deems proper , and shall
issue to such as he appoints a commission to act as
special policemen . The Governor shall designate
the premises and the area surrounding the prem-
ises in which each appointee may exercise his
powers as policeman .
(2 ) Every person appointed to act as a special
policeman under this Act shall execute an official
bond approved by the Governor , and take the oath
required by Section 228 of the Constitution . The
Secretary of State shall have custody of the bond.
and shall make a record of the certificate issued and
keep the record in his office . The execution of the
bond and the taking of the oath of office shall be
endorsed upon the commission issued to the special
policeman . The person making application for ap-
pointment under the provisions of this Act shall
pay a fee of $ 1.00 only to the Secretary of State to
be placed in the Revolving Fund and used by the
Secretary of State only to defray the expenses of
administering this Act .
(3) Special policemen appointed under this Act
shall possess and exercise the power of sheriffs in-
making arrests and shall be subject to all the lia-
bilities of sheriffs while in the discharge of their
duties . The powers of special policemen appointed

CHAPTER 13 111
under this Act shall not be limited by any other
statute relating to non -elective peace officers or pri-
vate policemen .
(4 ) Special police appointed under this Act
shall, while on duty, wear a metallic badge or shield
with the words " special police " and the name or
initials of the person for whom appointed engraved
thereon . The badge must be worn on the outside of
the clothing , except that when the policeman is act-
ing as a detective he may wear it concealed .
(5 ) The compensation of special policemen
shall be paid by the person who applied for their
appointment and shall be such as may be agreed
upon between the policemen and the person paying
them .
(6) When the person who applied for the ap-
pointment of any special policeman no longer
requires the service of such policeman , he may file
notice to that effect in the office of the Secretary of
State . The Secretary of State shall note the filing
of such notice upon the margin of the record where
the commission is recorded , and thereupon the
power of such policeman shall cease .
( 7 ) This Act shall continue in force until the
President of the United States proclaims that the
present National Emergency is over , and upon such
proclamation this Act shall stand repealed and the
powers of every special policeman appointed under
this Act shall cease .
( 8 ) Whereas , there is a grave National Emer-
gency and an immediate need for the protection of
Defense industries in this State , an emergency is
declared to exist and this Act shall become effective
immediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Approved by the Governor February 6 , 1942 .

CHAPTER 14
( S. B. 10 )
AN ACT amending the Reorganization Act, and declaring an
emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 4618-89 Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1936 Edition , and Section of Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , be amended and reenacted
so as to read as follows :
The division of purchases and public properties
under the direction of the Commissioner of Finance ,
shall purchase for the combined requirements of all
spending agencies of the State all supplies , ma-
terials , and equipment , except library books , sci-
entific equipment , or shall control or delegate the
purchase thereof . The division shall have super-
vision over all purchases by the various spending
agencies , except as otherwise provided in this Act ,
and may prescribe rules to govern purchasing by or
for all such agencies , subject to the approval of the
Commissioner of Finance ; and no purchase or con-
tract shall be binding on the State or any agency
thereof unless approved by the division or made
under general regulations which the Department
may prescribe . The division shall maintain per-
petual inventories of plant and equipment . It shall
have power , with the approval of the Commissioner
of Finance , to transfer between departments , to
salvage , to exchange , and to condemn supplies and
equipment . It shall sell all property , including real
estate , of the State determined , with the approval
of the Commissioner of Finance and the Governor ,
not to be needed for public use or to have become
unsuitable for such use . All deeds covering the sale
of real estate shall be executed and signed by the
Commissioner of Finance and approved and signed
1.

CHAPTER 14 113
·
by the Governor . It shall purchase all property
(including real estate ) determined to be needed.
for State use , upon the approval of the Commis-
sioner of Finance and the Governor as to the de-
termination of need and as to the action of pur-
chase . Nothing in this provision shall affect the
power now vested by existing law for the purchase
of real estate by State agencies or divisions thereof .
It shall fix standards of quality and quantity , and
shall develop standard specifications in consulta-
tion with the several spending agencies of the State .
It shall maintain records of supplies for various
classes of purchases , unit costs under contracts ,
trends of prices and costs , and other matters in-
cident to performance of its functions as the Com-
missioner of Finance directs . It shall attempt in
every practicable way to insure the State's supply-
ing its real needs at the lowest possible cost .
The division shall operate a mail and messenger
service for all agencies located at the seat of gov-
ernment and shall handle all postage for such agen-
cies , so far as practicable by postage meter , and
may maintain a control supply service including
control storeroom for stationary and office supplies .
It shall provide such duplicating and minor print-
ing services to other agencies as can practicably
and economically be rendered by means of avail-
able types of office equipment . It shall provide , so
far as practicable , clerical and stenographic serv-
ices as needed for unusual work demands of other
agencies at the seat of government above the nor-
mal demands that can be met by their permanent
staff.
It shall be responsible for the custody , manage-
ment , maintenance , improvement , and operation of
all buildings and grounds owned or leased for the
use of agencies of the State other than buildings

114 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and grounds maintained for the exclusive use of
any one agency . It shall have authority to allocate
the space in the State Capitol and any other build-
ing or buildings in its custody to the various agen-
cies , according to their several needs and the space
available , and to lease and allocate space for the
housing of agencies that cannot be so accommodated
when funds are made available for that purpose .
The division of purchases and public properties
shall be headed by a purchasing agent , who shall
have had four years of responsible experience in
purchasing , or equivalent experience .
If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are enacted at
the present session of the General Assembly , this
Act shall, upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , be substituted in place of KRS
42.060 , and thereafter be treated as an amendment
to KRS 42.060 rather than an amendment to Sec-
tion 4618-89 of Carroll's Statutes .
Whereas , the program of rehabilitation of state
institutions will require immediate purchase and
sale of certain lands , and whereas it is necessary .
that such property be purchased and sold as soon
as practical , now therefore , an emergency is de-
clared to exist and this act shall take effect im-
mediately after its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Approved by the Governor February 6 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 15
(S. B. 50)
AN ACT authorizing cities of the first class to provide for
off - street parking facilities and for the operation and regu-
lation of such facilities ; to acquire and hold property for
such purpose by purchase , condemnation , or otherwise ; to

CHAPTER 15 115
issue revenue bonds on any such property ; to establish and
fix the fees for the use of such parking facilities ; and pro-
vide for the disposition of revenues .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. It is hereby declared that the free
circulation of traffic of all kinds through the streets
of any city of the first class is necessary to the
health, safety , and general welfare of the public ;
that in recent years the greatly increased use by
the public of motor vehicles of all kinds and the
parking of such vehicles in the streets of such city
has created such traffic congestion as to constitute
a public nuisance ; that such parking prevents the
free circulation of traffic in , through , and from such
city, impedes the rapid and effective fighting of
fires and disposition of police in such city , threatens
irreparable loss in values of city property which
can no longer be readily reached by vehicular traf-
fic , and endangers the health , safety , and welfare
of the general public ; that this parking nuisance is
not capable of being adequately abated except by
provisions of sufficient off - street parking facilities ,
conveniently located in commercial and residential
districts of such city ; that adequate off- street park-
ing facilities have not been provided by private
capital, and the private parking spaces now exist-
ing must be supplemented by off - street parking
facilities provided by public action or undertaking ;
and that the provisions of this act are hereby de-
clared to be a public necessity .
Section 2. Any city of the first class shall have
and may exercise all powers necessary or con-
venient to carry out the purpose of this act , which
is hereby declared to be the acquisition , creation ,
and operation in any manner hereinafter provided ,
under public regulation , of public off- street parking

116 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
facilities in any such city as a necessary incident to
insuring in the public interest the free circulation
of traffic in, through and from such city . Such
powers shall include , but shall not be limited to , the
powers enumerated in the following paragraphs ( a )
to (i ) inclusive
(a) The power to purchase , in the manner pre-
scribed in Section 2823 of the Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision , and acquire by gift , be-
quest , devise or grant , real property , or any in-
terest therein , in any area of any such city in which
the Director of Works in such city , subject to the
approval of the Mayor , shall have made a deter-
mination that public parking facilities are therein
required, and to hold , use, and dispose of same in
the manner prescribed by law .
(b ) The power to condemn , in the manner pre-
scribed in Section 2831 of Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision real property , or any
interest therein , in any area of any such city in
which the Director of Works in such city , subject
to the approval of the Mayor , shall have made a
determination that public parking facilities are
therein required , and hold , use , or dispose of same
in the manner provided by law.
(c) The power to clear and improve , in the man-
ner provided by law , any property acquired under
this act , and to construct or cause to be constructed
thereon such structures suitable for parking facil-
ities , as the Director of Works , subject to the
approval of the Mayor , shall have determined to be
necessary .
(d ) The power to maintain and operate for
parking , by the public , of vehicles , any property , or
interest in property acquired under this act .
(e ) The power to contract with any corporation ;
firm , individual , or association , private or public ,

CHAPTER 15 117
subject to the provisions of this act, for the opera-
tion as a public parking facility of any property , or
interest in property , acquired under this act , for
such period not exceeding three years , as the Direc-
tor of Works , subject to the approval of the Mayor ,
shall determine .
(f) The power to lease to any corporation , firm ,
individual , or association , private or public, subject
to the provisions of this act , for the operation there-
of as a public parking facility any property , or

(g ) The power to sell , exchange , transfer , or

(h ) The power to establish and revise from time

nance , operation , use , and charge for the use of all

(i ) The power to engage in or contract for the
construction , reconstruction , alteration , repair , and

118 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
maintenance of any public parking facilitiesoperated or to be operated under this act, as theDirector of Works , subject to the approval of theMayor , shall determine .
Section 3. Any contract or lease made under theprovisions of this act shall contain a provision for ,
and be subject to, termination at the option of suchcity acting by or through the Director of Works of
such city, subject to the approval of the Mayor .
Section 4. The rates or fees to be charged forthe use of such public parking facilities shall be
