CHAPTER 42
(S. B. 23
)
AN ACT authorizing the judges of all courts in this Common-
wealth
toconfiscate any deadly weapon found on the per-
son of
, or used by any person in the commission of
acrime
upon said person being convicted
inany of said courts and

CHAPTER 43 311
authorizing the judges of said courts to confiscate or other-
wise dispose of all such weapons now held in the custody
of said courts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Upon the conviction of any person for the viola-
tion of any law of this Commonwealth in the unlaw-
ful use of any deadly weapon , any such weapon
used by such person in the commission of said of-
fense or found in his possession or control shall be
confiscated , donated to any officer of the Federal ,
State or County , Government , or sold by the Sheriff
of the county by order of the court wherein such
conviction is had .
The judge of any court now having in its pos-
session or under its control any deadly weapon
taken from any person heretofore convicted or
charged with any offense for the violation of the
use of such weapons is hereby authorized to con-
fiscate , donate to any officer of the Federal , State
or County Government, or to have same sold by
the Sheriff of the County . If said weapons are
sold by the Sheriff, any fund received from the
sale thereof shall be turned over to the school
fund of said county .
Not approved by the Governor .
CHAPTER 43
(H. B. 24)
AN ACT to enable the several cities and counties of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky to enter into inter - city or inter-
county compacts for the purposes of establishing single
purchasing units and setting up the merit system in such
cities and counties . Providing for the appointment of an
inter -city or inter -county Comptroller and the selection of
an inter -city or inter - county Commission to direct the poli-
cies of the said cities or counties entering into such com-
pacts .

312 ACTS OF THE GENERAL Assembly
WHEREAS the operation of local governments are becoming
increasingly more difficult due to the mounting cost of
supplies , and whereas more rigid economy must be effected
by the various local units of government in order that our
citizens may the better assist in our program of National
Defense , and whereas a great saving may be effected by
mass purchasing of supplies and the induction of the merit
system for personnel in the said local units of government ,
therefore-
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Two or more cities , or two or more
counties , or counties and cities together within the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , in order to promote
greater economy , may enter into an inter - city or
inter -county compact for the purpose of establish-
ing a single purchasing unit and the merit system .
Section 2. All of the expenses incurred due to
the operation of said inter - city or inter -county com-
pact shall be borne by the several cities or counties
who have entered into such a compact , and each
city or county shall bear its share of such expense
in proportion to its population in relationship to the
other cities or counties who have entered into said
compact . Such an inter -city or inter -county com-
pact unit shall make its own rules and regulations
subject to the limitations of the existing statutes
and further provisions of this Act .
Section 3. A. Each city or county shall desig-
nate some person who is a member of either the
Fiscal Court of the County, Council or Commis-
sion of the city to represent such city or county in
all of the business transactions by the inter -city or
inter -county commission according to the provisions
of this Act . Such a representative or commissioner
shall receive no compensation except in those cases
where traveling expense is involved .

CHAPTER 43 313
B. Each commissioner shall have one vote , ex-
cept in the case of a tie vote , at which time the
Chairman shall cast two votes . The Commission
shall determine the policies of the inter -city or
inter -county compact in accordance with the pro-
visions of this Act.
C. The commission shall appoint a Comptroller
who shall be a certified public accountant or its
equivalent , as may be determined by the examiner
of his qualifications . He shall also have had some
experience in a purchasing division of a city ,
county , or large private enterprise . This rule shall
be adhered to except in those cases where a person
with such qualifications cannot be secured , in which
case the comptroller who shall be appointed shall
serve temporarily unless he agrees to qualify him-
self to hold the position permanently.
Section 4. A. The comptroller shall assume full
charge of the procurement of supplies and may set
up a merit system for personnel , except in those
cases where cities or counties have already adopted
a merit system .
Section 5. The inter -city or inter -county com-
mission shall hold regular meetings , and keep rec-
ords of proceedings and make periodic reports to
each city and county of the enter -city or inter-
county compact .
Section 6. A. The comptroller shall have access
to all records , of cities or counties who have con-
tracted to enter into this compact , that relate to
personnel and the procurement of supplies . In no
case shall the comptroller exercise authority over
persons who have been appointed by elective of-
ficials whose terms have not expired at the time of
the institution of the provisions of this Act , and at

314 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the expiration of the term of such official , the ap-
pointee may be retained if he qualifies by passing
the prescribed merit test .
B. The comptroller shall purchase all supplies
for the commission , except those things that may be
classified as emergencies as determined by the com-
mission. The comptroller shall set up standards
for the type of equipment to be ordered by him .
Before purchasing any materials other than emer-
gencies , competitive bids shall be required and the
lowest bidder with approved quality goods shall re-
ceive the order .
C. At certain stated times , the various cities or
counties of the said inter -city or inter -county com-
pact shall file their requisitions for needed sup-
plies . The comptroller , after examining the budget
of such a city or county and determining the need
for such materials , may approve or veto such a
requisition . If he approves the order of the city
or county , the process of purchasing may proceed
and the respective city or county be charged with
the account . If the requisition is vetoed , the com-
mission may set aside the veto only by unanimous
vote.
D. The comptroller shall serve under contract
for a period of not less than two years and if it is
desired to remove the comptroller during any fiscal
year, that may only be done for cause and by a
majority vote of the city or county administrative
officials who are parties to the contract .
Section 7. This Act shall not apply to the Legal
Departments , Courts , or Boards of Education , or
their personnel , of any city or county that may
enter into such a compact .
Not signed by the Governor .

CHAPTER 44
(H. B. 99 )
AN ACT pertaining to Trial Commissioners to be appointed
by county judges .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

and re -enacted so that when re - enacted said Section
shall read as follows :
The Judge of the County Court , in each county

may authorize, is hereby authorized to appoint a

office at the pleasure of the Judge , and shall dis-

The commissioner shall report his actions to the

from the amount of population , the fiscal court may
fix the salary of the commissioner at not more than
thirty - six hundred dollars per annum . The salary
of the commissioner shall be paid in the same way
as the county judge is paid . "

acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, section one of this Act shall , upon the taking

CHAPTER 45
(H. B. 84 )
AN ACT relating to drainage districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. If at any time any person or
persons owning lands within any drainage district
desires to withdraw his lands in whole or in part
from the drainage district , such person or persons
upon the approval of a majority of the qualified
members of the drainage board may petition the
County Court for a withdrawal of his or their lands
from the district . The judge shall docket the
petition upon the docket of his court which convenes
not less than fifteen days after the filing thereof
with him , and issue and cause to be served upon the
drainage board notice that, at the next term of his
court which convenes not less than ten days after
the service , he will consider the petition . Upon
proper showing that the lands should be withdrawn
from the drainage district , and if no valid reason is
shown against the withdrawal of the lands from
the drainage district , the judge shall enter an order
withdrawing the lands . After the order of with-
drawal is entered , the lands shall cease to be within
the drainage district , but an appeal may be taken
from the order of the circuit court , and from the
circuit court to the Court of Appeals . All cost in

CHAPTER 46 317
any action filed to be paid by the petitioner or
petitioners regardless of the result of the action .
SECTION 2. The withdrawal of lands from a
drainage district as provided for in Section one
shall not relieve lands already within a drainage.
district from the payment of its proportionate part
of any bonds or other outstanding indebtedness
which were issued or incurred in the district , but
shall only relieve the lands from the payment of
maintenance assessments on the ditches .
Approved February 25 , 1942
CHAPTER 46
(S. B. 74 )
AN ACT relating to the conversion and wrongful use of
water , electricity and gas , artificial , natural or mixed , the
interference with appliances used in the transportation and
distribution of same , and the possession of devices used or
intended to be used to accomplish conversion or wrongful
use of same .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any person who , without the consent of a
person engaged in the manufacture or distribution
of water , electricity or gas , artificial , natural or
mixed , unlawfully converts to his own use from any
wire or pipes of such person any water , electric
current or gas , or who prevents such water , electric
current or gas , or any part thereof , from passing
through any meter provided for measuring it by
such person , or who in any manner prevents such
meter from correctly measuring the water , electric
current or gas passing through it, or aids in any
manner in the unlawful conversion to his use or the
use of another any portion of such water, electric
current or gas , shall be fined not less than fifty
dollars nor more than Five Hundred Dollars , or

318 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
imprisoned for not less than three months nor more
than twelve months , or both so fined and imprisoned .
§ 2. Any person who unlawfully and maliciously
destroys , injures or otherwise interferes with any
meter , meter lock or seal , pipe , conduit , wire , line ,
pole , lamp or other apparatus belonging to any per-
son engaged in manufacturing , distributing or
selling water, electricity or gas , artificial , natural
or mixed , shall be fined not less than fifty dollars
nor more than five hundred dollars , or imprisoned
for not less than ten days nor more than one year,
or both so fined and imprisoned .
§ 3. Any person who has in his possession any
machine , appliance or contrivance , of any character ,
used or intended to be used to prevent a meter from
correctly registering the water , electric current or
gas passing through the meter or for diverting the
water, electric current or gas that should pass
through the meter , shall be fined not less than fifty
dollars nor more than five hundred dollars , or im-
prisoned for not less than three months nor more
than twelve months , or both so fined and im-
prisoned . The possession of such a machine , ap-
pliance or contrivance shall be prima facie evidence
of intent to violate and of the violation of this sec-
tion , and any such machine , appliance or contrivance
found on any premises or attached to a meter or
wires or pipes through which water , electric cur-
rent or gas is distributed shall for the purposes of
this section be deemed to be in the possession of the
user of the water, electric current or gas at or upon
the premises where so found .
§ 4. Any person who knowingly accepts and re-
ceives the use and benefit of any water , electric
current or gas diverted in violation of section one
of this Act shall be fined not less than fifty dollars
nor more than five hundred dollars , or imprisoned

CHAPTER 47 319
in the county jail for not less than three months nor
more than one year , or both so fined and imprisoned .
5. An indictment shall be valid which charges
any or all of the acts denounced in this Act , in one
count , and all of said acts may be charged and em-
braced in a single count in an indictment.
§ 6. Sections 1252a - 1 to 1252a -5 , inclusive , and
1262a - 1 to 1262a -5 , inclusive , of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , are repealed .
$ 7. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, this Act shall be treated as repealing KRS
433.520 to 433.610 , regardless of the date of enact-
ment or taking effect of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
Approved February 26 , 1942 .

CHAPTER 48
( S. B. 4 )
AN ACT providing for the consolidation of counties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. When a petition signed by not less than five
percents ( 5 % ) of the registered legal voters of any
County, and no petition shall be signed by less than
200 legal voters of any County shall be filed at a

CHAPTER 48 321
regular term of the county court thereof , asking
that said county be consolidated with a named ad-
joining county , it shall be the duty of the county
court to notify the county court of the adjoining
county . If within six months from the time of said
notice of the first county to the second county a
petition , similarly signed by not less than five per-
cents ( 5 % ) of the registered legal voters of any
County, and no petition shall be signed by less than
200 legal voters of any County , has been filed , for
the consolidation of the two counties , the county
court of the second county shall notify the county
court of the first county of such action .
2. If such a petition is filed with the county
court of the second county more than six months.
from the time of the filing of the petition in the
first county , the petition filed in the first county
shall be of no effect , but a new petition , signed as
provided in the previous section , may be filed in the
first county , and , if it is filed within six months of
the filing of the petition in the second county , it
shall be effective for the purpose of authorizing the
calling of an election in each county as provided in
the following section .
3. If such a petition is filed in the second county
within six months of the filing of the petition in the
first county , or if a new petition is filed in the first
county within six months of the filing of the petition
in the second county , it shall be the duty of each
of the county courts of the two counties , by orders
entered of record , to call an election to be held , and
direct a poll to be taken in the various precincts
in each county on the day of the election to be held
in said counties on the first Tuesday after the first
Monday in November following the filing of the
petition as provided herein , which does not occur
within less than ninety days of the time of the filing
Acts- 11

322 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of an effective petition in the county in which one
is last filed , at which election the proposition for
the consolidation of the two named counties shall be
submitted to the legal voters of each of the two
counties for their approval or rejection .
4. It shall be the duty of the county court of each
county , by order of record , to direct the sheriff of
that county to advertise said election , and the object
thereof, in the newspapers published in the county ,
and if none should be published therein , then by
printed handbills posted up at not less than four
of the most public places in each precinct and at
the court house door.
5. It shall be the duty of the officers of said
election in each voting precinct to hold said elec-
tion the same hours they are by law required to
hold election for county officers and said election
shall be held and conducted in all respects under
the general election laws obtaining in this Com-
monwealth at the time of the election .
6. The result of the election in each county shall
be certified by the county election commissioners
to the county judge in each county . If a majority
of the voters of each county , voting on the propo-
sition , approve the consolidation , the county judge
of each county shall , within 30 days after the elec-
tion , appoint a commission to be composed of three
voters of the county for the purpose of dividing the
area of the new county into districts for the election
of members of the fiscal court for the new county .
The said commissioners representing the two coun-
ties shall meet together within fifteen ( 15 ) days
after their appointment and proceed to divide the
area of the new county into three districts , each to
be as nearly equal in area and population as pos-
sible . The commissions also shall select not less
than two and not more than three names for the

CHAPTER 48 323
new county , and shall select not less than two nor
more than three sites for the county seat of the new
county . The report of the commissions , which shall
act jointly, shall be filed with the county court of
each county not less than ninety (90 ) days after the
appointment of the commissions .
If, at the expiration of ninety days after the six
commissioners have been appointed as provided for
herein , a majority of said commissioners have failed
to agree upon the selection of three names for the
proposed county or three places for the county seat
to be submitted to the voters of the county as pro-
vided for herein, the Governor of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky shall appoint a seventh person
to act as Chairman of said Board of Commissioners
and said Chairman shall not have a vote on any
question except in case of tie vote . The report of
the Commissioner, when Chairman is appointed ,
shall be filed with County Court of each County ,
not less than thirty ( 30 ) days after appointment
of chairman .
7. The report of the commissions shall be en-
tered on the records of each county court . At the
next general election to be held in the counties for
the election of county officers , officers for the new
county shall be chosen . Said election shall be held
and conducted in all respects under the general
election laws obtaining in this Commonwealth at
the time of the election . Members of the fiscal
court for the new county shall be elected , one from
each of the three districts created by the commis-
sions , under the provisions of the act providing for
the election of county commissioners under the com-
mission form of government for the counties , or any
amendments thereto .
8. At the general election for the election of new
officers for the new county , the voters of the com-

324 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
bined area to be embraced within the new county
shall select a name for the new county and a site for
the county seat from among the names and sites
recommended by the commissions as provided
herein . The question of the selection of a name and
county seat for the new county shall be submitted

CHAPTER 48 325
The new county shall bear the name approved by

Whenever an election is held in any County on

9. The territory formerly embraced within each
of the old counties is hereby made into a separate

taxes , and the separate debt of each county shall be
a first lien on the territory formerly embraced
within that county . The fiscal court of the new
county shall fix the rate of tax levy for each of the
two special taxing districts , and the property within
each shall be assessed and the taxes collected by
the same county officers of the new county , and in
the same manner that the law provides for the
assessment of property and the collection of taxes
by counties . The money collected as taxes for each
of the special taxing districts shall be kept separate
and shall be used only in payment of the debts of the
old county that formerly embraced the territory of
that special taxing district .
10. Where the two old counties were in the same
representative , senatorial , circuit court , judicial ,

326 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
congressional , or railroad commissioner district or
districts , the new county shall remain in the same
district or districts . Where the two old counties
were not in the same district or districts , the new
county shall be placed in the following district or
districts : In the representative , senatorial , circuit
court, judicial , congressional , or railroad commis-
sioner district or districts , respectively , in which
the old county , having the largest population , was
located ; provided , that if each of the two old coun-
ties was a separate district the new county shall be
divided into two such districts , each district to be
coterminous with the boundary lines of the old
counties ; provided further , that if any of the elec-
tion districts formed under the provisions of this
act as a result of county consolidation are in viola-
tion of the requirements of the Constitution , such
consolidation shall not be effective unless the
General Assembly, at its first session after the con
solidation , shall redistrict the state in compliance
with the provisions of the Constitution .
Section 11. All records , supplies , and equipment
of the old counties shall be turned over to the proper
officials of the new county .
Section 12. In case of the consolidation of coun-
ties as provided in this Act , the counties as con-
solidated shall constitute one county school district
and the members of the boards of education of the
county school districts thus consolidated may serve
out the terms for which they were elected and
qualified . The board of education of this consoli-
dated district shall operate the schools of said dis-
trict in accordance with the laws providing for the
operation of county school districts .
The district resulting from the consolidation of
said school districts shall take over all the assests *
* Assets .

CHAPTER 48 327
and legal liabilities of the districts joining in the
consolidation ; the tax levy authorized for the pay-
ment of interest and the retirment of bonds or the
provision for sinking funds for such purposes shall
continue to be levied and collected over the same
area by or for the new board in accordance with the
laws under which the levies were originally made
until all bonded obligations of the old district or
districts shall have been retired .
The county superintendents in office at the time
of said consolidation may serve for the remainder of
their contract periods . They shall cast lots at the
first meeting of the new board of education to deter-
mine which one will serve as superintendent and
which one will serve as assistant superintendent for
this remaining contract period .
Section 13. In case either , or both , of the coun-
ties that have consolidated under the provisions of
this Act has a county health department , county
library board , water district board , and other
similar county agencies , the members thereof shall
serve out the terms for which they were respectively
elected . In case both counties have a board of the
same type the consolidated county shall constitute
a consolidated territory for the purpose of exercis-
ing all the functions of such boards , and such boards
shall operate together as one board until the expira-
tion of the terms of all members . Provision shall
be made for the election of a new board for the
condolidated + county as the expiration of the terms
of the old members requires . In case only one of
the counties has a board of the type contemplated
by this section the authority of such board shall be
restricted to the confines of the territory that com-
posed the original territory .
Approved March 22 , 1942 .

CHAPTER 49
(S. B. 85 )
AN ACT relating to commissioners of the Court of Appeals
and amending Section 962b - 1 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 962b - 1 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition is hereby amended and re-
enacted to read as follows :
The judges of the Court of Appeals , a majority of
them concurring , are hereby authorized to appoint
four commissioners , who shall hold office at the
pleasure of the court and who shall discharge such
duties as may be assigned to them by the court .
Every commissioner so appointed must have the
qualifications prescribed by law for a judge of the
Court of Appeals and as herein prescribed . Each
commissioner shall receive the same salary , to be
paid at the same time , and in the same manner , as a
judge of the Court of Appeals , and shall also be
allowed for clerical assistance the same amount
which is allowed by law to a judge of the Court of
Appeals . Each commissioner shall be provided
with an office in the Capitol at Frankfort , and the
same shall be furnished in a way suitable for his
duties by the Division of Purchases and Public
Properties , and the expenses therefor shall be paid
by a warrant to be drawn by the Department of
Finance upon the State Treasurer . When the busi-
ness of the court does not require their services the
court shall terminate the appointment of any or all
of the commissioners .
In addition to the four commissioners herein pro-
vided for , the judges of the Court of Appeals are
hereby authorized to , and may in their discretion ,

CHAPTER 50 329
appoint as additional commissioners such judges of
the Court of Appeals as may hereafter retire from
office either voluntarily or because of the expiration
of their terms and who have served continuously
more than eight years as a member of said Court ,
upon the same terms and conditions of tenure and
appointment and with the same salaries and priv-
ileges as are applicable to other commissioners , and
they shall perform such duties and render such
services as may be required by the court . They
shall also be eligible for appointment by the Gover-
nor under Section 117 of the Constitution as special
judges of the Court of Appeals , and when so ap-
pointed shall serve without additional compensa-
tion.
2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly, section one of this Act shall , upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of KRS 21.150 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 21.150
rather than an amendment to § 962b - 1 of Carroll's
Statutes .
Approved March 2 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 50
(S. B. 88 )
AN ACT requiring instruction in the public schools and col-
leges as to the effect of alcholic liquors and narcotics upon
the human system
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
The nature of alcoholic liquor and of narcotics
and their injurious effects on the human system shall
be taught in each of the grades , four to ten inclusive ,
of the common schools . It shall be the duty of the

330 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
superintendent and principal of every school and
the president of every university , college or
academy to have presented for a period of 30 min-
utes to the entire student body in assembly , at
least on two occasions each term or semester by an
appropriate program , the scientific , social and
moral aspects of alcoholic beverages , stimulants
and narcotics .
The failure of any superintendent , principal or
teacher to comply with the terms of this act
shall be deemed a cause for the revocation of his
contract of employment , and upon notice to the
Board of Education employing such superintendent ,
principal or teacher that he has failed to do so it
shall be the duty of said Board to conduct a hearing
and if the charge be proved to dismiss or discharge
said superintendent , principal or teacher .
When text books on physiology and hygiene
shall be hereafter adopted or approved for the
schools , such books shall contain a substantial text ,
to be approved by the Superintendent of Public
Instruction , devoted to the nature of alcoholic
liquors and narcotics and their effect upon the
human system .
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Approved March 2 , 1942 .

CHAPTER 52
(H. B. 57)
AN ACT pertaining to the covenants of county officers ; surety
bond ; and premium to be paid by the county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
All elective or appointive county officers of any
county containing a population of 200,000 or more
who , upon assuming their respective offices , are re-
quired under the law to execute a bond for the faith-
ful discharge and performance of their duties , and
who shall have such bond executed by any incorpo-
rated surety company authorized to do a surety
business in the State of Kentucky , shall pay the
premium on such bond , and the amount paid for

332 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
such premium shall be and become a valid claim
against the county , except where the law provides
that it be paid by the state , and shall be paid in the
same manner as other claims are paid . In each

premium on the bond executed as above set out , the
officer executing the bond as principal shall verify
the claim by his affidavit .
Approved March 2nd ., 1942 .

CHAPTER 54
(H. B. 129 )
AN ACT relating to a transfer out of interest in the Unem-
ployment Trust Fund , and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
The Kentucky Unemployment Compensation
Commission shall, notwithstanding any other pro-
visions of law , authorize and direct the Secretary of
the Treasury of the United States to transfer to the
railroad unemployment insurance account out of
interest hereafter credited to the State's account in
the unemployment trust fund pursuant to Section.
904 of the Social Security Act , an amount equal to
(a ) such proportion of the balance in the pooled
account as of June 30 , 1939 as the amount of contri-
butions paid by employees and employers subject to

334 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the Railroad Unemployment Insurance Act and
credited to such pooled account bears to the total of
all contributions theretofore collected under the
Kentucky Unemployment Compensation Law and
credited to such pooled account , plus (b ) contribu-
tions paid by employees and employers subject to
the Railroad Unemployment Insurance Act and
credited to the pooled account after June 30 , 1939
and prior to January 1 , 1940 , plus ( c ) an additional
amount equal to two and one -half per centum per
annum on the untransferred balances on the fore-
going amounts directed to be transferred , computed
from the date the Social Security Board determined
this State's " preliminary amount " and " liquidating
amount " pursuant to Section 13 ( c ) of the Railroad
Unemployment Insurance Act until the transfer

come effective upon its passage and approval by the
Governor . Approved March 2 , 1942 .

CHAPTER 56
(H. B. 128 )
AN ACT relating to the commission form of Government in
cities of the fifth and sixth class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Fifth and sixth class cities may re - organize
government under this law . That any city of the
fifth or sixth class , under the laws of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky may become organized and
governed under the provisions of this Act by pro-
ceeding as hereinafter provided . Organizing under
this act shall not change the corporate entity of any
such city , but the body politic and corporate shall
remain the same body that it now is .
§ 2. Existing laws and ordinances remain in
force where not in conflict herewith .-All laws
applicable to and governing cities of the fifth or
sixth class and not inconsistent with the provisions.
of this act shall continue to apply to and govern
each city that may organize under this act . And all
by-laws , ordinances and resolutions in force in any
such city and not inconsistent with the provisions of
this act , shall continue to be in force until altered or
repealed in manner provided for in this act .

336 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 3. Election to be called ; ballot ; duty of county
clerk and sheriff ; canvassing board , duty of.— ( 1 )
The county judge in the county in which is located a
city proposing to take the sense of its voters upon
the question of adopting the commission form of
government , shall , upon the application by written
petition signed by a number of legal voters of the
city equal to twenty -five per cent of the votes cast in
the city at the last preceding general election ,
receive the petition , and at the next regular term
after that make an order in his book directing an
election to be held in the city at the next regular
election , and not earlier than sixty days nor later
than ninety days after the application is lodged with
the judge . The order shall direct the sheriff or
other officer of the county who may be appointed to
hold the election , to open the polls at each voting
place in the city for the purpose of taking the sense
of the qualified voters of the city upon the question
of whether or not the citizens in the city are in favor
of the organization and government of the city
under this act . The question to be submitted to the
voters shall be : " Are you in favor of the organiza-
tion and government of the city of
under the commission form of government ? "
( 2 ) The county clerk shall give to the sheriff of
the county , or to such officer as may be appointed to
hold the election , a certified copy of the order of the
county court as it appears on the order book within
five days after the order is made . The sheriff or
other officer shall have the order published in some
weekly or daily newspaper published or circulated
in the county , for at least two weeks before the
election , and also advertise the same by printed or
written handbills , posted in conspicuous places in
the city, for the same length of time . If there is no
weekly or daily newspaper published in the city , or
"

CHAPTER 56 337
the proprietor of the paper refuses to publish the
notice , the printed or written handbills provided for
shall be sufficient notice . The sheriff or other such
officer shall have the advertisement and notices
posted as required by this section within seven days
after he receives the order of the county court .
(3 ) If a majority of the legal votes cast at the
election were given for or against the proposition
submitted , the canvassing board shall certify that
fact . The certificate shall be delivered to the county
clerk and a copy of the certificate shall be delivered.
to the clerk of the city , and at the next regular term
of the county court the county judge shall cause the
certificate to be spread on the order book of the
court . The entry of the certificate in the order book ,
or copy of it shall be prima facie evidence of the
facts contained in it.
(4 ) When a majority of the votes cast are in
favor of organizing the governing the city under
this act , that fact is certified , as provided in sub-
section ( 3 ) , by the canvassing board of the county to
the county clerk, a copy of the certificate is certified
to the city clerk, and a copy of the certificate is
spread upon the records of the county , and of the
city at the next regular meeting of the city legisla-
tive body , the city shall be organized and governed
by this act .
Section 4. Offices abolished . All the present
officers of any city which has voted in favor of
adopting the commission form of government shall
hold their offices until the expiration of their terms ,
and all of such offices , except that of police judge ,
shall be ipso facto abolished at the expiration of
their terms .
Section 5. Election of commissioners . If the
commission form of government is adopted , an

338 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
election for commissioners shall be held at the
regular November election next following the elec-
tion at which it was voted to adopt the commission
form of government , and at the regular November
election every two years thereafter . If, at the time
of the first election for commissioners , the term of
the mayor then in office will not expire for two
years , there shall be elected two commissioners who ,
with the mayor , shall constitute the board of com-
missioners . Under all other circumstances three
commissioners shall be elected .
$ 6. Term of office ; salary ; mayor; mayor pro-
tem .-Each commissioner shall be elected for a term
of two years beginning the first Monday of the
month following his election . Each commissioner
shall receive an annual salary of not less than
twenty -four dollars nor more than three hundred
dollars , the exact amount to be decided by the out-
going legislative body and mayor or board of
trustees at their meeting before retiring . The com-
missioners shall within thirty days after their
election meet and organize themselves , one to be
selected as presiding officer , or mayor and one to be
elected mayor protem .
$ 7. Board of commissioners ; members , powers ,
quorum .- ( 1 ) The mayor and two commissioners
shall constitute a board of commissioners . In this
board of commissioners shall be vested all the
legislative , executive and administrative power of
the city .
( 2 ) Two members of the commission shall con-
stitute a quorum and the affirmative vote of at least
two members shall be necessary to the adoption of
any motion , resolution or ordinance , to the making
or approval of any contract or to the passage of any
measure .

CHAPTER 56 339
-§ 8. Qualification of commissioners ; bond .
Each commissioner shall be able to read and to
write any section of the Constitution of this state
and shall execute a bond to the city , upon which an
action may be maintained by any person interested
in the keeping of the covenants contained in the
bond . The bond shall be in the penal sum of five
hundred dollars and shall be approved by the county
judge .
$ 9. Meetings of board of commissioners .-The
board of commissioners shall meet at least twice each
month for the transaction of its business . It shall
fix by ordinance the times of holding its regular
meetings . Any special meeting may be called by the
mayor or by two commissioners . All meetings shall
be public .
$ 10. Employes , appointment , duties , compensa-
tion, merit system to be used .— ( 1 ) The board of
commissioners shall, at its first meeting or as soon
after that as may be practicable , appoint all such
employes as may be necessary for the proper and
efficient conduct of the affairs of the city .
(2 ) All such employes shall be agents , not
officers , of the city , and shall perform such duties
and for such compensation as the board of commis-
sioners may by ordinance prescribe . Each employe
shall be selected with reference solely to his fitness
and for the good of the public service , without
reference to his political faith or party affiliation ,
and the board of commissioners shall adopt such
regulations looking to the appointment and dis-
charge of employes as will carry out the spirit of
this section and lead to the establishment of the
merit system in public service .
$ 11. Abandonment of commission government ;
election by voters .-Whenever the citizens of any

340 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
city which has been organized and governed under
this act for a period of four years desires that the
organization and government of the city under this
act shall cease , they shall proceed to ascertain the
sense of the voters by the same process by which the
commission form of government was established .
The question to be submitted to the voters shall be :
" Shall the city of
abandon its organization and government under the
commission form of government ?" If a majority
of the votes cast are in favor of abandonment the
city shall be organized and governed by such laws
as apply to cities of the fifth and sixth class , inde-
pendent of this act .
§ 12. Sections 2741f- 1 to 2741f- 11 of Carroll's
Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , are repealed .
§ 13. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly, sections 1 and 2 of this Act shall stand
repealed as of the effective date of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , section 3 to 11 of this Act shall be
substituted in place of KRS 89.300 to 89.380 , and
KRS 89.010 and 89.020 shall remain in full force and
effect and shall not be treated as affected by this
Act .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 57
(S. B. 96 )
AN ACT authorizing counties , municipalities and other taxing
districts to invest their sinking funds or other funds eligible
for investment in United States Defense Bonds or any
other obligations for the payment of which the faith and
credit of the United States Government is pledged , and
declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , many classes of municipalities and taxing districts
are restricted to investment in specified United States bonds

CHAPTER 57 341
and securities which excludes them from all others includ-
ing the United States Defense Bonds now on the market , and
WHEREAS , many cities and other taxing districts have idle
sinking and other funds eligible for investment which could
be safely invested in United States Defense bonds and assist
the Government in this National emergency,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section of of Chapter 21 of the Acts
of 1940 is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) This state and all public officers , cities ,
political subdivisions , and public bodies , all banks ,
trust companies , savings institutions , building and
loan associations , savings and loan associations ,
investment companies and other persons carrying
on a banking business , all insurance companies , and
other persons carrying on an insurance business ,
and all fiduciaries may legally invest any sinking
funds , moneys or other funds belonging to them or

tions issued by a housing commission pursuant to

amendments thereto , or issued by any public hous-
ing authority or agency in the United States , when
these bonds or other obligations are secured by a
pledge of annual contributions to be paid by the
United States or any agency thereof.
( 2 ) These bonds and other obligations shall be
authorized security for all public deposits and shall
be fully negotiable in this state .
( 3) Nothing in this section with regard to
legal investments shall relieve any individual or
corporation from any duty of exercising reasonable
care in selecting securities .
(4 ) Any county , municipality or other taxing
district may legally invest its sinking funds , money

342 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or other funds belonging to it or within its control in
United States Defense Bonds or any other bonds or
obligations for the payment of which the faith and
credit of the United States Government is pledged .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly, section 1 of this Act shall , upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes be
substituted in place of KRS 386.050 , and shall
thereafter be treated as an amendment to KRS
386.050 rather than an amendment to Section 1 of
Chapter 21 of the Acts of 1940 .
Section 3. Because of the present pressing
necessity for assisting the Federal Government , an
emergency is declared and this Act shall go into
effect from and after its final passage and approval
by the Governor .
Approved February 26 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 58
(S. B. 89)
AN ACT relating to Old Age Assistance , Aid to Dependent
Children and Needy Blind
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. All applications for Old Age Assist-
ance , Aid to Dependent Children and Needy Blind
and all records concerning applicants for , or
recipients of , Old Age Assistance , Aid to Dependent

CHAPTER 58 343
to Old Age Assistance , Aid to Dependent Children

Section 2. No disclosure of the names and

cipients of Old Age Assistance , Aid to Dependent

of 1940 is repealed
Section 5. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are

Approved February 26 , 1942 .

344 ACTS OF THE General ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 59
(H. B. 100 )
AN ACT relating to registration of voters .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 1486bb -10 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as
follows :
( 1 ) When a voter registered outside of cities of
the first class changes his place of residence from
one precinct to another in the same county , he may
apply to the county clerk in person or by letter or
by form which may be provided to have his regis-
tration record transferred to the proper precinct .
The clerk shall then mail a notice to the address of
the voter appearing on the existing registration
records , stating that such application has been made
and the particulars thereof, and that the change will
be made unless protest is filed at the office of the
county clerk within ten days from the date of the
notice . If no protest is filed within the period of
ten days the county clerk shall change the regis-
tration record to the proper precinct as requested .
If protest is filed within the ten -day period , the
change shall not be made until the clerk has in-
vestigated the matter and given the protesting
voter an opportunity to be heard . When a change
is so made , the clerk shall , under the head
" remarks " on his original and copy registration
record forms , write the words " removed to
precinct , " and the date . Such
transfers shall be registered only on those days
when registrations are allowed under section
1496-14 .
( 2 ) When the boundaries of any precinct are
changed by law and the change places a registered

CHAPTER 59 345
voter in a new or different precinct, the county
clerk shall transfer the individual registration
record of the voter to the precinct registrar for the
precinct in which he is placed by the change of
boundary . The transfer shall be made auto-
matically , without any application therefor by the
voter . The date on which the change is made and
the reason therefor shall be noted on the indi-
vidual's registration record , and a notice thereof
shall be mailed by the county clerk to the voter , ad-
vising him thereof and the reason therefor .
§ 2. Section 1496-14 of Baldwin's 1938 Supple-
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , as amended
by Chapter 75 of the Acts of 1940 , is amended to
read as follows :
(1 ) Any person possessing the qualifications
for registration as a legal voter may appear before
the county clerk of the county of his residence , or at
the permanent office or a branch office of the board.
of registration commissioners if he resides in a city
of the first class , and register , or change his party
affiliation , at an hour of any day that the office of
the county clerk or the board of registration com-
missioners is open for business . The county clerk
of every county and the board of registration com-
missioners of every city of the first class shall
cause all registration books or lists to be closed
fifty - nine days prior to any primary or general
election , and no voter shall be registered or be per-
mitted to change his registration during the period
that the books are closed . The county clerk and
board of registration commissioners shall not again
reopen their books for registration of voters or the
reregistering or changing of party affiliation of
voters until ten days after any primary or general
election .

346 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(2 ) In counties containing a city of the third
class that is not the county seat, the county clerk
shall take the registration books for each voting
precinct in the city to the courthouse or city hall
of the city and keep them there during the period
the books are open . Any person possessing the
qualifications for registration as a legal voter of
any voting precinct in the city may appear before
the clerk during the period the books are open at
the office or place in the courthouse or city hall of
the city where the registration books are taken , at
any hour of the day the office or place is open for
business , and register or change his party affiliation .
(3) Any voter who wishes to change his party
affiliation and who does not wish to apply in person
may apply to the county clerk, or to the board of
registration commissioners if he resides in a city of
the first class , by letter or by form which may be
provided . The clerk or board shall then mail a
notice to the address of the voter appearing on the
existing registration records , stating that such ap-
plication has been made and the particulars thereof ,
and that the change will be made unless protest is
filed at the office of the clerk or the board within ten
days from the date of the notice . If no protest is
filed within the ten -day period, the county clerk or
the board shall change the party affiliation of the
voter as requested . If a protest is filed , the change
shall not be made until the clerk or the board has
investigated the matter and given the protesting
voter an opportunity to be heard . The date on
which the change is made shall be noted on the
voter's registration record . No change of party
affiliation shall be made except on those days when
registrations are allowed under sub - section ( 1 ) of
this section .

ments to KRS 117.100 and 117.050 rather than

shall be substituted for the number " section

CHAPTER 60
(H. B. 144 )
AN ACT to regulate the practice of dental hygiene , and vest-
ing the regulation thereof in the Kentucky State Board of
Dental Examiners .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Definitions.- ( a ) The term " dental
hygiene " as used in this act shall mean the treat-
ment of human teeth by removing therefrom cal-
careous deposits , and by removing accumulated ac
cretion from directly beneath the free margin of the
gums , and by polishing the exposed surface of the
teeth : Provided that nothing of this act shall be so
construed as to affect the practice of medicine or
dentistry nor to prevent students of a dental col-
lege or university from practicing dental hygiene
under supervision of their instructors .

348 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(b) The term " dental hygienist " as used in this
act shall mean any person who practices dental
hygiene .
(c ) The term " Board " as used in this act shall
mean the Kentucky State Board of Dental Ex-
aminers , existing pursuant to the provisions of Sec-
tion 2636-1 of the Kentucky Statutes , and amend-
ments thereto .
Section 2 . Administration .- The Board is here-
by vested with authority and charged with the duty
of administering the provisions of this act .
Section 3. Powers and Duties of Board . The
Board shall meet as often and at such places as it
deems necessary for examination of applicants who
desire to practice dental hygiene in this State . The
Board shall keep a record of the names , addresses ,
and license numbers , of all persons legally entitled
to practice dental hygiene in this State .
The Board may adopt and enforce such rules and
regulations as it deems necessary and reasonable to
carry out the provisions of this act .
Section 4. Compensation to Members of Board .
-The members of the Board shall be compensated
and shall receive their expenses for administering
the provisions of this act to the same extent and in
the same manner as they are compensated and re-
imbursed for expenses in administering the pro-
visions of Chapter 148 of the Acts of the 1938
General Assembly , as provided by Section 10 of said
Chapter .
Section 5. Eligibility .-Any person of good
moral character over twenty -one years of age who is
a citizen of the United States of America , a grad-
uate of an accredited high school , and has success-
fully completed two years of training in a school of

CHAPTER 60 349
dental hygiene that is approved by the Board , shall
be eligible to take the examination for the practice
of dental hygiene .
Section 6. Examination , Licenses , and Fees.-
Any eligible person desiring to practice dental
hygiene shall make written application to the Board
to take the examination . Such application shall be
accompanied by a written recommendation of two
reputable dentists of this State , and by an examina-
tion fee of Twenty ( $ 20 . ) dollars , which sum is
authorized to be charged for each examination by
the Board .
As soon as the Board has determined that an
applicant has successfully passed the examination ,
a license to practice dental hygiene in this State
shall be issued by the Board to such applicant . The
license shall be for one calendar year or for the
remaining portion thereof if issued as of a date
after January 1. Each license shall be renewed
from year to year , except for cause as herein
provided .
The Board shall charge three ( $3 . ) dollars for
each license or duplicate license or renewal license
issued , which sum shall be paid in advance or at
the time the license is issued .
Section 7. Practitioners .-No person shall prac-
tice dental hygiene nor hold himself or herself out
as a dental hygienist without a license issued by the
Board , and unless he or she is an employee of a
licensed and practicing dentist of this State and is
under the continuous supervision and inspection of
such dentist . The license shall be continuously dis-
played in a conspicuous place in the office of the
licensee .
Section 8. Signature , Seal , etc. , on License .-All
licenses issued by the Board shall bear a serial

350 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
number , the full name of the applicant , the date of
the issue , the seal of the Board , and be signed by a
majority of the members , and attested by its Presi-
dent and Secretary .
Section 9. Revocation or Suspension of License .
-The Board may , upon complaint or upon its own
motion , after a hearing , and after ten days notice ,
in writing , to the licensee of the time and place of
hearing , revoke or suspend or refuse to renew the
license of any dental hygienist by entering an order
to that effect , together with its findings in respect
thereto , if it shall be determined that such licensee :
( 1 ) Has violated any of the provisions of this
act or any regulation made hereunder ; or
(2) Has made a material false statement in his
application for examination ; or
( 3) No longer possess good moral character ; or
(4 ) Directly or indirectly advertises or solicits
for dental hygiene business ; or
(5 ) Is grossly inefficient in his profession .
In any proceeding to revoke , suspend or refuse to
renew the license of any licensee a certified copy of
any order entered by the Board shall immediately
be delivered or sent to the licensee by registered
mail at his last known address .
Any licensee aggrieved by an order of the Board
may , within thirty days from the date of entry of
said order , appeal to the circuit court in and for
the county in which was held the meeting of the
Board at which the action of the Board complained
of was taken . In case a person desires to take such
an appeal he shall file with the clerk of the circuit
court to which the appeal is taken a petition setting
forth the grounds of appeal , and shall file with said
clerk an appeal bond with good and sufficient se-
curity , to be approved by said clerk , to the State of

CHAPTER 60 351
Kentucky , conditioned for the speedy prosecution
of such appeal , and the payment of such costs as
may be charged against him upon such appeal .
Summons on such an appeal shall be issued and
may be served on the President or Secretary of the
Board in any county in this State where either of
them may be found . Within ten days after the
service of summons on such appeal , the Secretary
shall transmit to the clerk of the circuit court , to
which said appeal is taken , a certified copy , under
the seal of the Board , all evidence considered and
orders entered by the Board in connection with said
proceeding . The clerk of such court shall thereupon
docket such appeal . The court shall only hear and
consider such documentary and other evidence as
was heard and considered by the Board , and that is
contained in the transcript upon which the appeal
is taken . Either party may appeal from the judg-
ment of the circuit court to the Court of Appeals in
the manner as civil actions may be appealed thereto .
The order of the board appealed from shall be
stayed from the date of the approval of said bond
until final determination of the appeal .
Section 10. Disposition of Fees . All fees im-
posed and collected under the provisions of this act
shall be paid to the Secretary -Treasurer of the
board for the use of the Board , and shall be de-
posited in the State Treasury to the credit of a
revolving fund for the use of the Board .
-Section 11. Penalties . Any person violating
any provisions of this act shall be deemed guilty of
a misdemeanor , and upon conviction thereof shall
be punished by a fine not to exceed fifty ( $50 . ) dol-
lars , or by punishment in the county jail for a term
not to exceed thirty days , or by both such fine and
imprisonment .

352 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 12. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly , the number " KRS 313.200 " shall be sub-
stituted for " Section 2636-1 of the Kentucky
Statutes , and amendments thereto , " in subsection
( c ) of Section 1 of this Act , and the words and
figures " Chapter 313 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , as provided in KRS 313.070 , 313.200 and
313.220 " shall be substituted in place of " Chapter
148 of the Acts of the 1938 General Assembly , as
provided by Section 10 of said Chapter " in Section
4 of this Act.
Approved March 2 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 61
(H. B. 320 )
AN ACT to provide for the condemnation of property by cities
of the second class during time of war .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. During such time as a state of war
may exist , pursuant to the declaration of the Con-
gress of the United States of America , The Board
of Commissioners or other governing body of any
city of the second class may , if the acquisition of
land be material to the national defense , or of any
governmental project directly related to the war
effort , by declaring such fact by resolution , order
the condemnation of said land , either within or out
of said city , pursuant to the provisions of Sections.
3095 to 3095g , with the following exceptions :
.
( 1 ) The judge shall, upon the filing of a peti-
tion , grant possession of said land by interlocutory
judgment rendered immediately after the filing of
the report by the commissioners duly appointed

CHAPTER 61 353
pursuant to the provisions of Section 3095a . Prior
to the rendering of such interlocutory judgment , the
city shall be required to pay into court the sum
awarded by the commissioners in their report . The
court may , after having rendered said interlocu-
tory judgment upon motion by the city , cause its
commissioners to execute to the city a deed of con-
veyance , which deed shall be executed as nearly as
practicable in accordance with the rules governing
the conveyance of property by commissioners when
sold under order of court .
( 2 ) The commissioners appointed pursuant to
Section 3095a may report at any time after sum-
mons has been duly issued or warning order at-
torney appointed , and the court shall thereafter
award possession to the city by interlocutory judg
ment , as hereinbefore set out . Thereafter , either
party may , before answer day , file written excep-
tions to said report , as provided in Section 3095c ,
except that the city's exceptions , if any , shall be
made prior to the entry of said interlocutory judg-
ment . The sum not in controversy shall be paid or
tendered to the parties defendant by the court prior
to the entry of said interlocutory judgment .
( 3 ) Before rendering the interlocutory judg-
ment , the court shall require the city to execute a
bond , with surety to be approved by the court, con-
ditioned upon the city paying to the parties de
fendant the difference between the sum paid into
court and that which may be thereafter determined
by a jury to be the value of the property and in-
cidental damages , if any , if the parties defendant
except to the report of the commissioners , unless
the city shall have sufficient funds duly appro-
priated and on deposit to pay any money judgments
that may be thereafter given against it , and such
fact be alleged in its petition .
Acts- 12

354 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(4 ) The city shall not be permitted to abandon
the proceeding after having obtained title to said
land unless none of the parties defendant will be
materially prejudiced thereby .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly, the references in section one of this Act
to "Sections 3095 to 3095g , 3095a and 3095c " shall
be admitted as referring to KRS 94.680 to 94.720 ,
94.690 and 94.700 , respectively .
Section 3. WHEREAS , a state of war exists
and the development and completion of projects.
material or directly related to the war effort de-
pends upon the immediate acquisition of the pos-
session of land required for such projects , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall take
effect upon its passage and approval by the Gov.
ernor .
Approved March 2 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 62
(H. B. 211 )
AN ACT to create the office of assistant county attorney in
counties having a population of less than one hundred and
fifty thousand ; to provide for his appointment by the
county attorney ; to fix his qualifications and term ; to define
his powers and duties ; to provide for his compensation ;
to repeal section 131 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936
edition ; and declaring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS duly elected and qualified county attorneys of
several counties in Kentucky are now absent in the mili-
tary service of the United States of America , and others
may be inducted into said service at any time , and suitable
and proper legislation providing for the exercise of their
powers and discharge of their duties should be enacted :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
4444

CHAPTER 62 355
§ 1. The office of assistant county attorney in
counties having a population of less than one hun-
dred and fifty thousand is hereby created .
§ 2. The assistant county attorney shall be ap-
pointed by the county attorney for the duration or
unexpired part of his term, but shall be subject to
removal at any time by the county attorney .
3. The assistant county attorney shall possess
the qualifications of a county attorney , and during
the absence from the county of the county attorney
or his inability to attend shall act as county at-
torney and have the same powers and perform all
the duties now or hereafter imposed on county
attorneys by law , and while so acting shall be paid
and receive the fees and commissions that the
county attorney would have received if present and
acting , the county attorney to continue to receive
the salary fixed by the fiscal court. But in no event
shall the total amount of salaries , fees and commis-
sions paid the county attorney and his assistant
county attorney acting under the provisions hereof
exceed the sum of $5,000.00 in any one year .
4. Section 131 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is hereby repealed .
5. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are enacted
at the present session of the General Assembly , this
Act shall be treated as repealing KRS 69.270 , rc-
gardless of the date of enactment or taking effect
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
6. Inasmuch as several county attorneys are
now absent in the military service of the United
States of America and there is now no suitable or
proper provision for the discharge of the duties of
said absentees during their absence , an emergency

356 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
is declared to exist , and this Act shall take effect
and be in force from and after its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .
Approved March 2 , 1942 .
