CHAPTER 65 359
CHAPTER 65
(H. B. 134 )
AN ACT providing for the creation and establishment of
public road districts in counties containing cities of the first
class in order to provide the public and person residing
upon such roads with all -weather roads , providing for the
filing of a petition for the creation of such a road district
by fifty- one percent of the property owners thereon , provid-
ing for a hearing before the County Court , for the filing of
objections by land owners , for appeals to the Court of
Appeals , for the appointment of a board of directors of the
road district , providing for their duties and salaries , provid-
ing for the holding of regular meetings of the board of
directors , the preparation of plans and specifications for the
construction of the road , advertisement for bids , levy of
assessments against abutting property owners , the creation
of lien upon such property for such assessments , providing
for the payment of such assessments either in cash or in
ten equal annual installments , providing for the pre-
payment of such installments , providing for notice to prop-
erty owners , for suit against delinquent property owners ,
for the issuance of bonds or apportionment warrants , for
the sale of bonds and providing for the treasurer to make
a fidelity bond .
WHEREAS , a great many subdivisions have been established
in Jefferson County , Kentucky, adjacent to the City of
Louisville , which subdivisions are thickly populated and
have running through them numerous public roads which
were not constructed by and have not been maintained by
either the Commonwealth of Kentucky or the County of
Jefferson and must be maintained solely by voluntary con-
tribution of the persons who use such roads ; and
WHEREAS , there are many other public roads in Jefferson
County of the same character which were not constructed
in connection with the establishment of any subdivision ;
and
WHEREAS , it is deemed desirable and for the best interests
and welfare of the public at large that a method be pro-
vided for improving such public roads , which are neither
State nor County roads , at the cost of the owners of the
property abutting thereon ; Now, therefore ,

360 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Public road districts may be estab-
lished in counties containing cities of the first class
for the purpose of providing the general public and
persons residing upon or owning property adjacent
to such roads with all -weather roads .
Section 2. A public road district may be estab-
lished in the following manner :
(a ) Any person or group of persons owning
property abutting upon any public road (which is
neither a County road nor a State road ) in counties
outside of and containing cities of the first class
may sponsor the creation of such a road district .
The sponsors shall first prepare or have prepared
for them a map of that section of such public road
which they desire to have improved . Such map shall
show the boundary lines of the road , the intersec-
tion with other roads and the terminal points on the
road desired to be improved, which shall always be
center lines of intersections with other roads , and
shall set forth on such map the names of the owners
of all property and the number of linear feet owned
by them abutting upon such road . The sponsors
of said road district shall also have estimated for
them by an engineer , who must be a private engineer
licensed by the Commonwealth of Kentucky , pur-
suant to Chapter 93 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly for the year 1928 , the approximate cost of
constructing the improvements desired , together
with a statement of the approximate cost which
shall be borne by each owner of property abutting
on the road, determined by the number of linear
feet of property owned by each abutting property
holder. Said sponsors of said road district shall
also prepare a petition and shall attach to said
petition the aforesaid maps and statements of cost .

CHAPTER 65 361
A
Section 3. The petition mentioned in the fore-
going section shall set forth :
(a ) The proposed name of said district , which
shall be " ( name of road ) Road
County , Ken-District of
tucky . "
(b) The necessity for the proposed work and
that it will be conducive to the public safety , com-
fort, convenience or welfare .
(c) A general description of the purpose of the
contemplated improvement and a brief general de-
scription of the road to be improved , setting forth
its width and terminal points , and referring to the
map which shall be attached to the petition for a
detailed description .
(d ) The petition shall pray for the organization
of the road district .
Section 4. When the petition has been signed by
the owners of fifty -one percent (51 % ) of the prop-
erty , determined in linear front feet abutting upon
and adjacent to the road to be improved , the
sponsors of the district may present the petition , to-
gether with the attached maps and estimates of
cost , to the County Judge , who shall turn over to the
County Engineer for his consideration the maps and
estimates of cost . The County Engineer shall ,
within ten days from his receipt of such documents
from the County Judge , examine said documents ,
examine the road proposed to be improved , study
the estimates of cost and type of surface proposed
to be used and return to the County Judge with
recommendations of approval or disapproval the
documents theretofore delivered to him . If the
County Engineer disapproves the plans and esti-
mates of cost submitted to him, he shall set forth his
reasons for such disapproval , and the County Judge

362 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall return said plans and estimates for revision
in accordance with the recommendations of the
County Engineer . After revision , they shall again
be submitted to the County Engineer , and if he
again disapproves , the Court shall nevertheless
proceed with a hearing, as hereinafter provided , to
determine whether or not said approval should be
granted or withheld .
In the event the County Engineer approves the
plans for the road and estimates of cost thereof ,
the County Judge shall notify the sponsors thereof
and shall select a day not earlier than two weeks
thereafter on which a public hearing shall be held to
determine whether or not said public road district
shall be created and organized . During said two
weeks period , the County Judge shall use such in-
formation as is available to him and shall make such
investigation as he deems necessary to satisfy him-
self of the genuineness of the signatures appearing
upon the petition and as to the ownership of
property of the persons signing said petition . The
question of genuineness of signatures and the owner-
ship of property of any person signing the petition
may be questioned at the hearing by anyone deny-
ing such validity .
When the County Judge has selected a day for the
hearing , it shall be the duty of the sponsors to ad-
vance sufficient funds to pay for the advertising of
the hearing , which advertising shall be had by in-
sertion of one notice in some newspaper of general
circulation in the county and by posting printed
notices in such number as the County Judge may
deem necessary or advisable , but in no event less
than ten , along both sides of the road to be im-
proved , either on telephone poles or on posts erected
for that purpose.

CHAPTER 65 363
Section 5. Any owner of land abutting upon the
road proposed to be improved who has failed or re-
fused to sign the petition seeking the formation of
the road district may , on the day fixed for the hear-
ing , file in the County Court his answer to said
petition setting forth his objections to the creation
of such district , and the County Judge shall there-
upon fix a day when both the sponsors and the ob-
jectors may introduce testimony , and shall at that
time hear the case upon its merits . The burden of
proof shall be upon the objectors to show cause why
the road district should not be created , and the case
shall be tried as a suit in equity . The order of the
County Court shall be final and shall be subject
to appeal directly to the Court of Appeals upon the
record made in the County Court . The Appellant
shall , within thirty ( 30 ) days after the order is
entered by the County Court , execute bond with
good Surety before the Clerk of the County Court
covenanting that the Appellant shall pay the costs
of the Appeal in the event of the affirmance of the
order of the County Court . Such appeal must be
asked for and granted by the County Court within
thirty ( 30 ) days from the date of the rendition of
the judgment . If no appeal is sought within such ·
time , the judgment of the County Court shall be
final . If no objections are filed on the day set for
the initial hearing , the County Court shall enter its
order approving the petition .
Section 6. In the event of a final judgment of the
County Court , either with or without appeal to the
Court of Appeals , ordering the creation of the road
district , the County Court shall enter an order de-
claring said district organized in the name provided
for in Section 3 of this Act , by which name it shall
thereafter be known . The County Judge shall there-
upon appoint three persons as members of the road

364 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
district board of directors , whose duty it shall be to
control and manage the affairs of the district . Each
director shall be at least twenty -five ( 25 ) years of
age, shall be an owner of land abutting upon the
road to be improved and shall have lived on such
land for a period of at least six ( 6 ) months prior to
his appointment . The members of the first board
of directors shall hold their offices , respectively , for
two , three and four years from the date of their ap-
pointment , and thereafter each director shall be ap-
pointed for a term of four years . Vacancies result-
ing from any cause other than expiration of a term
of office shall be filled by the County Judge and only
for the unexpired term .
Section 7. Each director , before entering upon
his official duties , shall take and subscribe to an oath
before the Clerk of the County Court that he will
honestly , faithfully and impartially perform the
duties of his office and that he will not be interested
directly or indirectly in any contract let for the pur-
pose of carrying out any of the provisions of this
Act . Said oath shall be filed among the minutes of
the road district and noted on the minutes of the
County Court .
Each director shall give a good and sufficient bond
in an amount to be fixed by order entered by the
County Court , said bond to be approved of by the
County Judge and to provide for the faithful per-
formance of his duties and as security for all monies
coming into his hands . The cost of such bonds shall
be borne by the road district .
Section 8. The board of directors , which is
hereby declared to be the governing body of the road
district , shall keep a record of its proceedings , shall
adopt and have a seal , and shall exercise all powers
and manage and control all the affairs and property

CHAPTER 65 365
of the district . Said board of directors shall elect
one of its members chairman , one secretary and one
treasurer . The board may employ an attorney and
an engineer , who shall serve in such capacities dur-
ing the pleasure of the board and for such reason-
able compensation as may be fixed by the board and
approved by order of the County Court . Each
member of the board may receive a salary of not in
excess of $ 200.00 per annum for his services , which
salary shall be fixed at the first meeting of said
board, and approved of by order of the County
Court , and thereafter may be decreased as the
duties of the members decrease . The board may
adopt such rules and regulations as are necessary
for its proper functioning . The chairman , secre-
tary and treasurer of the district shall perform such
duties as are usually performed by such officers .
Section 9. The board of directors shall fix the
time and place for regular meetings of the board
and may hold such other meetings as are called by
the chairman or any two members of the board upon
two days ' notice in writing to all members of the
board . A majority of the directors shall constitute
a quorum , and the concurrence of the majority in
any matter within the scope of its duties shall be
decisive . All actions taken by the directors shall be
by resolution .
Section 10. Upon qualification of the members
of the board of directors , the board shall take up
with its engineer the plans and specifications pro-
posed for the construction of the road to be im-
proved , and may make such changes therein as may ,
within the scope of the improvement petitioned for ,
appear necessary . The cost of preparation of such
plans and specifications and any other reasonable
costs borne by the sponsors of the project prior to
the organization of the district which are properly

366 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
proved by vouchers shall be included in the cost of
construction of the project and shall be repaid to
such sponsors out of monies collected or received
for the construction of such project .
Section 11. Upon the final approval of the plans
and specifications , the board of directors shall adopt
a resolution approving and accepting said plan , and
advertising for bids for the construction of the road
according to such plans and specifications . The
board shall advertise for bids for the construction
of such work by publishing notice thereof in a news-
paper of general circulation in the County once each
week for two consecutive weeks ( publication being
complete on the date of the second publication ) ,
calling for reception of bids in not less than one
week after the second publication . The contract for
such work shall be let to the lowest responsible
bidder , who shall give bond with approved and
adequate security for the faithful performance of
the contract . The notice of the letting of the con-
tract shall specify the general nature of the work to
be done , the time and date of reception of bids , and
the place where detailed information as to the work
to be performed can be obtained . All bids shall be
opened and read at the time and place specified in
the notice , and the board of directors may either
accept the lowest and best bid or , in their sound
discretion reject all bids and re - advertise .
Section 12. When a bid has been accepted and
the cost of constructing the road improvement has
thus been determined , the board of directors shall
prepare a statement of all costs of constructing the
project (including the cost of organizing the dis-
trict , preliminary expenses , fees of directors , engi-
neer and attorney , and a sum sufficient to provide
for any unforeseen contingencies up to and including
the completion of the project , together with a fund

CHAPTER 65 367
adequate to provide for continuing costs of the dis-
trict for a period of ten years ) , and shall thereupon
determine the front-foot cost of constructing the
road improvement . The board of directors shall
then assess against each owner of property abutting
upon the road improved within the limits of the im-
provement that proportion of the cost of construct-
ing the entire improvement which his front footage
abutting upon the road bears to the total front foot-
age of all properties abutting upon the road within
the area to be improved . In determining the total
front footage , intersections shall be included .
Section 13. The assessment made by the board
of directors against each owner of property abutting
upon the road improvement shall be and become a
lien on the property of such owner and shall be prior
and superior to all other liens except those of taxes .
Section 14. The board of directors shall notify
each property owner in writing of the assessment
made against him and shall also cause printed
notices setting forth the assessments against each
property owner to be posted in at least ten public
places along the road to be improved . The property
owner may pay the assessment against his property
in cash without interest within thirty ( 30 ) days
from the date of the notice , or may elect to pay such
assessment by paying same in ten equal annual in-
stallments , the first of which shall be paid within
such thirty ( 30 ) days without interest , and an agree-
ment executed to pay the other nine installments
annually thereafter with interest at the maximum
rate of six (6 % ) percent per annum payable semi-
annually . The notice of assessment shall state said
terms .
Section 15. Any property owner who desires to
exercise the privilege of payment by installment
shall , before the expiration of said thirty ( 30 ) days ,

368 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pay in cash one -tenth of the assessment against him
and enter into an agreement in writing with the
road district that , in consideration of such priv-
ilege , he will make no objection to any illegality or
irregularity in the assessment , levy or imposition of
the assessment against his property . Any property
owner entering into such an agreement shall be con-
cluded thereby and shall waive any defense which
he might otherwise have against the payment of
the assessment levied against his property . Said
agreement shall be filed and recorded among the
records of the board of directors of the road district ,
and shall be binding upon any successor in title to
the property .
After the first payment of one -tenth of the assess-
ment shall have been made in cash at the time the
property owner elects to pay the balance on the in-
stallment plan, the remaining installments shall be
due and payable one each year on the anniversary
of the last day of the thirty -day period for executing
the installment payment agreement , together with
interest as herein provided for nine years .
Section 16. In all cases where agreements to pay
assessments in installments have not been filed
within the time limit , the entire amount of the as-
sessment shall forthwith be payable in cash . Any
assessment not paid within thirty ( 30 ) days from
the date of notice , as aforesaid , shall bear interest
thereafter at the rate of six ( 6 % ) percent per an-
num from the date of the assessment and levy and
shall be collected by suit .
Section 17. If any property against which an
assessment has been levied is owned by an infant ,
by a person of unsound mind or a life tenant , the
agreement herein provided for may be executed by
the statutory guardian of such infant or the com-
mittee or curator of such insane person , with the

CHAPTER 65 369
same effect as if executed by a person free from dis-
ability , or by the life tenant , whose agreement shall
be binding upon the owner of the fee .
Section 18. Any person may, at any interest-
payment period after the fifth installment of his as-
sessment has become due and has been paid , pay the
entire balance of such assessment against his
property with accrued interest to the next succeed-
ing interest period . In the event that an owner who
has entered into an installment payment agreement
desires to pay his assessment in full before the fifth
installment thereof has become due , he may pay the
same by paying the face amount of his obligation ,
plus all installments of interest up to and including
the next interest period following the fifth install-
ment .
Section 19. It shall be the duty of the board of
directors to cause to be mailed to each property
owner liable for an assessment , to his last known
address , a notice of the amount falling due ten days
prior to the date on which any installment of prin-
cipal becomes due . The failure of such property
owner to receive a notice shall neither exempt him
from liability for the assessment nor prejudice the
collection thereof or the enforcement of the lien
therefor .
Section 20. If any person liable for any part of
said assessment defaults in the payment of any in-
stallment thereof or interest thereon for one month
after payment becomes due , a penalty of ten per-
cent of the installment in arrears shall be added
thereto , which shall constitute a like lien as the as-
sessment , and thereafter all unpaid installments of
the assessment against such person shall , at the
option of the district , or of any holder of an ap-
portionment warrant or bond secured thereby ,
forthwith become due and payable . It shall be the

370 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
duty of the district , for the use and benefit of the
owner or owners of such bonds or apportionment
warrants to institute , in its own name and at its own
cost , proper proceedings for the enforcement of such
lien , except that the costs of the action , including
attorneys ' fees , shall be added to the amount of the
delinquent assessment and shall be first payable out
of the monies received through enforcement of the
district's lien . If the district fails to institute such
proceedings for thirty (30 ) days after notice of such
default , any owner of a bond or apportionment
warrant secured by such lien may , in his own name
and on his own behalf and on the behalf of other
owners of such bonds or apportionment warrants ,
institute and prosecute such proceedings in any
court of competent jurisdiction .
Section 21. It shall be the duty of the district
treasurer promptly to apply all monies paid in on
installments of principal or interest and on penalties
to the payment of the bonds and coupons or ap-
portionment warrants issued hereunder . If the
fund accumulated from the collection of assess-
ments , interest and penalties be more than sufficient
to pay all principal and interest due , said treas-
urer shall refund to each person paying an assess-
ment for the project a proportionate amount of the
excess . Failure on the part of the district to collect
any payment when due shall create no liability
against the district and shall create no liability
against the members of the board of directors ,
except for gross negligence or malfeasance in office .
Section 22. In the event that a property owner
fails or refuses to pay his assessment in cash within
thirty ( 30 ) days or to pay one -tenth of his assess-
ment in cash within thirty (30 ) days and execute an
agreement to pay the balance in nine equal annual
installments with interest, as aforesaid , or in the

CHAPTER 65 371
event that a property owner fails or refuses to pay
any semi -annual installment of interest or annual
installment of principal due under such agreement
when the same becomes due , then the board of
directors shall , after making reasonable efforts to
collect the same , direct its attorney to file a suit in
equity to foreclose the lien of the road district
against such delinquent property owner . In all
actions to enforce liens , a copy of the resolution of
the board of directors authorizing the work to be
done and assessing the lien against the property
owners , attested by the secretary of said board , shall
be filed with the petition and shall constitute prima
facie evidence of all facts necessary to be estab-
lished by the plaintiff in such an action to entitle it
to relief . In such actions , the court shall provide
in its order confirming any report of sale that any
defendant or defendants or anyone claiming through
him or them may, within two years from the date
of the order confirming a report of sale , redeem
the property sold by paying to the purchaser the
purchase price , with interest thereon from the day
of sale at the rate of ten ( 10 % ) percent per annum
to the date of payment ; in the event that there be
no redemption within the time allowed , the order of
confirmation shall be final and a deed shall be ex-
ecuted to the purchaser or his assignee .
Section 23. At the end of the thirty -day ( 30 )
period following the notification of all property
owners of the assessment against their property , the
attorney for the district , upon receiving instruc-
tions so to do , shall file in the office of the County
Clerk a lis pendens notice against each parcel of
property the owner of which has entered into an
agreement to pay his assessment in installments or
who has failed or refused either to pay his assess-
ment in cash or to enter into an agreement , and the

372 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
lien hereinbefore created shall thereupon be and be-
come effective as against each parcel of property so
affected .
Section 24. In order to obtain funds sufficient to
pay for the construction of the proposed improve-
ment , being the balance required after the receipt
of full cash payments and initial installment pay-
ments , but not including in such balance payments
due from owners who failed or refused to make full
cash payments or to enter into installment pay-
ment agreements , the board of directors may , in its
discretion , provide by resolution for the issuance of
interest -bearing apportionment warrants against
each parcel of property or may direct the issuance
and sale of road district bonds , as hereinafter pro-
vided . No bonds shall be issued unless the aggre-
gate amount of installment payments , subsequent
to the first cash payment is $ 1,000.00 or more .
Section 25. In the event that the board of
directors determines to issue apportionment war-
rants , the same shallsame shall be issued against each
parcel of property against which there is a lien for
an unpaid balance of the assessment , shall bear
interest at the rate of a maximum six (6 % ) percent
per annum , payable semi -annually , and shall be pay-
able , as to principal , in nine equal installments .
Said apportionment warrants may be sold at public
or private sale , and may not be sold for less than
their face value , except to the contractor , who must
accept the same in full payment of the assessments
which they represent . Said apportionment warrants
shall be executed by the chairman and the secretary
of the board of directors and may be sold either at
public or private sale .
Section 26. In the event that the board of di-
rectors determines to issue road improvement
bonds , it may pledge the special assessment and

CHAPTER 65 373
liens on the property for the payment of the prin
cipal and interest of such bonds and shall apply
the proceeds thereof exclusively to the payment
thereof. The bonds shall bear a date not less than
thirty ( 30 ) days nor more than sixty ( 60 ) days
after the date which was the last day for the ex-
ecution by the property owner of installment pay
ment agreements . The bonds shall be negotiable
and payable to bearer , shall provide for the semi-
annual payment of interest at a rate of not in ex-
cess of six (6 % ) percent per annum , shall bear
coupons to provide for the payment of such interest ,
shall be numbered consecutively , shall be in denom-
inations of $ 100.00 or its multiple (provided that
any odd amount not in multiple of $ 100.00 may be
included in the last bond to mature ) , shall be
divided into nine series as nearly equal as prac
ticable , one series maturing each year , and shall be
payable at the office of the treasurer of the road dis-
trict or at a bank or trust company named in the
bonds . The bonds shall have the name of the road
for the improvement of which they are issued
printed or engraved thereon , shall state the
character and extent of the improvement , shall be
signed by the chairman and the secretary of the
board of directors , and the coupons shall be signed
by the secretary either personally or by his en-
graved or lithographed facsimile signature .
It shall not be necessary in the bonds to recite the
steps taken for the improvement of the road or in
making the assessment of the cost thereof , but it
shall be sufficient to make a general reference to
such proceedings and to this Act. The bonds shall
be exempt from all taxation by or in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky . The bonds shall convey and
transfer to the owner thereof all lien , right , title
and interest in and to the assessments and liens

374 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
upon the respective parcels of land abutting upon
the improvement . Said liens , until released after
payment , as herein provided for, shall be security
for such bonds and coupons until they are paid .
The bonds shall be subject to the prepayment and
redemption at any interest period thereof which
does not occur within four years from the date
thereof . The treasurer of the district shall , when
funds have accumulated from any special assess-
ment sufficient to redeem in excess of the next series
falling due , by lot select from the next succeeding
issue falling due thereafter bonds of a par value
equal to the excess in said fund arising from the
excess collection and shall advertise the designa-
tion and numbers of the bond or bonds so drawn
once each week for three consecutive weeks prior
to the next interest period in a newspaper of general
circulation in the county where the district is
located to the effect that on said date , the bonds will
be redeemed at their par value and accrued interest .
The bonds specified in such notice shall bear no
interest after the date fixed for their redemption ,
which provision shall be set forth in substance on
the bonds .
Section 27. The bonds shall be sold at public
sale for no less than par and accrued interest to
the highest responsible bidder . Such sale shall be
advertised by publication of a notice of the time and
place of the sale , and such other information as
may be pertinent , in one issue of a newspaper of
general circulation in the county where the road dis-
trict is located at least one week prior to the taking
of bids . If no bidder offers par and accrued interest
for the purchase of such bonds , then the road dis-
trict shall have the right to turn them over to the
contractor in full payment of that portion of the
contract price of the improvement represented by

CHAPTER 65 375
par and accrued interest . The contractor shall
accept such bonds and give credit on the contract
price for the full face value and accrued interest
thereon . Any premium realized from the sale of the
bonds and any penalties collected on account of the
delinquency of any installment payments of assess-
ments shall be added to the bond fund for the pay-
ment of the bonds and interest thereon . Said
bonds and interest shall be payable out of funds
actually paid to and collected by the road district
on account of the improvement assessment in an-
ticipation of which the bonds were issued .
Section 28. The treasurer of the road district
shall be charged with the keeping of all monies col-
lected on account of the special assessment , interest
and penalties , and he shall disburse the same only
in payment of the construction costs , apportion-
ment warrants or bonds , as the case may be , or for
regular expenses of the district . The treasurer
shall be bonded in an amount to be fixed by order
entered by the County Court and shall be liable upon
his official bond for the safe -keeping of the funds
of the district . The sale of any parcel of land for
the non -payment of any special tax or assessment
thereon or for any general tax shall not thereby
release said parcel of ground from other special tax
or assessment existing thereon unless the same be
asserted in the action in which such parcels are sold .
In any action to foreclose its lien brought by the
road district or by any bondholder , it shall not be
necessary to make the other bondholders parties .
The proceeds of the suit shall be paid to the treas
urer of the district and by him applied to the re-
demption of matured bonds as if the payment had
been made without suit .
In such a suit , an allegation in substance that the
improvement of the road had been duly made and

376 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
completed and such assessment levied therefor and
bonds for unpaid portion of the cost had been issued
pursuant to resolutions and proceedings duly
adopted and had by the board of directors in ac-
cordance with law , shall be a sufficient pleading of
such resolutions and proceedings without setting the
same out in full or making further reference thereto .
The road district is hereby authorized to bid an
amount not in excess of its lien and costs for any
property sold in such suit.
Section 29. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly , the number " KRS Chapter 322 " shall
be substituted for " Chapter 93 of the Acts of the
General Assembly for the year 1938 " in section two
of this Act .
Approved March 2 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 66
(H. B. 151 )
AN ACT pertaining to the use of police radios and providing
who may and may not use and have in their possession
radio equipment capable of receiving or sending or inter-
fering with police radio messages and declaring a violation
of this act to be a misdemeanor and providing a penalty
therefor including the escheat , forfeiture and destruction
of any equipment had or used in violation of this act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
It shall be unlawful for any person except a peace
officer duly authorized to make arrests , and also
holding either a first , second or third class radio
operators license duly issued by the Federal Com-
munications Commission , or a member of a police
department or police force which regularly main-
tains a police radio system authorized or licensed

CHAPTER 66 377
by the Federal Communications Commission , to
have in his or her possession , or in an automobile or
other vehicle , or to equip or install in or on any auto-
mobile or other vehicle , any mobile radio set or
apparatus capable of either receiving or trans-
mitting radio or other messages or signals within
the wave length or channel now or which may here-
after be allocated by the Federal Communications
Commission, or its successor , for the purpose of
police radios , or which may , in any way intercept or
interfere with the transmission of radio messages
by any police or other peace officers , and it shall be
unlawful for any car , automobile or other vehicle
other than one publicly owned and entitled to and
bearing an official license plate issued by the state
issuing a license for said car , to have , or be equipped
with such sets or apparatus even though said car
is owned by an officer . Provided , however , the pro-
visions of this act shall not apply to any automobile
or other vehicle owned or operated by any oper-
ating public utility company and , in the event a
license is required , that same be secured from the
Federal Communications Commission or other
Federal agency having the authority to license
same .
Any person guilty of violating any of the pro-
visions of this act shall be guilty of a misdemeanor ,
and , upon conviction , shall be punished by a fine of
not less than Fifty Dollars ( $ 50.00 ) and not exceed-
ing Five Hundred Dollars ( $ 500.00 ) , or imprison-
ment not exceeding twelve ( 12 ) months , or both so
fined and imprisoned .
It shall be the duty of any and all peace officers
to seize and hold for evidence any and all equip
ment had or used in violation of the provisions of
this act , and , upon conviction of the person having ,
equipping or using such equipment , it shall be the

378 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
duty of the Trial Court to order such equipment or
apparatus destroyed , forfeited or escheated to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , or to any county , city
or town within which such Court sits or holds its
sessions and said property may be ordered de-
stroyed , forfeited or escheated as above provided
without a conviction of the person charged with
violating this act .
Approved March 2 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 67
(S. B. 5 )
AN ACT to protect property by making criminal certain
unlawful entries on , injuries to , and interferences with prop-
erty or an attempt to do any of said things ; authorizing the
closing of streets , bridges , rights - of - way , and highways ;
providing penalties ; providing for the termination of this
act when the President declares the emergency at an end ;
and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. Definitions . As used in this Act :
" Highway ” includes any alley , sidewalk , private
or public street , or any other way or place , used for
travel by vehicle or pedestrian to or from property .
"Public utility " includes any pipe line , gas , elec-
tric , heat , water , oil , sewer , telephone , telegraph,
railway , railroad , airplane , transportation , com-
munications , or any other system , by whomsoever
owned or operated for public use .
" National defense industry " shall mean any in-
dividual , partnership , association or corporation en-
gaged in or preparing to engage in the manufacture ,
transportation or storage of any product to be used
by the United States or by any of the States in the

CHAPTER 67 379
prosecution of defense or of war , or any individual ,
partnership , association or corporation engaged in
or preparing to engage in the manufacture , trans-
portation or storage of any article necessary to
and to become part of any product to be used in the
prosecution of defense or of war by the United
States or by any of the States .
§ 2. Intentional Injury To or Interference With
Property. Whoever intentionally destroys , im-
pairs , injures , interferes or tampers with real or
personal property with reasonable grounds to be-
lieve that such act will hinder , delay or interfere
with the prosecution by the United States or by any
of the States of defense or of war shall be punished
by imprisonment for not less than one ( 1 ) nor more
than ten ( 10 ) years , or by a fine of not more than
Ten Thousand ( $ 10,000 ) Dollars , or both .
If such act or acts be committed upon or about
real property or personal property in which are
housed , or about which there are reasonable grounds
to believe there are , human beings , whoever com-
mits such act or acts shall be punished by death in
the manner prescribed by the general law of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
§ 3. Intentionally Defective Workmanship .
Whoever intentionally makes or causes to be made
or omits to note on inspection any defect in any
article or thing with reasonable grounds to believe
that such article or thing is intended to be used in
connection with the prosecution by the United
States or by any of the States of defense or of war ,
or that such article or thing is one of a number of
similar articles or things , some of which are in-
tended so to be used , shall be punished by imprison-
ment for not less than one ( 1 ) nor more than ten
(10 ) years , or by a fine of not more than Ten Thou-
sand ( $ 10,000 ) Dollars , or both .

380 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 4. Attempts . Whoever attempts to commit
any of the crimes defined by this Act shall be liable
to punishment by imprisonment for not less than
one ( 1 ) nor more than five (5 ) years . In addition
to the acts which constitute an attempt to commit
a crime under the laws of this State , the solicitation
or incitement of another to commit any of the crimes
defined by this Act not followed by the commission
of the crime , the collection or assemblage of any
materials with the intent that the same are to be
used then or at a later time in the commission of
such crime , or the entry, with or without permission ,
of a building , inclosure , right - of-way, or restricted
area , or other premises of another , with the intent
to commit any such crime therein , thereon or there
about , shall constitute an attempt to commit such
crime .
§ 5. Conspirators . If two or more persons con-spire to commit any crime defined by this Act , each
of such persons is guilty of conspiracy and subject
to the same punishment as if he had committed the
crime which he conspired to commit , whether or not
any act be done in furtherance of the conspiracy .
§ 6. Witnesses ' Privileges . No person shall be
excused from attending and testifying , or producing
any books , papers , weapons , devices , or other
documents or materials , before any court , magis-
trate , referee or grand jury , or city prosecuting
attorney , county attorney , or Commonwealth's at
torney, upon any investigation , inquiry , proceeding
or trial , for or relating to or concerned with a
violation of any section of this Act, or attempt to
commit such violation , upon the ground or for the
reason that the testimony or evidence , documentary
or otherwise , required of him by the State may tend
to convict him of a crime or to subject him to a
penalty or forfeiture ; but no person shall be

CHAPTER 67 381
prosecuted or subjected to any penalty or forfeiture
for or on account of any transaction , matter or thing
concerning which he may so testify or produce
evidence , documentary or otherwise , and no testi-
mony so given or produced shall be received against
him , upon any criminal investigation , proceeding or
trial , except upon a prosecution for perjury or con-
tempt of court based upon the giving or producing
of such testimony.
§ 7. Unlawful Entry on Property . Any indi-
vidual , partnership , association , corporation , mu-
nicipal corporation or state , or any political sub-
division thereof , engaged in , or preparing to en-
gage in, the manufacture , transportation or storage
of any product to be used in the prosecution by the
United States or by any of the States of defense or
of war , or the manufacture , transportation , distrib-
ution or storage of gas , oil , coal , electricity or water ,
or any of said natural or artificial persons oper-
ating any public utility , whose property , except
where it fronts on water or where there are en-
trances for railway cars , vehicles , persons or things ,
is surrounded by a fence or wall , or a fence or wall
and buildings , may post around his or its property
at each gate , entrance , dock or railway entrance
and every one hundred (100 ) feet of water front,
a sign reading " No Entry Without Permission " .
Whoever without permission of such owner shall
wilfully enter upon premises so posted shall be
punished by imprisonment for not more than ninety
( 90 ) days , or a fine of not more than One Hundred
($100.00 ) Dollars , or both .
§ 8. Questioning and Detaining Suspected Per-
sons . Any peace officer or any person employed as
watchman , guard , or in a supervisory capacity , on
premises posted as provided in Section Seven of
this Act, may stop any person found on any pre-

382 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
mises , or any restricted area as defined and pro-
vided for by Section Nine of this Act , to which entry
without permission is forbidden by Section Seven ,
and may detain him for the purpose of demanding ,
and may demand of him, his name , address and busi-
ness in such place . If said peace officer or such em
ployee has reason to believe from the answers of the
person so interrogated that such person has no
right to be in such place , said peace officer shall
forthwith arrest such person without a warrant on
the charge of violating the provisions of Section
Seven ; and such employee shall forthwith turn over
such person to a peace officer , who may arrest such
person without a warrant on the charge of violating
the provisions of Section Seven .
9. Closing and Restricting Use of Highways
and Other Areas . Any individual , partnership ,
association , corporation , municipal corporation or
state , or any political subdivision thereof , engaged
in or preparing to engage in the manufacture ,
transportation or storage of any product to be used
in the prosecution by the United States of defense
or of war , or in the manufacture , transportation ,
distribution or storage of gas , oil , coal , electricity or
water, or any of said natural or artificial persons
operating any public utility , who has property so
used which he or it believes will be endangered if
public use and travel is not restricted or prohibited
on one or more highways or parts thereof upon
which such property abuts , may petition the mayor ,
the county judge , or the Governor of the Common-
wealth, to close one or more of said highways or
parts thereof to public use and travel or to restrict
by order the use and travel upon one or more of said
highways or parts thereof .
Upon receipt of such petition , the officer receiving
the same shall immediately hold a hearing , and if

CHAPTER 67 383
the facts to be divulged are of a confidential nature ,
or if to the best interests of the Nation that they
should not be made public , a private hearing , and
issue whatever order the facts may justify closing
to the public use and travel , or reasonably restrict-ing the public use and travel upon , one or more ofsaid highways or parts thereof ; provided , the officer
entering the same may issue written permits to
travel over the highways so closed or restricted to
responsible and reputable persons for such term ,
under such conditions and in such form as said
officer may prescribe . Appropriate notices in letters
at least three ( 3 ) inches high shall be posted con-spicuously at each end of any highway so closed or
restricted by such order . The officer entering said
order may at any time revoke or modify any order
so made .
Any mayor, county judge, and , if beyond thejurisdiction of any of the aforenamed officers , the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , shall
have the right to declare any territory necessaryand incident to the defense of the Nation a restricted
area , and , if deemed advisable by any of the afore-
named officers , they shall , by a suitable order , close
to public use , travel , or private use , if necessary ,
any of such territory ; provided that any one of the
officers , to-wit : the mayor, the county judge , the
Governor of the Commonwealth , may issue a writtenpermit for the use of such restricted area in themanner and form and under such conditions as such
permits may prescribe . Any declaration or order so
entered may be by the officer making the same re-
voked or modified at any time . Any permit issuedby any officer may be by such officer revoked at any
time .
No mayor or county judge upon demand of theGovernor of the Commonwealth shall refuse to de-

384 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
clare any territory incident and necessary to the
defense of the Nation a restricted area .
§ 10. Penalty for Going upon Closed or Re-
stricted Highway or Area . Whoever violates any
order made under Section Nine of this Act shall be
punished by imprisonment for not more than ninety
( 90 ) days or a fine of not more than One Hundred
( $ 100.00 ) Dollars , or both .
$ 11. Suspension of Injunctive Relief . No per-
son feeling that he will be aggrieved by the act of a
mayor , county judge , or the Governor of the Com-
monwealth , in declaring a territory a restricted
area or in closing or restricting a highway to pub-
lic use and travel in the manner provided by this
Act , shall have the right to enjoin the action of such
officer ; but any person feeling himself aggrieved ,
after such territory has been declared a restricted
area , or after such highway has been closed or re-
stricted to public use and travel , may proceed in the
same manner as now provided by law for a man-
damus against such officer in the county of the
officer's residence and office in the case of the mayor
or the county judge , and in the County of Franklin
in the case of the Governor of the Commonwealth .
Pending an appeal from the order of mandamus , in
the event one should be issued , the order declaring
any territory a restricted area , or closing or re-
stricting any highway to public use and travel , shall
remain in full force and effect without such officer
against whom such proceedings are prosecuted
executing a supersedeas bond .
The mayor , the county judge , or the Governor of
the Commonwealth , in the event such mandamus is
sought , shall not be compelled to reveal by his tes-
timony any information received by him from the
United States or any of its agencies or from any

CHAPTER 67 385
peace officer relative to the reasons which justify his
action .
§ 12. Severability . If any provision of this Act
or the application thereof to any person or circum-
stances is held invalid , such invalidity shall not
affect other provisions or applications of the Act
which can be given effect without the invalid pro-
vision or application , and to this end the provisions
of this Act are declared to be severable .
$ 13. Short Title . This Act may be cited as the
Sabotage Prevention Act .
§ 14. Relation to Other Statutes . All acts and
parts of acts inconsistent with this Act are hereby
suspended in their application to any proceedings
under this Act. No word , phrase , sentence , para-
graph , or section of this Act shall suspend any law
now in existence in the Commonwealth of Kentucky
relating to the rights of Labor , nor shall any word ,
phrase , sentence , paragraph , or section of this Act
be construed as impairing , effecting , or abridging
the rights and grants of Labor . If conduct pro-
hibited by this Act is also made unlawful by an-
other or other laws , the offender may be convicted
for the violation of this Act or of such other law or
laws .
§ 15. Emergency Clause . Whereas , the Legisla-
ture is cognizant of the fact that the United States
is at war and a national emergency exists , making
necessary the enactment of law as set forth in this
Act,
Therefore , a state emergency is declared to exist ,
and this Act shall take effect immediately upon its
passage and approval by the Governor , and shall
continue in force until the President of the United
States declares the emergency at an end .
Approved 2nd March , 1942 .

386 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 68
(H. B. 214)
AN ACT to provide for the conservation of wild animals , wildbirds , fish and amphibians .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions . As used in this Act unless the
context requires otherwise :
(1 ) "Angling " means taking fish by hook and
line in hand , rod in hand , set- lines or trot lines , butdoes not include the use of grappling naked , snatchhooks , gang hooks , gigs or nets of any kind , or thetaking of fish where the bait or hook is not volun-tarily taken in the mouth by the fish .
(2 ) "Buy" includes offering to buy , acquiring
or possessing through purchase , barter , exchange
or trade .
(3) " Commission " means Game and Fish Ad-visory Commission .
(4 ) " Device " means any article , instrument orequipment of whatever nature or kind which may be
used to take wild animals , wild birds or fish .
(5) " Director " means Director of Game andFish Division .
(6) " Division " means Division of Game and
Fish in the Department of Conservation .
(7) " Minnows " means all fish under six inches
in length , except bass , rock bass , goggle -eye , trout ,
crappie, jack salmon , walleyed pike or striped bass ,
sand pike or sauger .
(8) " Navigable waters" means any waterswithin this state under lock and dam .
(9) " Nonresident " is a person who has not re-sided in this state for one year prior to his applica-tion for a license .

CHAPTER 68 387
( 10 ) " Public waters " means all waters within
the state flowing in natural channel streams .
(11 ) " Resident " is a person who has resided in
this state for one year prior to his application for a
license .
( 12 ) " Sell " includes offering to sell , having or
possessing for sale , barter , exchange or trade .
( 13 ) "Take " means hunt , kill , trap or capture
in any way or attempt to hunt , kill , trap or capture
in any way.
(14 ) "Transport " includes offering or receiving
for transportation .
( 15 ) " Wild bird " and " wild animal " includes
such wild bird or wild animal or any part thereof .
§ 2. Division of Game and Fish ; director ; com-
mission. ( 1 ) The affairs of the Division of Game
and Fish shall be administered by the director under
the supervision of the Commissioner of Conserva-
tion and the Game and Fish Advisory Commission .
( 2 ) The commission shall consist of seven mem-
bers , appointed by the Governor for terms of four
years each . The Governor shall appoint three mem-
bers in 1942 , one in 1943 , two in 1944 and one in
1945 , to succeed the present incumbents whose re-
spective terms expire in those years . Each mem-
ber shall be well informed on the subject of wild life
conservation and restoration .
§3 . Expenses of commission . Members of the
commission shall receive no compensation but shall
be entitled to reimbursement for actual and neces-
sary expenses incurred by them in the discharge of
their official duties , up to the amount of one thou-
sand dollars per annum , to be paid from the Game
and Fish Fund .
$ 4. Offices and meetings of commission . ( 1 )
The commission shall have its principal office in
Frankfort , Kentucky .

388 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 2) The commission shall hold regular quarterly
meetings in the Capitol and at such other times and
places as it shall designate .
§ 5. Officers of commission . When the office
of chairman of the commission becomes vacant the
Governor shall select a chairman from its members
to hold the position for the remainder of his term
or for such period of time as the Governor may
designate . The position of secretary shall be filled
from the membership of the commission .
§ 6. Oath and bond of director . ( 1 ) Before
entering upon the duties of his office the director
shall, in addition to taking the Constitutional oath
of office , swear or affirm that he holds no other po-
litical office nor any position under any political
committee or party . The oath or affirmation shall
be filed in the office of the Secretary of State . ·
( 2) The director shall execute and file with the
State Treasurer a bond in the sum of five thousand
dollars . The premium on the bond shall be paid
from the Game and Fish Fund .
§ 7. Director's powers and duties . ( 1 ) The
director shall have general supervision and control
of all activities , functions and employes of the Game
and Fish Division and shall enforce all laws relat-
ing to wild animals , birds and fish and shall exer-
cise all necessary incidental powers thereto , under
the supervision of the Commissioner of Con-
servation .
( 2 ) The director shall make out a monthly item-
ized account of the expenses of the Game and Fish
Division and submit it to the Department of Finance
which shall draw warrants on the State Treasurer
for the amount to be paid out of the Game and Fish
Fund .

CHAPTER 68 389
(3) Notwithstanding any other provision of this
Act , the director may authorize conservation officers
or any other persons to destroy or bring under cou-
trol in such manner as he deems necessary any wild
animals , fish or wild birds protected or unprotected
which are causing damage to persons or property ,
and which in his judgment should be eliminated or
controlled to prevent further damage to persons or
property .
$ 8. Conservation officers ; appointment ; bond .
( 1 ) The director may appoint , with the approval of
the Commissioner of Conservation and the Gover-
nor , as many conservation officers as are necessary
to efficiently enforce this chapter .
( 2 ) Before discharging his official duties , each
conservation officer shall give bond in the sum of
five hundred dollars , with two or more sureties ap-
proved by the director . The bond shall be filed in
the office of the Secretary of State .
§ 9. Conservation and peace officers to enforce
chapter. (1 ) Conservation officers or other per-
sons appointed by the director and all peace officers
and their deputies shall , enforce the provisions of
this chapter . They may arrest on sight , without a
warrant , any person detected by them or any of
them in the act of violating any of the provisions of
this chapter . They shall have the same right as
sheriffs to require aid in arresting without process
or with process any person found by them violating
this chapter and may seize without process anything
declared by this chapter to be contraband . No
liability shall be incurred by any person so charged
and directed .
( 2 ) Conservation officers and other offices charged
with the enforcement of this Act shall have author-
ity to call for and inspect the license or badge of any

390 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
person engaged in any activity where a license is re-
quired under this Act.
(3 ) No person shall resist or obstruct or interfere
with or threaten or attempt to intimidate or in any
other manner interfere with any officer in the dis-
charge of his duties under the provisions of this Act .
§ 10. Execution of process by director and em-
ployes . The director , all members of the division
and officers and persons appointed by the director
may execute all process issued by a court , enforcing
the provisions of this chapter , or any law relating to
the protection or propagation of game or fish in the
same manner as any constable or sheriff . To accom-
plish that purpose they may call any peace officer
or other person to their aid.
§ 11. Enforcement of law as to mussels . The
director and conservation officers may arrest , with-
out warrant , anyone violating any of the provisions
of Sections 51 to 54 or any of the rules or regula-
tions made by the Game and Fish Division for pro-
tection of mussels under this chapter . They may
inspect mussels in any warehouse , boat , store , car
or receptacle when they have good cause to believe
that any of the provisions of Sections 51 to 54 or the
regulations made under them have been violated .
This authority does not include the right to enter
any dwelling house without a search warrant . When
such officers find mussels in the possession of any-
one in violation of this chapter , the mussels shall be
confiscated and sold in the manner provided by
§ 12 for the sale of confiscated fish and game .
·
§ 12. Seizure and sale of contraband . ( 1 ) The
director , all conservation officers , persons appointed
by the director , and all peace officers and their
deputies shall seize and take possession of any and
all furs , fish , wild animals , wild birds , guns , dogs , in-
struments , boats or devices which have been taken ,

CHAPTER 68 391
used , transported or possessed contrary to any law
relating to fish or game . Upon complaint showing
probable cause for believing that any of the wild
animals or wild birds protected by any law are
illegally kept in any building , car or receptacle , any
court having jurisdiction may issue a search war-
rant and cause it to be searched . Any wild birds ,
wild animals , fish , articles , instruments or devices.
seized in accordance with this section shall be im-
pounded by the arresting officer and shall be taken.
before the court trying the person arrested .
( 2 ) Upon conviction the court or the jury trying
the case shall, except as provided in subsection ( 4 ) ,
have the discretion of determining whether or not
the things seized shall be declared contraband . Any
animal, bird , or fish taken , and any device used or
possessed contrary to the provisions of this chap-
ter is subject to being declared contraband . When
an item is declared contraband , the court shall enter
an order accordingly . A copy of the order shall be
sent to the director and the contraband shall be
placed in the custody of the arresting officer , to be
delivered to the director .
( 3 ) The director may sell to residents of this
state , at the highest market price obtainable , with
the approval of the Governor and the Director of
Purchases and Public Properties , all contraband
which comes to his possession under the order of
any court , or which has been seized under this
chapter and declared to be contraband by any law
relating to fish or game . One -half of the net pro-
ceeds of sale shall be paid to the officer who seized
the article of contraband , and the other half shall
be paid into the Game and Fish Fund . A record
of the sale, including the name of the purchaser
and the price paid , shall be kept by the director .

392 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(4 ) When a person is convicted of using a gun
not plugged in the manner required by § 31 or § 37 ,
the gun shall be turned over to the director to be
disposed of according to the provisions of sub-
section (3 ) .
(5 ) No person shall have in his possession
while taking any wild birds , wild animals or fish
any illegal device or other thing prohibited by law,
for the taking of wild birds , wild animals or fish .
§ 13. County and Commonwealth's attorneys
to enforce chapter ; employment of other attorneys .
County attorneys and Commonwealth's attorneys
shall enforce the provisions of this chapter . The
Director of the Game and Fish Division may em-
ploy attorneys to perform legal services required
by the director . The attorneys shall appear for the
division in all civil actions in which it or any of its
officers , employes or wardens may be interested
officially , and may assist the county attorneys or
Commonwealth's attorneys in the prosecution of
criminal actions arising under this chapter . When
for any reason the county attorneys or the Com-
monwealth's attorneys do not prosecute criminal
actions , attorneys employed by the director shall
conduct the prosecution on the part of the state with
the same authority as the county attorneys or the
Commonwealth's attorneys have . The compensa-
tion of the attorneys shall be fixed by the director
and paid out of the Game and Fish Fund .
§ 14. Report and payment of moneys by justices
and clerks. Each justice or clerk of the court be-
fore whom any prosecution under this chapter or
any law or regulation for the protection of wild
life , animals , birds or fish may be commenced or
shall go on appeal shall, within twenty days after
a decision has been rendered therein , report in writ-
ing to the director , the result of the prosecution ,

CHAPTER 68 393
and the amount of fine collected or penalty im-
posed , if any. All moneys collected as fines or
penalties for the violation of any law or regulation ,
which according to law goes to the Game and Fish
Fund shall, within thirty days , be paid , by the
court, justice or other officer collecting or receiving
the moneys , to the director who shall, within thirty
days , pay the moneys to the State Treasurer .
§ 15. Game and Fish Fund . ( 1 ) Except as
otherwise provided in this chapter , all moneys
derived from the sale of licenses or from any other
source connected with the administration of this
chapter shall be promptly paid over to the State
Treasurer , who shall deposit such moneys in a
special fund known as the Game and Fish Fund .
The Game and Fish Fund shall be used to carry
out the purposes of this chapter and any law or
regulation for the protection of wild animals , birds
or fish and shall be used for no other purpose .
( 2 ) All funds received under § 11 and §§ 51 to
54 shall be used by the Game and Fish Division for
the purpose of enforcing those sections and for the
protection and propagation of mussel beds . Any
surplus remaining in the fund at the close of each
calendar year shall be turned into the general fund
of the division .
§ 16. Disposition of fines and costs . ( 1 ) Any
fine imposed for a violation of this chapter shall be
divided as follows : Forty percent shall be given to
the county attorney or Commonwealth's attorney
who is present and prosecutes the case and the re-
maining sixty percent to the director , to be paid
into the Game and Fish Fund . If no county or Com-
monwealth's attorney actually prosecutes , the en-
tire fine shall be paid into the Game and Fish Fund .
(2 ) Every judgment entered upon the trial of
any violation of this chapter , shall recite whether

394 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or not the county attorney or the Commonwealth's
attorney was present and actively prosecuted the
action on behalf of the Commonwealth . Unless
there is such an entry on the judgment book of the
court showing the presence and participation of
such officer in the trial, he shall not be entitled to
any part of the fine .
(3 ) There shall be taxed as costs in each case ,
where there is a conviction , under this chapter , a
fee of five dollars to be paid to the arresting
officer .
§ 17. Hunting , trapping , fishing or purchasing
pelts without license prohibited . ( 1 ) Except as
provided in subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of this section :
(a ) No person sixteen years of age or older
shall use a gun or take wild animals or wild birds ,
or take fish by angling or buy the pelts of wild
animals for commercial purposes , without first hav-
ing secured a license therefor as provided in this
Act ;
(b) No person under sixteen years of age shall
take wild animals except by trapping or buy the
pelts of wild animals for commercial purposes or
take wild birds without having first procured a
license therefor as provided in this Act ; and
(c) It is the specific purpose of this Act to pro-
hibit the taking of any wild life or wild animals
protected or unprotected , or the angling in any
stream or body of water, whether public or private ,
without the securing of the license provided for
herein .
(2 ) Landowners , their resident children and
lessees on their lands may without a license take
fish by angling from the waters therein and wild
animals and wild birds therefrom subject to the
further provisions of this Act .

CHAPTER 68 395
(3) Nonresidents attending and participating in
any annual state or national field trial need not
obtain a hunting license .
§ 18. Buying , selling or transporting protected
game and fish . ( 1 ) Rabbits , mussels , fish other
than black bass , channel or fiddler cats under fifteen
inches in length , sturgeon or hackleback , crappie,
jack salmon or walleyed pike or striped bass , sand
pike or sauger , and the pelts of fur -bearing animals
may be bought and sold , if they are in legal
possession . No person buying wild animals shall
have in his possession at any one time a greater
number thereof than the legal limit allowed in the
possession of the hunter .
(2 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 4 ) of this
section and in § 29 , no wild animal, wild bird or fish ,
other than those named in subsection ( 1 ) , protected
by this chapter shall be bought or sold .
(3 ) Except as provided in subsection (4 ) of this
section and in § 29 , no person for himself or as
agent or employe shall transport or direct to be
transported any wild birds , wild animals or fish pro-
tected by this chapter , except that a person who
has been issued a license under this chapter and
has the license in his possession may transport any-
where as part of his personal baggage a quantity
of wild birds , wild animals or fish not in excess of
two days ' bag limit at the time of transporting .
(4) This section shall not in any way interfere
with the buying , selling and transporting of the
skins of fur -bearing animals , nor the transporting
of any wild animals , wild birds or fish by the author-
ized officers of the Division of Game and Fish .
§ 19. Licenses and badges ; design of; applica-
tion for. The commission and director may adopt
the design of the licenses required by this chapter
and make all regulations for their issuance . The

396 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
design , however , must contain a badge or button to
be worn on the licensee's outside clothing. The
license shall only be issued pursuant to a written
application subscribed and sworn to by the ap
plicant and giving the following information : suf-
ficient general description of the applicant to
identify him, his name , place of residence and house
number or the name of his nearest neighbor . No
person shall make a false statement or give false
information in the application .
$ 20. Licenses and forms furnished to county
clerk by director . The director shall deliver to
each county clerk, ten days before January 1 of each
year , as many licenses , tags and forms as may be
required, and charge each clerk for the number
issued to him . On January 15 of each year , and
within ten days after that, each county clerk shall
return to the division all unused licenses , stubs and
unused tags , which he has received for the preced-
ing year . The licenses and tags shall specify the
calendar year for which they are issued and all
licenses , tags and permits shall expire on Decem-
ber 31 of that calendar year .
§ 21. Printing of licenses and tags ; expense of.
The blank licenses , metal tags and printed matter
necessary to carry out this chapter shall be pre-
pared under the direction of the director , subject
to the approval of the Director of Purchases and
Public Properties , and shall be paid for in the same
manner as other public printing . This expense shall
be charged to the Game and Fish Fund .
$ 22. License fees ; badge to be worn . (1) The
fees for licenses for residents of Kentucky shall be
as follows :
(a) Fishing license $ 1.00
(b ) Seining license and one tag for one
hundred feet of seine or part thereof 5.25

(3 ) The director may issue to any qualified per-
son a permit to take and transport wild animals ,
birds or fish at any time for scientific or educa-

one year and shall show the period of their validity .
The fee for such permit shall be one dollar .
(4) No person shall take wild animals , birds or

license provided therefor in his possession and the

ner , and if a person takes fish with a seine or hoop

§ 23. County clerk's fees ; issuance of license .

ter , the county clerk shall issue all licenses and tags

plying with this chapter , only on written applica-
tion subscribed and sworn to before the clerk upon
forms provided by the division . The clerk shall sign
the licenses and shall require the persons to whom
the licenses and tags are issued to sign their names
on the margin . The clerk shall keep a correct and
complete copy of all licenses issued in a book to be
furnished by the division , which record shall re-
main in his office and be open to the inspection of
the public during all office hours .
The director§ 24. Contract for game refuge.
with the advice and consent of the Commissioner
