Commissioner of Finance may contract with any
landowner of the state , for a specified term of years
to set aside and maintain land as a game refuge .
The contracts may be recorded in the county clerk's
office .
$ 25. Contract for wild life preservation or
propagation . The division with the approval of
the Commissioner of Finance and with the consent
of the Governor , may enter into any contract with
the United States Government , or any department
or agency thereof, or with any person in regard to
the preservation , protection or propagation of fish ,
game , birds or animals , it may deem to the advan-
tage of the state to enter into .
$ 26. Wild life restoration in cooperation with
Federal Government . Kentucky assents to the
provisions of the Act of Congress entitled " An act
to provide that the United States shall aid the
States in wild life restoration projects , and for
other purposes , " approved Sept. 2 , 1937. The De-
partment of Conservation shall perform such acts
as may be necessary to the conduct and establish-

400 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ment of cooperative wild life restoration projects
in compliance with said Act and with rules and
regulations promulgated by the Secretary of Agri-
culture thereunder . No funds accruing to the state
from license fees paid by hunters shall be diverted
for any other purpose than the administration of
the Division of Game and Fish .
§ 27. Consent to acquisition by United States of
migratory bird reservations . ( 1 ) Kentucky con-
sents to the acquisition by the United States by
purchase , gift, devise or lease of such areas in Ken-
tucky as the United States may deem necessary for
the establishment of migratory bird reservations in
accordance with the Migratory Bird Conservation
Act , approved February 18 , 1929 ( 45 Stat . 1222 ) .
( 2 ) Kentucky reserves complete jurisdiction
and authority in , and over all such areas not incom-
patible with the administration , maintenance , pro-
tection , and control thereof by the United States
under the terms of Acts of Congress . This section
shall not deprive the courts of Kentucky of the
right to try and punish those who violate any of the
criminal or penal statutes of Kentucky in such
areas .
§ 28. Propagation farms ; permits . The direc-
tor may issue permits to propagate fish , frogs , fur-
bearing animals and other game , and he shall make
and publish regulations governing such industry .
The application for a breeder's permit shall be in
writing addressed to the director , be signed by the
applicant , describe the land or waters owned or
leased by the breeder to be used for breeding pur-
poses and contain other facts required by the di-
rector . When it appears that the application is
made in good faith , the director may issue a permit ,
which shall continue in force for one year , upon the

CHAPTER 68 401
payment of a fee of two dollars and fees for tagging
which shall be fixed by the director .
§ 29 Sale of animals by breeders . ( 1 ) A
breeder may sell and transport fish , frogs , fur-
bearing animals and other game , at all times , alive
for propagation , and for food during seasons pre-
scribed by the director . Such fish , frogs , fur-
bearing animals and other game shall be identified
either by marking packages or by individual tag-
ging , as the director may prescribe .
( 2 ) No breeder shall sell game procured from
other than a licensed propagation farm .
$ 30. Poaching . No person shall , without per-
mission, enter upon the premises of a propagation
farm and take fish , birds or quadrupeds , or upon a
pond and take fish or amphibians or foul the waters
of the farm or pond with a substance injurious to
the life or growth of fish or amphibians or break or
destroy a dam , reservoir or embankment , or divert
the water , or willfully damage the farm or pond .
§ 31. Gun required for taking birds ; lights pro-
hibited . ( 1 ) No person shall take a wild bird ex-
cept with a gun which gun shall not be larger than
a ten gauge fired from the shoulder . If a shotgun
is used , the magazine shall be plugged with a solid
wooden or metal plug in such a way that the gun
will hold not more than three cartridges .
(2 ) No persons shall hunt birds with lights or
other means used to blind birds at night .
$ 32. Birds not protected ; nests and eggs .
(1 ) No person shall take any wild bird except
game birds for which there is an open season , either
by the laws of Kentucky or the United States , and
those birds mentioned in subsection ( 2 ) .
( 2 ) This chapter does not protect or limit in
any way the taking of the Cooper's hawk, sharp-
shinned hawk, the crow, the starling , the English

402 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sparrow or the great horned owl, but any person
taking them with a gun must have a hunting
license .
(3) No person shall take the nests or eggs of
any wild birds .
33. Open seasons ; migratory birds . ( 1 ) No
person shall take or have in his possession any wild
birds except during the open season for a particu-
lar species . The open season for quail or bob white
shall be November 24 to January 9. There shall be
no open season for ruffed grouse , wild turkey, ring-
neck pheasant and Hungarian partridge . No per-
son shall take quail except from sunrise to sunset .
( 2 ) No person shall take , pursue , possess ,
transport , purchase or sell or attempt so to do , any
migratory birds , except as authorized by the Mi-
gratory Bird Treaty Act ( 40 Stat . 755 ) and regu-
lations under it . No person over sixteen years of
age shall hunt any wild ducks , geese or brant unless
at the time , in addition to the appropriate state
hunting license , he has on his person a valid migra-
tory bird hunting stamp of current issue as re-
quired by the Migratory Bird Hunting Stamp Act
(48 Stat . 451 ) as amended .
§ 34 Bag limit of birds . ( 1 ) No person shall ,
in any one day , take more wild birds for which
there is an open season , than the bag limit for
the species as set out herein :
Quail 12
Doves
Ducks
Geese
Federal Regulations apply .
Federal Regulations apply .
Federal Regulations apply .
Coots and Gallinule ...... Federal Regulations apply .
Wood Cock
Snipe
Federal Regulations apply .
Federal Regulations apply .

CHAPTER 68 403
$ 35. Bird dogs not to run at large , when . No
owner or keeper or a bird dog shall permit the dog
to run at large either on the premises of the owner
or keeper or elsewhere , from May 15 to August 15
of each year . Each day a dog is permitted to run
at large shall constitute a separate offense .
§ 36. Ferrets . No person shall use ferrets in
hunting . No persons shall keep a ferret as a pet or
for any purposes other than hunting , unless he has
secured a ferret permit from the Director . A per-
mit must be obtained within ten days after the per-
son acquires possession of a ferret and one permit
entitles the holder to keep any number of ferrets .
If the owner of any ferret uses it contrary to the
provisions of this Act , the Director may revoke his
permit and confiscate all his ferrets . Each use of a
feret in hunting shall constitute a separate offense .
§ 37. Wild animals ; how may be taken ; lights
prohibited , when . ( 1 ) No person shall take wild
animals protected by this chapter except by trap-
ping , gig , gun , gun and dog or dog .
( 2) A gun used in taking wild animals shall be
no larger than a ten gauge and fired from the shoul-
der . If it is a shotgun , the magazine , if any , shall
be plugged with a solid wooden or metal plug in
such a way that the gun will hold not more than
three cartridges .
(3 ) No person shall hunt rabbits with lights or
other means used to blind rabbits at night .
(4 ) No person shall use a gig other than one of
the following sizes and dimensions : The gig shall
not have more than four prongs ; no prong shall be
longer than two and one -half inches ; the prongs
shall be made out of not heavier than 14-gauge wire
or metal , and shall not have more than one barb on
each prong ; and the gig shall be attached to a pole

404 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
which together with the gig will not exceed five feet
in length .
§ 38. Open season for wild animals . ( 1 ) No
person shall take any wild animal protected by this
chapter except during the open season for the par-
ticular species . No person shall have in possession
any wild animal or freshly skinned hide or fur
thereof unless it was taken during the open season
for the particular species . The open season for
mink, opossum , raccoon , skunk , rabbit , and musk-
rat shall be November 24 to January 9 ; for squirrel ,
August 15 to October 31 ; for red fox , December 1
to December 31 ; and pond , bull or jumbo frogs ,
June 15 to April 15. There shall be no open season
for otter and beaver . Opossum and raccoon may
also be hunted and taken by dog only from Novem-
ber 1 to February 1 , inclusive .
(2 ) Subsection (1 ) shall not apply to persons
who hunt red fox at night with dogs for sport and
not to kill ; but this exception in favor of sportsmen
shall not extend to anyone engaged in the business
of a fur dealer or a trapper or professional hunter .
(3 ) The possession of any wild animals , fur
bearers , or the freshly skinned hide or fur thereof ,
when the season is not open shall be prima facie
evidence that the animal was taken out of open sea-
son , but this presumption shall not apply to the
possession of any skunk , muskrat or mink , or
freshly skinned hide or fur thereof, between Jan-
uary 9 and February 15 , nor to the possession of
any raccoon or opossum , or freshly skinned hide or
fur thereof between February 1 and February 15 .
$ 39. Bag limits of wild animals . No person
shall in any one day take more than six squirrels ,
eight rabbits or fifteen pond , bull or jumbo frogs ;
nor have in his possession more than two days ' bag

CHAPTER 68 405
limit of any particular animal for which a bag limit
is established .
$ 40. Hunting deer , wild turkey or bear pro-
hibited . No person shall take , pursue , or molest
any elk , deer , wild turkey or bear .
§ 41. Traps ; type permitted ; permission of
landowner ; must be visited . ( 1 ) No person shall
set , use or maintain , for the capture of fur -bearing
animals , any trap , snare , or other device that does
not either take alive unhurt or kill at once . It shall
be lawful to use any leghold traps which take and
hold without pain or injury , any box or wire -cage
trap , the old - fashioned deadfall and the instant-
killing traps for very small animals . Instant -killing
traps shall be set within a burrow or hole , so as not
to injure domestic animals .
( 2 ) No person shall set traps on the land of an-
other person without written permission from the
owner of the land .
( 3 ) Each person who sets a trap must visit it at
least once in each twenty -four hours and release
any animal found therein .
§ 42. Permission to trap to be exhibited ; traps
to be tagged . No person shall set or attempt to set
any trap on the land of another without the written
permission of the owner in his possession and he
shall exhibit said written consent to anyone lawfully
demanding to examine it . Any trap set on the land
of another shall be marked with a metal tag , giving
the owner's name and place of residence .
43. Fur buyer's license ; issuance . ( 1 ) Be-
fore a person engages in the business of buying
furs and skins of fur -bearing animals , he shall se-
cure a license from the director .
( 2 ) No license shall be issued to a resident of a
state or county which does not grant equal priv-
ileges to citizens of this state .

406 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
$ 44. Purchasing and shipping pelts ; license re-
quired when . No person , without a license , shall
purchase furs or skins from any person other than
a regular licensed dealer . Individuals may ship
furs and skins owned by them to dealers outside
the state .
§ 45. Fish ; method and time of taking . ( 1 ) Ex-
cept as is otherwise allowed by this chapter , no per-
son shall take fish from the public waters of the
state except by angling.
(2 ) No person shall take fish or attempt to take
fish from any of the public waters of this state
during the month of May each year , except with a
pole and line in navigable streams below the last
lock and dam .
§ 46. Use of nets and seines . ( 1 ) Minnows and
crawfish to be used for bait for angling may be
taken from the public waters of this state with
seine , but the seine shall not be more than six feet
in length and four feet in width or height , with
mesh not larger than one -fourth inch . Any fish
caught which are excluded from the definition of
minnows in § 1 shall be immediately returned with-
out injury to the water from which they are taken .
( 2 ) Any person may take by the use of seines
and hoop nets without wings , the mesh of which is
not less than two inches square , any fish from
streams forming part of the boundary of this state
and from the navigable streams of this state . But
no person shall take or attempt to take any fish by
seines or nets or place any seines or nets in any
of these streams within two hundred yards of the
mouth of any stream entering into those waters , nor
within two hundred yards of the mouth of any
navigable stream within the state nor within two
hundred yards of any lock or dam located on the
stream nor above the last lock or dam in any

CHAPTER 68 407
navigable stream . Any fish protected by this Act
caught in seines or nets shall immediately be re-
turned without injury to the water from which
taken .
(3 ) No person shall operate or have in posses-
sion any seines or nets unless he has procured a
license so to do and a metal tag for the use or pos-
session of each net or seine and complied with the
provisions of this chapter . The metal tags shall be
affixed to and displayed on the seines or nets at
all times . This section does not require dealers or
manufactures to obtain licenses and tags for seines
and nets in their possession for the purpose of sale .
§ 47. Injurious substances and explosives not
to be used . ( 1 ) No person , whether acting in a
private or public capacity , shall place in any pub-
lic waters any substance that causes the waters
to be unfit for the support of acquatic life . Each
day that this subsection is violated shall constitute
a separate offense .
( 2 ) No person shall willfully and knowingly
kill , injure , shock or stun , or attempt to kill or
injure , shock or stun any fish by any explosive
agent or similar substance .
(3 ) No person shall willfully place or attempt to
place in any of the public waters of the state , for
the purpose of taking fish, any substance which has
a poisonous or intoxicating affect on aquatic life .
§ 48. Limit on number and size of fish taken .
(1 ) No person shall take or have in his possession
more than ten black bass , ten trout , fifteen rock bass
or goggle -eye , fifteen crappie , fifteen jack salmon
or walleyed pike , fifteen sand pike or sauger , or
fifteen striped bass in any one day , except that
any person having fished two or more days in suc-
cession may have in possession a total of not more
than twice the number allowed for one day.

408 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(2 ) No person shall take or have in his posses-
sion any black bass under eleven inches in length ,
nor any trout under seven inches in length, nor
any crappie under eight inches in length , nor any
channel or fiddler cat under fifteen inches in length ,
nor any jack salmon or walleyed pike , sand pike or
sauger under thirteen inches in length .
§ 49. Fish ladders or roads . ( 1 ) All persons
owning dams constructed across any stream , where
the annual tides are not sufficiently high to admit
the passage of fish over the dams , shall erect and
maintain over the dams , during April , May and
June of each year , fish ladders or roads , that will
allow the passage of fish over the dams .
( 2 ) This section shall not apply to locks and
dams constructed across large rivers to facilitate
navigation ; nor to dams constructed for the pur-
pose of generating electricity , where the height or
type of the dams is such that the erection and
maintenance of fish ladders or roads would not be
effective in allowing the passage of fish over the
dams .
50. Live bait dealers . No person shall sell
live bait to be used in angling unless he has ob-
tained from the Director a live bait dealers license .
No live bait dealer shall export any minnows or
crawfish from this state or sell them to any non-
resident if he knows the non -resident plans to take
them out of the state .
(1 ) No person$ 51 . License for musseling.
shall take mussels for commercial purposes without
a license issued by the director .
( 2 ) The director may , upon application , issue
a license to take or kill mussels . Upon making ap-
plication for a musseling license , residents of this
state shall pay a fee of five dollars and nonresi-
dents shall pay a fee of twenty dollars to the Game

CHAPTER 68 409
and Fish Division . All licenses shall expire on De-
cember 31 following their issue .
(3 ) The licenses shall be consecutively numbered
as issued and records of licenses shall be kept in
the office of the Game and Fish Division . A license
shall state whether it is a resident or nonresident
license , and shall entitle the holder to operate one
boat only. An operator desiring to operate more
than one boat shall secure a separate license for
each boat . The holder of a license while taking ,
catching or killing mussels for commercial pur-
poses shall have his license with him ready for ex-
hibition , and shall exhibit it when required to do
so by an authorized officer .
$ 52. Restriction uponupon musseling operation .
A musseling license shall entitle the holder to op-
erate on any shellbed , but not more than four boats
may operate to the mile of shell bed .
$ 53. Report of musseling operations. On or
before December 31 of the year in which any license
is issued , the holder shall make a written report to
the Game and Fish Division , stating the total weight
of mussels taken under his license , the names and
location of the waters from which the mussels
were taken and the amount received for shells sold .
Upon the failure to make such report , the director
shall not issue another license until such report
is made .
$ 54. Division may regulate musseling . The
Game and Fish Division shall make such rules and
regulations governing the operation of boats in the
taking of mussels as it may deem best for the
proper enforcement of this chapter . To prevent
the depletion of the mussel beds and to insure
proper propagation of the mussels the Game and
Fish Division may close any beds to operators at any
time and for any period that it deems necessary .

410 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
During such closed season on any bed no one shall
take mussels from that bed . When an order is
issued closing any mussel bed due notice of the
order shall be published in at least three consecu-
tive issues of a newspaper published in the county
where the bed is located .
$ 55. Penalties . (1 ) Each bird , fish or animal
taken , possessed , bought , sold or transported and
each device used or possessed contrary to the pro-
visions of this chapter shall constitute a separate
offense . The penalties prescribed in this section
shall be for each offense .
(2 ) Any person who violates any of the pro-
visions of this Act or any regulation made by the
director under authority of this Act , for which no
different fine or imprisonment is fixed by this sec-
tion , shall be fined not less than fifteen nor more
than one hundred dollars .
( 3) Any person who violates any of the pro
visions of §§ 30 , 33 , 40 , 50 , subsection ( 3 ) of § 9 ,
subsection ( 2 ) of § 18 or subsection ( 1 ) of § 47 of
this Act shall be fined not less than twenty -five dol-
lars nor more than two hundred dollars or im-
prisoned for not more than six months , or both .
(4) Any person who violates subsection ( 2 ) or
subsection ( 3 ) of § 47 of this Act shall be guilty of
a felony and imprisoned not less than one nor more
than three years and fined not more than $500 .
(5 ) Any licensee who violates any provision of
this Act or any regulation made by the director
under authority of this Act shall , in addition to the
penalties provided in subsections ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) and ( 4 )
of this section , forfeits his license .
$ 56 . The Division of Game and Fish in the De-
partment of Conservation is continued . The di-
vision , headed by a director , shall enforce all the
laws relating to wild life and exercise all necessary

CHAPTER 69 411
powers incident thereto , under the supervision of
the Commissioner of Conservation . The Advisory
Game and Fish Commission shall be attached to the
Division of Game and Fish .
§ 57. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section 56 of this Act shall stand repealed as of
the effective date of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
and KRS 146.020 and 146.030 shall remain in full
force and effect and shall not be treated as affected
by this Act.
$ 58 . Sections 1392a - 1 to 1392a -3 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , $§ 1893d - 9 ,
1893d -10 , 1893d - 11 and 1954d -1 to 1954d -73 of
Baldwin's 1938 Kentucky Statute Service , and
Chapters 85 , 86 , 87 , 88 and 90 of the Acts of 1940
are repealed .
§ 59. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, 58 of this Act shall be treated as repealing
all of KRS Chapter 150 , regardless of the date of
enactment or taking effect of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
Approved 2nd March , 1942
CHAPTER 69
(H. B. 297)
AN ACT relating to the Federal food or cotton stamp plan .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
" Any person , other than the person authorized
by the regulations prescribed by the Secretary of
Agriculture of the United States governing the food
stamp plan or the cotton stamp plan , who shall have

412 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
in his possession , or who shall purchase , sell or ex-
change any food stamps or cotton stamps , books ,
book covers or any other instruments or documents
relating to food stamps or cotton stamps , for
money or for any other article or articles other than
those foods or cotton goods authorized by the regu-
lations prescribed by the Secretary of Agriculture
of the United States governing the food stamp plan
or the cotton stamp plan or for foods or cotton
goods the value of which is less than the face amount
of such food stamps or cotton stamps , or in any
other manner in violation of the regulations pre-
scribed by the Secretary of Agriculture of the
United States governing the food stamp plan or the
cotton stamp plan shall be fined from $ 100.00 to
$250.00 for each offense .
Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 70
(H. B. 309 )
AN ACT to authorize the creation and establishment of
County Housing Commissions by counties , of Regional
Housing Commissions by two or more counties , and Con-
solidated Housing Commissions by two or more munici-
palities to undertake slum clearance and housing projects
to provide dwelling accommodations for persons of low
income ; to provide that such Commissions shall be public
bodies corporate and politic , and to define the rights ,
powers , duties and immunities of such Commissions ; to
provide for the exercise of such powers including acquir-
ing property , borrowing money, issuing bonds and other
obligations and giving security therefor ; to specifically
authorize County and Regional Housing Commissions to
undertake housing projects for farmers of low income ; to
authorize Housing Commissions of municipalities to operate
in other municipalities with their consent ; to authorize
Housing Commissions to cooperate with one another and to
provide certain security for their obligations , to define the

CHAPTER 70 413
area for their meetings and the residence requirements for
their commissioners ; to authorize certain security for
certain obligations of Housing Commissions ; to repeal
Chapter 23 of the Acts of 1940 and to declare an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions and Construction . ( 1 ) As used
in this Act , unless the context requires otherwise :
(a) " Bonds " means any bonds , notes , interim
certificates , debentures , and other obligations issued
by the Commission pursuant to this Act .
(b) " Clerk " means the clerk of the city or the
clerk of the county , as the case may be , or the officer
charged with the duties customarily imposed on
such clerk .
(c) "Commission " or " Housing Commission "
means any of the public corporations created pur-
suant to this Act .
(d ) "Federal Government " includes the United
States of America , the United States Housing
Authority or any other agency or instrumentality ,
corporate or otherwise , of the United States of
America .
(e) "Governing body" means , in the case of a
city , the City Council , the Commission , the Board
of Trustees or other legislative body of the city ;
and in the case of a county , the fiscal court .
(f) " Housing Project " means any work or
undertaking : To demolish , clear or remove build-
ings from any slum area ; such work or undertak-
ing may embrace the adaptation of such area to
public purposes , including parks or other recrea-
tional or community purposes ; or to provide decent ,
safe and sanitary urban or rural dwellings , apart
ments or other living accommodations for persons
of low income ; such work or undertaking may in-

414 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
clude buildings , land , equipment , facilities and
other real or personal property for necessary , con-
venient or desirable appurtenances , streets , sewers ,
water service , parks , site preparation , gardening ,
administrative , community , health , recreational ,
welfare or other purposes ; or to accomplish a com-
bination of the foregoing . The term " housing
project " may also be applied to the planning of the
buildings and improvements , the acquisition of
property , the demolition of existing structures , the
construction , reconstruction , alteration and repair
of the improvements and all other work in connec-
tion therewith .
(g ) " Mayor ' means the Mayor of the city or
the officer thereof charged with the duties customar-
ily imposed on the Mayor or executive head of the
city .
(h) " Obligee of the Commission " or " obligee "
shall include any bondholder , trustee or trustees for
any bondholders , or lessor demising to the Commis-
sion property used in connection with a housing
project , or any assignee or assignees of such lessor's
interest or any part thereof , and the Federal Gov-
ernment when it is a party to any contract with the
Commission .
(i) "Persons of low income " means persons orfamilies who lack the amount of income which is
necessary ( as determined by the Housing Commis-
sion undertaking the housing project ) to enable
them , without financial assistance , to live in decent ,
safe and sanitary dwellings , without overcrowding .
(j ) " Real property ' includes all lands , including
improvements and fixtures thereon , and property
of any nature appurtenant thereto , or used in con-
nection therewith , and every estate , interest and
right , legal or equitable , therein , including terms

CHAPTER 70 415
for years and liens by way of judgment, mortgage
or otherwise and the indebtedness secured by such
liens .
(k) " Slum " means any area where dwelling's
predominate which , by reason of dilapidation ,
overcrowding , lack of ventilation , light or sanitary
facilities , or any combination of these factors , are
detrimental to safety , health and morals .
(2 ) The powers contained in this Act shall be
in addition and supplemental to the powers con-
tained in any other Act .
§ 2. Creation of County and Regional Housing
Commissions . ( 1 ) If the governing body of a
county by resolution declares (upon findings pre-
scribed in this Act ) that there is a need for a Hous-
ing Commission to be created for such county to
exercise therein powers and other functions pre-
scribed for a Housing Commission of a county , a
County Housing Commission shall thereupon exist
for such county .
( 2 ) If the governing body of each of two or
more contiguous counties by resolution declares
(upon findings prescribed in this Act ) that there
is a need for one Housing Commission to be created
for all of such counties to exercise in such counties
powers and other functions prescribed for a
Regional Housing Commission , a Regional Housing
Commission shall thereupon exist for all of such
counties and exercise its powers and other functions
in such counties . If there is a County Housing
Commission created for a county , the governing
body of that county shall not adopt such resolution
unless first , all holders of any outstanding bonds ,
notes , or other evidences of indebtedness of such
County Housing Commission consent in writing to
such action and the County Housing Commission

416 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
adopts a resolution not to thereafter initiate any
project within such county .
.
§ 3. Area of Operation of County and Regional
Housing Commissions . The area of operation of
Housing Commission created for a county shall in-
clude all of the county for which it is created and
the area of operations of a Regional Housing Com-
mission shall include ( except as otherwise provided
in this Act ) all of the counties for which such
Regional Housing Commission is created and estab-
lished . But no County or Regional Housing Com-
mission shall undertake any housing project or
projects within the boundaries of a city unless a
resolution has been adopted by the governing body
of the city ( and also by any Housing Commission
which has been created for that city pursuant to
this Act or the Municipal Housing Commission Act ,
Chapter 113 of the Acts of 1934 and amendments )
declaring that there is a need for the County or
Regional Housing Commission to exercise its
powers within that city .
§ 4. Increasing Area of Operation of Regional
Housing Commission . The area of operation of a
Regional Housing Commission shall be increased
from time to time to include one or more additional
continguous * counties not already within a Regional
Housing Commission if the governing body of each
of the counties then included in the area of opera-
tion of the Regional Housing Commission , the com-
missioners of the Regional Housing Commission
and the governing body of each such additional
county or counties each adopt a resolution declaring
that there is a need for the inclusion of such ad-
ditional county or counties in the area of operation
of the Regional Housing Commission . Such resolu-
tions shall not be adopted if there is a County Hous-
* Contiguous .

CHAPTER 70 417
ing Commission created for any such additional
county , unless first , all holders of any outstanding
bonds , notes or other evidences of indebtedness of
that County Housing Commission consent in writ-
ing to such action and the County Housing Com-
mission adopts a resolution not to thereafter initiate
any project within that county .
§ 5. Decreasing Area of Operation of Regional
Housing Commission . The area of operation of a
Regional Housing Commission shall be decreased
from time to time to exclude one or more counties
from such area if the governing body of each of the
counties in such area and the commissioners of the
Regional Housing Commission each adopt a resolu-
tion declaring that there is a need for excluding
such county or counties from such area . No action.
shall be taken pursuant to this section if the
Regional Housing Commission has outstanding any
bonds , notes or other evidences of indebtedness ,
unless first , all holders of such evidences of in-
debtedness consent in writing to such action . If
such action decreases the area of operation of the
Regional Housing Commission to only one county ,
the Commission shall thereupon constitute and be-
come a Housing Commission for that county , in the
same manner as though the Commission were
created pursuant to § 2 , and the Commissioners of
such County Housing Commission shall be there-
upon appointed as provided for the appointment of
commissioners of a Housing Commission created
for a county .
§ 6. The governing body of each of the counties
in the area of operation of the Regional Housing
Commission and the commissioners of the Regional
Housing Commission shall adopt a resolution de-
claring that there is a need for excluding a county
or counties from such area if :
Acts- 14

418 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) Each of the governing bodies of the coun-
ties to remain in the area of operation of the
Regional Housing Commission and the commis-
sioners of the Regional Housing Commission find
that (because of facts arising or determined sub-
sequent to the time when such area first included
the county or counties to be excluded ) the Regional
Housing Commission would be a more efficient or
economical administrative unit to carry out the pur-
poses of this Act if such county or counties were
excluded from such area ; and
( 2 ) The governing body of each such county or
counties to be excluded and the commissioners of
the Regional Housing Commission each also find
that (because of the aforesaid changed facts ) the
purposes of this Act could be carried out more
efficiently or economically in such county or counties
if the area of operation of the Regional Housing
Commission did not include such county or counties .
§ 7. Any property held by a Regional Housing
Commission within a county or counties excluded
from the area of operation of such Commission , as
herein provided , shall ( as soon as practicable after
the exclusion of said county or counties respec-
tively ) be disposed of by such Commission in the
public interest .
§ 8. No governing body of a city or county shall
adopt a resolution under this Act declaring that
there is a need for a Housing Commission to be
created , or to exercise its powers within such city or
county , or that there is a need to include such city
or county within the area of operation of a Housing
Commission, unless such governing body shall have
found substantially that :
( 1 ) Insanitary or unsafe inhabited dwelling ac-
commodations exist in such city or county or that
there is a shortage of safe or sanitary dwelling ac-

CHAPTER 70 419
commodations in such city or county available to
persons of low income at rentals they can afford ;
and
( 2 ) These conditions can be best remedied
through the exercise of the Housing Commission's
powers within the territorial boundaries of such
city or county .
9. In determining under this Act whether
dwelling accommodations are unsafe or insanitary
a governing body shall take into consideration the
safety and sanitation of dwellings , the light and air
space available to the inhabitants of such dwellings ,
the degree of overcrowding , the size and arrange-
ment of the rooms and the extent to which con-
ditions exist in such dwellings which endanger life
or property by fire or other causes .
§ 10. The governing body of a county shall not
adopt any resolution authorized by Sections 2 , 4,
5 or 6 with regard to a Regional Housing Commis-
sion unless a public hearing has first been held .
The clerk of that county shall give notice of the
time , place , and purpose of the public hearing at
least ten days prior to the day on which the hearing
is to be held , in a newspaper published in that
county , or if there is no newspaper published in that
county , then in a newspaper having a general cir-
culation in that county . Upon the date fixed for
such public hearing an opportunity to be heard
shall be granted to all residents of that county and
to all other interested persons .
§ 11. In any suit, action or proceeding involving
the validity or enforcement of or relating to any
contract of a Commission , the Commission shall be
conclusively deemed to have become created as a
public body corporate and politic upon proof of the
adoption of the resolution or resolutions of the gov-
erning body or bodies declaring the need for the

420 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Commission as required in this Act . Such resolu-
tion or resolutions shall be deemed sufficent if it
declares that there is such need for the Commission
and finds in substantially the foregoing terms (no
further detail being necessary ) that the conditions
enumerated in § 8 exist in the city or county , as the
case may be. A copy of the resolution or resolu-
tions duly certified by the Clerk shall be admissible
in evidence in any suit , action or proceeding .
§ 12. If a Housing Commission is created for a
county , the governing body of that county shall
appoint five persons as commissioners of the Hous-
ing Commission . Three of the five commissioners
who are first appointed shall be designated to serve
for terms of one , two or three years and two of such
commissioners for terms of four years , respectively ,
from the date of their appointments , but thereafter
commissioners of the Housing Commission shall be
appointed as aforesaid for a term of office of four
years except that all vacancies shall be filled by the
governing body for the unexpired terms .
§ 13. ( 1 ) The governing body of each county
included in a Regional Housing Commission shall
appoint one person as a commissioner of such Con-
mission, and each such commissioner to be first ap-
pointed by the governing body of a county may be
appointed at or after the time of the adoption of the
resolution declaring the need for such Regional
Housing Commission or declaring the need for
the inclusion of such county in the area of
operation of such Regional Housing Commission .
When the area of operation of a Regional Housing
Commission is increased to include an additional
county or counties as provided above , the governing
body of each such county shall thereupon appoint
one additional person as a commissioner of the
Regional Housing Commission . The governing

CHAPTER 70 421
body of each county shall appoint the successor of
the commissioner appointed by it . If any county
is excluded from the area of operation of a Regional
Housing Commission , the office of the commissioner
of such Regional Housing Commission appointed
by the governing body of such county shall be there-
upon abolished .
(2 ) If the area of operation of the Regional
Housing Commission consists at any time of an
even number of counties , the commissioners of the
Regional Housing Commission appointed by the
governing bodies of such counties shall appoint one
additional commissioner whose term of office shall
be as herein provided for a commissioner of a
Regional Housing Commission except that such
term shall end at any earlier time that the area of
operation of the Regional Housing Commission
shall be changed to consist of an odd number of
counties . The commissioners of such Commission
appointed by the governing bodies of such counties
shall likewise appoint each person to succeed such
additional commissioner ; provided that the term of
office of such person begins during the terms of office
of the commissioners appointing him.
(3 ) The commissioners of a Regional Housing
Commission shall be appointed for terms of four
years except that all vacancies shall be filled by the
appointing authority for the unexpired term .
§ 14. ( 1 ) A certificate of the appointment of
any commissioner shall be filed with the clerk (pro-
vided that in the case of appointment of a commis-
sioner of a Regional Housing Commission by its
other commissioners , as provided above , such cer-
tificate shall be signed by the commissioners mak-
ing the appointment and filed with the other records
of the Regional Housing Commission ) and such

422 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
certificate shall be conclusive evidence of the due
and proper appointment of such commissioner
(2 ) No commissioner shall be an officer or em-
ployee of a county for which the Commission is
created . A commissioner shall hold office until his
successor has been appointed and has qualified , ex-
cept as otherwise provided herein .
(3) A commissioner shall receive no compensa-
tion for his services for the Commission , in any
capacity , but he shall be entitled to the necessary
travel and other expenses incurred in the discharge
of his duties .
§ 15. The commissioners shall constitute the
Housing Commission . A majority of the commis-
sioners shall constitute a quorum for the purpose
of conducting business and exercising powers and
for all other purposes . Action may be taken by the
Commission upon a vote of a majority of the com-
missioners present , unless in any case the bylaws of
the Commission require a larger number . A Com-
mission shall select from among its commissioners
a chairman and a vice -chairman , and it may employ
a secretary (who shall be executive director ) ,
technical experts and such other officers , agents
and other employees , permanent and temporary , as
it may require , and shall determine their qualifica-
tions , duties and compensation . For such legal
services as it may require, a Commission may call
upon the chief law officer of the city or a county for
which it is created , or may employ its own counsel
and legal staff . A Commission may delegate to one
or more of its agents or employees such powers or
duties as it deems proper .
§ 16. Meetings of the commissioners of a Hous-
ing Commission (including any Housing Commis-
sion created pursuant to the Municipal Housing
Commission Act ) may be held anywhere within the

CHAPTER 70 423
perimeter boundaries of the area of operation or
territorial jurisdiction of the Commission or within
any additional area where the Housing Commission
is authorized to undertake a housing project , and
any person may be appointed as a commissioner of
the Housing Commission who resides within such
boundaries or such additional area , and who is
otherwise eligible for such appointment under this
Act or the Municipal Housing Commission Act , as
the case may be.
$ 17. Interested Commissioners or Employees .
No commissioner or employee of a Commission shall
acquire any interest direct or indirect in any hous-
ing project or in any property included or planned
to be included in any project , nor shall he have any
interest direct or indirect in any contract or pro-
posed contract for materials or services to be
furnished or used in connection with any housing
project . If any commissioner or employee of a
Commission owns or controls an interest direct or
indirect in any property included or planned to be
included in any housing project , he immediately
shall disclose the same in writing to the Commis-
sion and such disclosure shall be entered upon the
minutes of the Commission . Failure so to disclose
such interest shall constitute misconduct in office .
Upon such disclosure such commissioner or em
ployee shall not participate in any action by the
Commission affecting such property .
§ 18. Removal of Commissioners . For ineffici
ency or neglect of duty or misconduct in office , a
commissioner may be removed by the authority ap-
pointing him, but a commissioner shall be removed
only after he has been given a copy of the charges
against him at least 10 days prior to the hearing
thereon and has had an opportunity to be heard in
person or by counsel . In the event of the removal

424 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of any commissioner , a record of the proceedings ,
together with the charges and findings thereon ,
shall be filed as required for the certificate of ap-
pointment of such commissioner .
$ 19. Consolidated Housing Commission . ( 1 )
If the governing body of each of two or more cities
(whether or not contiguous ) by resolution declares
that there is a need for one Housing Commission to
be created for all of such cities to exercise in such
cities the powers and other functions prescribed
for a Consolidated Housing Commission , a public
body corporate and politic to be known as a Con-
solidated Housing Commission (with such corpo-
rate name as it selects ) shall thereupon exist for
all of such cities and exercise its powers and other
functions within its area of operation ( as herein
defined ) , including the power to undertake projects
therein .
(2 ) The creation of a Consolidated Housing
Commission and the finding of need therefor shall
be subject to the same provisions and limitations of
this Act as are applicable to the creation of a
Regional Housing Commission and that all of the
provisions of this Act applicable to Regional Hous-
ing Commissions and the commissioners thereof
sjall * be applicable to Consolidated Housing Com-
missions and the commissioners thereof .
(3 ) The area of operation of a Consolidated
Housing Commission shall include all of the ter
ritory within the boundaries of each city joining in
the creation of such Commission , except that such
area of operation may be changed to include or ex-
clude any city or cities in the same manner and
under the same provisions as provided in this Act
for changing the area of operation of a Regional
* Shall .

CHAPTER 70 425
Housing Commission by including or excluding a
county or counties .
(4) If the area of operation of a Consolidated
Housing Commission is decreased to only one city ,
such Commission shall thereupon constitute and
become a Housing Commission for such city , in the
same manner as though such Commission were
thereupon created pursuant to the Municipal Hous-
ing Commission Act ; and for all such purposes the
term " county " shall be construed as meaning
"city " , the terms " County Housing Commission ' '
and " Regional Housing Commission " shall be
construed as meaning " Housing Commission of the
city" and "Consolidated Housing Commission " ,
respectively , and the term " governing body" as
used in §§ 12 , 13 and 14 of this Act shall be con-
strued as meaning " mayor", unless a different
meaning clearly appears from the context .
$ 20. Powers of Commission . A Commission
shall constitute a public body corporate and politic ,
exercising public and essential governmental
functions , and having all the powers necessary or
convenient to carry out and effectuate the purposes
and provisions of this Act , including the following
powers in addition to others herein granted :
( 1 ) To sue and be sued ; to have a seal and to
alter the same at pleasure ; to have perpetual suc-
cession ; to make and execute contracts and other
instruments necessary or convenient to the exercise
of the powers of the Commission ; and to make and
from time to time amend and repeal bylaws , rules
and regulations , not inconsistent with this Act , to
carry into effect the powers and purposes of the
Commission .
( 2 ) Within its area of operation ; to prepare ,
carry out, acquire , lease and operate housing
projects ; to provide for the construction , recon-

426 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
struction , improvement , alteration or repair of any
housing project or any part thereof .
( 3 ) To arrange or contract for the furnishing
by any person or agency , public or private , of
services , privileges , works , or facilities for , or in
connection with , a housing project or the occupants
thereof ; and ( notwithstanding anything to the con-
trary contained in this Act or in any other pro-
vision of law ) to include in any contract let in con-
nection with a project , stipulations requiring that
the contractor and any subcontractors comply with
requirements as to minimum wages and maximum
hours of labor , and comply with any conditions
which the Federal Government may have attached
to its financial aid of the project .
(4 ) To lease or rent any dwellings , houses , ac-
commodations , lands , buildimgs ,* structures or
facilities embraced in any housing project and
(subject to the limitations contained in this Act ) to
establish and revise the rents or charges therefor ;
to own , hold, and improve real or personal prop-
erty ; to purchase , lease , obtain options upon ,
acquire by gift , grant , bequest , devise , or otherwise
any real or personal property or any interest
therein ; to acquire by the exercise of the power of
eminent domain or condemnation any real prop-
erty ; to sell , lease , exchange , transfer , assign ,
pledge or dispose of any real or personal property
or any interest therein ; to insure or provide for the
insurance of any real or personal property or oper-
ations of the Commission against any risks or
hazards .
(5 ) To invest any funds held in reserves
sinking funds , or any funds not required for im-
mediate disbursement , in property or securities in
which savings banks may legally invest funds sub-
* Buildings .

CHAPTER 70 427
ject to their control ; to purchase its bonds at a price
not more than the principal amount thereof and
accrued interest , all bonds so purchased to be
cancelled .
(6) To exercise all or any part or combination
of powers herein granted .
§ 21. Rural Housing Projects . County Hous-
ing Commissions and Regional Housing Commis .
sions are specifically empowered and authorized to
borrow money , accept grants and exercise their
other powers to provide housing for farmers of low
income . In connection with such projects , any such
Housing Commission may enter into such leases
or purchase agreements , accept such conveyances
and rent or sell dwellings forming part of such proj-
ects to or for farmers of low income , as such Hous-
ing Commission deems necessary in order to as-
sure the achievement of the objectives of this Act .
Such leases , agreements or conveyances may in-
clude such covenants as the Housing Commission
deems appropriate regarding such dwellings and
the tracts of land described in any such instrument ,
which covenants shall be deemed to run with the
land where the Housing Commission deems it neces-
sary and the parties to such instrument so stipulate .
Nothing in this section shall limit any other powers
of any Housing Commission .
$ 22. Farmers of Low Income Defined . " Farm-
ers of low income , " as used in this Act , shall mean
persons or families who at the time of their admis-
sion to occupancy in a dwelling of a Housing Com-
mission :
( 1 ) Live under unsafe or insanitary housing
conditions ;
( 2 ) Derive their principal income from oper-
ating or working upon a farm ; and

428 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(3 ) Had an aggregate average annual net in-
come for the three years preceding their admission
that was less than the amount determined by the
Housing Commission to be necessary , within its
area of operation, to enable them , without financial
assistance , to obtain decent , safe and sanitary hous-
ing , without overcrowding .
$ 23. Tenant Selection . Except with regard to
housing for farmers of low income , a Housing Com-
mission shall be subject to the same limitations and
restrictions on rentals and tenant selection which
are applicable to Housing Commissions created for
cities under the Housing Commission Act .
$ 24. Condemnation . A Commission shall have
the right to acquire by condemnation any real prop-
erty which it may deem necessary for its purposes
under this Act after the adoption by it of a resolu-
tion declaring that the acquisition of the real prop-
erty described therein is necessary for such pur-
poses . Proceedings for such condemnation shall be
in the circuit court of the county in which the prop-
erty lies , and shall be conducted in the name of the
Commission by the attorney designated by the Com-
mission , and the judgment of the court shall vest
title to the property condemned in the Commission .
In all other respects , the form and manner of the
proceedings shall be the same as that provided for
the condemnation of land for road purposes by
fiscal courts .
§ 25. Bonds . ( 1 ) A Commission shall have
power to issue bonds from time to time in its dis-
cretion , for any of its corporate purposes . A Com-
mission shall also have power to issue refunding
bonds for the purpose of paying or retiring bonds
previously issued by it.
(2 ) A Commission may issue such types of
bonds as it may determine , including (without

CHAPTER 70 429
limiting the generality of the foregoing ) bonds on
which the principal and interest are payable :
(a ) Exclusively from the income and revenues
of the housing project financed with the proceeds
of such bonds ;
(b ) Exclusively from the income and revenues
of certain designated housing projects whether or
not they are financed in whole or in part with the
proceeds of such bonds ; or
( c ) From its revenues generally .
(3) Any such bonds may be additionally secured
by a pledge of any grant or contributions from the
Federal Government or other source , or a pledge
of any income or revenues of the Commission , or a
mortgage of any housing project , projects or other
property of the Commission.
(4) In connection with the issuance of bonds or
the incurring of obligations under leases and in
order to secure the payment of such bonds or obliga-
tions , a Housing Commission ( including any Hous-
ing Commission created pursuant to the Municipal
Housing Commission Act ) , in addition to its other
powers , shall have power to agree to sell a project
or projects , and to make and enter into such
covenants and conditions and to do any and all such
acts and things as may be necessary or desirable in
order to secure its bonds , or, in the discretion of
said Commission , as will tend to make the bonds
more marketable .
(5 ) In connection with the issuance of bonds or
the incurring of other obligations , a Regional Hous-
ing Commission may covenant as to limitations on
its right to adopt resolutions relating to the in-
crease or decrease of its area of operation .
$ 26. Neither the commissioners nor any person
executing the bonds shall be liable personally on the
bonds by reason of the issuance thereof . The bonds

430 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and other obligations of a Commission ( and such
bonds and obligations shall so state on their face )
shall not be a debt of the city , the county , the Com-
monwealth or any political subdivision thereof and
neither the city or the county , nor the Common-
wealth or any political subdivision thereof shall be
liable thereon , nor in any event shall such bonds or
obligations be payable out of any funds or prop-
erties other than those of the Commission . The
bonds shall not constitute an indebtedness within the
meaning of any constitutional or statutory debt
limitation or restriction . Bonds of a Commission
are declared to be issued for an essential public and
governmental purpose and to be public instru-
mentalities and , together with interest thereon and
income therefrom , shall be exempt from taxes .
$ 27. Form and Sale of Bonds . ( 1 ) Bonds of
a Commission shall be authorized by its resolution
and may be issued in one or more series and shall
bear such date or dates , mature at such time or
times , bear interest at such rate or rates not ex-
ceeding six per centum ( 6 % ) per annum , be in such
denomination or denominations , be in such form ,
either coupon or registered , carry such conversion
or registration privileges , have such rank or
priority, be executed in such manner , be payable in
such medium of payment , at such place or places ,
and be subject to such terms of redemption (with or
without premium ) as such resolution, its trust
indenture or mortgage may provide .
( 2 ) The bonds may be sold at public or private
sale at not less than par .
(3 ) In case of any of the commissioners or
officers of the Commission whose signatures appear
on any bonds or coupons shall cease to be such com-
missioners or officers before the delivery of such
bonds , such signatures shall , nevertheless , be valid

CHAPTER 70 431
and sufficient for all purposes , the same as if they
had remained in office until such delivery . Any
bonds issued pursuant to this Act shall be fully
negotiable .
(4 ) In any action or proceedings involving the
validity or enforceability of any bond of a Com-
mission or the security therefor , any such bond
reciting in substance that it has been issued by the
Commission to aid in financing a housing project to
provide dwelling accommodations for persons of
low income shall be conclusively deemed to have
been issued for a housing project of such character
and said project shall be conclusively deemed to
have been planned , located and constructed in ac-
cordance with the purposes and provisions of this
Act .
§ 28. Housing Commission Operations in Other
Municipalities . In addition to its other powers , a
Housing Commission created for a city pursuant to
the Municipal Housing Commission Act may ex-
ercise any or all of its powers within the territorial
boundaries of any other city for the purpose of
planning , undertaking , financing , constructing and
operating a housing project or projects within the
other city , if a resolution has been adopted by the
governing body of the other city in which such Com-
mission is to exercise its powers and by the Housing
Commission of the other city (if one has been there-
tofore created for such other city ) declaring (upon
findings prescribed in § 8 ) that there is a need for
the Housing Commission of the aforesaid city to
exercise its powers within the other city . The gov-
erning body of the other city shall not adopt a
resolution pursuant to this Section unless a public
hearing has first been held . The clerk of such city
shall give notice of the time , place and purpose of
the public hearing at least ten days prior to the date

432 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
on which the hearing is to be held , in a newspaper
published in such city , or if there is no newspaper
published in such city, then in a newspaper pub-
lished in the state and having a general circulation
in such city . Upon the date fixed for such public
hearing an opportunity to be heard shall be granted
to all residents of such city and to all other in-
terested persons .
oľ
$ 29. Cooperation of Commissions . Any two
more Commissions ( including any Housing
Commission created under the Municipal Housing
Commission Act ) may join or cooperate with one
another in the exercise , either jointly or otherwise ,
of any or all of their powers for the purpose of
financing (including the issuance of bonds , notes or
other obligations and giving security therefor ) ,
planning , undertaking , owning, constructing , oper-
ating or contracting with respect to a housing proj-
ect or projects located within the area of operation
of any one or more of said Commission . For such
purpose a Commission may by resolution prescribe
and authorize any other Housing Commission or
Commissions , so joining or cooperating with it , to
act on its behalf with respect to any or all of such
powers . Any Commissions joining or cooperating
with one another may by resolutions appoint from
among the commissioners of such Commissions an
executive committee with full power to act on behalf
of such Commissions with respect to any or all of
their powers , as prescribed by resolutions of such
Commissions .
§ 30. Aid from Federal Government . In ad-
dition to the powers conferred upon a Commission
by other provisions of this Act, a Commission is
empowered to borrow money or accept contribu-
tions , grants or other financial assistance from the
Federal government for or in aid of any housing.

project or undertaking constructed or owned by the
Federal government , and to these ends , to comply
with such conditions and enter into such mortgages ,
trust indentures , leases or agreements as may be
necessary , convenient or desirable . It is the pur-
pose and intent of this Act to authorize every Hous-
ing Commission to do any and
