§ 32. Repeal. Chapter 23 of the Acts of 1940
is hereby repealed .
$ 33. Emergency . Whereas it is necessary to
authorize immediately the creation of Housing
Commissions by counties and by groups of counties
and groups of cities to undertake housing projects
for persons of low income , especially in rural areas
and in defense areas of the Commonwealth , and
whereas there is no adequate law in this Common-
wealth authorizing the creation of such Commis-
sions with power to obtain financial assistance from
the Federal government , an emergency is declared
to exist , and this Act shall become effective im-
mediately after its passage and approval by the
Governor .
$ 34. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , the numbers " KRS 80.010 to
80.250 " shall be substituted for "the Municipal

CHAPTER 70 435
Housing Commission Act " wherever those words
appear in this Act and KRS 80.020 , 80.030 , 80.040 ,
80.050 , 80.060 , 80.090 , 80.150 , and 80.190 shall stand
amended to read as follow :
80.020 Cities of all classes may acquire , establish
and operate , within their limits , low - cost housing
projects , under the provisions of this chapter , for
the purpose of providing adequate and sanitary
living quarters for individuals or families . They
may create city housing commissions and they and
the commissions created by them , shall have all
powers necessary and appropriate to engage in
low-cost housing and slum clearance projects .
80.030 A city housing commission in cities of
the first five classes shall consist of the mayor , ex
officio , and four persons appointed by him with the
approval of the city legislative body . A city hous-
ing commission in cities of the sixth class shall con-
sist of the chairman of the board of trustees , ex
officio , and four persons appointed by him with the
approval of the board of trustees .
80.040 Each person appointed to a city housing
commission shall be at least twenty -five years of
age and a bona fide resident of the city for which
he was appointed for at least one year preceding
the appointment . No officer or employe of the city ,
whether holding a paid or unpaid office , is eligible
to hold an appointment on the housing commission .
No more than two appointees on any city housing
commission shall be affiliated with the same political
party . Appointees to a city housing commission
shall be originally appointed for terms of four
years . Upon the expiration of the term of the first
appointees , their successors shall be appointed for
terms of one , two , three and four years , respec-
tively , and upon the expiration of the term of each
of the second group of appointees his successor

436 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall be appointed for a term of four years . Va-
cancies shall be filled for unexpired terms in the
same manner as the original appointment .
80.050 The persons appointed as provided in
KRS 80.030 and 80.040 shall constitute a body cor-
porate in the name of Municipal Housing Commis-
sion , with power to contract and be contracted
with , to sue or be sued , and to adopt a seal and alter
it at will . Each commission may establish bylaws ,
rules and regulations for its own government . Each
commission shall elect a chairman and a vice chair-
man from its members and a secretary and treas-
urer who need not be a member of the commission .
The duties of the offices of secretary and treasurer
may be performed by the same person .
80.060 Each member of a housing commission ,
except an ex officio member , shall receive compensa-
tion either as a salary or as payment for meetings
attended . The compensation of members of a hous-
ing commission shall be fixed by the legislative body
of the city . The compensation of the chairman of
a housing commission shall not exceed two thou-
sand dollars in any year and that of any other mem-
ber shall not exceed four hundred dollars in any
year . The housing commission shall fix the com-
pensation of the secretary and treasurer , but the
city legislative body may fix or limit the salary .
80.090 . Removal of member for incompetency ,
neglect or malfeasance . Whenever it appears to
the appointing officer that a member of a housing
commission appointed by him is incompetent or
guilty of neglect of duty or malfeasance , the ap-
pointing officer shall require the member to appear
before him to show cause why he should not be re-
moved from office . At least fifteen days ' written
notice of the hearing shall be given to the member
whose conduct is in question and to all other mem-

CHAPTER 70 437
bers of the commission . At the hearing the member
may be represented by counsel and may appear
personally and present such pertinent evidence as
he wishes or as the appointing officer requests . If
after the hearing the appointing officer determines
that a member has been incompetent or has been
guilty of neglect of duty or malfeasance , he shall
remove the member from the commission within
seven day* , and there shall be a vacancy of the
office .
80.150 . Condemnation authorized ; procedure .
If it becomes necessary to condemn property for
the purpose of constructing any low- cost housing
project , or securing right of ways leading thereto ,
the commission may , by resolution reciting the
need , order the condemnation of any land or im-
provement or interest in land , that it deems neces-
sary .
Proceedings for the condemnation shall be in the
circuit court of the county in which the property
lies , and shall be conducted in the name of the com-
mission . The city attorney shall conduct the pro-
ceedings , for the commission . The judgment of the
court shall vest title to the property condemned in
the commission . In all other respects the form and
manner of the proceedings shall be the same as that
provided in KRS 416.120 . No payment or award in
any condemnation proceedings , or for the cost or
expense of the proceedings , shall be made except
from funds provided under the authority of this
chapter .
80.190 . Project not to be operated for profit .
The commission shall manage and operate its hous-
ing projects in an efficient manner so as to enable it
to fix the rentals for dwelling accommodations at
the lowest possible rates consistent with its provid-
* Days .

438 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ing decent , safe and sanitary dwelling accommoda
tions , and no commission shall construct or operate
any project for profit , or as a source of revenue to
the city .
§ 35. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As
sembly , this Act shall be treated as repealing KRS
80.100 , 80.210 , 80.220 and 80.260 , regardless of the
date of enactment or taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes . Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 71
(H. B. 310)
AN ACT to declare valid and legal the creation and estab-
lishment of Municipal Housing Commissions , all bonds ,
notes , contracts, agreements , obligations and undertakings
of such Housing Commissions , and all proceedings , acts and
things heretofore undertaken , performed or done with
reference thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Validation of Creation of Municipal Hous-
ing Commissions . The creation and establishment
of Municipal Housing Commissions pursuant to the
provisions of the Municipal Housing Commission
Act (Chapter 113 of the Acts of the General Assem-
bly of 1934 , as amended ) together with all proceed-
ings , acts and things heretofore undertaken , per-
formed or done with reference thereto , are hereby
validated , ratified , confirmed , approved and de-
clared legal in all respects notwithstanding any
defect or irregularity thereon or any want of statu-
tory authority .
§ 2. Validation of Contracts and Undertakings .
All contracts , agreements , obligations and under-

CHAPTER 71 439
takings of Municipal Housing Commissions here-
tofore entered into relating to financing or aiding
in the development , construction , maintenance or
operation of any housing project or projects or to
obtaining aid therefor from the United States
Housing Authority , including ( without limiting the
generality of the foregoing ) loan and annual con-
tributions contracts and leases with the United
States Housing Authority , agreements with cities ,
counties or other public bodies ( including agree-
ments which are pledged or authorized to be
pledged for the protection of the holders of any
notes or bonds issued by Municipal Housing Com-
missions or which are otherwise made a part of the
contract with such holders of notes or bonds ) re-
lating to cooperation and contributions in aid of
housing projects , payments (if any ) in lieu of taxes ,
furnishing of municipal services and facilities , and
the elimination of unsafe and insanitary dwellings ,
and contracts for the construction of housing
projects , together with all proceedings , acts and
things heretofore undertaken , performed or done
with reference thereto , are hereby validated , rati-
fied , confirmed , approved and declared legal in all
respects notwithstanding any defect or irregularity
therein or any want of statutory authority .
3. Validation of Notes and Bonds . All pro-
ceedings , acts and things heretofore undertaken ,
performed or done in or for the authorization , is-
suance , execution and delivery of notes and bonds
by Municipal Housing Commissions for the pur-
pose of financing or aiding in the development or
construction of a housing project or projects and
all notes and bonds heretofore issued by Municipal
Housing Commissions are hereby validated , rati-
fied , confirmed , approved and declared legal in all

440 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
respects , notwithstanding any defect or irregu-
larity therein or any want of statutory authority .
Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 72
(H. B. 311 )
AN ACT to authorize Housing Commissions , and any cities
creating them, to undertake the development or administra-
tion of projects to assure the availability of safe and
sanitary dwellings for persons engaged in national - defense
activities who would not otherwise be able to secure such
dwellings within the vicinity thereof ; to limit the initiation
of the development of such projects until December 31 ,
1944 ; to authorize Housing Commissions to cooperate with
or act as agent of the Federal Government in the develop-
ment and administration of such projects of the Federal
Government , to acquire or lease such projects and to sell
certain projects to the Federal Government ; to authorize
public bodies to assist such projects of Housing Commis-
sions and of the Federal Government ; to make obligations
issued for such projects of Housing Commissions legal
investments and security for deposits ; and to declare an
emergency.
WHEREAS , it is hereby found and declared that the national-
defense program involves large increases in the military
forces and personnel in this Commonwealth , a great
increase in the number of workers in already established
manufacturing centers and the bringing of a large number
of workers and their families to new centers of defense
industries in the Commonwealth ; that there is an acute
shortage of safe and sanitary dwellings available to such
persons and their families which impedes the national-
defense program ; that it is imperative that action be taken
immediately to assure the availability of safe and sanitary
dwellings for such persons to enable the rapid expansion
of national - defense activities in this Commonwealth and to
avoid a large labor turnover in defense industries which
would seriously hamper their production ; that the pro-
visions hereinafter enacted are necessary to assure the
availability of safe and sanitary dwellings for persons

CHAPTER 72 441
engaged in national - defense activities which otherwise
would not be provided at this time ; and that such pro-
visions are for the public use and purpose of facilitating the
national -defense program ; and
WHEREAS , it is further declared to be the purpose of this
Act to authorize Housing Commissions to do any and all
things necessary or desirable to secure the financial aid of
the Federal Government , or to cooperate with or act as
agent of the Federal Government , in the expeditious
development and the administration of projects to assure
the availability when needed of safe and sanitary dwell-
ings for persons engaged in national - defense activities ;
now therefore :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ (1) Definitions and construction . As used in
this Act :
( a) "Administration " means any and all un-
dertakings necessary for management , operation
or maintenance , in connection with any project , and
shall include the leasing of any projects , in whole
or in part , from the Federal Government .
(b ) " Development " means any and all under-
takings necessary for the planning , land acquisi-
tion , demolition, financing , construction or equip-
ment in connection with a project (including the
negotiation or award of contracts therefor ) , and
shall include the acquisition of any project (in
whole or in part ) from the Federal Government .
(c) " Federal Government " means the United
States of America or any agency or instrumentality ,
corporate or otherwise , of the United States of
America .
66
(d ) Housing Commission " means any Hous-
ing Commission established pursuant to 1934 ,c.113 ,
or 1940 ,c.23 , or H. B. No. of the General
Assembly of 1942 and shall include the city which

442 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
established the Housing Commission pursuant to
1934 ,c.113 .
(e) "Persons engaged in national -defense ac-
tivities " includes , without limiting the generality
thereof , enlisted men in the military and naval
services of the United States ; employees of the
War and Navy Departments assigned to duty at
military or naval reservations , posts or bases ;
workers engaged or to be engaged in industries
connected with and essential to the national-defense
programs ; and the families of the aforesaid per-
sons who are living with them .
(f) " Persons of low income " means persons or
families who lack the amount of income which is
necessary , as determined by the Housing Commis
sion undertaking the housing project , to enable
them , without financial assistance , to live in decent ,
safe and sanitary dwellings , without overcrowding .
( 2 ) The development of a project shall be
deemed to be " initiated , " within the meaning of
this Act , if a Housing Commission has issued any
bonds , notes or other obligations with respect to
financing the development of such project of the
Commission , or has contracted with the Federal
Government with respect to the exercise of powers
hereunder in the development of such project of
the Federal Government for which an allocation of
funds has been made prior to December 31 , 1944 .
(3 ) The powers conferred by this Act shall be
in addition and supplemental to the powers con-
ferred by any other law , and nothing contained
herein shall be construed as amending or extending
any other law.
§ 2. Defense Housing by Housing Commissions .
Any Housing Commission may undertake the de-
velopment or administration , or both , of projects to
assure the availability of safe and sanitary dwel !-

CHAPTER 72 443
ings for persons engaged in national-defense ac-
tivities whom the Housing Commission determines
would not otherwise be able to secure safe and sani-
tary dwellings within the vicinity thereof , but no
Housing Commission shall initiate the development
of any such project pursuant to this Act after De-
cember 31 , 1944 .
§ 3. In the ownership , development or admin-
istration of such projects , a Housing Commission
shall have all the rights , powers , privileges and im-
munities that such Commission has under any pro-
vision of law relating to the ownership , develop-
ment or administration of slum clearance and hous-
ing projects for persons of low income , in the same
manner as though all the provisions of law appli-
cable to slum clearance and housing projects for
persons of low income were applicable to projects
developed or administered to assure the avail-
ability of safe and sanitary dwellings for persons
engaged in national - defense activities as provided
in this Act ; except that during the period ( herein
called the " National -Defense Period " ) that a
Housing Commission finds (which finding shall be
conclusive in any suit , action or proceeding ) that
within its area of operation , or any part thereof ,
there is an acute shortage of safe and sanitary
dwellings which impedes the national -defense pro-
gram in this state and that the necessary safe and
sanitary dwellings would not otherwise be provided
when needed for persons engaged in national-
defense activities , the Housing Commission ( and
any Housing Commission cooperating with it ) shall
not be subject to the limitations provided in 1934 ,
c.113 ,§ 11 , nor the restrictions on the making of
construction contracts contained in 1934 , c.113 , § 4 ,
nor the limitations on tenant selection contained in
clause ( c ) of 1940 , c.26 , § 2 , clause ( c ) of 1940 ,c.23 ,

444 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 14 , and Section 15 of H. B. No. of the
General Assembly of 1942 , with respect to any
project developed or administered in such area pur-
suant to this Act with the financial aid of the Fed-
eral Government (or as agent for the Federal Gov-
ernment as hereinafter provided ) .
§ 4. In the development or the administration
of projects under this Act or in otherwise carrying
out the purposes hereof, a Housing Commission of
a city may exercise its powers within its territorial
boundaries and within an area ten miles from its
boundaries excluding the area within the territorial
boundaries of any other city which has heretofore
established a Housing Commission . After the
National -Defense Period , any such projects owned .
and administered by a Housing Commission shall
be administered for the purposes and in accordance
with the provisions of 1934 ,c.113 , or 1940 ,c.23 , or
H. B. No. of the General Assembly of
1942 , as the case may be , except as otherwise pro-
vided in Section 3.
§ 5. Acting for Federal Government on Defense
Housing . A Housing Commission may exercise
any or all of its powers for the purpose of cooperat-
ing with , or acting as agent for, the Federal Gov-
ernment in the development or administration of
projects by the Federal Government to assure the
availability of safe and sanitary dwellings for per-
sons engaged in national -defense activities and may
undertake the development or administration of
any such project for the Federal Government . In
order to assure the availability of safe and sanitary
housing for persons engaged in national - defense
activities , a Housing Commission may sell to the
Federal Government any housing project , or part
thereof, developed for persons of low income but
not yet occupied by such persons ; such sale shall be

CHAPTER 72 445
at such price and upon such terms as the Housing
Commission prescribes and shall include provision.
for the satisfaction of all debts and liabilities of the
commission relating to such project .
§ 6. Cooperation by Public Bodies on Defense
Housing . Any public body , as defined in 1940 , c.24 ,
§ 3, shall have the same rights and powers to co-
operate with Housing Commissions , or with the
Federal Government , with respect to the develop-
ment or administration of projects to assure the
availability of safe and sanitary dwellings for per-
sons engaged in national - defense activities that
such public body has pursuant to such Act for the
purpose of assisting the development or adminis-
tration of slum clearance or housing projects for
persons of low income , and any such public body
may furnish the aforesaid cooperation to such proj-
ects located within the territorial boundaries of
such public body or within ten miles thereof.
$ 7. Bonds for Defense Housing Legal Invest-
ments . Bonds or other obligations issued by a
Housing Commission for a project developed or
administered pursuant to this Act shall be security
for public deposits and legal investments to the
same extent and for the same persons , institutions ,
associations , corporations , bodies and officers as
bonds or other obligations issued pursuant to
1934 ,c.113 , or 1940 , c.23 , or H. B. No. .............. of
the General Assembly of 1942 , for the development
of a slum clearance or housing project for persons
of low income .
$ 8. Removal of Restrictions for Defense Hous-
ing . This Act shall constitute an independent au-
thorization for a Housing Commission to undertake
the development or administration of projects to
assure the availability of safe and sanitary dwell-
ings for persons engaged in national -defense ac-

446 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tivities as provided in this Act and for a Housing
Commission to cooperate with , or act as agent for ,
the Federal Government in the development or
administration of similar projects by the Federal
Government . In acting under this authorization , a
Housing Commission shall not be subject to any
limitations , restrictions or requirements of other
laws, except those relating to land acquisition , pre-
scribing the procedure or action to be taken in the
development or administration of any public works ,
including slum clearance and housing projects for
persons of low income or undertakings or projects of
public corporations or cities or other political sub-
divisions or agencies of the state . A housing Com-
mission may do any and all things necessary or
desirable to cooperate with , or act as agent for , the
Federal Government , or to secure financial aid , in
the expeditious development or in the administra-
tion of projects to assure the availability of safe
and sanitary dwellings for persons engaged in
national -defense activities and to effectuate the
purposes of this Act .
$ 9. Emergency . Whereas, it is necessary to
immediately authorize Housing Commissions to
undertake projects to assure the availability of
dwelling accommodations for persons engaged in
national -defense activities to facilitate the national-
defense program in the Commonwealth , an emer-
gency is declared to exist , and this Act shall become
effective immediately after its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .
§ 10. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , the number " KRS 80.240 " shall
be substituted for " 1934 ,c.113 , § 11 ," and the num-

CHAPTER 73 447
ber " KRS 80.130 " shall be substituted for " 1934 , c .
113 , § 4 ' , and the number " subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
80.200 " shall be substituted for " clause ( c ) of
1940 , c.26 ,§ 2 , clause ( c ) of 1940 ,c.23 , § 14 , " in sec-
tion three of this Act .
§ 11. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , the numbers " KRS 80.010 to
80.260 " shall be substituted for " 1934 , c.113 , or
1940 , c.23 " in section four of this Act .
§ 12. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , the numbers " subsection ( 3 ) of
KRS 80.010 " shall be substituted for 1940 , c.24 ,§ 3
in section six of this Act .
§ 13. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , the numbers "KRS 80.010 to
80.260 " shall be substituted for " 1934 , c.113 , or
1940 , c.23 , " in section seven of this Act .
§ 14. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes , the numbers " KRS 80.010 to 80.260 "
shall be substituted for " 1934 , c.113 or 1940 ,c.23 "
and for " 1934 , c.13 " where they appear in section
one of this Act . Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 73
(H. B. 312 )
AN ACT authorizing cities to adopt ordinances relating to
the repair , closing and demolition of dwellings unfit for
human habitation ; providing for the remedies and pro-

448 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cedure in connection with action taken under such
ordinances and declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , it is hereby found and declared that the existence
and occupation of dwellings in cities of this Commonwealth
which are unfit for human habitation are inimical to the
welfare , and dangerous and injurious to the health , safety
and morals of the people of this Commonwealth , and that
a public necessity exists for the repair , closing or demolition
of such dwellings ; now , therefore :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. (1 ) Definitions and Construction . As
used in this Act , unless the context requires other .
wise :
(a ) " Dwelling " means any building , or struc-
ture , or part thereof , used and occupied for human
habitation or intended to be so used , and includes
any outhouses and appurtenances belonging thereto
or usually enjoyed therewith .
(b ) " Governing body " means the general coun-
cil , board, or commissioners , or other legislative
body , charged with governing a city .
(c ) " Public officer " means the officer or officers
who are authorized by ordinances adopted here-
under to exercise the powers prescribed by such
ordinances and by this Act .
(d ) " Public authority " means any Housing
Commission or any officer who is in charge of any
department or branch of the government of the city ,
county , or state relating to health , fire , building
regulations , or to other activities concerning dwell-
ings in the city .
(e ) " Owner " means the holder of the title in
fee simple and every mortgagee of record .
(f) " Parties in interest " means all individuals ,
associations and corporations who have interests .
of record in a dwelling and any who are in pos-
session thereof .

CHAPTER 73 449
( 2 ) The powers conferred upon cities by this
Act shall be in addition and supplemental to the
powers conferred by any other law.
§ 2. Unfit Dwellings in Municipalities —Exer-
cise of Police Power Authorized . Whenever any
city finds that there exist in such city dwellings
which are unfit for human habitation due to dilapi-
dation , defects increasing the hazards of fire , ac-
cidents or other calamities , lack of ventilation ,
light or sanitary facilities , or due to other condi
tions rendering such dwellings unsafe or insani-
tary , or dangerous or detrimental to the health ,
safety or morals , or otherwise inimical to the wel-
fare of the residents of the city , the city may exer-
cise its police powers to repair , close or demolishi
such dwellings in the manner provided in this Act .
§3. Ordinance Authorized as to Repair . Upon
the adoption of an ordinance finding that dwelling
conditions of the character described in Section 2
exist within a city , the governing body of the city
may adopt ordinances relating to the dwelling's
within the city which are unfit for human habitation .
Such ordinances shall provide that a public officer
be designated or appointed to exercise the powers
prescribed by the ordinances , and shall include the
provisions of §§ 4 to 7 of this Act .
§ 4. Whenever a petition is filed with the public
officer by a public authority or by at least five resi-
dents of the city charging that any dwelling is unfit
for human habitation or whenever it appears to the
public officer ( on his own motion ) that any dwelling
is unfit for human habitation , the public officer shall ,
if his preliminary investigation discloses a basis
for such charges , issue and cause to be served upon
the owner of and parties in interest in such dwelling
a complaint stating the charges in that respect .
The complaint shall state : That a hearing will be
Acts- 15

450 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
held before the public officer ( or his designated
agent ) at a place therein fixed not less than ten days
nor more than thirty days after the serving of the
complaint ; that the owner and parties in interest
may file an answer to the complaint and to ap-
pear in person , or otherwise , and give testimony at
the place and time fixed in the complaint ; and that
the rules of evidence prevailing in courts of law or
equity shall not be controlling in hearings before
the public officer .
§ 5. If, after the notice and hearing , the public
officer determines that the dwelling under consid-
eration is unfit for human habitation he shall state
in writing his findings of fact in support of such
determination and shall issue and cause to be served
upon the owner thereof an order requiring the
owner :
•
(1 ) To the intent and within the time specified
in the order , to repair , alter or improve the said
dwelling to render it fit for human habitation , or ,
at the option of the owner , to vacate and close the
dwelling as a human habitation if the repair , alter-
ation or improvement of the dwelling can be made
at a cost that is not more than 50 % of the value of
the dwelling ; or
( 2 ) Within the time specified in the order , to
remove or demolish the said dwelling if the repair ,
alteration or improvement of the dwelling cannot
be made at a cost that is not more than 50 % of the
value of the dwelling .
§ 6. ( 1 ) If the owner fails to comply with an
order to repair , alter or improve or , at the option
of the owner , to vacate and close the dwelling , the
public officer may cause the dwelling to be repaired ,
altered or improved , or to be vacated and closed .
The public officer may cause to be posted on the
main entrance of any dwelling so closed , a placard

CHAPTER 73 451
with the following words : " This building is unfit
for human habitation ; the use or occupation of this
building for human habitation is prohibited and
unlawful ."
( 2 ) If the owner fails to comply with an order
to remove or demolish the dwelling , the public
officer may cause such dwelling to be removed or
demolished .
(3 ) The amount of the cost of repairs , altera-
tions or improvements , or vacating and closing , or
removal or demolition shall be a lien upon the real
property upon which such cost was incurred . If
the dwelling is removed or demolished by the public
officer , he shall sell the materials of the dwelling
and shall credit the proceeds of such sale against
the cost of the removal or demolition and any bal-
ance remaining shall be deposited in the circuit
court by the public officer , shall be secured in such
manner as may be directed by such court , and shall
be disbursed by such court to the persons found to
be entitled thereto by final order or decree of such
court .
$ 7. Standards . The public officer may deter
mine that a dwelling is unfit for human habitation
if he finds that conditions exist in such dwelling
which are dangerous or injurious to the health ,
safety or morals of the occupants of such dwelling ,
the occupants of neighboring dwellings or other
residents of the city . Such conditions may include
the following (without limiting the generality of
the foregoing ) : defects increasing the hazards of
fire , accident , or other calamities ; lack of adequate
ventilation , light , or sanitary facilities ; dilapida-
tion ; disrepair , structural defects ; uncleanliness .
Additional standards to guide the public officer , or
his agents , in determining the fitness of a dwelling

452 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for human habitation , may be provided by ordi-
nance .
§ 8. Service of Complaints and Orders . Com-
plaints or orders issued by a public officer pursu-
ant to an ordinance adopted under this Act shall
be served upon persons either personally or by reg-
istered mail , but if the whereabouts of such person
is unknown and the same cannot be ascertained by
the public officer in the exercise of reasonable dili-
gence , and the public officer shall make an affidavit
to that effect , then the serving of such complaint or
order upon such persons may be made by publish-
ing the same once each week for two successive
weeks in a newspaper printed and published in the
city, or , in the absence of such newspaper , in one
of general circulation in the city in which the dwell
ings are located . A copy of such complaint or order
shall be posted in a conspicuous place on premises
affected by the complaint or order , and shall be
recorded in the office of the circuit clerk of the
county wherein the dwelling is located .
§ 9. Remedies . Any person affected by an order
issued by the public officer may , within sixty days
after the posting and service of the order , petition
the circuit court for an injunction restraining the
public officer from carrying out the provisions of
the order , and the court may issue a temporary in-
junction restraining the public officer pending the
final disposition of the cause . Hearings shall be
had by the court on such petition within twenty
days , or as soon thereafter as possible . In all such
proceedings the findings of the public officer as to
facts , if supported by evidence , shall be conclusive .
Costs shall be in the discretion of the court. The
remedies herein provided shall be exclusive reme-
dies and no person affected by an order of the pub-
lic officer shall be entitled to recover any damages

CHAPTER 73 453
for action taken pursuant to any order of the pub-
lic officer , or because of non -compliance by such per-
son with any order of the public officer .
§ 10. Additional Powers of Public Officer . The
governing body of the city may by ordinance auth-
orize the public officer to exercise such powers as
are necessary or convenient to carry out and effect-
uate the purposes and provisions of this Act , in-
cluding the following powers in addition to others
herein granted :
( 1 ) To investigate the dwelling conditions in
the city in order to determine which dwellings there-
in are unfit for human habitation ;
( 2) To administer oaths , affirmations , examine
witnesses and receive evidence ;
(3) To enter upon premises for the purpose of
making examinations , but such entries shall be
made in such manner as to cause the least possible
inconvenience to the persons in possession ;
(4) To appoint and fix the duties of such officers ,
agents and employees as he deems necessary to
carry out the purposes of the ordinances ; and
(5 ) To delegate any of his functions and powers
under the ordinance to such officers and agents as
he designates .
§ 11. Administration of Ordinance . The govern-
ing body of any city adopting an ordinance under
this Act shall as soon as possible thereafter pre-
pare an estimate of the annual expenses or costs to
provide the equipment , personnel and supplies nec-
essary for periodic examinations and investigations
of the dwellings in such city for the purpose of de-termining the fitness of the dwellings for human
habitation and for the enforcement and adminis-
tration of its ordinances adopted under this Act ;
and any such city may make such appropriations
from its revenues as it may deem necessary for this

454 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
purpose and may accept and apply grants or dona-
tions to assist it in carrying out the provisions of
such ordinances .
§ 12. Emergency . Whereas the powers con-
ferred hereby are urgent and necessary to enable
cities to provide for the remedying or elimination
of dwelling accommodations which are inimical to
the welfare and dangerous and injurious to the
health , safety and morals of the people of the Com-
monwealth , an emergency is declared to exist , and
this Act shall become effective from and after its
passage and approval . Approved March 5 , 1942 ,

CHAPTER 75
(H. B. 80 )
AN ACT relating to the city manager form of government for
cities .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 3235dd -34 of Carroll's Kentucky

CHAPTER 75 455
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as fol-
lows :
" The city manager shall be responsible to the
board of commissioners for the proper administra-
tion of all affairs of the city, and to that end shall
make all appointments , except of members of the
board of equalization who shall be appointed by
the board of commissioners , and except as otherwise
provided in this law . Except when the board of
commissioners is considering his removal , the city
manager shall be entitled to be present at all meet-
ings of the board of commissioners and of its com
mittees and to take part in their discussions . "
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assen-
bly , section one of this Act shall stand repealed as
of the effective date of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , and the following shall take effect in lieu
thereof :
( 1 ) The city manager shall be the executive agent
of the mayor and board of commissioners in the
management of the city affairs . He shall be re-
sponsible to the board for the proper administra-
tion of all the affairs of the city , and to that end
he shall make all appointments , except as otherwise
provided in this chapter , and except that in cities
of the second class he shall not appoint the members
of the board of equalization , but they shall be ap-
pointed by the board of commissioners . Except
when the board is considering his removal , he may
be present at all meetings of the board and of its
committees , and take part in the discussion .
( 2 ) Neither the mayor , board of commissioners
nor any commissioner shall dictate the appoint-
ment of any person to office or employment by the
city manager , or in any manner interfere with
the city manager or prevent him from exercising

456 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
his own judgment in the appointment of officers and
employes in the administrative service . Except for
the purpose of inquiry the board of commissioners
and its members shall deal with the administrative
service solely through the city manager , and neither
the board nor any member thereof shall give orders
to any of the subordinates of the city manager . The
provision so substituted by this section shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 89.570
rather than an amendment to § 3235dd -34 of Car-
roll's Statutes .
Approved 5th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 76
(H. B. 81 )

for cities
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 3235dd-42 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as fol-
lows :
" (1 ) Every ordinance or resolution ordering the
construction or reconstruction of any street or
sewer , or making or authorizing any contract involv-
ing the expenditure of more than one thousand dol-
lars , or granting any franchise of the right to use
or occupy the streets , highways , bridges , or public
places of the city for any except a merely tem-
porary purpose shall after its introduction and be-
fore its adoption remain on file at least one week
for public inspection in the completed form in which
it shall be put upon its final passage . No such
ordinance or resolution shall go into effect until the

CHAPTER 76 457
expiration of ten days after its passage , except in
case of emergency the public health or safety shall
require that it take immediate effect , which fact
shall be declared by the unanimous vote of the board
of commissioners .
" (2) Before a city of the second class shall enter
into any contract involving the expenditure of more
than one thousand dollars the board of commission-
ers shall by resolution specify the nature of the
work to be contracted for , or the quality
and quantity of the supplies or material to
be purchased , and authorize the city man-
ager to advertise for and receive bids un-
der seal for the execution of such work or the pur-
chase of such supplies or material . The city man-
ager shall advertise for bids by notice posted up
at the city hall door and by inserting notice of the
request for bids in at least one issue of a daily news-
paper published in the city, and the notices shall
be published at least seven days prior to the open-
ing of bids . No bid shall be opened within ten days
from date on which the resolution is adopted , and
no bid shall be received after the hour fixed for the
opening of bids . All bids received by the city man-
ager up to the time fixed by the resolution for the
opening of bids shall be filed with the board of com-
missioners who may let the contract to the lowest
responsible bidder . The city manager may, for and
on behalf of the city , enter into any contract involv-
ing the expenditure of one thousand dollars or less ,
but in the event such contract involves the expendi-
ture of more than five hundred dollars he shall ad-
vertise for bids in the manner provided in this sub-
section and let the contract to the lowest respon-
sible bidder . The board of commissioners or the
city manager may reject all bids , in which event

458 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
they may advertise for new bids in the manner pro-
vided in this subsection . "
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, subsection ( 1 ) of section one of this Act shall
stand repealed as of the effective date of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , subsection ( 2) of section
one shall be treated as being subsection ( 2 ) of KRS
89.590 , and KRS 89.590 shall otherwise remain in
full force and effect .
Approved 5th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 79
(H. B. 191 )
AN ACT providing for the disposition of property where
there is no sufficient evidence that persons have died other-
wise than simultaneously , and to make uniform the law
with reference thereto.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. NO SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE
OF SURVIVORSHIP . Where the title to prop-
erty or the devolution thereof depends upon priority
of death and there is no sufficient evidence that the
persons have died otherwise than simultaneously ,
the property of each person shall be disposed of as
if he had survived , except as provided otherwise
in this act .
SECTION 2. BENEFICIARIES OF ANOTHER
PERSON'S DISPOSITION OF PROPERTY .
Where two or more beneficiaries are designated to

CHAPTER 79 467
take successively by reason of survivorship under
another person's disposition of property and there
is no sufficient evidence that these beneficiaries have
died otherwise than simultaneously the property
thus disposed of shall be divided into as many equal
portions as there are successive beneficiaries and
these portions shall be distributed respectively to
those who would have taken in the event that each
designated beneficiary had survived .
SECTION 3. JOINT TENANTS OR TENANTS
BY THE ENTIRETY . Where there is no sufficient
evidence that two joint tenants or tenants by the
entirety have died otherwise than simultaneously
the property so held shall be distributed one -half
as if one had survived and one -half as if the other
had survived . If there are more than two joint
tenants and all of them have so died the property
thus distributed shall be in the proportion that one
bears to the whole number of joint tenants .
SECTION 4. INSURANCE POLICIES . Where
the insured and the beneficiary in a policy of life
or accident insurance have died and there is no
sufficient evidence that they have died otherwise
than simultaneously the proceeds of the policy shall
be distributed as if the insured had survived the
beneficiary .
SECTION 5. ACT NOT RETROACTIVE . This
act shall not apply to the distribution of the prop-
erty of a person who has died before it takes effect .
SECTION 6. ACT DOES NOT APPLY IF DE-
CEDENT PROVIDES OTHERWISE . This act
shall not apply in the case of wills , living trusts ,
deeds , or contracts of insurance wherein provision
has been made for distribution of property differ-
ent from the provisions of this Act .
SECTION 7. UNIFORMITY OF INTERPRE-
TATION . This act shall be so construed and in-

468 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
terpreted as to effectuate its general purpose to
make uniform the law in those states which enact it .
SECTION 8. SHORT TITLE . This act may be
cited as the Uniform Simultaneous Death Act .
SECTION 9. REPEAL . All laws or parts of
laws inconsistent with the provisions of this act are
hereby repealed .
SECTION 10. SEVERABILITY . If any of the
provisions of this act or the application thereof to
any persons or circumstances is held invalid such
invalidity shall not affect other provisions or ap-
plications of the act which can be given effect with-
out the invalid provisions or application , and to
this end the provisions of this act are declared to
be serverable . *
Approved 5th March , 1942 .
* Severable .

monwealth of every refrigerator car company , tankcar company , coal car company , and every like car

son so nominated is not approved by the Court ,
or if the minor , after summons , fails to nominate a
suitable person , or if the minor resides out of the
State , or if the testimentary guardian fails for
three months to qualify , the Court may appoint a
guardian of its own selection . "
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are enact-
ed at the present session of the General Assembly,
section one of this Act shall, upon the taking effect
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substituted
in place of KRS 387.050 , and shall thereafter be
treated as an amendment to KRS 387.050 rather
than an amendment to § 2022 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 5th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 82 473
CHAPTER 82
(H. B. 249 )
AN ACT relating to the business of insurance companies
other than life .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 687 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition, is amended to read as fol-
lows :
( 1 ) Subject to the provisions of subsection ' ( 3 ) of
this section , any company authorized by law to en-
gage in the business of insurance , other than life in-
surance , in this state , may do the business of fire
insurance , which shall include insurance against
loss or damage to property and loss of use and
occupancy by fire , lightning , storm , hail , tempest ,
earthquake , frost or snow , explosion with fire en-
suing and explosion with no fire ensuing , except ex-
plosion by steam boilers or fly wheels ; against loss
or damage by water caused by the breakage or leak-
age of sprinklers , pumps or other apparatus, water
pipes , plumbing or their fixtures, erected for ex-
tinguishing fires , and against accidental injury to
such sprinklers , pumps , other apparatus , water
pipes , plumbing or fixtures ; against the risk of in-
land transportation and navigation upon vessels ,
freights , goods , money , effects , and money loaned on
bottomry or respondentia ; against the perils of the
sea and other perils usually insured against by
marine insurance ; upon automobiles , whether sta-
tionary or operated under their own power, against
loss or damage by any of the causes or risks men-
tioned herein , including also transportation , col-
lision , liability for damage to property resulting
from owning, maintaining or using automobiles and
including burglary and theft , but not including loss

474 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or damage by reason of bodily injury to the per-
son ; and by and with the approval of the Director
of Insurance against other loss or damage to prop-
erty or interest therein the insurance against which
is not exclusively delegated to another class or
kind of company .
Any mutual insurance company , other than an
assessment or co -operative company , authorized by
law to engage in the fire insurance business in Ken-
tucky , shall have the authority to transact the kind
of business described in this subsection .
( 2 ) Subject to the provisions of subsection ( 3 )
of this section , any company authorized by law to
engage in the business of insurance , other than life
insurance , in this state , may write any of the fol-
lowing kinds of insurance :
(a ) Upon the health of persons , or against in-
jury , disablement or death of persons resulting from
traveling or general accidents by land or water ,
and against the liability of employers for injuries
to persons in their employment ;
(b ) To guarantee the fidelity of persons hold-
ing places of trust , private or public, and to act as
surety on official bonds , and for the performance
of other obligations ;
(c) Upon the lives of horses , cattle or other live-
stock ;
( d ) Upon plate glass against breakage ;
(e ) Upon steam boilers against explosion and
against loss or damage to life or property resulting
therefrom ;
(f) Against loss by burglary or theft , or both ;
(g ) To examine titles to real property and chat-
tles real, to procure and furnish information in re-
lation thereto , and to make and guarantee the cor-
rectness of searches for all instruments , liens or
charges affecting the same , and guarantee or insure

CHAPTER 82 475
bonds and mortgages , and the owners of real prop-
erty and chattels real , and others interested therein
against loss by reason of defective titles thereto ,
and other encumbrances thereon ;
(h) To guarantee and indemnify merchants , trad-
ers and those engaged in business and giving credit ,
from loss and damage by reason of giving and ex-
tending credit to their customers and those dealing
with them , and to cause itself to be insured against
any loss or risk it may have incurred in the course
of its business , and upon the interest which it may
have in any property by means of any loans which
it may have made on mortgages ;
(i ) Upon automobiles and all other cars and ve-
hicles , including public liability , property damage ,
collision , fire and burglary and theft insurance , and
to insure the owners of such automobiles and all
other cars and vehicles against such losses as arise
out of the ownership , operation or maintenance of
the same , and generally to do and perform all other
matters and things proper to promote these objects .
(3 ) Companies organized to write the classes of
business described in subsection ( 1 ) of this sec-
tion shall write only those classes of business auth-
orized therein , and companies organized to write
the classes of business described in subsection (2 )
of this section shall write only those classes of bus-
iness authorized therein , and no company organ-
ized to do either of such classes of business shall
undertake any business or risks not provided for
in this section .
(4 ) Any company organized for the purposes
named in the subsection ( 2 ) of this section may be-
come sole surety in all cases where , by law , two or
more sureties are required for the faithful per-
formance of any trust or office , but no officer shall
be required to accept any such company as surety.

476 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Any officer may , before accepting a domestic com-
pany as surety, require an examination of the con-
dition and affairs of the company , and before accept-
ing a foreign company as surety shall be satisfied
that the company will be sufficient surety , and for
that purpose the officers and affairs of a foreign
company shall be subject to examination by the of-
ficer . If the company is accepted as surety , the
court or officer taking the bond , or any officer or
fiduciary for whom the company becomes surety ,
may place any securities , money or valuables in the
custody of such company for safekeeping .
(5) No company doing business in this state shall
undertake to do more than one of the several kinds
of insurance named in subsection ( 2 ) of this sec-
tion unless the company first has on deposit , with
the Custodian of Insurance Securities , or with the
insurance commissioner or chief financial officer
of some other state of the United States , the sum
of one hundred thousand dollars , and an additional
sum of fifty thousand dollars for each additional
kind of insurance more than one that the company
may undertake in this state . The deposit shall be
invested as required by law . Any company doing
more than one kind of business named in subsection
( 2 ) of this section shall make a separate report and
sworn statement for each kind of business done ,
such as is now required of companies doing but one
kind of business in this state . No company organ-
ized or transacting business in this state shall ex-
pose itself to loss on any one risk or hazard to an
amount exceeding ten percent of its paid-up capi-
tal and surplus , exclusive of the amount of any such
risk secured by collateral , unless the excess is re-
insured by the company in some good and reliable.
company having authority to transact business in
this state , but this limitation shall not apply to any

CHAPTER 83 477
bonds or securities furnished to the United States ,
or to any court or officer thereof . If the directors
allow to be insured on a single risk a larger sum
than the law permits , they shall be liable for any
loss thereof above the amount they might lawfully
insure . If a company is under liability for losses
equal to its net assets , and the president and di-
rectors , knowing it , make or assent to further in-
surance , they shall be personally liable for any loss
under such insurance .
Section 2. Sections 723 and 762a -23 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , are repealed .
Section 3. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , subsections ( 1 ) , ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of section one
of this Act shall, upon the taking effect of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , be substituted in place of
KRS 298.030 ; subsection ( 4 ) of section one of this
Act shall be substituted in place of KRS 298.050 ;
subsection (5 ) of section one of this Act shall be
substituted in place of KRS 298.040 ; this Act shall
thereafter be treated as amending the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes rather than Carroll's Statutes ; and
the number " KRS 298.030 " shall be substituted for
"this section " in subsections (4) and ( 5 ) of sec-
tion one of this Act.
Approved 5th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 83
(H. B. 270)
AN ACT relating to the preparation and printing of the
Journals and Acts of the present session of the General
Assembly .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

478 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 1. Inasmuch as the Kentucky Revised Statutes
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly are to be printed in a separate volume ,
therefore , in the official printing of the Acts en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , and in the official copying and printing of the
Journals of the House and Senate of the present
General Assembly , the statute laws designated as
the Kentucky Revised Statutes and enacted by
H. B. No. 271 shall not be set forth in full , but there
shall be inserted at the end of the first paragraphi
of section one of that Bill the following : " Here
followed the Kentucky Revised Statutes . "
"
2. Whereas it is customary for the Journals
and Acts of the General Assembly to be prepared
for printing before the expiration of ninety days
from the close of the session , and it is imperative
that this Act take effect prior to the time that the
Journals and Acts are prepared for printing in
order that those charged with the duty of prepar-
ing and printing may be fully advised as to their
duties , an emergency is declared to exist and this
Act shall take effect upon its passage and approval
by the Governor .
Approved 5th March , 1942
CHAPTER 84
(S. B. 163 )
AN ACT providing for the payment of salaries of clerks of
the respective courts , of sheriffs and of jailers in counties
having a population of 75,000 or more and of their deputies
and necessary office expenses not to exceed 75 per cent of
the fees collected by said officers and covered into the state
treasury ; and providing for the payment to the fiscal courts
of the respective counties of 25 per cent of the fees collected
by said officers , respectively , and covered into the treasury .

CHAPTER 85 479
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
In counties having a population of 75,000 or
more , the salaries of the clerks of the respective
courts thereof, of sheriffs and of jailers and of their
deputies and all necessary office expenses , includ-
ing the equipping , furnishing , maintaining and op-
eration of said offices , shall be paid out of the state
treasury in amounts not to exceed 75 per cent of
the fees collected by said officers respectively , and
received into the treasury ; and 25 per cent of the
fees so collected by said officers respectively , and re-
ceived into the state treasury shall be paid in the
manner provided by law for the payment of other
claims against the state to the fiscal courts of said re-
spective counties . Said amount of 25 per cent of
said fees collected by said officers respectively dur-
ing each calendar year shall be paid to the fiscal
courts of the respective counties by April 1 of each
year succeeding the calendar year during which said.
fees were received by the Department of Finance .
This act shall become effective on July 1 , 1944 , and
shall apply only to fees properly received into the
custody of the said officers on and after that date .
Approved March 5 , 1942
CHAPTER 85
(H. B. 316 )
AN ACT relating to juries .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 2243 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :

480 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(1 ) At each term of circuit court, of circuit courts
having terms , held within one year after the com-
mencement of the term at which the commissioners
were appointed , the judge shall, in open court , draw
from the drum a sufficient number of names to pro-
cure the names of twenty -four persons qualified to
act as grand jurors , record the names of the twenty-
four qualified upon paper , certify and sign it . The
judge shall then , after having locked and revolved
or shaken the drum , reopen it and draw therefrom
the names of not less than thirty nor more than
thirty -six persons , in the discretion of the judge , to
act as petit jurors , record their names on paper ,
certify and sign it . Sessions of a court having con-
tinuous session held at the county seat of a county
in which there is a city of the second class at which
part of its sessions are authorized to be held shall
be deemed terms of court within the meaning of this
section .
( 2 ) In courts of continuous session , except the
courts in counties containing a city of the first -class ,
the judge of the court , or if the court has branches
the judge of the criminal branch , shall, in open court ,
on Monday next before the Monday upon which they
shall be summoned to appear , draw from the drum
a sufficient number of names to procure twenty-
four persons qualified to act as grand jurors , record
the name of the twenty -four qualified upon paper ,
certify and sign it . In such courts the judge of the
court, or if the court has branches the judge of
each division requiring the services of a petit jury ,
shall , in open court , every two weeks , on Monday
next before the Monday upon which they shall be
summoned to appear , draw from the drum the
names of not less than thirty nor more than thirty-
six persons to act as petit jurors , record their names
on paper, certify and sign it. In such courts the

CHAPTER 85 481
names of all persons drawn as grand and petit
jurors shall be publicly announced when drawn ,
and entered of record in the order drawn .
(3 ) In courts of counties containing a city of the
first -class , the judge of the criminal branch shall ,
on Monday next before the Monday on which they
shall be summoned to appear , draw from the drum
a sufficient number of names , in the discretion of
the judge , to procure a sufficient number of per-
sons qualified to act as grand jurors , and shall
make a written record thereof, which he shall cer-
tify and sign . In such courts the judge of each
division requiring the services of a petit jury shall ,
every two weeks , on Monday next before the Mon-
day upon which they shall be summoned to appear ,
draw from the drum a sufficient number of names ,
not to exceed sixty , in the discretion of the judge ,
to procure twenty -four persons qualified to act as
petit jurors . The clerk of the court or the official
stenographer , as directed by the court , shall pre-
pare a record thereof on paper , which shall be cer-
tified and signed by the judge .
(4 ) From the list of names of persons qualified
to act as grand jurors , the next grand jury shall be
drawn , and from the list of names of persons quali-
fied to act as petit jurors , not less than twenty -four
nor more than thirty jurors shall be selected , in the
discretion of the judge , to constitute the regular
panel of the next petit jury .
( 5 ) The judge shall place the lists in separate
envelopes , of good paper, seal and endorse them
so as to show that the envelopes contain jury lists ,
and the month of the term, and in courts having
continuous session , the weeks and year , the jurors
are selected for and the date of selection and sign
his name across the seal of it . Immediately after
drawing the names from the drum , the judge shall
Acts- 16

482 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
lock the drum . The judge shall deliver the lists
and the drum to the clerk, at the same time admin-
istering to the clerk and his deputies the oath pre-
scribed in § 2241 of Carroll's Statutes , except that
in courts of counties containing a city of the first
class the judge may , in his discretion , retain pos-
session of the drum, and in lieu of the oath pre-
scribed in § 2241 shall administer to the clerk and
his deputies the following oath : " You solemnly
swear that you will not open the envelope contain-
ing the list of petit (or grand or petit jurors , as
the case may be ) jurors , until the time fixed by law ;
that you will not directly nor indirectly converse
with anyone summoned as a grand juror or petit
juror concerning any suit for trial in this court at
its next term , unless by leave of court , so help you
God . " In counties containing a city of the first
class neither the clerk nor the sheriff shall disclose
to any person , other than those prescribed by law,
any name or information concerning the name of
any person who may be summoned as either a grand
juror or a petit juror .
(6 ) The judge shall destroy the slips on which
are written the names placed on the lists as soon as
the names are recorded on the lists . If in drawing
the grand jurors the names of a person not having
the qualifications of a grand juror is drawn , it
shall be forthwith returned to the drum , and if at
any drawing the name of a person temporarily dis-
qualified is drawn , it shall be replaced in the drum ,
but if the name of any person permanently disquali-
fied for any jury service is drawn , the slip on which
it is written shall be destroyed .
( 7 ) In circuit courts having continuous sessions
and more than one judge , if the judge of any di-
vision cannot attend upon the day provided for
the drawing of names for juries , the judge of any

CHAPTER 86 483
division of the court may attend and draw names
for the juries . In circuit courts having continuous
sessions and only one judge , if the judge cannot
attend upon the day provided for the drawing of
names for juries , he shall draw the names for the
juries upon the first day thereafter he does at-
tend . Any special judge presiding at a term of
court at which the regular judge is absent , or over
any division of a circuit court having continuous
sessions , may draw the names for the juries in the
same manner as the regular judge .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, then , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , subsections ( 1 ) , ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) and
(4) of section one of this Act , shall be substituted
in place of KRS 29.130 , subsections ( 5 ) and ( 6 ) of
section one of this Act shall be substituted in place
of KRS 29.140 , and subsection ( 7 ) of section one
of this Act shall be substituted in place of KRS
29.150 ; the number " subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
29.080 " shall be substituted in place of " § 2241 of
Carroll's Statutes in subsection (5 ) of section one
of this Act , and this Act shall thereafter be treated
as amending the Kentucky Revised Statutes rather
than Carroll's Statutes .
