CHAPTER 86
(H. B. 269)
AN ACT making provision for a Statute Revision Commis-
sion and a Reviser of Statutes ; providing for their appoint-
ment , qualification and compensation , and for furnishing
them with adequate facilities ; prescribing and defining their
powers , duties and functions , and the effect of the perform-
ance thereof ; relating to the printing of the Statutes ; and
abolishing the Statute Revision Committee .

484 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Governor shall , on or before March
1 every four years , beginning with 1942 , appoint a
Statute Revision Commission composed of four
members , to be selected from a list of eight persons ,
learned in the law , submitted by the Board of Com-
missioners of the Kentucky State Bar Association .
The members of the commission shall serve for
four years and until their successors are appointed
and have qualified . Vacancies in the commission
shall be filled by appointment by the Governor from
a list of two names for each vacancy submitted by
the Board of Commissioners of the Kentucky State
Bar Association . The members of the commission
shall receive no salaries , but shall receive their rea-
sonable and necessary expenses in connection with
the performance of their duties , to be paid out of
sums appropriated to the commission . The com-
mission shall constitute an agency independent of
the administrative departments provided for in
Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
§ 2. The Division of Purchases and Public Prop-
erties shall provide the commission with suitable
office space convenient to the State Law Library .
The State Librarian shall furnish the commission
with a complete set of the Kentucky Reports , begin-
ning with Volume 79 , and with advance sheets and
permanent volumes as they are published . The
State Librarian shall also furnish the commission
with a complete set of the printed Acts , beginning
with the Acts of 1891 , and with future volumes of
the Acts as published . The clerk of each house of
the General Assembly shall furnish to the commis-
sion a copy of each bill introduced in the house of
which he is clerk , as soon as the bill is introduced .
The contractor for state printing of the first class

CHAPTER 86 485
shall , at the time of printing legislative bills for the
use of the members of the General Assembly , and
at the time of printing legislative bills for enroll-
ment , print one additional copy of each bill , from
the same type , and furnish it to the commission ,
any expense connected therewith to be paid from
the appropriations made for expense of legislative
sessions .
3. The commission shall formulate , supervise
and execute plans and methods for the future re-
vision , clarification , classification , codification , ar-
rangement , annotation , indexing, printing , binding ,
publication , copyrighting , sale and distribution of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes , including annota-
tions and supplements , and all editions thereof . The
commission shall determine when editions shall be
published , and shall fix the prices thereof . Such
printing shall not be considered printing of the first
class within the meaning of the statutes relating to
printing of the first class . The commission shall
report to the General Assembly whenever called
upon , and shall prepare and submit to the General
Assembly such consolidation , revision and other
matters relating to the statutes as can be completed
from time to time .
§ 4. The commission shall control and supervise
the use of any type acquired by the state in con-
nection with the printing of editions of the stat-
utes , and may permit the use of the type for the
printing of pamphlets of particular portions of the
statute laws requested by any state department or
agency , under such terms and conditions as the
commission considers reasonable .
§ 5. The commission , in preparing editions of
the statutes for publication and distribution , shall
not alter the sense , meaning or effect of any Act
of the General Assembly , but may renumber sec-

486 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tions and parts of sections of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly, change the wording of head -notes ,
rearrange sections , change reference numbers to
agree with renumbered chapters or sections , sub-
stitute the proper section or chapter numbers for
the terms " this Act , " " the preceding section , " and
the like , strike out figures where they are merely a
repetition of written words , change capitalization
for the purpose of uniformity , and correct mani-
fest clerical or typographical errors . When any
edition of the statutes is published by the commis-
sion , the Reviser of Statutes shall prepare a cer-
tificate certifying that he has compared each section
printed in such edition with the original section in
the enrolled Kentucky Revised Statutes , or, in the
case of sections of Acts enacted after the adoption
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , with the original
section in the enrolled bill, and that , with the ex-
ception of the changes in form permitted by this sec-
tion , the sections in the printed edition are correctly
printed . One copy of the edition , with the original
certificate affixed thereto , shall be filed in the office
of the Secretary of State . All other copies of the
edition shall contain a printed copy of the certifi-
cate . Any edition so certified shall constitute prima
facie evidence of the law in all courts and proceed-
ings , and any section in such edition may be amend-
ed or repealed by reference to the section number ,
without reference to the legislative Act from which
it was compiled . No compilation of the statute laws
of Kentucky not bearing such a certificate shall be
admissible as evidence of the law in any court or
proceeding .
§ 6. The commission shall , as soon as possible
after its appointment , appoint a Reviser of Stat-
utes , who shall serve for a term of four years and
until his successor is appointed and has qualified ,

CHAPTER 86 487
subject to removal at any time at the discretion of
the commission . The reviser shall consider , pre-
pare , submit and assist in carrying out the plans
and methods of the commission . He shall keep an
accurate and complete record in the office of the
commission of all legislation enacted by the Gen-
eral Assembly and of all bills introduced in the Gen-
eral Assembly , and shall study the same for the
purpose of considering and determining the in-
tention and effect thereof . He shall prepare and
keep accurate and complete annotations of court
decisions construing the statutes . He shall advise
and assist the members of the General Assembly
and the various departments and agencies of the
state government in the preparation and revision
of all legislation and matters connected therewith .
He shall , when requested by the General Assembly
or either house thereof, report to the General As-
sembly on bills introduced , calling attention to their
effect on existing legislation and to any deficiencies
in their form .
§ 7. The commission may appoint , and remove at
pleasure , such assistants as may be necessary to
the discharge of its duties , and shall fix the salary
of the reviser and the assistants , within the limits
of the appropriations made to the commission .
§ 8. Sections 2438a -5 to 2438a - 11 , inclusive , of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , as amend-
ed by Chapter 41 of the Acts of 1938 , are repealed ,
except that the committee heretofore appointed un-
der section 2438a - 5 shall remain in office until the
commission provided for by section one of this Act
is appointed . The commission shall be the imme-
diate successor of the committee , and any unex-
pended funds appropriated to the committee shall
be used by the commission .

488 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 9. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , this Act shall be treated as repealing KRS
447.050 and 447.060 , regardless of the date of en-
actment or taking effect of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
§ 10. As there is an immediate need for the serv-
ices of the Statute Revision Commission and of the
Revisor of Statutes in order that all future Acts
of the General Assembly may be enacted with ref-
erence to the Kentucky Revised Statutes , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall take
effect upon its passage .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 87
(H. B. 273)
AN ACT to amend section 125b - 1 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , relating to the office of Common-
wealth's Attorney in counties containing a population of
one hundred and fifty thousand or more .
WHEREAS , in counties containing a population of one
hundred and fifty thousand or over , a large portion of the
population of said counties is composed of persons of the
colored race , and it is deemed essential to the general wel-
fare of said counties and people to work in harmony with
said race and to employ whenever possible persons of that
race to work among them without doing violence to , or
discriminating against , any person because of race or
previous condition of servitude , and
WHEREAS , the work in said office in said counties has
greatly increased and it becomes necessary to increase the
number of district detectives in the office of Common-
wealth's Attorney in said counties, now therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 125b - 1 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :

CHAPTER 88 489
In counties having a population of 150,000 or
more the Commonwealth's Attorney shall appoint
four district detectives for a term of four years ,
subject to removal at any time by the Common-
wealth's Attorney . The district detectives shall
each be paid a salary not to exceed twenty -four
hundred dollars ( $ 2400.00 ) annually for each detec-
tive, to be paid out of the county levy in equal
monthly installments .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substi-
tuted in place of subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 69.110 , and
shall thereafter be treated as an amendment to sub-
section ( 1 ) of KRS 69.110 rather than an amend-
ment to $ 125b - 1 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved March 5 , 1942
CHAPTER 88
(H. B. 259)
AN ACT to establish Cooperative Live Stock Protective
Associations , providing for the incorporation of same , pro-
viding for the collection of assessments from members and
for payment of claims to members for the death and destruc-
tion of live stock resulting from depredation , disease , and
death caused by dogs and other causes , providing for the
organization , registration , operation , and dissolution of said
corporative and repealing all laws in conflict .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Declaration of policy . In order to promote ,
foster , and encourage the raising of live stock in
the State and for the further purpose of permitting
live stock producers to organize under the laws of

490 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Kentucky and form Cooperative Live Stock Cor-
porations whereby the members thereof may asso-
ciate themselves together for protection under the
laws of the State and through such association
provide a suitable means to the members and for
reimbursing said members for losses of live stock
due to depredation , disease and death caused by
attacks from dogs and other causes , this law is
enacted .
2. Definitions , As used in this Act , unless the
content for such requires otherwise , the term ( 1 )
"Live Stock" shall mean sheep , cattle , hogs , horses ,
jacks , mules , poultry or any other animal or bird
commonly kept on the farm . ( 2 ) The term " Farmer '
shall mean any individual , firm , partnership , cor-
poration or farm management group which derives
a portion or all of its income from the production of
live stock domiciled on a farm within the Common-
wealth . ( 3 ) The term " Member " shall include
actual members of the association organized under
this Act. ( 4 ) The term " Association " means any
corporation organized under this Act . (5) The
term " Department " shall mean the Department of
Agriculture , Labor and Statistics .
Associations organized hereunder shall be termed
non -profit inasmuch as they are not organized to
make profit for themselves .
3. Who may organize . Any Twenty ( 20 ) or
more farmers whom are residents of one or not
more than five counties of this State , whether they
own the land they farm or not , may form a non-
profit Live Stock Protective Corporation without
capital stock under the provisions of this Act .
4. Purposes . An association may be organized
in any section of the State for the purpose of afford-
ing protection to the farmers in that locality in the
raising of live stock on their farms .

CHAPTER 88 491
5. Articles of incorporation . Each association
formed under this Act must prepare and file
Articles of Incorporation , setting forth :
(a ) The name of the association .
(b ) The place where its principal business will
be transacted .
(c) The term for which it is to exist ; the num-
ber of directors thereof which must not be less than
five (5 ) and may be any number in the excess
thereof ; the term of office of such directors ; and the
names and addresses of those who are to serve as
incorporating directors for the first term , or until
election and qualification of their successors .
( d ) Define the property rights of the members
and whether the interest of each member will be
equal or unequal ; and if unequal , the rule or rules
applicable to all members by which the property
rights and interests , respectively , of each member
shall be determined and fixed ; and provision for
the admission of new members who shall be entitled
to share in the property of the association with the
old members , in accordance with such general rule
or rules.
6. Amendments to articles of incorporation .
The Articles of Incorporation may be altered or
amended at any regular or special meeting called
for that purpose . An amendment must first be ap-
proved by two -thirds ( 2/3 ) of the directors and
then adopted by a vote representing a majority of
all the members of the association . Amendments
to the Articles of Incorporation , when so adopted ,
shall be filed in accordance with the provisions of
the general corporation law of this State . The fee
to be charged by the Secretary of State for filing
such amendments shall be One ( $ 1.00 ) Dollar .

492 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
7. SUBSCRIPTION OF THE ARTICLES OF
INCORPORATION , PROVISIONS FOR FILING
AND FEE TO BE CHARGED THEREFOR .-The
Articles of Incorporation must be subscribed by
the incorporators and acknowledged by one of
them before an officer authorized by the law of
this State to take and certify acknowledgments
of deeds of conveyances ; and shall be filed
in accordance with the provisions of the gen-
eral corporation law of this State ; and when
so filed the said Articles of Incorporation , or copies
thereof shall be received in all the courts of this
State and other places as prima facie evidence of
the facts contained therein and of the due incor-
poration of such association . A certified copy of
the Articles of Incorporation shall also be filed with
the Department of Agriculture of the State of Ken-
tucky within ten ( 10 ) days after the filing of the
Articles of Incorporation with the County Court
Clerk of the County in which is located the prin-
cipal place of business of the association ; copy of
said Articles of Incorporation must be filed with
the Secretary of State of the State of Kentucky
and the corporation may proceed to do business
after such Articles have been duly filed . The said
County Court Clerk shall be entitled to a filing fee
of $ 2.50 for Articles of Incorporation so filed and
the said Secretary of State shall be entitled to a
fee of $ 2.50 for Articles so filed and such fees are
to be in lieu of all other fees charged by either of
these officers .
8. By-Laws. Each association incorporated
under this Act must , within thirty ( 30 ) days after
its incorporation , adopt for its government and
management , a code of By-laws , not inconsistent
with the powers granted by this Act . Such By-laws
shall be adopted by a majority of the Board of Di-

CHAPTER 88 493
rectors . Each association , under its By-laws , may
provide for any or all of the following matters :
(a) The time , place and manner of calling and
conducting its meetings .
(b) The number of members constituting a
quorum .
(c) The right of members to vote by proxy or
by mail or both ; and the conditions , manner , form ,
and effects of such votes .
( d) The number of directors constituting a
quorum .
( e ) The qualifications , compensation , and duties
and term of office of directors and officers ; time of
their election and the mode and manner of giving
notice thereof .
(f ) Penalties for violations of the By -laws .
(g ) The amount of entrance , organization , and
membership fees , if any ; the manner and method
of collection of the same ; and the purposes for
which they may be used .
(h ) The amount which each member shall be
required to pay annually or from time to time , if at
all , to carry on the business of the association ; the
charge , if any , to be paid by each member or stock-
holder for services rendered by the association to
him and the time of payment and the manner of
collection .
(i ) The method of appraisal of livestock and
the manner of payment of indemnities for same .
(j ) The number and qualification of members
of the association and the conditions precedent to
membership ; the method , time , and manner of per-
mitting members to withdraw ; the manner of as-
signment and transfer of the interest of members ;
the conditions upon which and time when member-
ship of any member shall cease ; the automatic sus-

494 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pension of the rights of a member when he ceases
to be eligible to membership in the association ; and
the mode , manner , and effect of the expulsion of a
member ; the manner of determining the value of a
member's interest and provision for its purchase
by the association upon the death or withdrawal of
a member , or upon the expulsion of a member or
forfeiture of his membership , or , at the option of
the association , the purchase of a price fixed by
conclusive appraisal by the board of directors . In
case the board of directors shall equitably and con-
clusively appraise his property interests in the
association and shall fix the amount thereof in
money , which shall be paid to him within one year
after such expulsion or withdrawal .
9. General and special meetings : How called .
In its By-laws , each association shall provide for
one or more regular meetings annually . The board
of directors shall have the right to call a special
meeting at any time ; and ten per cent of the mem-
bers may file a petition stating the specific business
to be brought before the association and demand a
special meeting at any time . Such meeting must
thereupon be called by the directors . Notice of all
meetings , together with a statement of the purposes
thereof, shall be mailed to each member at least ten
days prior to the meeting ; provided , however , that
the By-laws may require instead that such notice
may be given by publication in a newspaper of
general circulation , published at the principal place
of business of the association .
10. Directors ; Election . The affairs of the
association shall be managed by a board of not less
than five directors , elected by the members from
their own number . The By-laws may provide that
the territory in which the association has members
shall be divided into districts and that the directors

CHAPTER 88 495
shall be elected according to such districts , either
directly or by district delegates elected by the mem-
bers in that district . In such a case the By -laws
shall specify the number of directors to be elected
by each district , the manner and method of re-
apportioning the directors and of redistricting the
territory covered by the association . The By -laws
may provide that primary elections shall be held in
each district to elect the directors apportioned to
such districts and that the result of all such primary
elections may be ratified by the next regular meet-
ing of the association or may be considered final as
to the association . The By-laws may provide that
one or more directors may be appointed by any
public official or commission or by the other direc-
tors selected by the members or their delegates .
Such directors shall represent primarily the interest
of the general public in such associations . The
director or directors so appointed need not be mem-
bers of the association ; but shall have the same
powers and rights as other directors . Such direc
tors shall not number more than one -fifth of the
entire number of directors .
An association may provide a fair remuneration
for the time actually spent by its officers and direc-
tors in its service and for the service of the mem-
bers of its executive committee . No director , during
the term of his office , shall be a party to a contract
for profit with the association differing in any way
from the business relations accorded regular mem-
bers of the association or others , or differing from
terms generally current in that district .
The By-laws may provide for an executive com-
mittee and may allot to such committee all the func-
tions and powers of the board of directors , subject
to the general direction and control of the board .

496 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
4744444
When a vacancy on the board of directors occurs
other than by expiration of term , the remaining
members of the board , by a majority vote , shall fill
the vacancy , unless the By-laws provide for an
election of directors by district . In such a case the
board of directors shall immediately call a special
meeting of the members in that district to fill the
vacancy .
11. Election of officers . The directors shall
elect from their number a president and one or
more vice presidents . They shall also elect a sec-
retary and a treasurer , who need not be directors
or members of the association ; and they may com-
bine the two latter offices and designate the com-
bined office as secretary -treasurer ; or unite both
functions and titles in one person . The treasurer
may be a bank or any depository, and as such , shall
not be considered as an officer , but as a function of
the board of directors . In such case , the secretary
shall perform the usual accounting duties of the
treasurer , excepting that the funds shall be de-
posited only as and where authorized by the board
of directors .
12. Removal of officer or director . Any mem-
ber may bring charges against an officer or director
by filing them in writing with the secretary of the
association , together with a petition signed by five
per cent of the members , requesting the removal of
the officer or director in question . The removal
shall be voted upon at the next regular or special
meeting of the association and , by a vote of a ma-
jority of the members , the association may remove
the officer or director and fill the vacancy .
director or officer against whom such charges have
been brought , shall be informed in writing of the
charges previous to the meeting and shall have an
opportunity at the meeting to be heard in person
The

CHAPTER 88 497
or by counsel and to present witnesses ; and the
person or persons bringing the charges against him
shall have the same opportunity .
In case the By -laws provide for election of direc-
tors by districts with primary elections in each
district , then the petition for removal of a director
must be signed by twenty per cent of the members
residing in the district from which he was elected .
The board of directors must call a special meeting
of the members residing in that district to consider
the removal of the director ; and by a vote of the
majority of the members of that district , the direc-
tor in question shall be removed from office .
13. Referendum . Upon demand of one -third of
the entire board of directors any matter that has
been approved or passed by the board must be re-
ferred to the entire memberhip for decision at the
next special or regular meeting ; provided , however ,
that a special meeting may be called for that
purpose .
14. Associations heretofore organized may
adopt the provisions of this act . Any corporation
or association organized under previously existing
statutes may , by a majority vote of its members , be
brought under the provisions of this Act by limiting
its membership and adopting the other restrictions
as provided herein . It shall make out in duplicate
a statement signed and sworn to by its directors to
the effect that the corporation or association has ,
by a majority vote of the members , decided to ac-
cept the benefits and be bound by the provisions of
this Act and has authorized all changes accord-
ingly . Articles of Incorporation shall be filed as
herein required except that they shall be signed by
the members of the then board of directors . The
filing fee shall be the same as for filing an amend-
ment to Articles of Incorporation .

498 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Where any association may be incorporated
under this Act , all contracts heretofore made by or
on behalf of same by the promoters thereof in an-
ticipation of such association becoming incorpo-
rated under the laws of this State , whether such
contracts be made by or in the name of some cor-
poration organized elsewhere and when same would
have been valid if entered into subsequent to the
passage of this Act , are hereby validated as if made
after the passage of this Act .
15. Members not liable for association debts .
No member shall be liable for the debts of the asso-
ciation to an amount exceeding the sum remaining
unpaid on his membership fee , including any un-
paid balance on any promissory notes given in pay-
ment thereof .
16. Sections of act declared severable . All laws
or parts of laws in conflict with the provisions of
this Act are hereby repealed to the extent of such
conflict . The provisions of this Act are hereby
declared to be severable , it being the intention of
the General Assembly to enact each section sepa-
rately , and if any such Acts or parts of Acts be held
invalid for any cause the remainder of this Act
shall continue in full force and effect , it being the
legislative intent now hereby declared that this Act
would have been adopted even if such unconstitu-
tional or void matter had not been included therein .
17. State Department of Agriculture directed to
cooperate . The State Department of Agriculture
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky is hereby di-
rected to assist in the organization of any associa-
tion desiring to incorporate under the provisions of
this Act when requested to do so in writing by any
twenty or more eligible persons residing within the
State .

CHAPTER 90 499
18. Associations incorporated under the pro-
visions of this Act shall be exempt from the pro-
visions of law , now or hereafter enacted , relating
to insurance companies or other organizations , of a

CHAPTER 89
(H. B. 245)
AN ACT providing that published financial statements shall
be filed with the Auditor of Public Accounts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. It shall be the duty of all public
officers who are required to publish the financial
statements as provided for under section 3747a - 1 ,
Kentucky Statutes , to file with the Auditor of Pub-
lic Accounts on or before the 31st day of August of
each year a copy of the newspaper containing such
financial statement .
Section 2. Any officer who shall fail to file said
copy of the newspaper containing said financial
statement which such officer is required to make , at
the time required , shall be guilty of a misdemeanor
and upon conviction thereof shall be fined not less
than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) nor more than five hun-
dred dollars ( $ 500.00 ) at the discretion of the court
or jury trying the case. Not approved .
CHAPTER 90
(H. B. 340 )
AN ACT relating to licenses for race tracks .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

500 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 1. Section 4223b -7 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as
follows :
Any such race track located within a county with
a population of two hundred thousand or over shall
pay for each day upon which races are conducted
at such track for any stake , purse or prize a license
tax of twenty-five hundred dollars per day. Any
such track located within a county having a popula-
tion of not less than one hundred thousand and not
more than two hundred thousand shall pay for each
day upon which races are conducted at said track
for any stake , purse or prize a license tax of one
thousand dollars per day . Any such race track
located in any other county shall pay for each day
upon which races are conducted for any stake , purse
or prize a license tax of five hundred dollars per
day .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall, upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substi-
tuted in place of subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 137.170 ,
and thereafter be treated as an amendment to sub-
section ( 2 ) of KRS 137.170 rather than an amend-
ment to § 4223b -7 of Carroll's Statutes .
§ 3. Inasmuch as it is necessary that the license
tax on race tracks should be fixed before the open-
ing of the racing season , and in the regular course
this act would not become effective before that time ,
an emergency is hereby declared to exist and this
act shall take effect upon its passage and approval
by the Governor .
Not approved .

AN ACT relating to casualty insurance for volunteer firemen .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

cretion of its governing body , provide for accident
and death benefits for the members of volunteer fire

CHAPTER 92
( H. B. 169 )
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation of gasoline .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 4281g -7 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is repealed .
§ 2. Section 4281g -19 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as
follows :
If any dealer files a false monthly report of the
date or information required by §§ 4281g- 1 to
4281g -20 , or fails , refuses or neglects to file the
monthly report required by those sections , even
though no tax is due , or to pay the full amount of
tax as required by those sections , the dealer may
be cited by the Department of Revenue to show
cause at a public hearing before the Kentucky Tax
Commission why his license should not be revoked
by the Commission . The dealer shall be notified
by registered letter or summons . The letter or sum-

502 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
mons shall appraise the dealer of the charge or
charges made against him and he shall have a
reasonable opportunity to be heard before his
license may be revoked . The summons may be
served in the same manner and by the same of
ficers or persons as provided by the Kentucky Civil
Code of Practice , or it may be served in said manner
by an employee of the Department of Revenue .
The hearing shall be set at least five ( 5 ) days after
the summons is served or the letter delivered . Any
aggrieved dealer may appeal from an order of the
Kentucky Tax Commission in the manner pro-
vided for appeals from other of its rulings subject
to the condition that he make bond sufficient in the
opinion of the Department of Revenue to protect
the Commonwealth from loss of revenue .
The Department is hereby given the power to
cancel the license upon request in writing of the
dealer , such cancellation to become effective sixty
( 60 ) days from the date of receipt of the request .
But no license shall be canceled upon the request of
any dealer until and unless the dealer , prior to the
date of such cancellation , pays to the state all exise
taxes payable under the laws of the state together
with any and all penalties and fines accruing by
reason of any failure on the part of the dealer to
make accurate reports as required or pay such
taxes and penalties .
§ 3. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem
bly, section two of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 138.340 , and shall there-
after be treated an * an amendment to KRS 138.340
rather than an amendment to § 4281g- 19 of Car-
roll's Statutes ; the numbers " KRS 138.220 to

CHAPTER 93
( H. B. 150 )
AN ACT relating to motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The provisions of §§ 2739j -42 to 2739j -95 of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , as
amended by Chapter 9 of the Acts of the Fourth
Extraordinary Session of 1938 , Chapter 100 of the
Acts of 1938 and Chapter 171 of the Acts of 1938
shall not apply to motor vehicles used exclusively
in transporting materials , supplies and equipment
for the construction , reconstruction or maintenance

Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 94
(H. B. 54)
AN ACT relating to Workmen's Compensation for Industrial
Accidents .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That " An Act to provide an elective
system of Workmen's Compensation for Industrial
Accidents , " and known as Chapter Thirty -three of

504 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the Acts of the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , approved by the Governor on
March 23 , 1916 and the amendments thereto , be
amended and the following added thereto :
Section 2. That when an employee , who has
been awarded disability compensation by the Work-
men's Compensation Board , shall die as a result of
such injury prior to the payment to him of the
amount of the award , then the dependents of the
deceased employee shall be allowed and paid all
allowed and unpaid awards made to such employee .
Section 3. Provided , however , that the depend-
ents of such deceased employee shall, within sixty
days after the death of such employee , file with the
Workmen's Compensation Board , in such form as
the Board may require , a written , verified applica-
tion , stating therein the date of the death of such
deceased employee ; the amount of such allowed and
uncollected award ; the names , ages , post - office ad-
dress , and the relation of each of said dependents
to the deceased . Thereupon the Workmen's Com-
pensation Board shall notify in writing the Com-
pany , person or Insurance Carrier against whom
such award was made ; and shall fix a time and place
for a hearing of which notice shall be given to de-
termine the dependability of those making such
claim ; and shall make an award or order for the
benefit of those found to be entitled thereto .
Provided , however , that the number of weekly
payments to be paid to the dependents shall be the
number of weeks remaining after deducting the
number of weekly payments made to the decedent
from the number of weeks allowed in the original
award .
The total amount paid and payable to the de-
cedent and his dependents shall not exceed the
amount now payable for death at the average weekly

CHAPTER 95 505
wage of the decedent at the time of his injury , and
the weekly payments to the dependents shall not
exceed the sum of twelve ( $ 12.00 ) dollars a week .
Section 4. That all laws and parts of laws in con-
flict herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved March 5, 1942 .
CHAPTER 95
(H. B. 161 )
AN ACT adopting and putting into effect daylight saving time
throughout the Commonwealth of Kentucky by advancing
standard time one hour to run contemporaneously and for
a period of time provided by the Federal Daylight Saving
Act, and declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , the National Government has adopted daylight
saving time in regard to interstate commerce and Federal
offices , agencies and works throughout the United States
and ,
WHEREAS , it is desirable to avoid confusion and conflict
that this Commonwealth and its subdivisions and agencies
in its various transactions be in accord with the Federal
Government in this matter .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the standard time as heretofore fixed and
existing in the United States pursuant to Act of
Congress and prior to the recent adoption of day .
light saving time during the period of the National
emergency be advanced by one hour , which time so
advanced shall apply to and govern all laws , regula-
tions and rules relating to the time or performance
of any Act by any officer or department of the Com-
monwealth , or of any county , city or subdivision or
agency thereof , or relating to the time that any
right shall accrue or determine , or within which any
act shall or shall not be performed by any person.

506 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
subject to the jurisdiction of the Commonwealth ,
and in all the public schools and institutions of the
Commonwealth and on the public works of the Com-
monwealth or any county , city or district thereof , or
in all contracts or choses in action made or to be
performed in the Commonwealth .
That said time adopted above shall continue so
long as the Federal Government continues its adop-
tion of the change of time and shall cease when the
Federal Government abandons and discontinues the
use of daylight saving time and reverts to standard
time and so promulgates .
That there is pressing necessity of putting into
effect this change of time to conform with the
Federal Government and an emergency is now de-
clared and this Act shall go into effect at midnight
following its final passage and approval by the
Governor .
Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 96
(H. B. 236 )
AN ACT relating to probation and parole .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. There is hereby created in the Department of
Welfare a Division of Probation and Parole , headed
by a director who shall make rules and regulations
governing parolees and shall have the same powers
of arrest as probation and parole officers . The
Division of Probation and Parole , under the direc-
tion of the Commissioner of Welfare , shall exercise
the functions vested in the Department of Welfare
relating to probation and parole .

( 2 ) The period of probation or postponement of

shall be stated in the court order fixing it . Upon the
satisfactory fulfillment of the conditions of proba-

court shall by order discharge the defendant .

Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Kentucky
A paroled prisoner shall make such written

CHAPTER 97
(H. B. 192 )
AN ACT for the judicial notice of the laws of other jurisdic-
tions and for proof thereof and to make uniform the law
with reference thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. JUDICIAL NOTICE . Every court
of this state shall take judicial notice of the com-
mon law and statutes of every state , territory and
other jurisdiction of the United States .
Section 2 . INFORMATION OF THE COURT .
The court may inform itself of such laws in such
manner as it may deem proper , and the court may
call upon counsel to aid it in obtaining such in-
formation .
Section 3. RULING REVIEWABLE . The de-
termination of such laws shall be made by the court
and not by the jury , and shall be reviewable .
Section 4 .4. EVIDENCE AS TO LAWS OF
OTHER JURISDICTIONS . Any party may also
present to the trial court any admissible evidence
of such laws , but , to enable a party to offer evidence
of the law in another jurisdiction or to ask that
judicial notice be taken thereof , reasonable notice
shall be given to the adverse parties either in the
pleadings or otherwise .

512 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 5. FOREIGN COUNTRY . The law of
a jurisdiction other than those referred to in Sec-
tion 1 shall be an issue for the court, but shall not
be subject to the foregoing provisions concerning
judicial notice .
Section 6. INTERPRETATION . This act shall
be so interpreted and construed as to effectuate its
general purpose to make uniform the law of those
states which enact it.
Section 7. SHORT TITLE . This act may be
cited as the Uniform Judicial Notice of Foreign
Law Act .
Section 8. REPEAL . All acts or parts of act
inconsistent with the provisions of this act are
hereby repealed , to the extent of such conflict .
Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 98
(S. B. 152 )
AN ACT relating to Pharmacy .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1 The Kentucky Board of Pharmacy is
hereby authorized to examine all persons who now
hold certificates as Assistant Registered Pharma-
cists in Kentucky . Persons who hold such cer-
tificates may be licensed to practice pharmacy as
full registered pharmacists , upon the payment of
the regular examination fee and passing a special
examination as prescribed by the Kentucky Board
of Pharmacy .
Sec . 2 All persons who are required to pay re-
newal fees to the Kentucky Board of Pharmacy as
registered or assistant registered pharmacists shall

CHAPTER 99 513
not be required to pay such fees during the time
such persons are actively serving in the Armed
Forces of the United States .
** * * * *
Declaration of an Emergency
WHEREAS a state of National emergency exists ,
and many registered pharmacists and registered as-
sistant pharmacists are absent from the State in the
service of the United States Armed Forces , and
therefore , stand to suffer forfeiture of their regis-
trations ; and whereas , the Kentucky Board of
Pharmacy should be given authority to protect such
persons and as near as possible to the beginning of
the calendar year , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist and this Act shall become a law and to be
effective upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Not approved .

520 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 104
(H. B. 219 )
AN ACT authorizing clerks , sheriffs and jailers who are re-
quired to pay their fees into the State Treasury , to require
depositories to execute bonds or to pledge assets of such
banks as collateral security for deposits , providing the
terms of such bonds or pledges of collateral securities and
providing that such bonds or pledges shall not alter the
rights of the Commonwealth or county to proceed on the
officer's bond or against the banks individually .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Clerks , sheriffs and jailers who are
required under Section 106 of the Constitution to
pay the fees collected by them into the State Treas-
ury shall have the right , before naming any bank
as a depository or permitting such bank to receive
monies collected by them , to require the bank either
to execute a bond with good surety or pledge assets
to secure the deposits , as hereinafter set forth .
Section 2. If the bank elects to execute a bond ,
the bond shall be for a sum not less than the highest
anticipated amount which may be on deposit in the
bank at any time . The principal and sureties must
be worth jointly not less than double the face
amount of the bond . The bond shall be approved
by the officer requiring it to be executed and shall be
filed with and recorded by the county clerk .
Section 3. If the bank elects to secure the deposit
by the pledge of assets of the bank , the officer may
accept a pledge for that purpose of warrants of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , bridge revenue bonds
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , United States
Government Bonds or such other bonds as are
guaranteed in full by the United States Govern
ment or any combination of such securities . The
pledge shall be in an amount at least ten percent in

CHAPTER 104 521
excess of the deposit based upon the current market
price of the securities , and the bank shall maintain
the value of the collateral securities at such figure
as of the first day of every month . The securities
shall be kept in a safe deposit box in a bank or trust
company under an agreement which prevents either
the officer or the bank from entering the box except
in the presence of the other party or his duly
authorized agent . The officer shall pay the rent of
the safe deposit box . While the securities are so
deposited , a list thereof shall be maintained by the
bank and shall be submitted to the officer when the
first deposit is made and each time thereafter that
any change in the amount or kind of collateral
securities is made , in order that the list may be kept
current at all times .
Section 4. The execution of the bond or the
pledge of securities as provided in this Act shall not
alter the existing liability of the officer and his
sureties upon his official bond ; nor shall the execu-
tion of the bond or pledge impair or delay the right
of the Commonwealth or county to recover on the
officer's bond for any misapplication of public funds
or other delinquency in office . The execution of the
bond or the pledge of securities by the bank shall
not in any way impair or delay the right of the Com-
monwealth or county to recover from any delinquent
or defaulting bank or the officers or stockholders
thereof , in the same manner as other depositories .
If a clerk , sheriff or jailer , authorized by this Act
to require a bond or pledge of securities of a de-
pository , fails to require such bond or pledge , he
shall not be relieved of his liability as insurer of
funds under his control as provided in § 186d-1 of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
Section 5. WHEREAS , clerks of courts , sheriffs ,
and jailers in counties having a population of

522 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
seventy -five thousand ( 75,000 ) , or more , are now
making bank deposits of monies coming into their
hands without any security being given guarantee-
ing payment of such monies on the part of the bank ;
and
WHEREAS , it is urgent that all such deposits be
secured as quickly as possible in order to prevent
any loss to the Commonwealth and to the respective
counties thereof .
NOW , THEREFORE , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall become effective
immediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Section 6. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , the number " KRS 62.080 " shall be substituted
for " 186d - 1 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes in
section four of this Act .
Not approved
CHAPTER 105
(H. B. 315 )
AN ACT relating to narcotic drugs .
WHEREAS , the Federal Bureau of Narcotics has recom-
mended the following amendments to the Uniform Narcotic
Drug Act and it is the desire of the General Assembly to
cooperate with the Bureau of Narcotics and to keep the Act
uniform ; now , therefore :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 3716-8 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as
follows :
Preparations exempted . Except as otherwise in
this Act specifically provided , this Act shall not
apply to the following cases :

CHAPTER 105 523
( 1 ) Administering , dispensing , or selling at re-
tail of any medicinal preparation that contains in
one fluid ounce , or if a solid or semi -solid prepara-
tion , in one avoirdupois ounce , not more than one
grain of codeine or of any of its salts .
( 2 ) The exemption authorized by this section
shall be subject to the following conditions ( a ) that
the medicinal preparation administered , dispensed ,
or sold, shall contain , in addition to the narcotic
drugs in it , some drug or drugs conferring upon it
medicinal qualities other than those possessed by
the narcotic drug alone ; and (b) that such prepara-
tion shall be administered , dispensed , and sold in
good faith as a medicine , and not for the purpose of
evading the provisions of this Act .
( 3 ) Nothing in this section shall be construed
to limit the quantity of codeine or of any of its salts
that may be prescribed , administered , dispensed ,
or sold , to any person or for the use of any person
or animal , when it is prescribed , administered , dis-
pensed , or sold , in compliance with the general pro-
visions of this Act .
§ 2. Subsection ( 5 ) of section 3716-9 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , is amended to read
as follows :
The form of records shall be prescribed by the
State Board of Health . The record of narcotic
drugs received shall in every case show the date of
receipt , the name and address of the person from
whom received , and the kind and quantity of drugs
received ; the kind and quantity of narcotic drugs
produced or removed from process of manufacture ,
and the date of such production or removal from
process of manufacture ; and the record shall in
every casecase show the proportion of morphine ,
cocaine , or ecgonine contained in or producible from

524 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
crude opium or coca leaves received or produced
and the proportion of resin contained in or pro-
ducible from the plant Cannabis Sativa L. The
record of all narcotic drugs sold , administered , dis-
pensed , or otherwise disposed of , shall show the
date of selling , administering , or dispensing , the
name and address of the person to whom , or for
whose use , or the owner and species of animal for
which the drugs were sold , administered or dis-
pensed , and the kind and quantity of drugs . Every
such record shall be kept for a period of two years
from the date of the transaction recorded . The
keeping of a record required by or under the Federal
Narcotic Laws , containing substantially the same
information as is specified above , shall constitute
compliance with this section , except that every such
record shall contain a detailed list of narcotic drugs
lost, destroyed , or stolen , if any , the kind and
quantity of such drugs , and the date of the discovery
of such loss , destruction , or theft .
§ 3. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 218.080 , and thereafter be
treated as an amendment to KRS 218.080 rather
than an amendment to § 3716-8 of Carroll's
Statutes
§ 4. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section two of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of subsection ( 5 ) of KRS 218.090 ,
and thereafter be treated as an amendment to sub-
section ( 5 ) of KRS 218.090 rather than an amend-
ment to § 3716-9 ( 5 ) of Carroll's Statutes .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 106 525
CHAPTER 106
(S. B. 161 )
AN ACT relating to attorneys for administrative depart-
ments , agencies , divisions and independent agencies of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky That :
Section 1. The term " department " as used in
this Act shall be construed to mean and to include
each and every administrative department , agency ,
division and independent agency , as said terms and
words are defined in the Governmental Re - Organ-
ization Act of 1936 .
Section 2. When any department deems it neces-
sary to employ an attorney or attorneys to render
any legal services for such department , the depart .
ment with the approval of the Governor shall have
the power and the authority to employ and fix the
terms of employment and the compensation to be
paid to the attorney or attorneys . The compensa-
tion and expenses of any attorney or attorneys em-
ployed under the provisions of this Act shall be
paid out of the appropriations made to such depart-
ment as other salaries , compensation and expenses
are paid . Before the employment of any attorney
under the provisions of this Act shall be made or
become effective , the Governor shall approve the
employment and the terms of employment by
Executive Order duly entered upon the Executive
Journal in the office of the Secretary of State .
An Attorney may be employed pursuant to the
provisions of this Act to render legal services for
one or more departments , and if employed by more
than one department he shall perform such services
as provided in the agreement entered into by the
departments and approved by the Governor . Ex-

526 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cept when employed by contract for the collection
of money on a contingent basis , the total compensa-
tion to be paid an attorney employed under the pro-
visions of this Act shall not exceed the sum of five
thousand dollars per annum , and he shall devote his
full time to providing legal services for the depart-
ment or departments by which he is employed .
Section 3. Any attorney or attorneys employed
pursuant to the provisions of this Act shall have
authority to appear as the chief attorney for and to
represent the department in the trial and argument
of all cases and proceedings in the Kentucky Court
of Appeals and in any and all other courts , and
before boards , governmental agencies and tribunals
in or out of this Commonwealth whenever such
department or any officer or employee thereof is a
party in interest or the official rights , powers or
duties of the department or of any officer or em-
ployee thereof are directly or indirectly affected .
Such attorney or attorneys may institute and
prosecute all suits , actions and proceedings neces
sary to cause the payment of all claims , accounts ,
demands and judgments of the Commonwealth , for
which such department is charged by law with the
collection or supervision of the collection thereof ,
and to take all necessary steps by suit , motion ,
action or otherwise to collect or cause to be col-
lected and paid into the State Treasury all such
claims , demands , accounts and judgments . Any
attorney or attorneys so employed shall attend to
all litigation and legal business within and without
the State , required of him or them by the terms of
his or their employment ; and also any litigation or
legal business that any officer or employee of such
department may have in connection with or grow-
ing out of his official duties or the official duties of

CHAPTER 106 527
the department ; and he or they , upon the written
request of any executive or ministerial officer of
the department , shall give such department or
officer his written opinion as to the duties of such
officer and shall prepare proper drafts of all in
struments of writing and perform such other legal
services pertaining to the functions of the depart-
ment as may be provided by the terms of employ-
ment .
Section 4. Section five ( 5 ) of Chapter 32 of the
Acts of the General Assembly of 1908 is repealed
and all other laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with , to the extent of such conflict , hereby are re-
pealed . Provided , however , that nothing in this act
shall be construed to affect the tenure or compensa-
tion of any Assistant Attorney General now ap-
pointed and serving pursuant to law ; and nothing
in this act shall be construed to limit the right of
any department or agency of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky to require the advice or services of the
Attorney General and the Assistant Attorneys
General as now provided by law .
Section 5. WHEREAS , the proper and efficient
functioning of the departments require adequate
and competent legal advice and services which are
not now fully and adequately available ; and
WHEREAS , under the provisions of Section 81
of the Kentucky Constitution , the Governor is
charged with the duty and responsibility of the en-
forcement of the laws, and it is necessary that the
powers granted in this Bill be given immediate
effect , an emergency hereby is declared to exist and
this Act shall become effective immediately upon
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 9 , 1942 .

528 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 107
(S. B. 117)
AN ACT relating to the manufacture , transportation and sale
of distilled spirits in this commonwealth ; providing for the
manufacture , transportation and sale of distilled spirits in
lieu of tax paid distilled spirits accidentally destroyed by
fire , which may have been or may hereafter be manu-
factured under the provisions of Section 4214a - 13 , Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's 1936 Edition , and providing for
the issual of duplicate tax free permits for such manufac-
ture , transportation and sale , and repealing all conflicting
laws .
Said bill is as follows , viz .:
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That in the event distilled spirits
have heretofore been manufactured under the pro-
visions of Section 4214a - 13 , of the Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's 1936 Edition , same being Section 2 ,
Chapter 149 of the Acts of the General Assembly of
1934 , or may hereafter be manufactured under the
provisions of said Section , or any amendment
thereto , and the taxes imposed by said Section paid ,
and said distilled spirits manufactured heretofore
or hereafter , shall have been or shall hereafter be ,
destroyed by accidental fire , through no fault of the
owner thereof , duplicate permits shall be issued to
the person who had manufactured said distilled
spirits and paid the tax thereon , for the privilege
of manufacturing , transporting and selling other
distilled spirits in lieu thereof , and no tax shall be
imposed or collected for the privilege of manufac-
turing , transporting and selling an equal amount of
such distilled spirits , provided the distilled spirits
so destroyed were destroyed prior to the sale of
such spirits by the actual manufacturer thereof and
prior to the exportation of said spirits from the

CHAPTER 107 529
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and while said spirits
were still stored on the premises where manufac-
tured , or in warehouses adjacent thereto .
The person desiring to manufacture distilled
spirits in lieu of tax-paid distilled spirits destroyed
as set out above shall apply to the Tax Commission ,
or other proper State Agency for the issual of dupli-
cate permits and the Tax Commission , or other
State Agency in charge of issuing permits shall at
once issue duplicate permits to the owner of the
distilled spirits so destroyed for the same number
of gallons as were destroyed by fire as set out above .
There shall be no charge for the issual of said per-
mits , but the manufacturer making such applica-
tion shall furnish definite proof ;
(a ) That the distilled spirits so destroyed were
tax-paid under the provisions of Sec . 4214a - 13 of
the Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , or said Sec-
tion as amended ;
(b) That the distilled spirits so destroyed had
not been sold by the manufacturer thereof prior to
the time of the destruction ;
(c ) That said spirits were still stored on the
premises where manufactured , or immediately ad-
jacent to the premises where manufactured ;
(d ) The number of proof gallons destroyed ;
(e ) The cause of said destruction and that said
destruction was accidental and not the fault of the
owner thereof .
Provided that the Tax Commission , or other
State Agency may accept the same proof of loss as
that furnished to the United States Government
under similar circumstances .
Section 2. It is hereby declared to be and to
have been the Legislative intent to allow and to
have allowed Kentucky manufacturers of distilled

530 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
spirits to place upon the market one proof -gallon
of distilled spirits , less shrinkage and leakage , for
each such gallon tax -paid under the provisions of
Section 4214a - 13 Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1936
Edition , and to allow said Kentucky manufacturers
of distilled spirits the same privileges under the
Kentucky law which they now have and heretofore
have had under the Federal law relating to acci-
dental destruction of such spirits by fire .
Section 3. All laws or parts of law in conflict
herewith are hereby repealed to the extent of such
conflict .
Section 4. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly , the number " KRS 243.680 " shall be
substituted for " Section 4214a -13 , of the Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1936 edition , same being Section
2 , Chapter 149 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1934 " and for " Sec . 4214a - 13 of the Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition " where these phrases appear
in section one of this Act .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 109
( S. B. 52 )
AN ACT to amend section 584 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Sec . 584 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Amount of deposits to be kept on hand ; Federal
reserve members .
( 1 ) Each bank and trust company , organized
under the laws of this state and authorized to re-
ceive deposits , shall keep on hand , at all times , at

532 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
least seven percent of its total demand deposits and
three percent of its total time and savings deposits ;
and , in cities which are reserve or central reserve
cities , under the Federal Reserve Act ( 38 Stat . 251 )
as amended , at least ten percent in reserve cities
and thirteen percent in central reserve cities of its
total demand deposits and three per cent of its total
time and savings deposits . One -third of all reserves
required by this subsection shall be in money and
the remainder may be in balances due from other
banks and subject to call .
( 2) The Director of banking , in his discretion ,
may require any bank or trust company organized
under the laws of this state to keep on hand not
exceeding twice the amount of deposits required to
be kept on hand by subsection ( 1 ) , but such require-
ment shall not require any greater amount of cash
to be kept on hand than is required by subsection
(1) .
( 3 ) Any bank or trust company , incorporated
under the laws of this state , which becomes a mem-
ber of a Federal Reserve Bank , shall be subject to
all the provisions of the Federal Reserve Act and
its amendments , and the regulations of the Federal
Reserve Board applicable to such bank or trust
company , and shall have all the powers and assume
all the liabilities conferred and imposed by the Act
in regard to state member banks . Such bank or
trust company shall comply with the reserve re-
quirements of the Federal Reserve Act and its
amendments , and such compliance shall relieve the
bank or trust company from compliance with the
laws of this state relating to the maintenance of
reserves .
(4) As used in this section :
(a) " Demand deposits " include all deposits
payable within thirty days ; and

CHAPTER 111 533
(b ) " Time deposits " include all deposits pay-
able after thirty days and all savings accounts and
certificates of deposit which are subject to not less
than thirty days notice before payment .
2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are enacted
at the present session of the General Assembly ,
section one of this Act shall , upon the taking effect
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substituted
in place of KRS 287.300 , and thereafter be treated
as an amendment to KRS 287.300 rather than an
amendment to Section 584 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 6th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 111
(S. B. 94 )
AN ACT relating to deposits by insurance companies .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 1 of Chapter 99 of the Acts
of 1940 , compiled as Section 648a - 10 of Baldwin's

534 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Η
1941 Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , is
amended to read as follows :
Deposits to protect policyholders : eligibility for
deposit : amount . Within ninety days after the net

tained as now required by law , every Domestic Life
Insurance Company shall deposit for the security
and benefit of its policyholders , with a custodian of

inafter set forth , lawful money of the United States

ance Companies may legally invest their funds , in

Provided , however , that if the legal reserve or the

cies , in which event such company shall deposit

CHAPTER 112 535
Provided , further , that no company shall be re-
quired to make the deposit herein required until the

visions of Kentucky Statutes , Section 648 , and then

-enacted at the present session of the General As-

The deposits required by KRS 297.210 and

ship of unencumbered real estate lawfully acquired

ties or evidence of ownership or other real estate of

297.230 rather than an amendment to Chapter 99 of

CHAPTER 112
(S. B. 95)
AN ACT providing for the enforcement of laws and ordi-
nances relating to hazards or risks insured by fire insurance

536 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

( 1 ) The prevention or reduction of loss by fire
