CHAPTER 123
(H. B. 135 )
AN ACT relating to the distribution of the common school
fund .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. § 4370-6 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
For each school year the Department of Finance ,
on the certification of the Superintendent of Public
Instruction , shall draw a warrant on the treasurer
for the amount of the school fund due each district .
The checks or warrants , in case the warrants are
stamped interest-bearing , shall be turned over to
the Superintendent of Public Instruction for dis-
tribution to the proper officials of the several school
districts when the districts have fully complied with
the school laws and rules and regulations of the
State Board of Education . The school fund shall
be distributed in the same number of installments
as there are months in the minimum school term
declared by the Superintendent of Public Instruc-
tion upon the approval of the State Board of Edu-
cation . The first installment shall be paid on or
before August 1 of each year to those districts
whose terms begin during the month of July and
the first installment shall be paid on or before Sep-
tember 1 of each year to all other districts and the
subsequent installments paid on or before the first
day of each succeeding month until all of the in-
stallments have been paid .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substi-

566 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tuted in place of KRS 157.050 , and shall thereafter
be treated as an amendment to KRS 157.050 rather
than an amendment to § 4370-6 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 6th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 124
(H. B. 324)
AN ACT to amend section 98a - 2 of the 1936 Edition of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 98a -2 of the 1936 Edition of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended so as to
read as follows :
( 1 ) The Court of Appeals of Kentucky shall
make such rules and regulations as it deems proper
fixing the moral qualifications of applicants and
standard of acquirements , academic , legal and offi-
cial service to entitle them to admission to practice
law in the courts of this Commonwealth .
( 2 ) No applicant shall be under the age of
twenty -one years , except that the Court of Appeals.
may , in its discretion, during the present National
Emergency admit persons to practice law in the
courts of this Commonwealth who are under the
age of twenty- one years if such persons are within
the age limits of the Selective Service Act and if
such persons have met all the other requirements .
necessary for such admission .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub
stituted in place of subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of KRS

CHAPTER 125 567
30.030 , and thereafter be treated as an amendment
to subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of KRS 30.030 rather
than an amendment to § 98a -2 of Carroll's Statutes .
§ 3. Whereas , many young men are now being
called to service in the Army and Navy and
whereas , many attorneys of this Commonwealth are
now or will be in service in the Army and Navy , an
emergency is hereby declared to exist and this Act
shall become effective immediately upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Approved 6th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 125
(H. B. 283 )
AN ACT relating to liens of mechanics and material men.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 2476 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as
follows :
If for any cause it should be improper to refer the
case to the master commissioner , he is then directed
to select some suitable person to act as commissioner
for the occasion and refer the case to him ; but before
such person shall proceed to act , he shall , before the
clerk, take an oath , and execute bond , with sufficient
surety, similar in all respects to the bond required to
be executed by the master commissioner , which bond
shall be preserved by the clerk , and reported to the
court at its next ensuing term .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substi-

568 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tuted in place of subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 376.110 , and
shall thereafter be treated as an amendment to sub-
section ( 2 ) of KRS 376.110 rather than amendment
to Section 2476 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 6th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 126
(H. B. 218 )
AN ACT relating to quarterly courts.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 1 of Chapter 47 of the Acts of 1940
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The quarterly court shall be presided over
by the county judge or by the person designated by
the county judge as county judge pro tem , as pro-
vided by law , to act in the absence of the county
judge or when the county judge is unable , for some
reason , to perform the duties imposed upon him as
judge of the quarterly court . At least four terms of
the quarterly court shall be held in each county each
year , at intervals of three months and upon such
days as the county judge may fix by order entered
upon the order book of said court . The county judge
may by an order so entered provide for the holding
of monthly terms , or for continuous sessions , but
such order must be made at a regular term of said
court and not to take effect until sixty days there-
after . At each term the court shall remain in session
as long as the business requires it.
( 2 ) The quarterly court shall be held in the
courthouse , except that in counties of less than
150,000 population and containing a city of the sec-
ond class where the terms of the circuit courts are

CHAPTER 127 569
held in the city of the second class as required by
law and the county does not own , operate or main-
tain a courthouse in a city of the second class , the
quarterly court shall be held in the city of the second
class as well as at the county seat .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substi-
tuted in place of KRS 25.450 , and thereafter be
treated as an amendment to KRS 25.450 rather than
an amendment to 1940 , c . 47 , § 1 .
Approved 6th March , 1942
CHAPTER 127
(S. B. 186 )
AN ACT authorizing the pledging of certain securities by
depositories of public funds to secure such funds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In addition to and under the same conditions
as the securities authorized by Section 4693 of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition to be
pledged with the State Treasurer to secure deposits
of public funds , there may be pledged , when
approved by the County Debt Commission for such
purposes , bonds voted and issued by counties of the
Commonwealth pursuant to Section 157a of the
Constitution of Kentucky , and bonds issued to
refund or to renew same . Only bonds of a county in
which the levy of the tax authorized by Section 157a
of the Constitution would have produced during the
last fiscal year 5% more revenue than the amount
required annually to meet all principal and interest

570 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
maturities when due may be approved for such pur-
pose by the County Debt Commission , and it may ,
in its sound discretion, refuse to approve any such
bonds of any such county for such purpose if it is not
convinced that the public funds of the Common-
wealth will be adequately protected thereby . The
County Debt Commission shall annually , on or about
September 1 , prepare a list of all such bonds that
may be pledged with the State Treasurer to secure
deposits of public funds , and the bonds included in
such list shall continue to be eligible for such pur-
pose until removed or until a new list is prepared .
Such lists shall be public records , and shall be filed
with the Secretary of State ; and the State Treasurer
shall distribute copies thereof to the state deposi-
tories . Bonds so approved for such pledge shall be
identified as the said Commission shall direct , which
identification shall include the name of the issuing
county , the rate of interest which the bonds bear ,
and the date of issue . The County Debt Commission
may at any time remove any bonds from said list by
notice to all state depositories or by exclusion from
the next annual list , in which latter event the list
shall constitute notice ; and any bond so removed
shall not be accepted to secure state deposits thirty
days after notice of removal from the approved list .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , the reference in section one of this Act to " sec-
tion 4693 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936
edition " shall be treated as a reference
"KRS 41.240 ."
to
§ 3. WHEREAS , because of the current finan-
cial stringency and the practically insurmountable
difficulties now encountered by state depository
banks in securing depository bonds , a flight of cap-
ital from the state , to the irreparable injury of agri-

CHAPTER 128 571
culture and industry is imminent , an emergency is
declared to exist and this bill shall take effect upon
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved 11th day of March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 128
(S. B. 196 )
AN ACT authorizing the Fiscal Court of any county to open,
establish or alter any public road or structure , providing
for the posting of certified copies of the Fiscal Court's order ,
and providing for appeals by persons aggrieved to the Cir-
cuit Court and to the Court of Appeals .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Whenever the Fiscal Court of any
county deems it to be in the best interest of such
county to open , establish or alter the location of
any public road, street , alley, ditch, culvert , bridge
or similar public way or structure in such county ,
said fiscal court shall adopt a resolution setting
forth the necessity for such public road or structure ,
and thereupon such public road or structure shall be
deemed opened , established or altered , as the case
may be , on behalf of the county . A certified copy of
said resolution or order shall be posted at the court-
house door of the county within five days after its
adoption and a certified copy of said resolution shall
be posted by the County Road Engineer of the county
along or at the proposed road or structure within
five days after its adoption.
Section 2. In all cases where such public roads
or structures have been established , any person or
persons aggrieved thereby may prosecute an appeal
from such order or resolution of the fiscal court by
filing a petition in equity in the circuit court of the

572 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
county setting forth his grievance , to which petition
shall be attached an attested or certified copy of such
order or resolution . Such petition shall be filed
within thirty-five days from the date such order or
resolution was entered . Such appeal shall be heard
and decided by the court without the intervention of
a jury . Any party so appealing shall execute and
file a bond for costs at the time such appeal is taken .
An appeal to the court of appeals will lie from the
judgment of the circuit court if taken within sixty
days after the date of the entry of the judgment in
the circuit court.
Approved 11th day of March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 129
(H. B. 195 )
AN ACT fixing limitations on revocations , countermands of
payments and stop payment orders relating to the payment
of checks or drafts against bank accounts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. No revocation , countermand or stop-
payment order relating to the payment of any check
or draft against an account of a depositor in any
bank or trust company doing business in this state
shall remain in effect for more than six months after
the service thereof on the bank, unless the same be
renewed , which renewals shall be in writing and
which renewals shall be in effect for not more than
six months from date of service thereof on the bank
or trust company , but such renewals may be made
from time to time ; provided , however , that before
the depositor shall be compelled to renew his revoca-
tion , countermand , or stop -payment order , the bank

CHAPTER 130 573
shall ten days prior to the expiration of the six-
months period notify said depositor by mail of the
expiration date of the six -months period .
Section 2. No notice affecting a check upon
which revocation , countermand or stop - payment
order has been made at the time of the taking effect
of this act shall be deemed to continue for a period
of more than six months thereafter ; provided , how-
ever , that before the depositor shall be compelled to
renew his revocation , countermand , or stop - payment
order , the bank shall ten days prior to the expira-
tion of the six-months period notify said depositor
by mail of the expiration date of the six -months
period .
Approved March 11 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 130
(S. B. 174 )
AN ACT providing for special committees to receive funds
from public agencies which disburse funds for the mainte-
nance and support of indigent persons.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The circuit and county courts shall have
jurisdiction within their respective counties , with-
out impanelling a jury , to appoint a special com-
mittee , to receive funds from any state , Federal , city
or county agency , which disburses funds for the
maintenance and support of indigent persons , for
any person who is a resident of their respective
counties , or for any person who is confined in any of
the charitable institutions in their respective coun-
ties , except that no such special committee shall be
appointed under this Act to receive funds disbursed

574 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
by the United States through the Veterans Admin-
istration or its successors .
§ 2. Such appointment shall be made only after
a verified statement has been filed by a reputable
citizen of the county , showing that the person is of
unsound mind or by reason of alcoholism , drug
addiction or from any other cause is so imbecile or
of such unsound mind as to render him incompetent
to receive , manage and disburse for his maintenance
and support , funds which may be allotted to him by
the state , Federal , city or county agency . The peti-
tion must be supported by the affidavit of some reg-
ular practicing physician showing that said person
is incompetent as alleged therein .
§ 3. Such special committee shall execute a bond
with surety approved by the court , but the court may
in its discretion , with the written consent of the
agency or agencies furnishing the maintenance or
support , where the amount to be received by said
special committee shall not exceed $ 200 per year ,
allow the special committee to qualify without surety
on the bond .
§ 4. The special committee shall have authority
to receive funds from the state , Federal , city or
county agency , which furnishes funds for the main-
tenance and support of indigent persons , and shall
receive and disburse them for the maintenance and
support of his ward , and shall report to the court
each year , showing the receipts and disbursements
of all funds received by him from any of said
Sources .
§ 5. The total compensation of the special com-
mittee shall not exceed $3.00 per year . The cost of
settlement shall not exceed the sum of $1.00 for
recording it, and $ 1.00 to the county judge or exam-
iner for examining and approving it.

CHAPTER 131 575
§ 6. Laws applying to committees generally, if
inconsistent with this Act, shall not apply to special
committees appointed under this section .
Approved March 11 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 131
(S. B. 182 )
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1 ) Before any person's name shall
be placed upon the ballot at any primary or regu-
lar election as a candidate for the office of county
tax commissioner , he shall hold a certificate from the
Department of Revenue that he has been examined
by it and that he is qualified for the office . During
the month of January 1945 and every four years
thereafter , the Department of Revenue shall pre-
pare a list of questions to be used in the examina-
tion of applicants for a certificate . Such questions
shall be formulated so as to fairly test the ability
of the applicant to serve as county tax commis-
sioner . The Department of Revenue shall hold the
examination on the second Monday in March and ,
if there is only one person qualified to be a candi-
date in the county ; on the second Monday in June
beginning at 9 A. M. , at some convenient place
in at least one county seat town in each Congres-
sional District in the State . The Department of
Revenue shall advise the county Attorney of each
county of the time and place of each examination
and the county attorney shall post a notice thereof
in a conspicuous place in the courthouse three

576 ACTS OF THE , GENERAL ASSEMBLY
weeks before such examination . Any person de-
siring to take such examination shall appear at
the time and place designated . The applicant shall
answer all questions in his own writing , and he
shall be barred from further participation and his
papers shall not be graded if he receives any as-
sistance in the examination .
(2 ) Any person giving out information in regard
to the questions for the examination provided for
in subsection ( 1 ) of this section or any person ob-
taining possession of them illegally shall be fined
not more than five hundred dollars .
( 3 ) Whenever necessary to effectuate the pro-
visions of this section , the Department of Revenue
may hold a special examination for applicants seek-
ing a certificate for the office of county tax com-
missioner . Such special examination shall be held
in the same manner as regular examinations .
(4 ) The Department of Revenue shall grade all
papers and shall issue , by May 1 to all persons pass-
ing this examination with a grade of seventy -five
per cent (75 % ), a certificate of their fitness and
qualification to fill the office of county tax commis-
sioner.
(5 ) All examination questions and answers made
by applicants on the examination shall be preserved
by the Department for twelve ( 12 ) consecutive
months after the examination , and they shall be
available to the inspection of any person who took
the examination who may request to see them at
the office of the Department .
(6 ) No person shall be eligible for the office
of county tax commissioner in any election held
in 1945 or in any year thereafter unless he holds
a certificate issued to him by the Department of
Revenue in accordance with the provisions of this

CHAPTER 131 577
section . Any certificate issued in 1945 or in any
year thereafter shall cease to be valid after the
holder has failed to serve as county tax commis-
sioner for five ( 5 ) consecutive years following the
date the examination was held .
Section 2. Each county tax commissioner may
appoint any persons approved by the Department
of Revenue as necessary and as having reasonable
qualifications as deputies to assist him to discharge
his duties . Each deputy must be more than twenty-
one ( 21 ) years of age and may be removed at the
pleasure of the county tax commissioner
Section 3. Before entering upon the duties of his
office , the county tax commissioner shall execute a
bond conditioned upon the faithful performance of
the duties of his office in the sum of two thousand
( $2,000 ) dollars and with a surety to be approved
by the Department of Revenue .
Section 4. The fiscal court of each county shall
provide for the county tax commissioner a suitable
office room or rooms in the county courthouse , or
when that is not possible in some other building
at the county seat , together with suitable furni-
ture . In that office shall be safely kept the books ,
maps , taxpayers ' lists , papers and all other records
pertaining to the assessment of property within the
county , except when such books or records are re-
quired by law to be placed in the custody of other
officers . The provisions of section 1197 of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , shall apply
to these public records .
The county tax commissioner shall keep his office
open daily during regular hours at the county seat
for the purpose of assessing property except Sun-
days and legal holidays from the first day of July
until the Board of Supervisors adjourns the fol-
Acts- 19

578 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
lowing year , except that in counties for which ex-
penses for deputy hire are expressly provided the
office shall be kept open throughout the year .
Section 5. The fiscal court of each county shall
have jurisdiction and the power to purchase and
supply to the county tax commissioner any maps ,
lists , charts , materials , supplies , equipment or in-
struments which are reasonably necessary for a
complete and accurate assessment of property in
the county . The Department of Revenue is au-
thorized to purchase and loan any county tax com-
missioner such maps , lists , charts , materials , sup-
plies , equipment or instruments as are urgently
needed by any county tax commissioner , provided
that the Department of Revenue keeps a record
thereof .
The fiscal court of any county shall provide for
the maintenance of all maps , lists , charts , mate-
rials , supplies , equipment or instruments owned by
a county or supplied to it by the Department of
Revenue or by any source in cooperation with the
Department of Revenue for the purpose of facili-
tating the assessment of property .
Section 6. Each tax commissioner , from his
knowledge , from information in property schedules
and from such other evidence as he may be able
to obtain , shall assess all the real and personal
property in his county at its fair cash value and
the property of one person shall not be assessed
wilfully or intentionally at a lower or higher rela-
tive value than the same class of property of an-
other . Any grossly discriminatory valuation shall
be construed as an intentional discrimination . It
shall be his duty personally or through a deputy
to see in person or call at the residence , usual
place of abode or business place of all taxpayers

CHAPTER 131 579
who have not listed their property or whose prop-
erty is not well known to him to secure the assess-
ment lists and to view the property when practi-
cable to do so .
If the county tax commissioner assesses any
property , except stocks and bonds at the market
value listed in recognized publications , at a greater
value than that listed by the taxpayer , the tax
commissioner shall serve notice on the taxpayer
of such increase before the beginning of the in-
spection period . The notice may be given by reg-
istered mail or as provided in the Civil Code of
Practice .
Any person who fails to supply the county tax
commissioner with a complete list of his property
and the value thereon in accordance with the re-
quirements of the schedule prescribed for the list-
ing of property within five days after the tax com-
missioner has given the taxpayer written notice
by registered letter to list, shall be subject to a
fine of not exceeding fifty dollars ( $ 50 ) , and may
be proceeded against as provided in Section 4061 ,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition .
When the tax commissioner gives a notice by
registered mail he shall secure a return receipt ,
The tax commissioner shall be allowed twenty
cents ( 20c ) for each notice given by registered mail
upon presentation of the return receipt to the De-
partment of Revenue from the appropriation from
which county tax commissioners are paid .
Any taxpayer may designate on his assessment
schedule any property which he does not consider
to be subject to taxation and it shall be the duty
of the tax commissioner to obtain and follow advice
from the Department of Revenue relative to the
taxability of such property .

580 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Any taxpayer who has a reasonable excuse for
not listing his property before the county tax com-
missioner or his deputy may appear before any
person authorized to administer an oath and make
the necessary affidavit required by the schedule
as to the value of his property and may then re-
turn the schedule to the county tax commissioner .
This shall be a compliance with the law in his par-
ticular case .
Section 7. All taxable property and all interest
in taxable property shall be assessed and valued
as of July 1 of each year unless otherwise pro-
vided by law . It shall be the duty of all persons
owning or having any interest in any real estate
situated in this state or owning or having any in-
terest in any personal property having a taxable
situs in this state to list or have listed between July
1 and September 1 of each year such property with
the county tax commissioner of the county where
it is located , except as otherwise provided by law.
All persons in whose name property is properly
assessed shall remain bound for the tax , notwith-
standing they may have sold or parted with it .
Real property or any interest therein shall be
listed in the county or district where it is located ,
by the owner of the first freehold estate therein ,
and it shall be assessed without reference to con-
flicting title . If the owner fails to list the property ,
the county tax commissioner shall list it . The
county tax commissioner may swear witnesses in
order to ascertain the person in whose name to
make the list .
Real property shall be assessed in the name of
the owner , if ascertainable by the tax commissioner ,
otherwise in the name of the occupant , if ascer-
tainable , and otherwise to " Unknown owner . " The

CHAPTER 131 581
undivided real estate of any deceased person may
be assessed to the heirs of such person without des-
ignating them by name .
Section 8. The assessor or his deputies shall read
and administer to every person listing property the
following oath : "You swear that the list of tax-
able property given by you contains a full and com-
plete list of all of your property and of all the
property in your possession which is not otherwise
listed as of the first day of July last , and that a fair
value has been placed on all such property required
to be valued . "
Section 9. Any informality or irregularity in the
making of an assessment or the tax bills shall not
vitiate the same , and the failure of the tax com-
missioner , or of anyone required to perform serv-
ices relative to the assessment , to call on each tax-
payer for an assessment list , to notify the taxpayer
to list or of an increase of assessment , to finish the
assessments or other duties within the times pro-
vided by law shall not make the assessment void .
Provided , however , that nothing in this section shall
be construed to release the tax commissioner or
anyone else from performing his duties in accord-
ance with the requirements of the law. Even
though the assessing officer may not have complied
literally with all the provisions of the statutes no
taxpayer shall be entitled to a final abatement of
the taxes by injunction or otherwise on any part
of a final assessment unless the taxpayer shows
that the assessment was excessive or discrimina-
tory or that the property was not taxable .
Section 10. Whenever it shall appear satisfac-
torily to the Department of Revenue upon com-
plaint made by the owners of not less than ten per
cent ( 10 % ) of the taxable property in the assess-

582 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ment district or upon the investigation of the De-
partment of Revenue or for any other just reason
that the assessment of property or any class of
property in such district is not in substantial com-
pliance with the law and that the interest of the
public necessitates a reassessment thereof , the De-
partment of Revenue shall have authority to order
a reassessment of all or any part of the taxable
property in such district to be made by one or more
persons appointed for that purpose by the said De-
partment . The order directing such reassessment
shall state the reasons therefor and a copy shall be
filed in the office of the clerk of the county court
where the property lies . No reassessment shall be
ordered after the fifteenth ( 15 ) day of February
following the assessment date . Any person ap-
pointed to make such a reassessment shall have the
same powers and duties as the tax commissioner .
Whenever the tax roll has been completed under a
reassessment and the tentative valuations have
been determined , the Department shall cause
to be published in the newspaper having the
largest bona fide circulation in the county a notice
as to the date when the tax roll will be ready
for inspection and the time available for such pur-
pose ; also a copy of the notice shall be posted on the
bulletin board of the courthouse . The tax roll shall
be delivered in such event to the county court clerk
by not later than the first ( 1 ) day of June and the
Board of Supervisors shall convene to hear any
appeals from such reassessment . Appeals shall be
taken and heard from such reassessments in the
same manner as appeals from regular assessments .
Section 11. Each county court clerk shall , on or
before the first day of August of each year , make
and certify to the county tax commissioner a com-
plete list of all conveyances made during the twelve

CHAPTER 131 583
months before the preceding July , showing the
names of the parties , description and location of
the property , the date of conveyances and the con-
sideration , for which service he shall be allowed a
reasonable compensation by the fiscal court . The
list shall be returned by the tax commissioner with
his tax books and schedules to the county court
clerk for the use of the Board of Supervisors .
Section 12. The Department of Revenue shall
recover from any county tax commissioner all com-
pensation paid to him for assessments that were
unauthorized or excessive when and to the extent
it is determined by a final order of the Board of
Supervisors , Kentucky Tax Commission or a court
of competent jurisdiction that such assessments
were unauthorized or excessive . Whenever the
county tax commissioner fails to render the services
required of him or he performs any of his duties.
in such a manner as to fail to comply substantially
with the requirements of the law , he shall be re-
quired to pay a sum that will reasonably compen-
sate the Commonwealth of Kentucky for its costs.
in rendering the duties required to be performed
by the county tax commissioner . The Department
of Revenue shall notify the county tax commis-
sioner by registered mail of any amount charged
to be due under this section and a statement of the
reasons therefor . The county tax commissioner
shall be entitled to a hearing before the Kentucky
Tax Commission , and an appeal may be taken from
the final action of the Kentucky Tax Commission
to the courts as provided by law .
Any sum that may become due from any tax
commissioner by reason of this section may be de-
ducted from any amount that the Commonwealth of
Kentucky may become obliged to pay such county

584 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tax commissioner , or it may be collected from the
bondsmen of the tax commissioner .
Section 13. The county tax commissioner shall
complete the tentative assessment of all the prop-
erty in his district before December 1 of each year
in accordance with the law governing the prepara-
tion of the tax roll and on or before that date he
shall file a recapitulation on blanks to be forwarded
by the Department of Revenue of all property as-
sessed on said tax roll with the Department of Rev-
enue with his official certificate attached thereto .
The county tax commissioner shall also file at the
same time the schedules containing a list of the
intangible personal property with the Department
of Revenue . The Department of Revenue shall ex-
amine the intangible schedules and the reapitula-
tion and compare them with such data as it may
obtain from any source to determine whether the
tentative assessments conform to law . Within four
(4 ) weeks after receiving the recapitulation and
schedules , the Department of Revenue shall ad-
vise the tax commissioner of the changes that are
necessary to correct the assessment , and it shall re-
turn to the tax commissioner such schedules as , in
its opinion , are necessary to be corrected by reason
of the undervaluation of any property or for any
other reasons . After the tax commissioner has
made the necessary correction in assessment and
records thereof , he shall return such intangible
schedules to the Department of Revenue , and it shall
preserve such schedules for a period of twelve ( 12 )
years from the assessment date .
Section 14. The county tax commissioner shall
have his tax roll revised and completed in accord-
ance with the recommendation of the Department
of Revenue by January 15 and a tentative valua-

CHAPTER 131 585
tion specified therein for the property of each tax-
payer . This tax roll shall be open for inspection
from January 15 to February 1 in the county tax
commissioner's office . Provided , however , in case
of necessity the Department of Revenue may order
a reasonable extension of time for the inspection
period of the tax roll before the final assessment
of the county tax commissioner , or it may order
that the inspection period be at a different time
than that provided herein . In the latter case it
shall be the duty of the tax commissioner to pub-
lish a notice as to the time of the inspection period
in the newspaper having the largest bona fide cir-
culation in the county at least three ( 3 ) days be-
fore the beginning of such period ; in any other
instance such notice may be so published with the
approval of the fiscal court . The cost of any such
notice shall be paid by the fiscal court of the county .
Such notice shall also be posted on the bulletin
board of the courthouse . Any county tax com-
missioner who , during the inspection period , adds
any property to the assessment roll or raises the
valuation of any assessment shall notify the tax-
payer as provided in section 6 of this Act at least
two (2 ) days before the expiration of the inspec-
tion period .
Section 15. As soon as the tax commissioner has
completed his work and the tax rolls have been
available in his office for inspection for fifteen days ,
such tax roll shall be delivered by the tax commis-
sioner to the county court clerk who shall make
and record a recapitulation ( second recap ) of the
tax commissioner's books in accordance with his
final assessments . The clerk shall file the recapitu-
lation with the Department of Revenue within five
(5 ) days after the books are received by him . For
making and recording this recapitulation the county

586 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
clerk shall be allowed ten cents a line for each line
across the page , including the last total value .
Section 16. Any taxpayer aggrieved by an as-
sessment made by the tax commissioner may appeal
to the Board of Supervisors . A letter or petition
stating the reasons for appeal shall be filed with
the county court clerk within five ( 5 ) days after
the Board convenes . The Board shall hold a public
hearing for all appeals , and after hearing all the
evidence , it shall make such changes as are neces-
sary to make appealed assessments valid . The
Board may on its own initiative increase an as-
sessment or assess unlisted property but in either
instance it shall give notice to the owner if known ,
otherwise , to the occupant , and the tax commis-
sioner or his deputy shall without further com-
pensation serve the notice in the manner in which
summons are required by the Civil Code of Prac-
tice to be served by the sheriff . The Board of
Supervisors shall have powed to issue subpoenas ,
compel the attendance of witnesses , administer
oaths , and adopt rules and regulations concerning
the conduct of its business . Any taxpayer feeling
himself aggrieved by the action of the Board of
Supervisors may within thirty days after its ad-
journment appeal to the Circuit Court or the Ken-
tucky Tax Commission by filing a statement of the
grounds for his appeal and a certified copy of the
action of the Board of Supervisors . Upon taking
such appeal to the Circuit Court the taxpayer shall
give notice in the manner provided by the Civil
Code of Practice to the Department of Revenue and
the County Attorney . The tax commissioner , the
county judge , the Department of Revenue , the chief
finance officer of any city using the county assess-
ment , if the city elects so to do , or the superintend-
ent of any school district in which property is lo-

CHAPTER 131 587
cated that was reviewel by the Board of Super-
visors may appeal to the Circuit Court or the Ken-
tucky Tax Commission in the same manner as a
taxpayer . The Court or tax commission shall hear
the evidence de novo and fix a value upon the prop-
erty which shall be equitable with assessments de-
termined for other property by the county tax com-
missioner or the Board of Supervisors . Any of the
parties may appeal from the judgment of the cir-
cuit court to the Kentucky Court of Appeals in the
manner provided by the Code of Civil Practice for
appeals from judgments of the circuit courts or from
the Kentucky Tax Commission to the Franklin
Circuit Court as provided by law .
It shall be the duty of the county attorney to rep-
resent the tax commissioner in all hearings before
the Board of Supervisors or appeals from its de-
cisions .
Section 17. ( 1 ) The county Board of Super-
visors shall be composed of three (3 ) reputable
property owners residing in the county . The county
judge shall appoint annually at the January term
the three members of the Board and he shall des-
ignate one of them to serve as chairman . A major-
ity of said Board shall have authority to determine
the action of the Board and make decisions there-
for . The county court clerk shall make three copies
of the order of appointment and deliver them to
the sheriff who shall deliver a copy to each super-
visor before February 1 and make due return
thereof to the clerk . A vacancy resulting from any
cause shall be filled by appointment made by the
county judge .
( 2 ) The supervisors , before entering upon the
discharge of their duties shall take the following
oath : " You swear that you will to the best of your
ability discharge the duties required of you and

588 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
that you will not assess intentionally any property
at a higher or lower relative value than the assess-
ment of other property of the same class in the
county . "
( 3 ) The failure of any member to be in attend-
ance promptly on the days fixed for the sessions of
the Board of Supervisors without reasonable ex-
cuse shall subject him to a fine of not exceeding
twenty -five dollars ( $ 25.00 ) .
Section 18. The Board of Supervisors shall con-
vene at the county seat on the first Monday in Feb-
ruary . Except , that no meeting shall be held until
the tax roll has been completed and the assessments
of the county tax commissioner have become final
in accordance with law. At the time of the meet-
ing , the county court clerk shall deliver to the Board
the tax commissioner's books .
The Board of Supervisors shall continue in ses-
sion until all appeals are heard and decided and its
other duties have been performed ; except that it
shall not continue in session more than fifteen ( 15 )
days after it convenes unless in a county containing
a city of the first , second or third class or an ex-
tension of time is authorized by the Department of
Revenue . Each member of the Board of Supervi-
sors in a county containing a city of the first or sec-
ond class shall be allowed ten dollars ( $ 10.00 ) for
each day of actual attendance of the meetings of
the Board . Each supervisor in all other counties
shall be paid five dollars ($5.00 ) for each day he
serves . This compensation shall be paid one -half
out of the county levy and the other half out of the
State Treasury .
Section 19. The county court clerk shall act as
clerk of the Board of Supervisors , and he shall
keep an accurate record of the proceedings and

CHAPTER 131 589
orders of the Board . The record shall show the
name of the owner of the property , the description ,
the class of property , the amount with which the
county tax commissioner assessed the property and
the amount of the assessment made by the Board of
Supervisors . It shall be filed in the office of the
clerk and a certified copy submitted to the Depart-
ment of Revenue within fifteen ( 15 ) days after the
adjournment of the Board . The clerk shall also
report as a part of this record the net increase or
decrease in assessed valuation for each class of
property considered by the Board . The clerk shall
be allowed three dollars ( $3.00 ) per day for his
services , and he shall be paid in the same manner
as members of the Boards of Supervisors are paid .
Provided , that the clerk shall be allowed not ex-
ceeding five (5 ) days after adjournment of the
Board of Supervisors for the correction of the tax
books . The clerk shall be entitled to the payment
of fifteen cents ( 15c ) for the preparation of each
schedule of omitted intangibles assessed by the
Board with reference to an appeal to it.
Section 20. The sheriff shall be entitled to a fee
of twenty -five cents ( 25c ) for serving a subpoena
for the Board of Supervisors . He shall also have
a fee of twenty-five cents ( 25c ) for serving a sub-
poena or notice for the Kentucky Tax Commission
regarding any proceeding for the assessment of
property subject to local taxation . Said fees shall
be paid out of the county levy .
Section 21. The county tax commissioner shall
attend all hearings before the county Board of Su-
pervisors and before the Kentucky Tax Commis-
sion and submit to examination and fully disclose
to the Board of Supervisors such information as
he may have relative to his assessment and any

590 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
other matters pertinent to the inquiry being made .
He shall receive three dollars ( $3.00 ) per diem for
these additional duties , which compensation ' shall
be paid in the same manner that members of the
Board of Supervisors are paid . He shall also be
entitled to reimbursement from the county for ex-
penses incurred in official business outside of his
county . If the Kentucky Tax Commission directs
him to perform official duties outside of his county ,
the expenses shall be paid by the Department of
Revenue from the appropriation for the payment of
the salaries of the county tax commissioners . Such
reimbursement shall be paid on the same basis as
employees of the Commonwealth are paid for travel
expenses . In any year during which the term of
the county tax commissioner expires , it shall be his
duty to attend the hearings of the Board of Super-
visors and of the Kentucky Tax Commission con-
cerning any assessment made by him and furnish
them the information above required .
Section 22. On or before February 1 of each year ,
each county court clerk shall file with the Depart-
ment of Revenue in accordance with regulations and
forms prescribed by the Department for that pur-
pose a list of voluntary sales of real estate as shown
by the deed books of his county for the preceding
calendar year .
The report of each sale shall contain the name
of the grantor and grantee , a description of the
property , evidence of the price paid , which is ac-
ceptable to the Department of Revenue , the final
equalized assessment of the property as of July 1
of the year before that covered by the report , the
date the deed was executed and such other informa-
tion prescribed on the form used for the report . The
county court clerk shall be paid out of the State
Treasury at the rate of five cents (5c ) for each

CHAPTER 131 591
conveyance for which the required information is
submitted .
If any clerk fails to file with the Department of
Revenue on or before February 1 in any year the
list of sales as provided in this section , he shall
pay a penalty of five dollars ( $ 5.00 ) for each day's
delay after February 1 , which may be deducted by
the Commonwealth from such sum as may be due or
become due the clerk for official duties .
Section 23. The Kentucky Tax Commission shall
equalize each year the assessments of the property
among the counties . It shall compare the recapitu-
lation of the county tax commissioner's books from
each county with the records of sales of land in such
county or with such other information that it may
obtain from any source and shall determine the
ratio of the assessed valuation of the property to
the fair cash value . The Kentucky Tax Commis-
sion shall have power to increase or decrease the
aggregate assessed valuation of the property of any
county or taxing district thereof or any class of
property or any item in any class of property .
The Kentucky Tax Commission shall fix the assess-
ment of all property at its fair cash value . When
the property in any county , or any class of property
in any county , is not assessed at its fair cash value ,
such assessment shall be increased or decreased
to its fair cash value by fixing the percentage of
increase or decrease necessary to effect the equali-
zation .
Section 24. When it is contemplated by the Ken-
tucky Tax Commission that it will be necessary to
raise the assessed valuation of property in any
county , it shall give notice of the contemplated ac-
tion to the county court, the superintendent of any
school district affected by such action , the mayor

592 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of any city which is affected and which has adopted
the assessment and to the taxpayers of that county
through the county judge who shall post the notice.
sent him on the courthouse door and certify that
this has been done to the Department of Revenue
and it shall fix a time and place for a hearing which
may be in Frankfort or any convenient place in or
nearer the county seat . If the property of an
individual owner is singled out for an increase ,
the owner shall be notified by the Department of
Revenue by registered mail .
Section 25. Any city may elect by an appropriate
order of its legislative body to use the annual as-
sessment of the county tax commissioner for prop-
erty situated within such city as a basis of ad valo-
rem tax levies ordered or approved by the legisla-
tive body of the city . In such event the assessment
of the county tax commissioner as of any July 1
shall serve as a basis of all city levies for the first
fiscal year commencing after the assessment date .
Any city which elects to use the county tax commis
sioner's assessment shall have access to the assess-
ment records as soon as completed and may obtain
a certified copy of that portion of the records which
represents the assessment of property within such
city by paying the reasonable cost thereof , and he
shall be subject to all provisions of the law that
apply to tax commissioners for a county .
Section 26. No tax rate for any county or school
district levy to be based upon the assessment of
property required to be equalized by the Kentucky
Tax Commission shall be determined before the
assessment of property for the county in which such
district lies is certified by the Kentucky Tax Com-
mission to the county court clerk as provided in
section 4128a - 2 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936
edition .

CHAPTER 131 593
Section 27. Each sheriff or collector shall
make his settlement with the Department of Rev-
enue for all taxes collected for the Commonwealth
on or before June the tenth of the second year of
his term and of each year thereafter . Provided ,
however , that any outgoing sheriff or collector who
does not qualify as special tax collector shall make
his settlement on or before January the tenth fol-
lowing the expiration of his term. Any person who
qualifies as special tax collector shall make his set-
tlement by June the tenth following the receipt of
tax bills . In the event that any sheriff or collector
resigns , dies or otherwise vacates his office , the
settlement shall be made within thirty ( 30 ) days
from the date that the office is vacated . No sheriff
or collector shall be required to make his annual
settlement with the Department of Revenue within
less than five ( 5 ) months from the time that the
annual tax bills are delivered to him , except as pro-
vided in this section . Any sheriff or collector who
shall fail to make the settlement as required by
law shall be subject to indictment in the county
of his residence and fined not less than one hundred
dollars ($ 100 ) nor more than five hundred dollars
( $500 ) .
Section 28. Whenever an encumbrance , by reason
of a tax claim exists on land which is divided both
as to ownership and area into two or more tracts ,
any person or persons owning any of such tracts
may make application , upon ten days ' notice given
to the owners of the other tracts and the county
attorney , to the county court of the county for an
apportionment of the assessment and said court is
authorized to make an apportionment of the amount
of the encumbrance among the owners of each tract
according to the value of their respective interest
as shown by the proof introduced by them . Any

594 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
owner of a tract for which the tax claim was ap-
portioned may have the encumbrance on his prop-
erty released therefrom by paying to the officer or
person to whom such taxes are payable , his pro
rata share thereof as ascertained by the judgment
of apportionment . Said judgment shall be final
unless an appeal therefrom to the circuit court ,
which is hereby given appellate jurisdiction , is pros-
ecuted within sixty ( 60 ) days from rendition of
such judgment . Appeal may be taken to the Court
of Appeals of Kentucky if instituted within thirty
( 30 ) days after the judgment of the circuit court .
Section 29. Whenever the Commonwealth of
Kentucky prosecutes an action in a circuit court
pursuant to section 4149b -7 of Baldwin's 1940 Sup-
plement , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , to enforce
a certificate of delinquency or an action to invali-
date a land tax sale and enforce a lien for taxes
pursuant to section 4154-1 of Baldwin's 1940 Sup-
plement , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , such court
shall have authority to assess property which has
been omitted for any reason , whenever necessary
to establish the total personal liability of any de-
fendant in such action or to establish the total
amount of any lien or liens against the property .
Provided , however , that neither the twenty per cent
(20% ) penalty provided in section 4260-1 and sec-
tion 4263 of Baldwin's 1938 Supplement , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , nor the fifteen per cent ( 15 % )
administrative fee provided in section 4154-1 and
section 4154-2 of Baldwin's 1940 Supplement , Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , shall apply to the amount
of taxes , penalties and interest due for any assess-
ment made pursuant to this section for any omission
which was caused through no fault of the person
owning the property on the assessment date .

CHAPTER 131 595
Section 30. Any person while serving as county
attorney who was required by law by reason of his
office to prosecute an action or to assist the Com-
missioner of Revenue in prosecuting an action to
enforce a claim of the State , county school district
and any other taxing district to any land which was
purchased by such districts at a sheriff's sale or
sales for delinquent taxes and who did not institutę
such action before he relinquished his office or other-
wise failed to perform substantially all the duties
of his office relative to the claim , shall not be en-
titled to receive any commission or compensation
for any such sale or sales when the redemption
costs are paid . Any county court clerk or other
person authorized to collect funds to satisfy unre-
deemed land sales shall be liable for any such money
distributed as a commission to any former county
attorney who is not entitled to it .
Section 31. Sections 4024 , 4025 , 4042a -3 , 4042a -4,
4042a - 11 , 4042a -12 , 4042a - 13 , 4042a - 14 , 4042a - 15 ,
4047 , 4049 , 4052 , 4053 , 4065 , 41141-14 , 4114i - 15 ,
4114i- 16 , 4114i - 17 , 4115 , 4116 , 4117 , 4118 , 4119 , 4120 ,
4121 , 4121-1 , 4121-2 , 4121-3 , 4122 , 4123 , 4124 , 4125 ,
4127 , 4128 , 4128a - 1 , 4240 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition and sections 4034 and 4042a - 10
of Baldwin's 1940 Supplement , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , and all acts and parts of acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly of Kentucky in conflict herewith to
the extent of such conflict , are hereby repealed .
Provided , however , that said sections and any sec-
tion of the Kentucky Statutes or any act of the
General Assembly or part thereof hereby repealed
or amended , either specifically or by implication ,
shall continue in full force and effect with respect
to the determination of the assessment as of July 1 ,
1941 .

CHAPTER 132 .
( S. B. 155 )
AN ACT to amend Section 3662a - 3 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition .

598 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Section 3662a -3 of Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1936 Edition , be amended to read
as follows :
Section 3662a -3. Defense may be made to the pe-
tition by any voter of the town , and if a defense is
made the court shall hear and determine the same
and may render judgment dissolving and annulling
the charter . The pleadings and practice , except as
herein provided , shall be the same as in equity
cases ; an appeal shall be from the judgment , pro-
vided the record is filed in the clerk's office of the
Court of Appeals twenty days prior to the second
term of the Court of Appeals after the rendition of
the judgment.
Whenever the charter of any town is dissolved in
the manner hereinabove provided , the judgment
shall direct the clerk of the circuit court wherein
such judgment is rendered , to certify a copy of said
judgment not later than ten days thereafter to the
Secretary of State whose duty it shall be to proper-
ly index and file the same as a permanent record in
his office .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of subsection ( 3 ) of KRS 88.020
and shall thereafter be treated as an amendment to
subsection (3 ) of KRS 88.020 rather than an
amendment to section 3662a -3 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 133 599
CHAPTER 133 .
(S. B. 154 )
AN ACT to amend Section 3715 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 3715 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , be amended to read as fol-
lows :
On the second day of the term the court shall , if
the proper notice has been given or publication
made , and no defense is interposed , enter a judg-
ment establishing a town in accordance with the
prayer of the petition , naming it and describing the
boundary thereof by metes and bounds , and ap-
pointing therefor not less than five trustees , a police
judge, marshal and assessor , who shall hold their
respective offices until the next election at which
officers for cities and towns may be elected . Defense
may be made to the petition by any inhabitant of the
proposed town , and if defense is made , the court
shall hear and determine the same , and render a
judgment establishing or refusing to establish a
town , as may seem proper . But if the court shall
be satisfied that the population within the pre-
scribed boundary is sufficient , and the proper notice
or publication has been made or given , it shall have
no discretion as to the establishment of the town .
The pleadings and practice , except as herein pro-
vided , shall be the same as in equity causes , but no
appeal shall lie from the judgment .
Whenever any town shall be established in the
manner above provided , the court shall in the judg-
ment direct the clerk of the court wherein such
judgment is entered to , not later than ten days
therafter , certify a copy thereof to the Secretary of

600 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
State , whose duty it shall be to properly index and
file the same as a permanent record in his office .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substi-
tuted in place of KRS 81.060 , and shall thereafter
be treated as an amendment to KRS 81.060 rather
than an amendment to § 3715 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 134 .
(S. B. 147 )
AN ACT relating to county judges .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$§ 1. Section 1072a of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
The regular county judge in every county is en-
titled to a vacation of two weeks in each year and in
counties having a circuit court of continuous session
the county judge is entitled to a vacation of one
month in each year . During his vacation the com-
pensation of the pro tem judge shall not be deducted
from the salary of the regular judge . The salary of
the pro tem judge shall be paid by the county in the
same manner and at the same rate per diem as that
of the regular judge .
$§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 25.270 , and shall thereafter
be treated as an amendment to KRS 25.270 rather .
than an amendment to § 1072a of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 135 601
CHAPTER 135 .
( S. B. 144 )
AN ACT relating to prostitution , lewdness and assignation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. That the term " prostitution " as
used in this Act shall be construed to include the
giving or receiving of the body for sexual inter-
course for hire , and shall also be construed to in-
clude the giving or receiving of the body for indis-
criminate sexual intercourse without hire . That the
term " lewdness " shall be construed to include any
indecent or obscene act . That the term " assigna-
tion " shall be construed to include the making of
any appointment or engagement for prostitution or
lewdness or any act in furtherance of such appoint-
ment or engagement .
SECTION 2. It shall be unlawful to engage in
prostitution or to aid or abet prostitution or to pro-
cure or solicit any person for the purposes of pros-
titution , or to keep or set up a house of

SECTION 3. Any person violating any pro-

or confined in the county jail for not more than one

Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 136 .
(S. B. 143 )
AN ACT relating to hours of labor on public work .
WHEREAS , our nation is now engaged in a conflict which
threatens its very existence ; a conflict which demands the
maximum of speed and efficiency in the construction and
maintenance of facilities of transportation ; and
WHEREAS , during this emergency, construction of these
defense facilities is greatly impeded by the requirement of
the eight hour day , and the forty hour week , and the pros-
ecution of our all - out war effort demands uninterrupted
construction of defense and other highways ; and
WHEREAS , the President of these United States has called
upon all industry to work twenty -four hours a day , seven
days a week , to produce the articles and facilities necessary
to defeat the enemies of Democracy ; and
WHEREAS , it is now unlawful to work more than eight hours
a day or forty hours a week on the construction of National
Defense Highways and Projects in Kentucky , now there-
fore , in order that we , in Kentucky , may do our full part in
this program ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 137 603
Section 1. Section 5 of Chapter 104 of the Acts
of 1940 of the Regular General Assembly of the
Commonwealth shall be suspended from the effec-
tive date of this Act until the President of the
United States shall declare the National Emergency
at an end insofar as it aplies to the construction or
repair of any road or highway which may be desig-
nated by the Commissioner of Highways as neces-
sary and incident to the National Defense .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , the number " KRS 337.540 " shall be sub-
stituted for " Section 5 of Chapter 104 of the Acts
of 1940 " and Section 1 of this Act .
Section 3. This Act shall remain in force until
the President of the United States shall declare
the National Emergency at an end .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 137 .
( S. B. 141 )
AN ACT revising and establishing provisions for the sale of
real property of infants and persons of unsound mind ; and
repealing and amending inconsistent provisions of the Code
of Civil Practice and Kentucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 489 of the Civil Code of Prac-
tice is repealed and the following is enacted in lieu
thereof :
Section 489. Sale of Real Estate of Infants and
persons of unsound mind .
1. Purposes of Sale . The real property or any
right , title or interest in real property of a person
under the age of twenty-one years , or of a person of

604 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
unsound mind , may be sold under or by decree of a
court of equity of the county in which the property
or any part there of is situated for any purpose
deemed by the chancellor to be necessary or proper
or beneficial to such person under disability .
2. Parties . The decree may be rendered on a
petition , counter -claim or cross petition filed by the
statutory guardian of an infant or by the committee
of a person of unsound mind , or filed by any party
against such guardian or committee , in any kind of
similar proceeding that may be maintained against
a person who is not under disability . It shall not be
necessary for the person under disability to be made
a party personally .
If such infant or person of unsound mind has no
guardian or committee , the county court having
jurisdiction to appoint such fiduciary may , upon
motion of any interested person , appoint a tem-
porary guardian or committee for such person
under disability for the purpose of representing
him in the suit . The motion shall fully disclose the
purpose of the appointment ; otherwise , the pro-
ceeding shall be in accordance with that prescribed
for the appointment and qualification of a perma-
nent guardian or committee . The temporary guar-
dian or committee so appointed shall execute a bond
to the Commonwealth for the use and benefit of the
person under disability , with good surety, approved
by the court, faithfully to discharge his trust . The
bond shall be of an amount equal to the approximate
value of the property sought to be sold . An attested
copy of such bond shall be filed with the pleading .
Such temporary guardian or committee shall have
and exercise the same rights and duties in respect
to the proceeding to sell the real property as a regu-
lar guardian , committee or curator . The circuit

CHAPTER 137 605
court shall allow him reasonable compensation , com-
mensurate with his services , to be taxed as costs of
the suit .
3. Process . Summons and notices in the suit
may be served upon such guardian or committee or
temporary guardian or committee , and same shall
constitute service upon the person under disability
and have the same effect as if such process had been
served on a party who is sui juris .
Provided : If the suit be filed by or against a tem-
porary guardian notice thereof and a copy of the
pleading seeking the sale shall be served ( a ) upon
the minor if he is over fourteen years of age ;
(b ) upon the father or mother or person in loco
parentis as is provided in Section 52 of the Civil
Code of Practice if the minor is fourteen years of
age or younger . If the suit be filed by or against
a temporary committee notice thereof and a copy
of the pleading shall be served ( a ) upon the person
of unsound mind if he is not confined in an institu-
tion for the insane or feeble minded and is not
under treatment of a physician who certifies that in
his opinion the service would be injurious to such
person ; (b ) if he has been so committed or is under
such care of a physician , then upon the superin-
tendent of the institution in which he is confined , or
upon such physician , and also , in either condition ,
upon the husband or wife , or the next of kin of such
lunatic residing in the county of his residence . Any
such person , as the natural guardian or representa-
tive of the person under disability , may become a
party to the suit .
4. Substance of the pleading . The pleading
seeking a sale shall file the title papers , or copies
thereof, under which the property is held ; shall
particularly describe the property and the interest

606 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
therein of the person under disability ; set forth
fully the reasons or purposes for which it is sought
to have the same sold ; and the nature and extent of
all liens upon the interest of the person , if any , so
far as the same may be known to the petitioner . The
pleading shall designate the natural guardian or
representative described in subsection 3 hereof,
upon whom notice shall be served . The pleading
shall be verified as required by Sections 116 and 117
of this Code .
5. Proof required . The allegations of the plead-
ing shall not be taken as confessed but shall be
proved by competent evidence heard orally by the
chancellor or on deposition . A transcript thereof
shall be filed as part of the record .
6. (a ) Discharge of temporary guardian or
committee . The circuit court may discharge a tem-
porary guardian or committee if it appears he is
not faithfully discharging his trust , or that the
interests of the person under disability are not
being protected , and appoint another , who shall
execute a similar bond in the circuit court with the
same effect as if originally executed .
(b) Approval of bond . Before entering judg-
ment of sale the circuit court shall approve or dis-
approve his bond by an order . If he disapproves it
he shall require the execution of a satisfactory
bond , which shall be retroactive in effect . Failure to
execute the same within a time fixed by the court
shall be cause for dismissal of the guardian .
(c) Payment of proceeds -Bond . None of the
proceeds of any sale shall be paid over to a tem-
porary guardian or committee . Same may be paid
to a guardian or committee provided that he shall
execute in the circuit court a bond with surety to the
Commonwealth for the use and benefit of the per-

CHAPTER 137 607
son under disability that he will faithfully discharge
his trust and will account for, pay and deliver to
said person all money and other property due or
belonging to him when he shall reach his majority ,
or if he be a person of unsound mind when he shall
be legally restored , or to his heirs or personal rep-
resentative , or in compliance with an order of any
court of competent jurisdiction . The court shall en-
dorse its approval on said bond , which shall be
recorded on the order book .
(d) Court to protect rights . The court shall at
all times enter such orders as will conserve and pro-
tect the rights of the person under disability .
7. Judgment — Guardian or Committee may make
sale . Subject to the above conditions , judgment
may be rendered and executed in the same manner
and on the same terms as judgments in similar cases
where no person under disability is a party .
Provided , that ( a ) the court may in its discretion
authorize the permanent guardian or committee of
such person to make said sale at public auction upon
such terms and conditions as the court may order ,
and to convey title thereto ; or ( b ) if the land or in-
terest to be sold be of an appraised value of less
than $ 1,000 , the court may , in its discretion , au-
thorize such fiduciary to make the sale privately
and convey title ; subject , however , to the approval
of the chancellor before consummation .
.
8. Private sale-Approval of Court . The guard-
ian of an infant or the committee of a person of
unsound mind may sell privately and convey any
interest in real estate owned by a ward for such
reasons and purposes and upon such terms and con-
ditions as he shall deem best for the interest of said
ward . Provided , however , that no such sale shall be
consummated until same shall have been approved

608 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
by the judge of the circuit court in the county where
the land or a portion thereof is situated in a suit in
equity filed by the guardian or committee against
said infant or person of unsound mind . The petition
shall set forth the reasons and purposes of the sale
and the terms thereof .
A guardian ad litem shall be appointed for the
infant or person of unsound mind . Proof shall be
taken only on notice to said guardian ad litem . It
may be taken by depositions or orally before the
chancellor and thereafter transcribed and filed in
the record of the case . The guardian ad litem shall
make a defense or file a report stating that after
careful examination of the case he is unable to make
a defense .
If the chancellor shall deem the sale advisable and
the terms and price satisfactory , he shall enter an
order approving the same : provided , however , that
the judgment shall not be entered until the guardian
or committee shall have executed a bond , with ap-
proved surety, in an amount not less than the pur-
chase price of the land , providing that he will faith-
fully discharge all his duties as such guardian or
committee and will comply with the judgment and
orders of the court, and account for , pay and de-
liver the proceeds of the sale or property in which
it shall be invested to his ward or other person en-
titled thereto . The bond shall be approved by the
court and recorded in the order book .
This proceeding may be tried and judgment ren-
dered in accordance with the provisions of Section
964b - 1 , Kentucky Statutes .
9. Proceeding not void-exception . No defect or
error in the proceeding shall render the judgment
or sale void unless the same is of the nature and
character that would render the proceeding void if
all parties were sui juris .

CHAPTER 137 609
10. Exclusive procedures . It is the intent of
this section to provide exclusive procedures for the
sale and conveyance of real property of persons
under disability except where same may be author-
ized by the terms of a will , deed or trust under which
title is held .
Section 2. Section 491 of the Civil Code of Prac-
tice is hereby amended so that as amended it shall
be as follows :
Sec . 491. Reversion or remainder may be sold .
In an equitable action by the owner of a particular
estate of freehold in possesion , or by a guardian or
committee as provided in Section 489 , against the
owner of the reversion or remainder , though he be
an infant or of unsound mind, and against the owner
of the particular estate if he be an infant or of un-
sound mind ; or , if the remainder be contingent ,
against the person , if in being , in whom it would
have vested if the contingency had happened before
commencement of the action , though he be an infant
or of unsound mind, and against the owner of the
particular estate if he be an infant or of unsound
mind-real property may be sold for investment
of the proceeds in other real property .
The court ordering the sale shall, by its commis-
sioner , retain the custody and control of the fund
realized by the sale until the same is reinvested in
real estate , or in such other property as the funds of
persons under disability may be invested in author-
ity of law , and the court shall order the money to
be paid , by its commissioner , directly to the per-
son from whom the purchase for reinvestment is
made ; or the court may authorize payment of the
proceeds of sale of land of an infant or person of
unsound mind to his guardian or committee as pro-
vided in Section 489 , subsection 6 ( c ) .
Acts- 20

610 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 3. Section 497 of the Civil Code of
Practice is hereby repealed and re -enacted so that
same shall be:
Sec . 497. Payment of purchase money .
1. Lien on land . Where the real property or
any interest therein of an infant or person of un-
sound mind shall be sold under the provisions of
section 489 , a lien shall remain on the land to secure
the payment of his share of the proceeds until same
shall be paid .
2. Collection of interest . The court may permit
the guardian or the committee to receive the in-
terest accruing on the unpaid purchase money with-
out executing the bond provided in Section 489 , sub-
section 6 (c ) .
3. Collection of proceeds . The infant upon be-
coming of age , or the person of unsound mind upon
his restoration to sound mind, or the guardian or
committee upon executing bond as provided in sec-
tion 489 , subsection 6 ( c ) , may collect and receive
the proceeds as same shall become due .
4. Purchaser may pay bonds . The purchaser
upon maturity of any bond or portion of the pur-
chase price may pay the amount due into court . If
the guardian or the committee does not execute
bond as provided in section 489 , subsection 6 ( c ) so
that same may be paid over to him , the court shall
direct the investment of such proceeds by the mas-
ter commissioner or receiver of the court , under
supervision of the court , until the guardian or the
committee shall qualify to receive the same, or the
infant or person of unsound mind shall become com-
petent to receive the same personally .
Sec . 4. Paragraphs 2 and 3 of Section 53 , Sec-
tions 489 , 492 , 493 , par . 5 of 494 , 497 , 498 and 574 of
the Civil Code of Practice , and Sections 2150 and

CHAPTER 138 611
2150a , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , and all other
laws or parts of laws in conflict with this act are
hereby repealed . Provided , however , that this act
shall not affect any suit or proceeding instituted
before it goes into effect . Any pending suit may
proceed to completion in accordance with the exist-
ing law under which it was brought .
Section 5. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , this Act shall be treated as repealing KRS
387.240 and subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 387.140 , regard-
less of the date of enactment or taking effect of the

Section 964b - 1 of Carroll's Statutes in subsection 8
of section 489 of the Civil Code shall be treated as
