CHAPTER 138 .
(S. B. 132 )
AN ACT to amend and reenact Section No. 2739-31 of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , or Section 352.250 ,
Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 2739-31 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , be repealed and reenacted
so that when repealed and reenacted it will read as
follows :
" 2739-31 . EXPLOSIVES , MAGAZINES ,
WHERE TO BE LOCATED . All distributing ex-
plosive magazines used for quantities of explosives
estimated as a daily supply shall be located not less
than one hundred feet from any mine opening or

612 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
building used or occupied by any person or persons ;
and the outside construction of such magazine shall
be of non -combustible material .
" All magazines used for storing powder or other
explosives in greater quantities than an estimated.
daily supply shall be located not less than five hun-
dred feet from any mine opening or building used
or occupied by any person or persons ; and the out-
side construction of such magazines shall be of non-
combustible material , provided , however , this shall
not apply to distributing or storage magazines al-
ready constructed prior to the passage of this act .
"Where Cardox or other permissible breaking
devices of a similar nature are manufactured at or
near the mine , the chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals shall have the authority to prescribe
the location and procedure of such installation . "
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall, upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 352.250 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 352.250
rather than an amendment to $ 2739-31 .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 139 .
(S. B. 130 )
AN ACT relating to reports made by the Auditor of Public
Accounts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$$ 4618-135 and 4636-4 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , are consolidated and amend-
ed to read as follows :

CHAPTER 139 613
§ 1. ( 1 ) Immediately upon completion of each
audit and investigation , except those provided for
in § 4636-1 , the Auditor shall prepare a report of his
findings and recommendations . He shall furnish one
copy of the report to the head of the agency to which
the report pertains , one copy to the Governor , one
copy to the Commissioner of Finance , and one copy
to the State Librarian . He shall prepare and trans-
mit to each member of the General Assembly , by
December 15 immediately preceding the convening
of each regular session of the General Assembly, a
printed report on his activities , summarizing the
findings and recommendations in his report on each
audit or investigation made since the last preceding
biennial report to the General Assembly , and pre-
senting such other findings and recommendations as
he sees fit to make . He shall file a copy of this re-
port with the Governor and five copies with the
State Librarian . All audit reports shall be public
documents to which taxpayers shall have access .
(2) The Auditor shall , within a reasonable time
after the examination of each county as provided
in § 4636-1 , make a written report to the Governor ,
the State Librarian and to the county judge of the
county examined , calling attention in specific terms
to any mismanagement , misconduct , misapplication
or illegal appropriation or extravagant use of
money received or disbursed by any officer of the
county examined .
(3) The reports required to be filed with the
State Librarian by this section shall be kept in the
Legislative and Law Library for the use of the
public .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking

614 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 43.090 , and shall thereafter
be treated as an amendment to KRS 43.090 rather
than an amendment to $$ 4618-135 and 4636-4 , and
the number " KRS 43.070 " shall be substituted for
§ 4636-1 in section one of this Act .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 140 .
(S. B. 129 )
AN ACT to amend Section 1162 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , relating to taking from a
church , chapel, meeting - house , schoolhouse , courthouse , or
any other public building , robbery in a dwelling -house ,
tent , fair, market , breaking and entering a dwelling - house
or any part thereof or outhouse belonging to or used with
the dwelling -house .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 1162 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision is amended to read
as follows :
If any person shall feloniously take out of or from
any church , chapel , or meeting -house , schoolhouse ,
courthouse , or other public building , any goods or
chattels , or other thing of value belonging thereto ;
or shall rob any person in his dwelling -house or
place , or in any booth or tent any fair or market , he ,
his wife , children or servants or other persons then
being within ; or shall feloniously break any dwell-
ing house or any part thereof , or any outhouse be-
longing to or used with any dwelling -house , with
intent to steal , or shall feloniously take therefrom
or destroy any goods , wares or other thing of value

CHAPTER 141 615
although the owner or any person may not be there ,
he shall be confined in the penitentiary for not less
than two or more than ten years .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking

tuted in place of KRS 433.180 , and thereafter be
treated as an amendment to KRS 433.180 rather
than an amendment to § 1162 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 141 .
(S. B. 128 )
AN ACT amending and re - enacting Section 1649b - 3 of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revision , relating to
the inadmissibility of confessions obtained by sweating .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 1649b - 3 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's Revision of 1930 , be and the
same is amended and re -enacted , and as so amended
and re -enacted shall read as follows :
Section 1649b -3 . Not admissible . No confession
obtained by means of sweating , as defined herein ,
shall be permitted as evidence in any court in this
state , but same shall be deemed to have been ob-
tained by duress , if it be shown that such confession
was made after the arrest of the party charged
with crime and while he was in custody of the law.
The trial judge shall determine the competency and
admissibility of any alleged confession under the
provisions of this statute from evidence heard by
him , independent of and without the hearing of the
jury trying the case .

616 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall stand repealed as
of the effective date of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , and the following shall take effect in lieu
thereof :
A confession obtained by methods prohibited by
Section one is not admissible as evidence of guilt in
any court . The trial judge shall determine the com-
petency and admissibility of any alleged confession
under the provisions of this section from evidence
heard by him , independent of and without hearing
the hearing of the jury trying the case .
The provision so substituted by this section shall
thereafter be treated as an amendment to subsec-
tion ( 2 ) of KRS 422.110 rather than an amendment
to § 1649b -3 of Carroll's Statutes , and subsec-
tion ( 1 ) of KRS 422.110 shall remain in full force
and effect and shall not be treated as affected by
this Act. Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 142
( S. B. 126 )
AN ACT to repeal and re - enact Section 126 of the Criminal
Code of Practice , Baldwin's Revision of 1932 , relating to the
manner of charging an offense in an indictment .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 126 of the Criminal Code of Prac-
tice , Baldwin's Revision of 1932 , be repealed and
re -enacted , and as re - enacted shall be as follows :
Section 126. INDICTMENT TO CHARGE BUT
ONE OFFENSE ; DEFECTS , VARIANCES AND
AMENDMENT .

CHAPTER 142 617
1. An indictment , except in the cases mentioned
in Section 127 , much charge , but one offense , but
if it may have been committed in different modes
and by different means the indictment may allege
the modes , means or results in the disjunctive or
alternative .
2. No indictment for an offense created or de-
fined by statute shall be invalid or insufficient mere-
ly for the reason that it fails to negative any ex-
ception , excuse or proviso contained in the statute
creating or defining the offense .
3. An indictment need not allege that the of-
fense was committed or the act done " feloniously " ,
or " traitorously ", or " unlawfully " , or " with force
and arms " , or " with a strong hand " , nor need it
use any phrase of like kind otherwise to charac-
terize the offense ; nor need it allege that the of-
fense was committed or the act done " burglar-
iously " , " wilfully " , " knowingly " , " maliciously " ,
or "negligently " , nor need it otherwise character-
ize the manner of the commission of the offense ,
unless such characterization is necessary to charge
the offense under section 122 .
4. No indictment shall be held invalid because
the accusatory portion charges a common law of-
fense and the descriptive portion describes a stat-
utory offense , or the converse if the offenses are
of the same nature or are commonly known by sub-
stantially the same name .
5. The court may at any time cause the indict-
ment to be amended in respect of any defect , im-
perfection or omission in the matter of form only.
Approved 11th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 144
(S. B. 82 )
AN ACT relating to the powers and duties of the Department
of Revenue .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The Department of Revenue may elect
coverage for the employees of any divisions or sec-
tions thereof under the Workmen's Compensation
Law of this Commonwealth .
Section 2. Any field representative of the De-
partment of Revenue who is authorized to collect
taxes or money due the Commonwealth may de-
posit to his special account as field representative
of the Department any money so collected in a
State or National bank in this Commonwealth .
He shall within forty - eight (48 ) hours after mak-
ing such deposits draw a check or checks payable
to the State Treasurer for the full amount of the
deposit and mail same to the Department or trans-
mit same in a manner approved by the Depart-

CHAPTER 145 619
ment . Nothing in this section shall be construed
as authorizing any field representative of the De-
partment to enforce or cash , even for the purpose
of a deposit , any check or other instrument of value
payable to the Commonwealth or any agency there-
of.
Deposits shall be made in such banks as the De-
partment may by regulation designate , and sub-
ject to such further conditions as the Department
may prescribe . Any reasonable service charges
made by the bank may be paid by the Department
from its appropriation as other claims against it
are paid .
Section 3. Income tax returns shall be kept for
seven (7 ) years ; primary accounting records of tax
payments , seven ( 7 ) years ; and copies of motor
vehicle registration receipts , seven ( 7 ) years .
Records of the Department which are not required
by this section or other statutory provisions to be
preserved for a fixed period may be kept or dis-
posed of according to the discretion of the De-
partment . Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 145
( S. B. 80 )
AN ACT to amend section 3720b -3 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1 . Section 3720b -3 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as fol-
lows :
" An unqualified order or promise to pay is un-
conditional within the meaning of this Act , though
coupled with :

620 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(1 ) An indication of a particular fund out of
which reimbursement is to be made , or a particu-
lar account to be debited with the amount ; or
(2 ) A statement of the transaction which gives
rise to the instrument .
An order or promise to pay out of a particular
fund is not unconditional , except that in all cases
where any county , city or taxing district of this
Commenwealth , or where any instrumentality or
agency of the Commonwealth , or of any political
subdivision or taxing unit thereof , has issued or
may hereafter issue pursuant to proper authority
duly granted , securities which, by such authority ,
or by the resolution or ordinance providing for the
same, are limited, as to the payment of the princi-
pal and interest thereof , to the proceeds of the
collection of special assessments , tolls , rents , spe-
cial taxes , earnings , or other limited sources of
revenue , or where such principal and interest are ,

terest by unlimited taxing power .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-

CHAPTER 146 621
bly, section one of this Act shall , upon the taking

stituted in place of KRS 356.003 , and shall there-

rather than an amendment to § 3720b - 3 of Car-

CHAPTER 146
(S. B. 79 )
AN ACT authorizing taxing agencies or instrumentalities to
comply with the provisions of Chapter IX of the Federal
Bankruptcy Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1 .
Any taxing agency or instrumentality as defined
in Chapter IX of the Federal Bankruptcy Act as
amended by the Acts of Congress of August 16 , 1937 ,

4, 1940 , Chapter 41 , June 28 , 1940 , Chapter 438 and

time , is hereby authorized to file a petition for the

cations shall be made in the plan of composition
after the filing of the petition without the ap-

622 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
proval of the State Local Finance Officer . The
State Local Finance Officer shall approve or disap-
prove the proposed plan of composition or any
changes or modifications thereof under the same
procedure and for the same reasons as bonds are
approved or disapproved by him as is provided in
the County Debt Act of 1938 and amendments
thereto .
Section 2 .
If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are enacted
at the present session of the General Assembly, the
numbers " KRS 66.280 to 66.390 " shall be substi-
tuted for the words and figures " the County Debt
Act of 1938 and amendments thereto " in section
one of this Act .
Approved 11th March , 1942
CHAPTER 147
(S. B. 59 )
AN ACT relating to jails and jailers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 2229 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Each jailer shall have the custody , rule and
charge of the jail in his county and of all persons
in the jail and shall keep the same himself or by
his deputy or deputies . He or one of his deputies
shall reside not more than four hundred yards from
the jail and where it is such as admits the resi-
dence of the same therein he or one of his deputies
shall reside in the jail .

CHAPTER 148 623
( 2) A violation of this section or of $ 2227 or
§ 2228 shall be deemed a misfeasance in office and
the County Court may fine the jailer therefor , or
he may be indicted and fined at the discretion of
a jury .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , subsection ( 1 ) of section one of this Act shall ,
upon the taking effect of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , be substituted in place of KRS 71.020 ,
and shall thereafter be treated as an amendment
to KRS 71.020 rather than an amendment to § 2229
of Carroll's Statutes ; subsection ( 2 ) of section one
of this Act shall then stand repealed and KRS
71.990 and 441.990 shall remain in full force and
effect .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 148
(H. B. 378 )
AN ACT relating to finances and revenue of cities of the
second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 1 of Chapter 169 of the Acts of the
1940 General Assembly is amended to read as fol-
lows :
( 1 ) Each city of the second class shall have a
board of equalization , to consist of three persons
selected by the city legislative body . No person
shall be appointed a member of the board of equal-
ization unless he is at the time a housekeeper and
owner of real estate in the city , and has been a res-
ident thereof for at least five years next preceding

624 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
his appointment . The board shall be paid such
compensation as is fixed by ordinance . The mem-
bers of the board shall take oath faithfully to dis-
charge their duties , and shall elect from among
themselves a chairman and a clerk .
( 2 ) The board may cause the custodian of any
city records to bring the records before the board
for its inspection , and may retain them for de-
fense , if necessary , by giving its receipt to the cus-
todian . The board may also interrogate any city
official , who shall , at its request , attend the meeting
of the board and respond to all questions . Each
member of the board may administer oaths , and the
board may compel attendance of witnesses . A ma-
jority of a quorum may determine any question be-
fore the board .
( 3 ) The board of equalization of each city of the
second class shall meet on the first Monday in Jan-
uary of each year , at a suitable place to be pro-
vided by the city . After taking office and electing
a chairman and a clerk , the board shall notify the
auditor that it is ready to receive the assessment
books , and the auditor shall then deliver them and
take the receipt of the board therefor .
( 4) The board shall compare the real estate
assessments with the plat books in the autditor's
office and see that every parcel of real estate in the
city has been assessed . If it finds that any real
estate has been omitted , it shall certify that fact to
the city solicitor , giving the number of parcels
omitted , and the city solicitor shall enforce against
the assessor the penalty provided for in § 3177 of
Carroll's Statutes . The board shall assess the par-
cels omitted .
(5 ) The board shall hear and determine all com-
plaints made against assessments . It shall in-
crease or decrease assessments on like property in

CHAPTER 148 625
order to make all assessments uniform , or to place
a true value on the property . It may , by a resolu-
tion or order signed by at least two members , in-
crease or decrease all assessments of real estate
uniformly by adding or subtracting a percentage
of the assessments , and need not give notice of such
an increase except by publication in the official
newspaper of the city . No increase of an assess-
ment shall be made , except a uniform increase as
above provided , without notice to the person whose
assessment is to be increased .
(6) Where an assessment has been made against
the property of a person who had actual notice to
appear and list his property or make statements
thereof but failed to do so , the board of equaliza-
tion shall not decrease , but may increase , the as-
sessment .
(7) The board shall remain in session as long as
the business requires , but not for more than four
weeks , unless the city legislative body , for cause
stated , extends the time . The assessor shall be in
constant attendance on the board and shall furnish
all information possible .
(8) The board shall not change any assessment
by erasure , but shall make all changes and addi-
tions in appropriate columns provided for that pur-
pose , and shall use ink of a different color from
that used by the assessor .
( 9) When the board has completed its work it
shall prepare , under the signatures of its members.
a statement showing the gross assessments of real
property and of personal property , and the total
of both , and the increase or decrease made by the
board in the total assessment . The board shall
return the statement to the city auditor , with all
assessment books , plats and papers received from
him , and shall take his receipt therefor .

626 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 10 ) The city auditor shall carefully verify the
statement of the board of equalization and the as-
sessment books , and if they are correct he shall
certify the statement . If he finds a mistake , he shall
cause the board of equalization to meet , and to-
gether with the board shall ascertain the correct
amount . The auditor shall transmit the statement
of the board, certified by him as correct , to the city
legislative body at its first meeting in March , as
a basis for the annual levy ordinance .
( 11 ) Any taxpayer whose assessment has been
raised or whose assessment upon his complaint has
not , after proper showing , been decreased by the
board of equalization otherwise than by the uniform
increase of assessments may, within thirty days
after the final adjournment of the board , appeal
to the quarterly court by filing with the clerk of that
court a copy of the action of the board, certified by
the clerk of the board . The clerk of the board shall
furnish the taxpayer, on demand , with such a cer-
tified copy of the action of the board as is necessary
for the appeal . Either party may appeal from the
quarterly court to the circuit court and thence to
the Court of Appeals as in other civil cases . Ap-
peals to the quarterly court or circuit court shall
be tried without the intervention of a jury , and in
case of appeals by the taxpayer the burden shall
be upon the taxpayer to show that his property has
been inequitably assessed . The cost of the ap-
peals shall be adjudged against the unsuccessful
party .
(12 ) Any person who refuses to attend or to be
sworn when called as a witness by the board of
equalization of a city of the second class , or re-
fuses to answer any question , shall be subject to
the penalties provided in § 3179 of Carroll's Stat-
utes for a like refusal to the assessor .

CHAPTER 149 627
§ 2. Section 3182 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition , is repealed .
$ 3. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , then , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of sec-
tion one of this Act shall be substituted in place of
KRS 92.240 ; subsections ( 3 ) to ( 11 ) , inclusive , of
section one of this Act shall be substituted in place
of KRS 92.440 ; subsection ( 12 ) of section one of
this Act shall be substituted in place of subsection
(4) of KRS 92.990 ; the words and figures " subsec-
tion ( 2 ) of KRS 92.990 " shall be substituted for
"§ 3177 of Carroll's Statutes " in subsection ( 4 )
of section one of this Act ; the words and figures
" subsection ( 6 ) of this section " shall be substi-
tuted for " § 3179 of Carroll's Statutes ' in subsec-
tion ( 12 ) of section one of this Act ; and this Act
shall thereafter be treated as amending the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes rather than Carroll's Stat-
utes .
$ 4. WHEREAS , there is doubt as to the con-
stitutionality of portions of section one of Chapter
169 of the Acts of 1940 , and the provisions of said
section will be used and applied in governing pro-
ceedings before the board of equalization of cities
of the second class before the expiration of ninety
days following the closing of the present legislative
session , an emergency is declared to exist and this
Act shall take effect upon its passage and approval
by the Governor .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 149
( S. B. 58)
AN ACT relating to holidays and providing for suspending
the observance of all holidays , other than Independence

628 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Day , Labor Day , and Christmas , during the continuation of
the present war , and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. The observance of all holidays other than In-
dependence Day , Labor Day , and Christmas Day
shall be suspended during the continuation of hostil-
ities in the present war with Japan , Germany , Italy ,
and any other enemies of the United States who
may become involved therein .
2. The observance of holidays now provided for
by law shall be renewed after the cessation of such
hostilities ; and upon the apparent permanency of
such cessation of hostilities , the Governor of the
Commonwealth shall issue a proclamation so de-
claring ; and thereupon the holidays now provided
by law shall again be observed .
3. Because of the urgent necessity for the con-
tinuous application of everyone to the existing war
effort of the United States , an emergency is de-
clared to exist and this act shall take effect on and
after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 150
(S. B. 56)
AN ACT relating to chattel mortgages .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 2 of Chapter 34 of the Acts of 1940
is amended to read as follows :
A recorded chattel mortgage shall be invalid as
against creditors , purchasers , junior mortgagees ,
other lienors and encumbrancers and third parties

CHAPTER 151 629
after the expiration of a period of seven years from
the time the chattel mortgage is filed as provided
in § 523b -8 , or from the time of filing of a statement
as provided in this section , unless before the ex-
piration of such term the mortgagee , or someone
in his behalf, files a statement , in the office of the
county clerk of the county in which the property
is then located , containing the names of the parties
to the mortgage , the time and place where the orig-
inal is filed , and the amount then due thereon for
principal and interest , in which case the lien of the
mortgage shall be extended for seven years from
the date of filing the statement . The county clerk
may charge a fee of one dollar for filing such state-
ment and shall enter it in his " Chattel Mortgage
Minute Book " as in case of a chattel mortgage .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 382.720 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 382.720
rather than an amendment to 1940 , c. 34 , § 2 , and
the number " KRS 382.670 " shall be substituted for
"§ 523b -8 " in section one of this Act .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 151
(H. B. 374 )
AN ACT relating to banks and trust companies .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Any national bank having the capital stock re-
quired by the laws of this state for a combined bank

and trust company located in the city in which such

CHAPTER 152
(H. B. 358)
AN ACT relating to property rights of husband and wife ;
amending sections 2129 , 2131 and 2145 , of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 2131 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
When a husband has been adjudged a lunatic by
a court of competent jurisdiction , the wife , by judg-
ment of a court of equity , may be empowered to sell
and convey by her own deed , or to mortgage by ap-
propriate instrument , any of her real estate freed
as to it and its proceeds from any claim of her
husband .
2. Section 2145 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
Whenever a married woman has become a con-
firmed lunatic , the circuit court of the county in
which is situated land belonging to the husband of
such married woman may , upon the petition of her
husband , adjudge the sale and conveyance , or the
mortgage , of the inchoate right of dower of the
married woman . The wife and her committee , if
she has one , shall be made defendants to the action ;
if she has no committee , the court shall appoint an
attorney to defend for her, to whom the court shall
make a reasonable allowance to be paid by the hus-
band . A description of the land shall be given in

CHAPTER 152 631
the petition and the husband's evidence of title
filed therewith . If the court is satisfied by the proof
that the wife is a confirmed lunatic , it may adjudge
the sale and conveyance , or mortgage , of her in-
choate right of dower in said land, and if she has
a committee , the court may direct that he unite
with the husband in the deed or mortgage ; if she
has no committee , the court shall appoint a com-
missioner who shall unite with husband in the deed
or mortgage . Before any judgment pursuant to
this section shall be rendered , the husband , with at
least two good sureties , shall execute before the
court a covenant to the Commonwealth for the ben-
efit of the wife , to be approved by the court , that
she will be paid the value of her right of dower in
the land should such right thereafter become com-
plete .
$ 3. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , sections one and two of this Act shall , upon
the taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
be substituted , respectively , in place of KRS 404.050
and 392.140 , and shall thereafter be treated as
amending KRS 404.050 and 392.140 rather than an
amendment to § § 2131 and 2145 of Carroll's Stat-
utes .
Section 4. Section 2129 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as fol-
lows :
A married woman may sell , convey or encumber
any of her lands and chattels real , but such sale ,
conveyance or incumbrance shall not bar the hus-
band's right to curtesy unless he joins in the in-
strument of sale , conveyance or incumbrance or re-
leases his right to curtesy by separate instrument .
Section 5. Section 506 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , is repealed .

632 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 6. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section four of this Act shall , upon the tak-ing effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
404.030 , and thereafter be treated as an amendment
to subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 404.030 rather than an
amendment to Section 2129 of Carroll's Statutes .
Section 7. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section five of this Act shall be treated as
repealing KRS 382.050 , regardless of the date of
enactment or taking effect of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 154
(H. B. 348)
AN ACT relating to insurance policies , contracts, bonds or
other evidences of insurance transactions with respect to
which the Commissioner of Highways has the power of ap-
proval or the power to award contracts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
No insurance policy , contract , bond or other evi-
dence of insurance required to be filed with and

CHAPTER 154 633
approved by the Commissioner of Highways , and
no insurance policy , contract , bond or other evi-
dence of insurance incident or pertaining to an
operation under a contract awarded by the Com-
missioner of Highways , shall be issued or delivered
in this state unless such insurance policy , bond ,
contract or other evidence of insurance shall be
countersigned by a duly licensed resident local
agent of Kentucky , and when the premium is paid
for any such insurance policy , bond , contract or
other evidence of insurance , the full commission for
same shall be collected by such countersigning
agent and it shall be unlawful for him to divide com-
mission with anyone except a duly licensed resident
local agent of this state .
Any insurance company violating any of the pro-
visions of this Act shall upon conviction be fined
not less than $ 1,000.00 , nor more than $5,000.00 , in
the discretion of the jury , and upon such conviction
its license to do business in this state shall be can-
celled and not renewed for a period of two years
thereafter .
Any agent violating any of the provisions of this
Act , upon conviction , shall be fined not less than
$100.00 nor more than $500.00 , in the discretion of
the jury , and upon such conviction said person's
license as insurance agent in this state shall be re-
voked or not renewed for a period of two years
thereafter .
Exceptions . None of the provisions of this act
shall apply to assessment or cooperative associa-
tions or companies , mutual companies , reciprocal
or inter -insurance exchanges , their attorneys in
fact , or their agents , servants or employees or rep-
resentatives .
Approved March 11 , 1942 .

634 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 155
(H. B. 339 )
AN ACT relating to taxation of insurance companies.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 4229 of Baldwin's 1938 Sup-
plement of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended.
to read as follows :
Every stock insurance company , other than life ,
doing business in this state shall, on or before the
first day of March of each year , return to the Di-
rector of the Division of Insurance a statement un-
der oath of all amounts paid to the company or
its representative , whether designated as premiums
or otherwise , for insurance or services incident
thereto , on property or risks in this state during
the preceding calendar year or since the last re-
turns were made , including amounts received for
reinsurance on Kentucky risks from unauthorized
companies , and shall give the name and location of,
and the amount of premiums received by each
agent , whether designated as premium or other-
wise , and the losses paid , and shall at the same time
pay into the State Treasury a tax of two dollars
upon each one hundred dollars of such amounts
paid to the company , less amounts returned on can-
celled policies and policies not taken .
Section 2. Section 762b - 5 of Baldwin's 1938
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Every stock fire insurance company , other than
life , doing business in this state shall pay to the
Director of the Division of Insurance on or before
the first day of March of each year , for the pur-
pose of defraying the expenses authorized by Sec-
tion 726b -6 to 762b -33 of Carroll's Statutes , one-

CHAPTER 155 635
half of one percent of all amounts paid to the com-
pany or its representative , whether such payments
are designated as premiums or otherwise , for in-
surance or services incident thereto upon all prop-
erty or other risks or hazards in this state during
the previous calendar year . In computing such
amounts there shall be deducted amounts returned
on policies cancelled or not taken . All amounts so
collected shall be paid into the State Treasury .
Section 3. Section 743a - 23 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes is amended to read as follows :
All mutual companies other than life doing busi-
ness under this law shall pay into the State Treas-
ury on or before the first day of March in each year ,
a tax of two percent of all amounts paid to the com-
pany or its representative , whether designated as
premiums or otherwise , for insurance or services
incident thereto including amounts paid for mem-
bership or policy dues or fees , on property or risks
in this State during the preceding calendar year ,
including amounts received for reinsurance on Ken-
tucky risks from unauthorized companies and shall
give the name and location of and the amounts re-
ceived by each agent whether designated as premi-
ums or otherwise , and the losses paid .
In addition to the foregoing tax mutual fire com-
panies shall pay an annual tax as prescribed for
stock fire companies by Section 2 of this Act .
In computing premiums upon which tax is to be
paid there shall be deducted , in both direct and re-
insurance business , return premiums on cancelled
policies and policies not taken , and dividends paid
or credited to policyholders .
The provisions of this section shall not apply to
domestic mutual companies , cooperative or assess-
ment fire insurance companies .

636 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 4. The legislative body of each city
which is authorized to impose and collect license
fees or taxes upon insurance companies for the
privilege of engaging in the business of insurance
shall file with the Director of the Division of Insur-
ance a copy of all ordinances and amendments
thereto which impose any such license fee or tax .
Any license fee or tax imposed by a city upon any
insurance company shall be based upon a percent-
age of the premiums received by the insurance
company for insurance on persons residing or prop-
erty located within the corporate limits of the city ,
but shall be not less than five dollars a year .
A life insurance company doing business within
the corporate limits of a city shall be subject to the
city license fee or tax only on premiums received on
or by reason of new policies or new contracts of in-
surance written during the taxable year .
Section 5. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section one of this Act shall , upon the tak-
ing effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of KRS 136.340 and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 136.340
rather than amendment to Section 4229 of Car-
roll's Statutes .
Section 6. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly , section two of this Act shall , upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of KRS 136.360 and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 136.360
rather than an amendment to section 762b -5 of Car-
roll's Statutes , and the numbers " KRS 227.010 to
227.180 and 298.240 to 298.300 " shall be substituted
for Sections 762b -6-762b - 33 of Carroll's Stat-
utes " in section two of this Act .

CHAPTER 156 637
Section 7. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section three of this Act shall , upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted in place of KRS 136.350 and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 136.350
rather than an amendment to section 743a - 23 of
Carrol's Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 156
(H. B. 331 )
AN ACT to amend section 8 , Chapter 79 , Acts 1940 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. That section 8 , Chapter 79 , Acts 1940
is amended to read as follows :
It shall be the duty of all state and national banks ,
trust companies , or other persons , and courts of
this Commonwealth or the agents thereof whether
holding estates or property as bailee , depository ,
debtor , trustee , executor , liquidator , administrator ,
distributor , receiver , or in any other capacity com-
ing within the purview of section 7 of this Act , to
report annually to the Department as of July 1 , all
property held by them declared by this Act to be
presumed abandoned . The report shall be filed in
the offices of the Department on or before Septem-
ber 1 of each year for the preceding July 1 , and
shall give the name of the owner , his last known
address , the amount and kind of property , and such
other information as the Department may require
for the administration of this Act . The report shall
be made in duplicate ; the original shall be retained
by the Department , and the copy shall be mailed

638 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to the sheriff of the county where the property is
located or held . It shall be the duty of the sheriff
to post said copy on the court house door or the
court house bulletin board . The sheriff shall im-
mediately certify in writing to the Department the
date when said copy was posted . Said copy must
be posted on or before October 1 of the year when
it is made , and shall be constructive notice to all in-
terested parties and shall be in addition to any other
notice provided by statute or existing as a matter
of law . Any person who has made a report of any
estate or property presumed abandoned , as required
by this Act , shall, between November 1 and No-
vember 15 of each year , turn over to the Depart-
ment all property so reported ; but if the person
making the report or the owner of the property
shall certify to the Department by sworn statement
that any or all of the statutory conditions neces-
sary to create a presumption of abandonment no
longer exist or never did exist , or shall certify the
existence of any fact or circumstance which has a
substantial tendency to rebut such presumption ,
then , the person reporting or holding the property
shall not be required to turn the property over to
the Department except on order of court . No per-
son shall be required to surrender any property on
a presumption of abandonment to the Department
if the period of time provided by any statute of
limitation applicable to the owner's rights as against
the holder has expired unless the court orders him
to do so . If a person files an action in court claim-
ing any property which has been reported under
the provisions of this Act , the person reporting or
holding such property shall be under no duty while
any such action is pending to turn the property
over to the Department , but shall have the duty of
794

CHAPTER 157 639
notifying the Department of the pendency of such
action .
The person reporting or holding the property or
any claimant thereof shall always have the right
to a judicial determination of his rights under this
Act and nothing therein shall be construed other-
wise ; and the Commonwealth may institute an ac-
tion to recover such property as is presumed aban-
doned whether it has been reported or not and may
include in one petition all such property within the
jurisdiction of the court in which the action is
brought provided the property of different persons
is set out in separate paragraphs .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall, upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 393.110 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 393.110
rather than an amendment to section 8 of Chapter
79 of the Acts of 1940 .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 157
(H. B. 330 )
AN ACT relating to the taxation of aviation motor fuel .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section one. Tax paid pursuant to the provisions
of section 4281g - 1 et seq . of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , upon liquid fuel designed for
use in aircraft motors and used in the operation
of aircraft engaged in the transportation of per-
sons and property in interstate commerce on regu-

640 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
larly scheduled flights may be refunded . No tax
shall be refunded except that paid upon such fuel
used exclusively in such aircraft motors .
Section two . No person shall be entitled to a
refund hereunder unless he shall have first filed
with the Department of Revenue a bond with ap-
proved corporate surety in an amount of one thou-
sand dollars ( $ 1,000 ) conditioned upon faithful
compliance with this Act and upon the payment
to the Commonwealth of any refunds to which he
was not entitled .
Section three . Applications for refund shall on
or before the fifteenth of each month be made to the
Department of Revenue on forms and in the man-
ner prescribed by it for the refund of tax paid on
aviation motor fuel used during the preceding
month . Each application for a refund shall show
the number of gallons of aviation motor fuel pur-
chased during the preceding month ; the date and
quantity of each purchase ; the vendor from whom
such fuel was purchased ; the number of gallons on
which refund is claimed ; and such other informa-
tion as the Department of Revenue may require .
The application shall be made under oath .
The Department of Revenue shall audit the ap-
plication and make such other investigation as it
deems necessary to determine whether it consti-
tutes a proper claim. When the Department of Rev-
enue is satisfied that a refund is proper , it shall
authorize the amount of tax paid to be refunded as
other refunds are made and the amount refunded
shall be deducted from current gasoline tax re-
ceipts .
Section 4. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky Re-

CHAPTER 158 641
vised Statutes , the numbers " KRS 138.210 to
138.340 ' shall be substituted for " 4281g - 1 et seq'
in section one of this Act .
Approved 11th March , 1942
CHAPTER 158
(H. B. 327)
AN ACT repealing and reenacting section 2380b - 30 , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Revised Edition , relating to the
date when drainage assessments become delinquent ; and a
penalty is added thereto .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That section 2380b - 30 , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 Revised Edition , is hereby re-
pealed and reenacted so that when reenacted it
shall read as follows :
,
2380-30 .—It shall be the duty of the sheriff or
tax collector of each county in which lands or other
property of any district organized under this act
are situated , to receive the " collector's drainage
assessment book " each year , and he is hereby em-
powered and it shall be his duty to promptly and
faithfully collect the assessments which shall be-
come due and payable on the lands and other prop-
erty therein set out , and to exercise all due dili-
gence in so doing . He is further directed and or-
dered to demand and collect such assessments at the
same time that he demands and collects state and
county taxes due on the same land and other prop-
erty . Where any tract or part thereof has been
divided or sold or transferred , the sheriff shall
receive assessments on any part of any tract , piece
or parcel of land or other property charged with

CHAPTER 159
(H. B. 304 )
AN ACT relating to cigarette taxes and amending § 4281e - 6
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes, 1936 edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 4281e -6 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Where any cigarettes for sale on which a
tax is imposed herein are manufactured for ship-
ment and sale without this state , or sold by a whole-
saler , whose place of business is within this state ,
to a non -resident retailer for resale outside the
state , the manufacturer or such wholesaler mak-
ing said sale shall cancel the tax stamp in a man-
ner prescribed by the Department of Revenue .
(2 ) If cigarettes carrying tax stamps so can-
celled are sold and transported by any method to
a regular dealer , either wholesaler or retailer , in
cigarettes in another state for resale outside this
state , the seller in this state shall be entitled to a
refund of the actual amount of tax paid , if he makes
an affidavit that the goods were sold and trans-
ported and furnishes from the purchaser written
acknowledgment that he has received the goods and
as part of the acknowledgment the amount of the
stamps thereon . The refund may be in the form
of stamps of sufficient value to cover the refund ,
or if the seller so desires , the Department of Rev-

646 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
enue shall certify to the Department of Finance
the refund to which the seller is entitled , and the
Department of Finance shall draw an order upon
the State Treasurer for the refund .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall, upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 138.160 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 138.160
rather than an amendment to § 4281e -6 of Carroll's
Statutes , and " by KRS 138.140 " shall be substi-
tuted for " herein " in section one of this Act .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 161
(H. B. 258)
AN ACT relating to clay mines.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions . As used in this Act , unless the
context requires otherwise :
(a ) " Drift " means an opening through the
strata , with opening grades such as to permit clay
to be hauled by mules or mechanical traction power ,
and capable of being used for the purpose of ventilation , drainage, ingress , egress and other pur-
poses in connection with the mining of clay .
·
(b ) " Mine " means a group or system of shafts ,
slopes , drifts or inclined planes connected by onegeneral system of mine railroads over which clay
may be delivered to one or more points outside themine and connected with excavations that pene-

650 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
trate clay strata and are ventilated by one general
air current or divisions thereof .
(c) " Mine foreman " means the person whom
the operator or superintendent places in charge of
the workings of the mine and of persons employed
therein .
(d) "Operator " means an individual , firm or
corporation operating a clay mine or any part
thereof .
(e) " Shaft " means a vertical opening through
the strata that is or may be used , in connection
with the mining of clay, for the purpose of ventila-
tion or drainage , or for hoisting men , clay or ma-
terials .
(f ) " Slope " . means an inclined opening used
for the same purpose as a shaft .
(g) " Superintendent " means the person who ,
on behalf of the operator , has immediate supervi-
sion of one or more mines .
§ 2. The ventilation of all clay mines at which
as many as five men are employed shall be pro-
duced by means of fans mechanically operated ,
located inside or outside the mine . Mines at which
less than five men are employed shall be ventilated
by the same means whose specifications have been
approved by the Department of Mines and Miner-
als for coal mines .
§ 3. In case of accident to the ventilating fan ,
or its machinery whereby the ventilation of the mine
would be seriously interrupted , the mine foreman
shall order the men to withdraw immediately from
the mine and he shall not allow them to return to
their work until the ventilation has been restored
and the mine has been thoroughly examined by him ,
or by an assistant mine foreman or fire boss , and
the mine is reported safe .

CHAPTER 161 651
§ 4. The operator , superintendent or mine fore-
man of every clay mine , whether worked by shaft ,
slope or drift , shall provide and hereafter maintain
for every such mine adequate ventilation affording
no less than one hundred cubic feet of air per min-
ute for each and every person employed in the por-
tion of the mine affected thereby and two hundred
cubic feet of air for each animal working in the
part of such mine affected thereby , which shall be
circulated around the main headings and cross head-
ings and room headings and working places to an
extent that will dilute , render harmless and carry
off any noxious and dangerous gases therein .
§ 5. No operator , superintendent or mine fore-
man shall permit any persons to work where they
are unable to maintain a sufficient amount of venti-
lation , but this shall not be construed to prohibit
the operator from employing men to make the
places of employment safe to comply with this re-
quirement ; provided , further , that while the repair
work necessary to get the mine in condition to com-
ply with the law is in progress , no person or per-
sons shall be permitted to enter that part of the
mine affected except those actually employed in
doing the necessary repair work , that repair work
to be done under the constant supervision of an
official designated by the mine foreman .
§ 6. All unused workings and abandoned parts
of the mines shall be protected by such safeguards
as will prevent , so far as practicable , the accumula-
tion or overflow of gas therein , and all avenues
leading thereto shall be so arranged and conducted
as to give cautionary notice to all persons of the
danger in entering therein ; and in order to secure
safety of the workmen in general against the dan-
ger in said unused or abandoned section of the
mine , notice shall be posted warning all persons not

652 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to enter such parts of said mine , except persons
authorized to make examination of such sections
and it shall be unlawful for any person , except as
aforesaid , to enter such parts of that mine .
Section 7. Any person who violates any of the
provisions of this Act shall be fined not less than
twenty -five ($25 ) dollars nor more than two hun-
dred dollars ($ 200 ) .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 162
(H. B. 199 )
AN ACT relating to the taxation of alcoholic beverages .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 4281c - 18 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
Except as otherwise explicitly provided by law ,
it is unlawful for any person , or persons , to know-
ingly possess any alcoholic beverage not bearing
the stamp required to evidence tax payment .
Section 4281c - 19 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
For the purpose of assisting in the enforcement
of Chapter 1 , Acts of the Third Extraordinary
Session of the 1936 General Assembly or any
amendments thereof, every licensee , except retailers ,
whether subject to the payment of taxes imposed by
said act or any amendments thereof , shall , on or
before the fifteenth day of each month , render to
the Department of Revenue a sworn or affirmed
statement in writing , of all his trafficking in alco-
holic beverages during the preceding month . Such
statement shall be taken directly from the records

CHAPTER 162 653
of the reporting licensee , and shall set forth on
forms furnished by the Department of Revenue
such information as shall be required by it . Such
statement shall include alcohol destined for sale
outside the state , as well as alcoholic beverages sub-
ject to the tax imposed by Chapter 1 , Acts of the
Third Extraordinary Session of the 1936 General
Assembly or any amendments thereof. Provided ,
that the Department of Revenue shall have au-
thority to require from retail licensees and other
licensees , other reports and statements at such
times as are necessary for the enforcement of Chap-
ter 1 , Acts of the Third Extraordinary Session of
the 1936 General Assembly or any amendments
thereof.
Section 4281c - 14 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
The sale or distribution of alcoholic beverages.
manufactured in or imported into this state for
shipment permanently out of the state to be sold
through retail outlets without the state and con-
sumed without the state shall not be subject to the
tax imposed by this act . Provided , however , the De-
partment of Revenue may , when necessary for the
purpose of control enforcement or protection of
revenue , prescribe the conditions under which con-
tainers of such alcoholic beverages for shipment
permanently out of the state to be sold through
retail outlets without the state and consumed with-
out the state may be kept and trafficked in without
payment of the tax . ·
4. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , sections 1 , 2 and 3 of this Act shall , upon
the taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
be substituted , respectively , in place of KRS
243.840 , 243.850 and 243.790 , and shall thereafter

654 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be treated as amendments to KRS 243.840 , 243.850
and 243.790 , rather than amendments to $$
4281c - 18 , 4281c - 19 and 4281c - 14 of Carroll's Stat-
utes ; the numbers " KRS 243.720 to 243.860 " shall
be substituted for " Chapter 1 , Acts of the Third
Extraordinary Session of the 1936 General Assem-
bly thereof " in section 2 of this Act , and the num-
ber "KRS 243.720 " shall be substituted for the
words "this Act " in section 3 of this Act .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 164
(H. B. 152 )
AN ACT to amend Section 4081 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 4081 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as
follows :
(1 ) If the lines or route of a domestic or foreign
railway company , gas company , water company ,
ferry company , bridge company , street railway
company , express company , electric light company ,
electric power company , telephone company , tele-
graph company , press dispatch company , bus line
company , common carrier trucking . company , air
transport company , palace car company , dining car
company , sleeping car company , chair car company ,
tank car company , coal car company , pipe line com-
pany , or other like company or business perform-
ing any public service , extend beyond the limits of
the state , that proportion of the value of the capital
stock which the length of the lines or route operated ,
owned , leased or controlled in this state bears to
the total length of the lines or route operated ,
owned , leased or controlled in this state and else-
where shall be considered in fixing the value of the

656 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
corporate franchise of such corporation liable for
taxation in this state .
(2 ) The corporate franchise unless provided by
law shall be liable to taxation in each county , city
or district through or into which such lines or route
pass or are operated , in the same proportion that
the length of the lines or route in such county , city
or district bears to the whole length of lines or
routes in this state .
(3 ) If any such domestic corporation has all of
its lines or routes outside of this state , the Depart-
ment of Revenue shall fix the value of its entire
capital stock as hereinbefore provided , and appor-
tion to this state for taxation therein the proper
proportion and not less than one perent of its capi-
tal stock . The amount so apportioned shall be the
value of its intangible property , including its cor-
porate franchise , stocks , bonds , securities and
choses in action subject to taxation in this state and
in the county , city and district where its principal
place of business in this state is located .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , subsection ( 1 ) of section 1 of this Act shall ,
upon the taking effect of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , be substituted in place of subsection ( 3 )
of KRS 136.160 ; subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of section
1 of this Act shall be substituted in place of sub-
sections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of KRS 136.170 ; and this Act
shall thereafter be treated as an amendment to
KRS 136.160 and 136.170 rather than an amend-
ment to 4081 of Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .

CHAPTER 165
(H. B. 148 )
AN ACT relating to credit for imprisonment during inability
to secure bail .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 1142a of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
If in any trial in a circuit , county , quarterly , jus-
tice's or police court of this state , the defendant has
been compelled to remain in jail , by reason of his
inability to execute bond , to answer the charge in
the court in which he is held, and , on trial had in
such court, he has been found guilty and his punish-
ment fixed at a fine or a term in the county jail , he
shall, in the case of a fine , be given credit at the rate
of two dollars per day for each day he was so com-
pelled to remain in jail , and in the case of a jail sen-
tence , shall be given credit on such sentence for the
time he was so compelled to remain in jail . The
court shall endorse on the judgment a credit for the
number of days the defendant was so compelled to
remain in jail , and , in the case of a jail sentence ,
shall make an entry on the commitment , and the de-
fendant shall be discharged from the county jail
when he has served the full term for which he was
convicted less such credit of time as he is to receive
as herein provided .
2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section one of this Act shall , upon the tak-
ing effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 431.150 and shall there
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 431.150
rather than an amendment to § 1142a of Carroll's
Statutes .
Approved March 11 , 1942 .
