CHAPTER 166
(H. B. 142 )
AN ACT to amend and reenact Section 772a - 5 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes, 1936 Revision , relating to the operation
of passenger train service on railroads in this Common-
wealth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 772a - 5 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Revision , is amended to read as
follows :
Upon the application of any carrier by railroad ,
and after such notice to the public and such hearing
as the Commission may by order prescribe , the
Railroad Commission of Kentucky shall have the
power and it shall be its duty to authorize common
carriers by railroad to discontinue passenger serv-
ice by railroad on any line of railroad or branch
thereof in this State , whenever in the opinion of
said railroad commission any such passenger serv-
ice by railroad has been and is being carried on at a
loss and there is no reasonable probability that such
condition will in the future change for the better ,
or whenever any passenger service by railroad has
for any reason become unnecessary in the public
interest ; and no carrier by railroad shall discontinue
any passenger train or substitute a mixed train for
a passenger train on any line of railroad or branch
thereof until said Railroad Commission shall have
authorized the discontinuance or substitution in
accordance with the provisions of this section , pro-
vided , however , this clause shall not apply to sea-
sonal passenger trains .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section one of this Act shall, upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be substi-

CHAPTER 167 659
tuted in place of subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 276.480 ,
and thereafter be treated as an amendment to KRS
276.480 rather than an amendment to § 772a -5 of
Carroll's Statutes .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 167
(H. B. 64 )
AN ACT to revise certain Probate Laws of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and to provide certain probate pro-
cedure in the county court ; to simplify the same by defini-
tions and a common statement of qualifications and duties ,
and permitting the designation of an attorney , if desired ,
and requiring written application and notice to heirs before
appointment of certain fiduciaries ; to amend Section 1058 of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , by permitting
the probate of wills at special sessions of the county court ;
to repeal , amend and reenact Sections 2149 and 216AA - 81
of said Statutes , relating to the jurisdiction of the appoint-
ment of committees ; to require publication of notice to
creditors of estates ; to repeal , amend and reenact Sections
3849 and 3855 of said Statutes , relating to the time of filing
an inventory ; to repeal , amend and reenact Sections 1065 ,
1067 , 2029 , 2037 , 3857 and 3858 of said Statutes , relating to
accounting by fiduciaries and providing penalties for failure
to account ; to provide for the publication of notice of settle-
ments ; to repeal , amend and reenact Section 3847 of said
Statutes , relating to when an action may be brought against
a personal representative ; to repeal , amend and reenact
Section 2087 of said Statutes , relating to the period of time
debts remain a lien on decedent's real estate ; to repeal ,
amend and reenact Section 3860 of said Statutes , relating to
when a distribution of an estate may be made .
WHEREAS , the Probate Laws of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky are in need of revision so as to shorten the period of
administration of estates , to protect trusts from defalcations
and to more fully safeguard the rights of creditors , distrib-
utees and wards ; and ,
WHEREAS , the following revisions are considered to be the

660 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
most urgent and except for such changes , the Probate Laws
will remain as at the present time ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Definition . The term " fiduciary " as used in
this act means any person , association , or corpora-
tion ( other than assignee or trustee for an insolvent
debtor , or a guardian under the uniform veterans '
guardianship act ) appointed by , or under the con-
trol of , or accountable to the county court , includ-
ing executors , administrators , administrators with
the will annexed , testamentary trustees , curators ,
guardians and committees .
2. Who May Be Appointed . Any resident of
the State of Kentucky , including married women ,
over 21 years of age and any National Bank located
in Kentucky having fiduciary powers and any State
Bank or Trust Company incorporated under the
Laws of the State of Kentucky and authorized by
law to act as such therein may be appointed as
fiduciary .
3. Appointment and Duties . Every fiduciary ,
before entering upon the execution of the trust ,
shall receive letters of appointment from the county
court having jurisdiction as now fixed by law . The
duties of a fiduciary shall be such as are required
by law , and such additional duties not inconsistent
therewith as the court may order
4. Representation by Attorney . At the time of
appointment of a fiduciary , he may designate an
attorney who will represent him in matters relating
to the trust and when so designated , notices to such
fiduciary shall also be sent by the court to such
attorney , but no fiduciary shall be required to be
represented by attorney .

CHAPTER 167 661
5. Application for Appointment as Fiduciary .
Before being appointed as executor , administrator ,
curator , or administrator with the will annexed ,
every person shall make and file in duplicate a
written application under oath , which must state
the names of the deceased's surviving spouse and
all of his heirs at law or such as are known , their
postoffice address if known , the date of death and
also a statement in general terms as to what the
estate consists of and the probable value of the per-
sonal and real estate and also a statement of any
indebtedness owing by the applicant to the de-
ceased . A duplicate copy of said application shall
be mailed by the clerk to the Commissioner of Rev-
enue of the State of Kentucky .
In case of intestacy , if there be no surviving
spouse , or if such spouse waives the right of ap-
pointment or is not qualified to act and does not
nominate a suitable administrator and there are
more than one resident heir at law entitled to ap-
pointment , the court shall thereupon appoint a time
for hearing such application . Notice of said hear-
ing shall be given to the surviving spouse and all
known heirs of the deceased residing in the State ,
in the manner herein provided .
Before being appointed as guardian or commit-
tee , every person shall make and file in duplicate a
written application under oath , which must state
the name , age and residence of the ward , the ward's
husband or wife and each of the ward's parents , or
if there be no spouse or parents living in the State ,
the names , ages and residence of the next of kin ,
the name of the person having custody of the ward ,
a brief description of the ward's estate and its
probable value , and the estimated annual income
therefrom . A duplicate copy of said application
shall be mailed by the Clerk to the Commissioner

662 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of Revenue of the State of Kentucky . The court
shall thereupon appoint a time for hearing such ap-
plication . Notice thereof shall be given to the
minor if more than fourteen years of age ; to the
person having custody of the ward ; to the husband
or wife and each parent of the ward , or if none , to
the next of kin residing in the State in the manner
herein provided .
6. Application for Probate of Will . When any
will is offered for probate , the court shall require a
verified application to be filed by the person offer-
ing the same . Such application shall state the resi-
dence of the testator at the time of his death and
such other facts as may be necessary to establish
the jurisdiction of the court , and the names , ages
and postoffice addresses of the testator's surviving
spouse and heirs at law, or such as are known . An
application for probate and for appointment as
executor or administrator with the will annexed
may be combined in one application .
7. Notice of Hearing . When notice of hearing
of an application for the appointment of an admin-
istrator , administrator with will annexed , guardian ,
or committee is required hereunder , it shall be given
by mailing written notice of the time , place and
purpose of such hearing at least five days before
said hearing . Proof of such notice shall be by
registered mail receipt or by applicant's affidavit
that the same was mailed to each of the parties
entitled thereto in a sealed envelope , postage pre-
paid , and the date when posted , which proof shall
be filed at the time of hearing . If all parties en-
titled to notice are under no disability and waive
notice in writing , the court may hear such applica-
tion as if notice had been given . A minor over four .
teen years of age may waive notice if present in
person at the time of hearing of application for

CHAPTER 167 663
appointment of his guardian ; provided , however
in any estate where the gross amount involved is
less than One Thousand Dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) the
court in its discretion may dispense with the re-
quirements of this act relating to notice .
8. That Section 1058 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , be amended by striking
therefrom the words " probating of a will , or " so
that said section , as amended , shall read as follows :
Section 1058. Monthly Terms ; Change ; Special
Terms . There shall be a regular term of the county
court held by the county judge in each county once
every month on Monday , and until changed as
herein provided , shall be held on the same day it
now is . The time of holding the county court in
any county may be changed by an order made by
the county judge and entered upon the records of
the county court at the last regular term held in the
year next preceding the year in which the change
is to be made . Special terms of the county court
may be held at any time for the transaction of any
business except granting tavern , liquor , or druggist
license ; and the court may adjourn from time to
time until the business is disposed of, but no ad-
journment shall be to a time beyond the commence-
ment of the next regular term .
9. That Sections 2149 and 216AA - 81 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , be repealed ,
amended and reenacted so as to read as follows :
Committees ; Appointment and Accounting of.
The county courts shall have exclusive jurisdiction
of the appointment and accounting of committees .
Application shall be filed and notice given in the
same manner required for guardians . There shall
be filed with said application a statement from the
clerk of the circuit court that the incompetent has
been adjudged as such . Appointment shall be made ,

664 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
bond executed , and accounting required in the same
manner as guardians and actions upon such bondmay be maintained by any person aggrieved by abreach thereof.
10. That Section 3849 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , be amended by changing" three months " to " two months " so that said Sec-
tion, as amended , shall read as follows :
Section 3849. Appraisers and Their Duty . Thecounty court in which a will is proved or admin-
istration granted, shall appoint three persons , any
two of whom may act, being first sworn to discharge
the duty assigned to them to the best of their judg-
ment . It shall be their duty to view and appraise
such estate as the personal representative shall ex-hibit to them , and subscribe and return such ap-
praisement to the clerk's office of the court appoint-
ing them within two months after their appoint-
ment . If the appraisers believe the whole personal
estate has not been exhibited to them , they shallreport the fact to the court .
11. That Section 3855 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition, be repealed , amended andreenacted so as to read as follows :
Section 3855. Inventory ; to be Returned ; Copies
Evivdence. It shall be the duty of a personal rep-
resentative of a decedent to return an inventory induplicate within two months from the time of quali-
fying as such , to the clerk's office of the court in
which he qualified , the original of which shall be
recorded by the clerk and the duplicate shall be
mailed by the clerk to the Commissioner of Rev-
enue. Copies from the record of the inventory or
appraisement shall be prima facie evidence for oragainst such representative .
12. To repeal , amend and reenact Sections 1065 ,
2029 , 2037 , 3857 and 3858 of Carroll's Kentucky

CHAPTER 167 665
Statutes , 1936 Edition , relating to accounting by
fiduciaries and penalties for failure to account , so
that said sections , as reenacted , shall read as fol-
lows :
Accounts; When Required ; Proof of Balances and
Securities . One year after appointment and an-
nually thereafter , unless otherwise provided by law,
every fiduciary must render an account of the ex-
ecution of his trust to the court by which he was
appointed , including in such account an itemized
statement of receipts and disbursements supported
by vouchers accompanying the same and a state-
ment of all investments on hand and changes in in-
vestments since the filing of his last account . Pro-
vided , however , that guardians , committees and
trustees under continuing trusts , after their first
accounting , shall render their accounts thereafter
bi-annually , or at shorter intervals if the court shall
require it . Such settlements , when approved and
confirmed by the court, shall be recorded and in-
dexed by the clerk, and the original and the
vouchers carefully kept by him in his office . An
account shall be rendered by the fiduciary at any
other time upon order of the court upon its own.
motion or that of any person interested in the trust ,
for good cause shown on affidavit . At the expira-
tion of his trust such fiduciary must fully account for
and pay over the trust estate to the person or per-
sons entitled thereto . Every such account shall list
all unpaid creditors whose claims have been allowed
and all creditors whose claims have been disallowed .
No account of a fiduciary except of corporate fidu-
ciaries under the supervision of State or Federal
banking authorities shall be approved until there
are exhibited to the court , for its examination , the
security or securities shown in said account as being
in the hands of the fiduciary , or the certificate of a

666 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
bank having possession thereof or in which they
have been deposited for safe -keeping , and a certi-
fied bank statement showing the funds to the credit
of the trust .
Penalty for Failure to Account . If a fiduciary
neglects or refuses to file an inventory or account
when due according to law , or when ordered by the
court, the court shall notify the fiduciary of his de-
linquency and fix a date when such inventory or
account must be filed , and any fiduciary failing ,
without good cause , therefor , to file his inventory or
account as required by said notice shall be fined by
the court for each day he so neglects or refuses
after the date fixed in said notice , the sum of Ten
( $ 10.00 ) Dollars , to be collected by rule or other
process . Unless there is pending in the circuit court
a suit to settle the estate such neglect or refusal
shall be grounds for removal by the court . If the
fiduciary fails to file such account within thirty days
after the date fixed in said notice , no allowance
shall be made for his services unless the court enters
upon its minutes an order that such delay was
justified .
Duties of Clerk and Judge . It shall be the duty
of the clerk to report in writing to the judge once
each month the names and addresses of all fidu-
ciaries who have during the previous calendar
month become delinquent in filing an inventory or
settlement . The judge shall notify by mail such
delinquent fiduciaries and shall warn them of the
penalties provided by law . If the clerk shall fail
to report such delinquents it shall be the duty of the
judge to secure a list of same from the court records .
There shall be taxed as costs in each delinquent case
a fee of Twenty -Five ( $ .25 ) Cents for the prepara-
tion of said list and the mailing of said notice , to be
divided between the clerk and the judge . If the

CHAPTER 167 667
list of said delinquents is secured by the judge he
shall be entitled to all of said fee . If the judge shall
fail to mail such notices it shall be the duty of the
clerk to do so and he shall be entitled to all of said
fee .
13. That Section 1067 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , be repealed , amended and
reenacted so as to read as follows :
Section 1067. Settlements Filed by Clerk ; Ex-
ceptions ; Vouchers , New Evidence . The clerk shall
endorse on the report the time of filing the same
and it shall lay over not less than ten days until the
first day of the next term of court, to be known as
the date of hearing , during which , or at which , ex-
ceptions may be filed by any person interested . If
no exceptions are filed the report , if made accord-
ing to law , shall be approved and recorded . If ex-
ceptions are filed , other evidence besides that
reported may be heard, and the court shall , upon
the whole case , reject , confirm , alter or amend the
report , and if confirmed , order it to be recorded .
The vouchers accompanying the report shall not be
recorded , but must be carefully kept on file with
the report in the clerk's office . Any new evidence
given in court must be reduced to writing and filed
with the report . Settlements so made and recorded.
shall be prima facie evidence between the parties
interested .
14. Notice of Filing Settlement . Not less than
ten days prior to the date of hearing , the clerk shall
cause notice of the filing of a settlement to be pub-
lished in a newspaper of general circulation in the
county , stating the name of the fiduciary , the trust ,
the nature of the account and the date of hearing,
with a statement that exceptions must be filed
before that time . The actual cost of such notice ,
or the proportionate part thereof, if more than one

668 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
settlement , shall be taxed as costs . Provided , how-
ever , that with the court's approval the fiduciary
may , in lieu of such publication , send a written
notice thereof to all unpaid creditors and distribu-
tees , which notice shall be mailed at least ten days
before said date of hearing . The fiduciary in such
cases shall file his affidavit that such notice has been
mailed .
15. That Section 3847 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , be amended so as to permit
the filing of a suit against a personal representative
after five months , so that said section , as amended ,
shall read as follows :
Section 3847. Action Against ; When May Be
Commenced . Five months must run after the date
of the qualification of the first personal representa-
tive of a decedent's estate , before an action shall be
commenced against any executor or administrator
thereof except against an executor de son tort . Any
action brought in violation of this action shall be
dismissed with costs .
16. That Section 2087 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , be repealed , amended and
reenacted so that the period of time during which
debts remain a lien on real estate will be reduced
from twelve months to six months , so that said sec-
tion , as reenacted shall read as follows :
Section 2087. Alienation before Suit Vests Pur-
chaser with Title ; Liability of Heir . When the
heir or devisee shall alien , before suit brought , the
estate descended or devised , he shall be liable for
the value thereof , with legal interest from the time
of alienation , to the creditors of the descendant or
testator ; but the estate so alienated shall not be
liable to the creditors in the hands of a bona fide
purchaser for valuable consideration , unless action

CHAPTER 167 669
is instituted within six months after the estate is
devised or descended to subject the same.
17. That Section 3860 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , be repealed , amended and
reenacted so as to permit the distribution of estates
after six months instead of nine months , so that
said section , as reenacted , shall read as follows :
Section 3860. Distribution of Estate . A per-
sonal representative may distribute the estate of a
decedent six months after qualification .

CHAPTER 168
(S. B. 77 )
AN ACT relating to Alcoholic Beverage Control .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1 . Section 2554b - 118 of Baldwin's 1938 Supple-
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended to
read as follows :
A distiller's , rectifier's or vintner's license shall
authorize the licensee to engage in the business of
distiller , rectifier , or vintner at the premises spe-
ww

672 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cifically designated in the license and to transport
for himself only any alcoholic beverage which he
is authorized under the license to manufacture or
sell . Such licensee shall transport alcoholic bever-
ages only by a vehicle owned and operated by him-
self, which has affixed to its sides at all times a sign
of form and size prescribed by the State Board ,
containing among other things the name and license
number of the licensee . No distilled spirits or wine
shall be transported on the same truck or vehicle
with malt beverages , except by a common carrier .
Provided , however , that during the period of the
national emergency that now exists by presidential
proclamation the Alcoholic Beverage Control Board
may promulgate rules and regulations changing or
altering the provisions contained in this section in
order to expedite delivery and conserve equipment .
2. Section 2554b - 120 of Baldwin's 1938 Supple-
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended to
read as follows :
Business Authorized Under a Wholesaler's Li-
cense . A wholesaler's license shall authorize the
licensee to purchase , receive , store or possess dis-
tilled spirits and wine , to sell them at wholesale ,
from the licensed premises only , and to transport
from a licensed premises for himself only any alco-
holic beverage which his license authorizes him to
sell , provided that he so transport such beverages
in the manner provided for manufacturers in sec-
tion 2554b -118 of Baldwin's 1938 Supplement to
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , provided further , that
he is to be permitted to transport distilled spirits
and wine from a manufacturer's warehouse or from
other licensed wholesalers ' premises to his licensed
premises , if consumer's spirits stamps have been
properly affixed to the spirits or wine which he so

CHAPTER 168 673
transports , and that no wholesaler shall transport
distilled spirits or wine in interstate commerce .
3. Section 3 of Chapter 15 of the Acts of 1940
is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Applications for Licenses : Issuance of
Same . Applications for any license provided for
in section 2554b - 114 of Baldwin's 1938 Supplement
to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , shall be made to the
Administrator of the Distilled Spirits Unit at his
office in Frankfort , Kentucky ; shall be in writing on
forms furnished by the Department of Revenue ,
and verified ; and shall set forth in detail such in-
formation concerning the applicant and the prem-
ises for which the license is sought as said Act
or the State Board shall by regulation require . Said
application shall be accompanied by a certified
check , or cash , or a postal or express money order
for the amount of money required by the Act for a
license of the kind applied for . If the Administrator
shall approve the application , he shall issue the
proper license in such form as shall be determined
by the State Board by regulation , subject to the
provisions of section 2554b - 136 of Baldwin's 1938
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
(2 ) No retail drink , retail package , or special
private club license shall be issued in less than ten
( 10 ) days or delivered in less than fifteen ( 15 ) days
from the time the application and remittances were
received by the Department of Revenue ; except that
a retail package , retail drink , or special private club
license shall not be issued to an applicant who has
not held a license during the same or preceding
license period , or for a premises for which a license
has not been issued during the same or preceding
license period , in less than twenty ( 20 ) days or
delivered in less than thirty ( 30) days from the
time the application and remittances were received.

, or any such license may be revoked for

CHAPTER 168 675
sufficient . A license may be revoked for any of the
reasons for which the Administrator would have
been required to refuse a license if the facts had
been known . Any license issued under this Act must
be revoked for the following causes :
(1 ) Conviction of the licensee or his agent or
employee for selling any illegal beverages on the
premises licensed .
(2 ) Making any false , material statements in an
application for a license .
( 3 ) Violation of the provisions of section
2554b -163 of Baldwin's 1938 Supplement to Car-

provisions of this Act or any Act heretofore or
hereafter in effect relating to the regulation of the

any of his clerks , servants , agents or employees

any misdemeanor directly or indirectly attributable
to the use of intoxicating liquors , or of one such
felony and one such misdemeanor .
( 5 ) Willful and deliberate failure or default of
a licensee to pay an excise tax or any part thereof ,
or any penalties imposed by or under the provisions
of any Statutes , ordinances or Acts of Congress
relative to taxation , or for a violation of any rules
or regulations of the Department of Revenue made
in pursuance thereof.
(6 ) Revocation of any license or permit pro-
vided in section 2554b -112 of Baldwin's 1938 Sup-
plement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes and section
2554b -113 of Baldwin's 1940 Supplement to Car-

5. Section 2554b - 145 of Baldwin's 1938 Supple-
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended to
read as follows :
Suspension of Licenses . Upon proceedings for
the revocation of any license under section 2554b - 142
of Baldwin's 1938 Supplement to Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes the Alcoholic Beverage Control
Board , or the local Alcoholic Beverage Adminis-
trator , may in its or his discretion , as the case may
be , order a suspension of the license for any cause
for which it may , but is not required to , revoke the
license under the provisions of section 2554b - 141 of
Baldwin's 1938 Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes ; provided however the licensee may have
the alternative , subject to the approval of the Alco-
holic Beverage Control Board or the local Alcoholic
Beverage Administrator , to pay in lieu of part or
all of the days of any suspension period , a sum as
follows :
Distillers , rectifiers , vintners, brewers and blend-
ers , one thousand ($1,000 ) dollars per day ; whole-
sale liquor licensees , four hundred ($400 ) dollars
per day ; wholesale beer licensees , one hundred
($ 100 ) dollars per day ; retail drink liquor licensees ,
twenty -five ( $ 25 ) dollars per day ; retail package
liquor licensees , twenty -five ( $ 25 ) dollars per day ;
retail beer licensees , ten ( $ 10 ) dollars per day ; all
remaining licensees , ten ( $ 10 ) dollars per day . Said
sums shall be paid to the Department of Revenue ,
deposited into the State Treasury and credited to
the General Expenditure Fund ; provided , however ,
that one -half of any amount paid in lieu of a sus-
pension of a wholesale liquor license , wholesale beer

CHAPTER 168 677
license , rectifier license , retail package license , re-
tail drink license , or retail beer license over which a
city or county administrator has jurisdiction shall
be returned to the officer authorized to issue the
local license in the respective city or county for
deposit and use as alcoholic beverage license tax
receipts of the city or county are deposited and
used .
Appeals from orders of suspension and the pro-
cedure thereon shall be the same as are provided
for orders of revocation in sections 2554b - 146 and
2554b -147 of Baldwin's 1938 Supplement to Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes .
6. Section 2554b -155 of Baldwin's 1938 Supple-
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended to
read as follows :
Persons Who May Not be Employed by Licensees .
No person holding any license under this Act shall
knowingly employ in connection with his business ,
in any capacity whatsoever , any person who :
(1 ) Has been convicted of a felony or any mis-
demeanor or offense directly or indirectly attrib-
utable to the use of intoxicating liquors , within
the last two years .
(2 ) Is under the age of twenty years , except in
a bottling house or room of a licensed distiller , vint-
ner or brewer or rectifier and except in an office of
a wholesaler or manufacturer maintained in a build-
ing separate from the warehouses or factory .
( 3 ) Is not a citizen of the United States or has
not had an actual bona fide residence in this Com-
monwealth for at least one year next before the date

CHAPTER 169
(S. B. 57)
AN ACT relating to elections, amending Sections 1460 and
1472 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes, Baldwin's 1936 Revision ,
and making provisions for the disposition of used stubs ,
ballots and other paper .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 1460 , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , be and the same
is hereby amended and as amended shall read as
follows :
( 1 ) The county clerks of the several counties
shall cause the names of all the candidates of their
respective jurisdictions , where nominations for any
office specified in the ballot have been duly made
and not withdrawn , in accordance herewith , to be
printed on one ballot all nominations of any party
or group of petitioners , as designated by them in
their certificate or petition ; or if none be desig-
nated , under some suitable title and device . If the
same device for designating candidates be selected
by two parties or groups of petitioners it shall be
given to the one which first selected it, and the
clerk shall select a suitable device for the other .
The arrangement of the ballot shall in general con-
form , as nearly as practicable , to the plan herein-
after given, and the devices named and lists of the
candidates of the various parties shall be printed

CHAPTER 169 685
in parallel columns , in such order as the Secretary
of State may direct , precedence , however , being
given to the party which polled the higher number
of votes for the head of the ticket in the last pre-
ceding election . The device of each party shall be
placed at the head of the list of candidates of the
party . The device shall not be enclosed in a square ,
but immediately under the device shall be placed a
circle of one inch in diameter . Immediately under
it shall be placed the name or title of the party
ticket, and immediately under the name or title the
list of candidates of the party , the name of each
candidate having immediately on its right a small
square large enough to contain the cross -mark by
which the voter is required to designate his vote .
Underneath the name of each candidate shall be left
a blank space large enough to contain a written
name .
(2) At the top of all official ballots to be used in
any primary , general or special election in any
county , and separated from it by a perforated line ,
shall be a stub , containing the consecutive number
of the ballot and a space for the name , residence
and registration number of the voter, and at the
bottom of said ballot there shall be a secondary
stub , separated from it by a perforated line , and
said secondary stub shall contain the consecutive
number of the ballot and a space for the name ,
residence and registration number of the voter.
(3 ) On the back shall be printed " Official Bal-
lot" , the date of the election and facsimile of the
signature of the clerk who has caused the ballot to
be printed . There shall also be printed on the back
of the ballot a blank line followed by the word
" Clerk " and a blank line followed by the word
'Judge' .
66

686 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(4) After the ballots have been detached from
the stub in the book of ballots and before the ballot
is delivered to the voter to be voted , the clerk shall
sign his name on the line designated for the clerk's
signature , and one of the judges shall sign his name
on the blank line designated for the judge's signa-
ture, and no ballot , not signed in the handwriting
by one of the judges shall be counted by the can-
vassing board whose duty it is to certify the results
of the election .
( 5 ) Should an elector desire to vote for each
and every candidate of one party he shall make a
cross -mark ( X ) in the circle under the device of
said party and the vote shall be counted for all
candidates under said device : Provided , however ,
if a cross -mark (X) be made in the circle under the
party device and a cross -mark (X ) be also made
after one or more candidates of a different party ,
or parties , the vote shall be counted for the candi-
dates so marked , and not for the candidates for the
same offices of the party so marked , but the vote
shall be counted for the other candidates of said
party .
§ 2. That Section 1472 , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , be and the same is
hereby amended and as amended shall read as fol-
lows :
(1 ) If the voter , upon examining his ballot , finds
that it is not signed on the back by the clerk of the
election and one of the judges of the election , the
voter shall return the ballot to the election officers
for said signatures before voting the ballot . After
marking his ballot the voter shall return it , proper-
ly folded, and in the presence of the election of-
ficers detach the secondary stub and deliver same
to one of the judges of the election . The judge of
the election shall return the ballot to the voter after

CHAPTER 169 687
the secondary stub has been detached therefrom and
the voter shall deposit the ballot in the ballot box .
It shall be the duty of the election officers to see that
all voted ballots are placed in the ballot box by the
voter as provided in this section , and if the voter
attempts to leave the room with the ballot he shall
be arrested by the sheriff of the election and the
ballot shall be taken from his possesion , marked
" spoiled " , with the name of the voter from whom
the ballot was taken and a statement that the ballot
was taken from him while attempting to leave the
room with it , and shall be signed by the clerk of the
election and one of the judges of the election and
returned with the spoiled ballots . It shall be un-
lawful for any election officer , after a ballot has
been delivered to a voter , to unfold the ballot or
to attempt , directly or indirectly , to ascertain how
the ballot has been voted .
( 2) Each voter shall mark his ballot without un-
due delay and shall not occupy a booth when occu-
pied by another and shall leave the booth and the
room when the ballot is marked and deposited by
him in said ballot box . Voters shall not occupy any
booth more than three minutes when others are
waiting to vote . No voter shall be permitted to
electioneer or converse with others while in the
voting room concerning their support or nonsup-
port of any candidate , party or issue to be voted on
except as herein provided . No one shall be permit-
ted in the voting room , except for the purpose of
voting , while the officers are holding the election .
Election officers will see that this provision is ob-
served . No election officer shall be permitted, while
any voter is voting , to go closer than fifteen feet of
any election booth , except to provide election uten-
sils , and not closer than fifteen feet of the ballot

688 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
box , which shall be stationed and placed near the
voting booth , but in plain view of the election of-
ficers , except to aid and assist blind and disabled
voters in depositing their ballots .
( 3 ) Upon the closing of the polls the election
officers shall string all used secondary stubs upon
a string provided by the county clerk for that pur-
pose , and they shall be placed in the envelope in
which the other election material is returned to the
county clerk .
(4 ) The stub books , secondary stubs , ballots ,
tally sheets , linen envelopes and voters ' affidavits
shall , after the results of the election have been
certified , remain in the office of the county clerk as
a part of his records for a period of six months . If
at the expiration of the said six months there is no
contest , recount or criminal prosecution pending
involving the same , the clerk shall deliver the same
to the appropriate authority for use in supplying
paper needed in National Defense . If a contest , re-
count or criminal prosecution is pending at the end
of the six months ' period , the clerk shall make a
similar disposition of such material when author-
ized by order of the judge of the court in which the
proceeding or prosecution is pending . After the
termination of the present National Emergency the
clerk shall , at the end of the six months ' period
above referred to , or when authorized by the cir-
cuit judge of the county , sell the material for the
best price obtainable and pay the proceeds into the
county treasury , after taking proper precautions to
see that no information as to how any voter has
voted can be obtained from examining such ma-
terial . If no sale can be made the material shall be
destroyed .

CHAPTER 169 689
( 5 ) Any violation of the provisions of subsec-
tion ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of this section shall be deemed a
high misdemeanor and upon conviction therefor ,
such person shall be fined not less than five hundred
dollars nor more than one thousand dollars or may
be imprisoned in the county jail not to exceed six
months , or both so fined and imprisoned in the dis-
cretion of the jury .
§ 3. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , then , as of the effective date of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , subsections ( 1 ) , (5 ) and ( 6 ) of
section one of this Act shall stand repealed ; sub-
section ( 2 ) of section one of this Act shall be sub-
stituted in place of subsection ( 2 ) ( b ) of KRS
118.170 ; subsection (3 ) of section one of this Act.
shall be substituted in place of subsection ( 2 ) ( c ) of
KRS 118.170 ; subsection ( 4 ) of section one of this
Act shall stand repealed and subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
118.280 shall be treated as amended to read as fol-
lows : "Any person desiring to vote and legally
entitled to vote shall give his name and residence.
to the clerk holding the ballots , who shall write the
same upon the stub of the ticket in the blank places
provided therefor , together with the voter's regis-
tered number . The clerk shall enter the same infor-
mation on the secondary stub . The clerk shall then
detach the ballot , with the secondary stub attached ,
from the main stub , sign his name on the line desig-
nated for the clerk's signature and deliver it to one
of the judges who shall sign his name on the blank
line designated for the judge's signature . No ballot
not so signed by one of the judges shall be counted
by the canvassing board . After the ballot has been
signed by the clerk and one of the judges , the clerk
shall hand it to the voter. The clerk shall give the

690 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
voter only one ballot, and on request of the voter
shall explain the manner of voting . When there are
separate ballots for offices or public questions to be
voted for by only a portion of the voters in a pre-
cinct , such ballots shall be given only to such voters
as reside in the portion of the precinct that is within
the territory for which the office is provided or with
respect to which the public question is submitted .
The separate ballots for the election of members of
boards of education shall be issued as provided in
KRS 160.240 ."
4. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , then , as of the effective date of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , subsection ( 1 ) of section two of
this Act shall be substituted in place of subsection
( 1 ) of KRS 118.290 ; subsection ( 2 ) of section two of
this Act shall stand repealed ; subsection ( 4 ) of sec-
tion one of this Act shall be substituted in place of
subsection ( 3 ) of KRS 118.390 ; subsection ( 5 ) of
section two of this Act shall stand repealed , and sub-
section ( 9 ) of KRS 118.990 shall be treated as
amended to read as follows : " Any voter or election
officer who violates any of the provisions of sub-
section ( 1 ) of KRS 118.290 or subsection ( 1 ) of
KRS 118.320 shall be guilty of a high misdemeanor
and shall be fined not less than five hundred nor
more than one thousand dollars and confined in the
county jail for not more than six months , or both
so fined and imprisoned . "
§ 5. Except as provided in sections ( 3 ) and ( 4 )
of this Act , KRS 118.170 , 118.180 , 118.280 , 118.290 ,
118.320 , 118.330 and 118.990 shall not be affected by
this Act.
Approved March 12 , 1942 .

CHAPTER 170 691
CHAPTER 170
( S. B. 195 )
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions . As used in this Act :
(1 ) " Persons in military service " means all of-
ficers and enlisted men in active service in the
United States Army, the United States Air Corps ,
the United States Marines , the United States Navy ,
and any and all other divisions of the United States
Military or Naval service now or hereafter estab-
lished by the Congress of the United States of
America or by the President of the United States
pursuant to any official orders or proclamations ,
but does not include reserves and persons on the
retired list until ordered to active service .
( 2 ) " Active service " includes the period during
which a person in military service is absent from
duty on account of sickness , wounds , leave or other
lawful causes .
(3 ) "Period of military service " includes the
time between the following dates : For persons in
active service at the date of the approval of this
act it shall begin with the date of approval of this
act ; for persons entering active service after the
approval of this act with the date of their entering
active service . It shall terminate with the date of
discharge from active service or death while in
active service but in no case later than the date when
this act ceases to be in force .
(4) "Termination of the War " means termina-
tion of the present War by treaty of peace as pro-
claimed by the President .
§ 2. When any person in military service , or any
person in his behalf, shall file with the sheriff , or
other officer whose duty it is to collect any ad

692 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
valorem property taxes , an affidavit stating that he
is a person in military service as defined in this Act ,
and that by reason of such military service his abil-
ity to pay ad valorem taxes on his property is ma-
terially affected , an extension of time for the pay-
ment of all ad valorem taxes which would otherwise
become due on the property of such person during
his period of military service shall be granted to
such person , and such taxes shall not become due
until twelve months after the termination of his
period of military service or twelve months after
the termination of the War , whichever is the earlier .
§ 3. Every person in military service who is
required by law to file an income tax return and pay
income taxes to the state of Kentucky shall not be
required to file such return and pay such taxes ,
which would otherwise become due during his period
of military service , until twelve months after the
termination of his period of military service or
twelve months after the termination of the War ,
whichever is the earlier .
§ 4. During the extension of time for the pay-
ment of taxes as provided in this Act , no interest
shall be charged and no penalties shall be assessed
against the tax payer to whom an extension of time
is granted by this Act . The extension of time
granted by this Act shall not be included in com-
puting the time of any applicable statute of limita-
tions with respect to the enforcement of any tax
liability .
§ 5. Whereas , many of the citizens of this Com-
monwealth are now serving in the armed forces and
many others are being inducted for service in the
armed forces , and
Whereas , an unreasonable and undue hardship
may result to such citizens and to their families and
dependents by reason of the enforcement of tax

CHAPTER 171 693
liabilities whicle they are serving in the armed
forces , an emergency is hereby declared to exist and
this Act shall become effective immediately upon its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 11 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 171
(S. B. 197 )
AN ACT to amend Section 246 of the Constitution of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky relating to compensation for
official services .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Section 246 of the Constitution
of Kentucky be and the same hereby is amended by
repealing said section in its entirety and enacting
in lieu thereof the following .
Section 2. The General Assembly, as soon as
practicable after the enactment of this amendment ,
shall regulate compensation of public officials and
employees , including those in office and those elected
to office at the time of adoption of this amendment ;
and provide for penalties and what deductions shall
be made for neglect of official duties . Thereafter ,
the compensation of public officials shall not be
increased or decreased during the terms for which
they are elected or appointed .
Section 3. This amendment shall be submitted to
the voters of the State for their ratification or re-
jection at the time and in the manner provided for
under Sections 256 and 257 of the Constitution of
Kentucky , and under the provisions of Section 1459
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision and
K. S. R. Section
Section 4. The substance of the amendment to be

694 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
stated on the ballot shall be as follows : Are you in
favor of amending Section 246 of the Constitution
to read as follows :
"The General Assembly, as soon as practicable
after the enactment of this amendment , shall regu-
late compensation of public officials and employees ,
including those in office and those elected to office at
the time of adoption of this amendment ; and pro-
vide for penalties and what deduction shall be made
for neglect of official duties . Thereafter , the com-
pensation of public officials shall not be increased
or decreased during the terms for which they are
elected or appointed ."
Approved March 11 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 172
( S. B. 184 )
AN ACT relating to building and loan associations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1 ) Whenever it appears that the
capital stock of any building and loan assocation
has been impaired , the Director of Banking shall
notify such building and loan association and each
director thereof to make such impairment good
within thirty days and to restrict withdrawals and
not to accept unpledged shares . The officers and
directors shall immediately call a special meeting
of the shareholders . The association may , with the
consent of the Director of Banking , reduce its capi-
tal to the extent of the impairment , if such reduc-
tion will not place its capital below the amount
required by law.

CHAPTER 172 695
(2 ) If the association fails for thirty days after
such notice to call a meeting of the shareholders to
adopt a program to eliminate such impairment , the
Director of Banking may , with the consent of the
Attorney -General, institute necessary proceedings
to wind up the affairs of the association . If the
association fails to make the impaired capital stock
good within ninety days after such notice , the Di-
rector of Banking may forthwith institute neces-
sary proceedings to wind up the affairs of the
association .
Section 2. The provisions of Sections 165a - 16
and 165a - 17 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes shall
apply to building and loan associations the same as
they apply to banks and trust companies , and all
powers vested in the Director of Banking as to
banks and trust companies by those sections are
vested in him as to building and loan associations .
Section 3. Sections 874g to 874k and 874p of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , are re-
pealed .
Section 4. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are enacted at the present session of the General
Assembly , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , the numbers " subsections ( 1 ) to

696 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 173
(H. B. 277)
AN ACT relating to fiscal courts and amending and con-
solidating §§ 1833 and 1836 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Sections 1833 and 1836 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 edition , are consolidated and
amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The fiscal court of each county shall consist
of the county judge , or in his absence the county
judge pro tem , and the justices of the peace of the
county ; or of the county judge , or in his absence
the county judge pro tem , and three county com-
missioners elected under §§ 1847 to 1849 or
§ 1851b - 1 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
( 2 ) The county judge, or in his absence the
county judge pro tem , shall preside , if present . If
neither the county judge nor the county judge pro
tem is present or cannot preside , a majority of the
justices of the peace or commissioners shall elect
one of their number to preside during the absence
or inability of the county judge or county judge pro
tem to preside . The county judge , or the county
judge pro tem or justice presiding in his place , shall
preserve order , and may fine and imprison for con-
tempt of court in the same manner as the presiding
judge of the county court.
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall, upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of KRS
67.040 , and shall thereafter be treated as an amend-
ment to subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of KRS 67.040
rather than an amendment to §§ 1833 and 1836 of

CHAPTER 174 697
Carroll's Statutes , and the numbers " KRS 67.050
and 67.060 " shall be substituted for " §§ 1847 to
1849 or § 1851b - 1 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes '
in section one of this Act and subsection ( 3 ) of
KRS 67.040 shall not be affected by this Act .
Approved by Governor March 11th , 1942 .
CHAPTER 174
(H. B. 377 )
AN ACT relating to elections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. KRS 118.070 is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Except as provided in KRS 118.110 , and sub-
ject to the provisions of subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3 )
of this section , the county clerk of each county shall
cause to be printed on the ballots for the regular
election the names of the following persons :
( a) Candidates of a political party , as defined in
KRS 119.010 , who have received certificates of nomi-
nation at the preceding primary election , or cer-
tificates of nomination under KRS 119.090 , and
whose certificates of nomination have been filed with
the clerk or the Secretary of State as provided in
KRS 118.120 , 118.130 and 119.090 .
(b ) Candidates of a political party , as defined
in KRS 119.010 , who have been nominated for an
unexpired term in a manner determined by the
governing authority of the party , as provided in
KRS 119.030 , and whose evidences of nomination
have been filed with the clerk or the Secretary of
State within the time prescribed in KRS 118.130 .
(c ) Candidates of a political party , as defined in
KRS 119.010 , who have been nominated by the

698 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
governing authority of the party to fill a vacancy
in the candidacy of a person nominated at the pre-
ceding primary election , as provided in KRS 119.020 ,
and whose certificates of nomination have been filed
with the clerk or the Secretary of State in sufficient
time before the regular election to enable their
names to be printed on the ballots at the time the

( d ) Candidates who have been nominated by a
political organization as provided in KRS 118.090 ,
and whose certificates or petitions of nomination
have been filed with the clerk or the Secretary of
State within the time prescribed in KRS 118.130 .
(e ) Candidates who have been nominated by
petition as provided in KRS 118.080 , and whose
petitions of nomination have been filed with the
clerk or the Secretary of State within the time pre-
scribed in KRS 118.130 .
(f) Candidates for President and Vice President
of the United States , of those political parties and
organizations who have nominated presidential
electors as provided in KRS 118.090 , where the cer-
tificate of nomination of such electors has been filed
with the Secretary of State within the time pre-
scribed in KRS 118.130 .
( 2 ) Those candidates whose petitions or cer-
tificates of nomination are required by law to be
filed with the Secretary of State shall have their
names printed on the ballots when certified to the
county clerk as provided in KRS 118.150 .
(3 ) Candidates for members of boards of edu-
cation shall have their names printed on ballots for
the regular election only when nominated as pro-
vided in KRS 160.220 .
(4 ) Except as provided in KRS 119.020 and
119.030 , the name of no candidate shall be printed
upon the ballots for any regular election as the

CHAPTER 174 699

119.010 , or under the emblem of any political party ,
as so defined , except those candidates who have been
duly and regularly nominated as nominees of that

Chapter 119 .
(5 ) No county clerk shall knowingly cause
to be printed , upon the ballot for any regular elec-
tion , the name of any candidate of a political party ,
as defined in KRS 119.010 , who has not been nomi-
nated in the manner provided in the primary elec-
tion laws .
§ 2. KRS 118.090 is ainended to read as follows :
(1 ) Any political organization not constituting a
political party within the meaning of KRS 119.010
but which cast two percent of the total vote of the
state at the last preceding regular election may
nominate , by convention or primary election held.
by the party in accordance with its constitution and
bylaws , candidates for any offices to be voted for at
any regular election , except the office of member of
a board of education , for which nominations shall
be made as provided in KRS 160.220 . Any political

candidates for members of legislative bodies of
cities of the fifth and sixth classes in the same man-
ner. Any political party , as defined in KRS 119.010 ,
and any political organization not constituting such
a political party but which cast two percent of the
total vote of the state at the last preceding regular
election , may nominate , by a convention or primary
election held by the party or organization in ac-
cordance with its constitution and bylaws , as many
electors of President and Vice President of the
United States as this state is entitled to elect .
( 2 ) The certificate of nomination by such a con-
vention or primary election shall be in writing ,

700 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall contain the name of each person nominated ,
his residence and the office to which he is nomi-
nated , and shall designate at title for the party or
principle that such convention or primary election
represents , together with any simple figure or de-
vice by which its list of candidates may be desig-
nated on the ballots . The certificate shall be signed
by the presiding officer and secretary of the con-
vention , or by the chairman and secretary of the

their signatures their respective places of residence ,

cate of nomination . In the case of electors of Presi-
Ident and Vice President of the United States the

(3 ) If the certificate of nomination of any state.

(4 ) In case of death , resignation or removal of

of President and Vice President of the United

CHAPTER 174 701

( 5 ) If any political party entitled to nominate

nees by petition for any office who are designated in

quired to be filed with the Secretary of State , be

When required to be filed with the county court

(1 ) Immediately after the expiration of the

participate in the election of the respective candi-

didate for each office , as specified in the certificates

§ 5. KRS 118.170 is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Except as provided in subsections (4 ) , ( 5 )

nated by them in their certificate or petition , or if

( 2 ) Except as provided in subsections ( 4 ) , ( 5 )

( a ) The lists of the candidates of the various

section ( 2 ) of KRS 118.410 .

(4 ) When there are offices or public questions

Acts- 23

706 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall also be printed for public questions when spe-
cifically required by the law providing for the sub-
mission of the question .

cal party as provided in KRS 118.090 .
§ 6. KRS 118.420 is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The State Board of Election Commission-
ers shall issue certificates of election where the suc-
cessful candidate was voted for by the state at large
or by a district greater than one county .
( 2 ) The State Board of Election Commission-
ers shall meet , to count and tabulate the votes re-
ceived by the different candidates as certified to
the Secretary of State under KRS 118.400 , when
the returns are all in , or on the third Monday after
the election whether they are in or not , but if all

CHAPTER 174 707
the returns are not made the right to contest an
election shall not be impaired . Any two of the mem-
bers of the board may act in the absence of the
third . The board shall make out the certificates of
election in the office of the Secretary of State , from
the returns made . The board shall make out dupli-
cate certificates of election , in writing , over the
signatures of its members . The board shall for-
ward the original certificate , by mail , to the elected
candidate , unless he has failed to comply with KRS
123.060 , 123.070 or 123.080 . The duplicate shall be
retained in the office of the board . In the case of the
election of a Representative in Congress , an ad-
ditional certificate shall be made and sent , by mail ,
to the clerk of the House of Representatives .
( 3 ) The Secretary of State , immediately after
the comparison of the returns , shall cause a state-
ment therefrom of the votes given in every county
for each candidate to be published in two news-
. papers .
(4 ) The certificates of election shall be issued to
the candidate receiving the highest number of votes
in the territory from which the election is to be
made . If two or more persons are found to have
received the highest and an equal number of votes
for the same office , the election shall be determined
by lot in such manner as the board directs , in the
presence of not less than three other persons . In
the case of elections for electors of President and
Vice President of the United States , the board shall
issue a certificate of election to each elector of the
political party or organization whose candidates
for President and Vice President received the
highest number of votes and the determination by
the board that the candidates of any political party
or organization for President and Vice President

708 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
have received the highest number of votes shall
constitute a determination that the electors nomi-
nated by that party have been elected .
Approved by Governor , 12th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 175
(H. B. 373 )
AN ACT relating to Alcoholic Beverages and amending Sub-
section ( e ) of Section 2 of Chapter 13 of the 1940 Acts of
the General Assembly .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
" Subsection ( e ) of Section 2 of Chapter 13 of the
1940 Acts of the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky be amended so that when
amended it shall read as follows :
" All mark-up resale prices to all licensees must be
calculated on the actual price inclusive of bottling
charges and all other charges paid for the distilled
spirits or wine sold , plus all taxes , plus all transpor-
tation charges , to be calculated on a minimum basis
of not less than twenty -five cents per case , pro-
vided , however , that in the computation of the
mark -up resale price there shall not be included any
taxes levied by the Federal Government subsequent
to the first day of March , 1942. "
Approved by Governor , March 11 , 1942 .

AN ACT relating to municipal and county planning and zon-

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Authority to create a City and County
Planning and Zoning Commission , and a Board of
Zoning Adjustment and Appeals . For the purpose of
the promotion of public health , safety , morals and
the general welfare , any city of the first class and
the county in which such city is located are hereby
authorized and empowered to enter into an agree-
ment to regulate the physical development , includ-
ing planning and zoning , of the incorporated and
unincorporated areas under their respective juris-
dictions . Said agreement shall be in writing and
submitted to the legislative body of such city and
the Fiscal Court of such county , for approval or

710 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
disapproval . If approved , the provisions of this act
and said agreement shall become effective upon
the adoption thereof by a mutual ordinance and
order passed by such legislative body and by such.
Fiscal Court , respectively , setting forth therein
said written agreement , and authoriging * the exe-
cution and delivery of same . Thereupon said agree-
ment shall be recorded in the office of the clerk of
the county court of such county .
Such city and such county are hereby authorized
and empowered to make , adopt , adjust , amend , ex-
tend and add to , by appropriate ordinances and
orders , joint and comprehensive city and county
planning and zoning plans , including rules and regu-
lations for the enforcement thereof ; and may jointly
create : ( 1 ) a joint city and county planning and zon-
ing commission , with the powers and duties and
functions hereinafter set forth , which shall be known

and county board of zoning adjustment and appeals
with the powers and duties and functions herein-
after set forth , which shall be known as "
(name of such city ) and (name of such

The agreement aforesaid may contain such terms

Any other incorporated municipality located in

CHAPTER 176 711
municipality , and the territory within its bound-
aries , shall be subject to the provisions of this act as

Any and all existing planning and zoning plans ,

and restrictions , or amendments thereof, in effect
within the respective territorial jurisdictions of

ords and the termination of the powers and duties
