until the completion of such plan or part thereof
or until all such pending matters have been decided .
Such postponements , however , shall not exceed a
period of more than six months .
§ 2. Organization of City and County Planning
and Zoning Commission . (1 ) Appointment and
Compensation of Commission . The Commission ,
when created , shall be composed of four ex-officio
members , who shall be the Mayor and the Director

712 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of Works of such city and the County Judge and
the Road Engineer of such county , and six ap-
pointed members , three of whom shall be appointed
by the Mayor of such city and three of whom shall
be appointed by the County Judge of such county .
The three members appointed by the Mayor shall
be residents within the corporate boundaries of
such city . The three members appointed by the
County Judge shall be residents of that portion
of the county lying outside of the corporate bound-
aries of such city . The appointments of the original
members shall be made immediately upon this act
becoming effective as herein provided . No ap-
pointed member shall hold any other public office
or position . The terms of office of ex officio mem-
bers shall correspond to their respective official
tenures . The term of office of each of the appointed
members shall be three years , except that the terms
of office of the members first appointed by said
Mayor and said County Judge , respectively , shall
be one each for one year , one each for two years
and one each for three years . Upon the expiration
of the term of office of any appointed member ,
his successor shall be appointed within five days
by the Mayor or the County Judge , as the case may
be. Vacancies occurring otherwise than through the
expiration of term of office shall be filled for the un-
expired terms only by the Mayor or the County
Judge , as the case may be . The members of the
Commission shall, before entering upon their duties
as such , qualify by taking the necessary oath of office
before the County Judge of the County Court of such
county . An appointed member may be removed by
the appointing officer , the Mayor or the County
Judge , as the case may be , for inefficiency , neglect
of duty, malfeasance , or improper conduct . The ap-

CHAPTER 176 713
pointing officer shall file with the Commission a
written statement setting forth the reasons for such
removal.
Each member of the Commission , except ex offi-
cio members , shall receive the sum of ten ( $10.00 )
dollars for each meeting of the Commission
attended by such member , but no member of said.
Commission shall be paid more than two hundred
and fifty ( $ 250.00 ) dollars during any fiscal year
of the Commission , nor for more than twenty -five
meetings held during any fiscal year of said Com-
mission.
(2) Organization and Rules . The Commission
shall elect , from among appointed members , a
Chairman and Vice -Chairman . In the absence of
the Chairman , or if and when there is no Chairman ,
the Vice -Chairman shall act as and have all the pow-
ers and duties of the Chairman . The terms of the
Chairman and Vice -Chairman shall each be for one
year . The Commission shall hold at least one regu-
lar meeting in each month . It shall adopt rules and
regulations for the transaction of business and shall
keep minutes and records of all of its proceedings ,
showing its resolutions , transactions , findings and
determinations and the vote of each member on each
question , or if absent or failing to vote , indicating
such fact , all of which shall be immediately filed in
the office of the Commission and shall be public
records . Both the ex officio members and the ap-
pointed members , except the Chairman shall be en-
titled to vote on any subject or matter before the
Commission .
In case of a tie vote or deadlock , the Chairman
shall have the power to vote and shall cast his vote.
and break said tie vote or deadlock .
( 3 ) Staff . The Commission is authorized to em-
ploy and appoint and fix reasonable salaries to be

714 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
paid to such employees as it may deem necessary
for its work , whose appointment , promotion , de-
motion , and removal shall be subject to such rules
and regulations not contrary to law as the Commis-
sion may adopt , but all salaries and compensation
shall be as nearly as practicable comparable with
salaries and compensation paid to corresponding or
comparable employees or such city and county . The
Commission is authorized to employ or contract
with planners, engineers , architects , and other con-
sultants for such services as it may require .
(4 ) Finances . The Commission shall and is
hereby declared to be the custodian of all funds ,
both as to said Commission and said Board , re-
ceived from any and every source for the purposes
of carrying out the provisions of this act ; and all
expenditures of said funds shall be made only by
order of said Commission by resolution duly ap-
proving same . All expenditures shall be made only
by checks drawn by the Commission . The Commis-
sion shall cause an annual audit to be made of all
its receipts and expenditures by an official of such
city or county , or by an auditor or accountant ap-
proved by the Mayor of said city and County Judge
of said County . The Commission shall annually
select a bank or banks for the current deposits of
the Commission's funds for the current operating
expenses , which bank or banks shall give and main-
tain a bond or other form of security to be approved
by said Mayor and said County Judge .
Neither the Commission nor the Board shall
incur or permit the incurrence of any indebtedness
in any manner or for any purpose in an amount ex-
ceeding , in any fiscal year of the Commission or
Board , the income and revenue provided for such
year through the levy of taxes and appropriation of
funds by the legislative body of such city and the

CHAPTER 176 715
Fiscal Court of such county for the purposes of
this act and other funds derived from any other
sources ; and any indebtedness contracted in viola-
tion thereof shall be void and neither said county
nor said city shall be liable therefor .
§ 3. Organization of City and County Board of
Zoning Adustment and Appeals .
( 1 ) Appointment and Compensation of Board .
The Board , when created , shall consist of five mem-
bers to be appointed immediately upon this act be-
coming effective , none of whom shall be a member
of the Commission or hold any other public office
or public position . One member shall be an archi-
tect of not less than ten years ' professional expe-
rience , one shall be a structural engineer of not less
than ten years ' professional experience , one shall be
a real estate dealer of not less than ten years ' expe-
rience , and two shall be appointed at large . The
Mayor of such city shall appoint the structural en-
gineer , the real estate dealer and one member at
large . The structural engineer , the real estate
dealer and the member at large , first appointed by
the Mayor , shall serve for terms of one, three and
four years , respectively . Thereafter the terms of
office of such members , shall be four years . The
County Judge of such county shall appoint the
architect and one member at large . The architect
and the member at large , first appointed by the
County Judge , shall serve for terms of four and two
years , respectively . Thereafter the terms of office
of all such members shall be four years . Upon the
expiration of the term of office of any member , the
successor to such member shall be appointed within
five days , by the Mayor or the County Judge , as the
case may be . Vacancies occurring otherwise than

716 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
through expiration of term of office shall be filled
for the unexpired terms only by the Mayor or the
County Judge , as the case may be .
The members of the Board shall , before entering
upon their duties as such , qualify by taking the
necessary oath of office before the County Judge
of the County Court of such County . Members may
be removed by the appointing officer , the Mayor or
the County Judge , as the case may be , for ineffi-
ciency , neglect of duty , malfeasance , or improper
conduct . The appointing officer shall file with the
Board a written statement setting forth the reasons
for such removal . Each member of the Board shall
receive the sum of ten ( $ 10.00 ) dollars for each of
the Board meetings attended by such member , but
no member of said Board shall be paid more than
two hundred and fifty ( $ 250.00 ) dollars during any
fiscal year of the Board , nor for more than twenty-
five ( 25 ) meetings held during any fiscal year of said
Board .
(2 ) Organization and Rules . The Board shall
elect from among its members a Chairman and
Vice -Chairman . In the absence of the Chairman , or
if and when there is no Chairman , the Vice -Chair-
man shall act as and have all of the powers and
duties of the Chairman . The terms of the Chairman
and Vice -Chairman shall each be for one year . The
Board shall hold at least one regular meeting in
each month . It shall adopt rules and regulations for
the transaction of business and shall keep minutes
and records of all of its proceedings , showing its
resolutions , transactions, findings and determina-
tions , and the vote of each member on each ques-
tion, or if absent or failing to vote , indicating such
fact, all of which shall be immediately filed in the
office of the Board and shall be public records .
(3 ) Staff and Finances . The Board is author-

CHAPTER 176 717
ized to employ and appoint and fix reasonable
salaries to be paid to such employees as it may
deem necessary for its work , whose appointment ,
promotion , demotion , or removal , shall be subject to
such rules and regulations not contrary to law as
the Board may adopt , but all salaries and compen-
sation shall be as nearly as practicable comparable
with salaries and compensation paid to correspond-
ing or comparable employees of such city and
county . Operating expenses of the Board and com-
pensation of Board members shall be paid by the
Commission from the Commission's funds .
$ 4. General Powers and Duties of Commission .
It shall be the function and duty of the Commission
to prepare and approve a comprehensive plan for
the physical development of the incorporated and
unincorporated areas of the entire county , and to
submit and recommend same to said Fiscal Court
and to the legislative body of said city for adoption
or rejection .
Said comprehensive plan shall show and include
( a ) a master plan , as defined and provided for in
Section 5 of this act ; (b) a zoning plan and regula-
tions and restrictions relating thereto , as defined
and provided for in Section 8 of this act ; and ( c )
subdivision control regulations and restrictions
relating thereto , as defined and provided for in Sec-
tion 11 of this act.
Such comprehensive plan shall be made with the
general purpose of guiding and accomplishing a co-
ordinated, adjusted and harmonious development of
the incorporated and unincorporated areas of the
entire county . The Commission shall make careful
and comprehensive surveys and studies of existing
conditions and probable future development of the
entire territory within said county .

718 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
In the preparation of the comprehensive plan ,
and at all times thereafter , it shall be the duty of
the Commission to consult and advise with public
officials and agencies , including the City and
County Board of Health or its successor or suc-
cessors , public utility companies , civic , educational ,
professional and other organizations , and with citi-
zens , regarding the preparation , protection and car-
rying out of the plan . The Commission , its mem-
bers , officers , and employees , in the performance
of their duties and functions , may enter upon any
land and make examinations and surveys , which do
not occasion damage or injury to private property .
In general , the Commission shall have such powers
as may be necessary to enable it to fulfill its duties
and functions , to promote municipal and county
planning , and to carry out the purposes of this act .
The Commission shall , from time to time , recom-
mend to the appropriate public officials programs
for the acquisition of land and the construction of
public structures and improvements , and for the
financing thereof .
The Commission shall have power to promote
public interest in and an understanding of the com-
prehensive plan , and part and parts thereof , and to
that end may publish and distribute copies thereof ,
or of any report relating thereto , and may employ
such other means of publicity and education as it may
determine . Members of the Commission and any of
its employees , when duly authorized by the Fiscal
Court of such county and the legislative body of
such city , may attend city or county planning con-
ferences or meetings , or legislative hearings upon
pending city or county planning legislation , and the
expenses for such attendance shall be borne and
paid in the manner provided by such authorization .

CHAPTER 176 719
§ 5. Preparation and Adoption of Master Plan .
(1 ) The master plan , which is a part of the
comprehensive plan , for the development of the ter-
ritory covered thereby , with accompanying maps ,
plats , charts , descriptive and explanatory matter ,
shall show and include the general location , char-
acter and extent of public streets , roads , major
streets and arterial highways , viaducts , bridges ,
waterways and water fronts , parkways , and other
public ways , public playgrounds , reservations , parks ,
airports and other public grounds , places and build-
ings ; the general location and extent of public utili-
ties and terminals , whether publicly or privately
owned , for water , light , power , heat , sanitation ,
transportation , communication , and other purposes ;
the proposed dedication , widening , removal , ex-
tension , relocation , narrowing , vocation , aban-
donment or change of use of the aforesaid public
ways , grounds , places , buildings , properties and
utilities or terminals ; the general character , location.
and extent of community centers , town sites or hous-
ing developments ; the general location and extent
of forest and open -development areas for purposes
of conservation of food, soil , and water supply , sani-
tary and drainage facilities , and for the protection
of urban and non -urban development .
(2 ) The Commission may prepare and approve
the master plan as a whole , or as the work of mak-
ing the whole plan progresses may from time to
time prepare and approve a part or parts thereof ,
which part or parts shall be coordinated generally
with one or more or all of the functional divisions
of the subject matter of the whole plan . Any such
part or parts may include such portion or portions
of the county as the Commission deems an appro-
priate territorial unit for such separate treatment
by reason of urbanization , municipal boundary

720 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
lines , the location of sanitary districts or public
or semi -public establishments , or other factors af-
fecting the need for the protection of the public
welfare .
(3 ) After the preparation of such master plan ,
or part of parts thereof, but before approval there-
of by the Commission , the Commission shall hold at
least one public hearing thereon , notice of the time
and place of which shall be given by the Commission
by publishing a notice at least once weekly for two
consecutive weeks in a daily newspaper having gen-
eral circulation throughout such city and county ,
and by such other means as the Commission may
determine . Such notice shall contain all such neces-
sary information as will adequately acquaint the
public with the purpose of the plan, or part or parts
thereof , as the case may be , the areas affected
thereby , and where said plan can be inspected .
Within ninety ( 90 ) days after any such hearing the
Commission shall approve such master plan , or part
or parts thereof , as the case may be , as prepared ,
or the Commission may revise same . If the Com-
mission revises same , it shall hold a hearing on such
revised plan , and give notice thereof , in the same
manner as provided for the original hearing . And
such procedure shall be continued until the Commis-
sion has made no further revision of such plan , or
part or parts thereof , subsequent to the last hearing
thereon . The Commission shall certify , transmit and
recommend such approved master plan, or part or
parts thereof, as the case may be , to the Fiscal Court
of such county and to the legislative body of such city
for adoption or rejection by them . Adoption thereof
may be by a majority vote of all of the members of
such Fiscal Court or such legislative body . Rejec-

CHAPTER 176 721
tion thereof shall be only by a two -thirds (2/3 ) vote
of all of the members of such Fiscal Court or legis-
lative body .
If said master plan, or any part or parts thereof ,
is rejected by said Fiscal Court or said legislative
body , such plan , or part or parts thereof , shall be
referred back to the Commission , together with a
written statement of ( a ) the reasons for such rejec-
tion and (b ) suggested corrections or changes , for
further study , revision and recommendation . There-
upon said Commission shall hold further hearings
and follow the same procedure as herein above pro-
vided until the Commission has made no further
revisions thereof , subsequent to the last hearing
thereon . The Commission then shall again certify
and recommend said plan , or part or parts thereof ,
as approved by the Commission , to said Fiscal
Court or legislative body , as the case may be , for
adoption or rejection in the manner above pro-
vided . If same is again rejected the same procedure
as above provided shall be followed until such mas-
ter plan , or part or parts thereof, is adopted .
(4 ) Certified copies , including all maps , charts
and descriptive matter , and all regulations and re-
strictions , composing said master plan, or part or
parts thereof , adopted by said Fiscal Court or said
legislative body , shall be filed in the office of the
Commission and filed and recorded in the office of the
clerk of the county court of such county in a man-
ner easily accessible to the public , and same shall
be public records .
§ 6. Adjustment in Master Plan .
( 1 ) After the original master plan , or part or
parts thereof, as the case may be , has been adopted
as provided in Section 5 of this act , all adjustments
therein or thereto shall become effective only by and
through the approval of such proposed adjustments

722 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
by the legislative body of such city or the Fiscal
Court of such county , or both , as the case may be , or
by the Commission if and when authorized to do so
by general ordinance and order of said legislative
body and said Fiscal Court , and by recording same in
the office of the clerk of the county court of such
county . After any such proposed adjustment has
been approved , the master plan , or part or parts
thereof, as the case may be , shall be deemed to be
and be the plan , or part or parts thereof , as the
case may be, as so adjusted . Said master plan and
said adjustments , when approved as provided in
this act , shall not be subject to attack in any court
in such county inferior to the circuit court thereof.
( 2) The Commission may itself propose , or
any person , firm , corporation , or any governmental
agency , board or body , may propose adjustments in
or to the master plan , or part or parts thereof , as
the case may be , which proposals shall be approved
or disapproved in the manner authorized by sub-
section ( 1 ) of this Section 6 of this act .
( 3 ) Before any proposed adjustment is ap-
proved or disapproved such proposal shall be the
subject of a public hearing before the Commission .
The Commission shall conduct such public hearing
in the manner provided , in subsection 3 of Section 5
of this act , for public hearings on the original mas-
ter plan , or part or parts thereof . Any person , firm ,
or corporation , or any governmental agency , board
or body , interested and affected by such proposed
adjustment may enter his or its appearance before
the Commission at or before such hearing , and shall
be heard .
( 4 ) If approval of such proposed adjustment or
adjustments is by said legislative body or said
Fiscal Court , or both , as the case may be , the Com-
mission shall certify and transmit such proposal ,

CHAPTER 176 723
together with its recommendations thereon , to said
legislative body or Fiscal Court , as the case may be.
Thereafter the procedure for approval thereof shall
be the same as that provided for the adoption of
the master plan as set forth in subsection 3 of Sec-
tion 5 of this act .
( 5 ) ( a ) If and when said Fiscal Court and
said legislative body authorize the Commission to
approve or disapprove proposed adjustments in or
to the master plan , or part or parts thereof, as the
case may be , the approval or disapproval thereof by
the Commission shall be in the manner provided in
subsection 6 of this Section 6 of this act .
(b ) In approving or disapproving any proposed
adjustment in or to the master plan , or part or parts
thereof , the Commission shall be guided and gov-
erned by the purposes and provisions of this act ,
and its action shall be particularly in accordance
with the provisions and purposes set forth in Sec-
tions 4 and 5 of this act , to the end that said master
plan , or part or parts thereof , as the case may be ,
as so adjusted shall accomplish a corordinated and
harmonious development of the incorporated and
unincorporated area of the entire county .
( c ) If the proposed adjustment is finally ap-
proved by the Commission , it shall be recorded as
provided in subsection ( 1 ) of this Section 6 of this
act . If it is finally disapproved by the Commission ,
the proposed adjustment shall not be recorded and
shall have no force or effect .
( 6 ) If the Commission is authorized by said
Fiscal Court and said legislative body to approve
such proposed adjustment or adjustments :
( a) The Commission , within ninety days after
the hearing provided for in subsection 3 of this
Section 6 of this act , shall either approve or revise.
or disapprove such proposal or proposals .

724 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(b) If the Commission revises same , it shall hold
a hearing on such proposal as revised , and give
notice thereof , in the same manner as provided for
the original hearing . And such procedure shall be
continued until the Commission has made no further
revision thereof , subsequent to the last hearing
thereon .
(c ) When the Commission has finally approved
such proposed adjustment , as submitted or revised ,
or has disapproved such proposed adjustment , it
shall immediately notify in writing all parties of
record in such proceeding of its action or decision .
(d ) Any person , firm , corporation , organiza-
tion , public official or governmental agency or de-
partment , being a party of record at such hearing ,
claiming to be injuriously affected or aggrieved by
any action or decision by the Commission , may
appeal from such action or decision , to the circuit
court of such county , and jurisdiction is hereby given
to such circuit court to hear and determine all ques-
tions and issues properly brought before it on such
appeal . An appeal shall stay all action by all parties
of record in the matter appealed from . Such ap-
peals shall be taken within thirty ( 30 ) days after
notice of such action or decision has been given , as
herein provided , by filing in the office of the clerk
of the circuit court of such county a statement of
appeal , setting forth the action or decision of the
Commission appealed from , the date thereon , the
reasons for said appeal , and attaching thereto as a
part thereof a certified copy of such action or de-
cision , and asking that an order to show cause be
issued against and served upon the Commission .
But the foregoing remedy shall not be exclusive .
(e ) After the appeal is taken the procedure
shall be the same as in common law actions , and all
parties to the proceeding shall have the right to any

CHAPTER 176 725
and all processes , including subpoena , allowed in
other cases in said circuit court. Upon filing an ap-
peal , notice thereof must be immediately given to the
Commission and to all parties of record , by actual
notice served or by service of summons . Hearings in
the circuit court shall be de novo and heard by the
Judge , and appeals may be taken from the decision
of said circuit court to the Court of Appeals of Ken-
tucky in the same manner as common law cases are
appealed .
(f) If the Commission approves such proposed
adjustment and no appeal is taken , said approval
shall be final . If the Commission approves such
proposed adjustment and an appeal is taken and
said action or decision is affirmed , or is remanded
with directions , then said action of the Commission
as affirmed , or as revised or modified in accordance
with the Court's decision , as the case may be , shall
be final . When the adjustment , as submitted or as
revised , as the case may be , is so finally approved ,
it shall be recorded in the manner provided in sub-
section ( 1 ) of this Section 6 of this act .
(g ) If the Commission approves such proposed
adjustment and an appeal is taken and said action
or decision is reversed and the court disapproves
said proposal , said proposed adjustment shall not
be recorded and shall have no force or effect .
(h ) If the Commission disapproves such pro-
posed adjustment and no appeal is taken , such
action shall be final . If the Commission disap-
proves such proposed adjustment and an appeal is
taken and said action is affirmed , such action shall
be final . Any proposed adjustment thus finally dis-
approved shall not be recorded and shall have no
force or effect .
(i ) If the Commission disapproves such pro-
posed adjustment and an appeal is taken and said

726 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
action is reversed and the court approves said pro-
posed adjustment or remands the case with direc-
tions to revise or modify said proposal , then said
proposed adjustment as so approved or as so re-
vised , as the case may be, shall be deemed to be and
be finally approved , and shall be recorded as pro-
vided in subsection ( 1 ) of this Section 6 of this act .
$ 7. Master Plan- Public Projects , Authoriza-
tion and Construction Under .
(1 ) (a ) Upon this act becoming effective , as
provided in Section 1 and subsection ( 6 ) of Section
9 of this act , no public street , public roadway, public
parkway , public ground , public place , public build-
ing or structure , or public utility , publicly or pri-
vately owned , shall be constructed or authorized
within the incorporated boundaries of such city or
in the unincorporated territory of such county ,
until and unless the proposed location and extent
of such public construction or authorization shall
have been approved and authorized by appropriate
ordinances , resolutions or orders passed by the leg-
islative body of such city or the Fiscal Court of such
county , as the case may be , or by order of the Com-
mission if and when said legislative body and said
Fiscal Court , by appropriate ordinances , resolu-
tions or orders , authorize said Commission to
so act .
ΟΙ(b) Before any proposed construction
authorization is approved or disapproved such pro-
posal shall be submitted to and be the subject of a
public hearing before the Commission for its recom-
mendations . The Commission shall hold at least
one public hearing on any such proposal , notice of
which shall be given by the Commission in such
manner as will be adequate to inform all parties
interested and affected thereby and not less than
one week prior to such hearing . Any and all parties

CHAPTER 176 727
interested and affected may enter their appearance
before the Commission at or before such hearing ,
and shall be heard .
( 2 ) If approval of such proposed construction
or authorization is made by said legislative body or
said Fiscal Court , as the case may be , the Commis-
sion shall certify and transmit such proposal , to-
gether with its recommendation thereon , to said
legislative body or Fiscal Court , as the case may be.
Thereafter the proceeding for approval thereof
shall be the same as that provided for the adoption
of the master plan as set forth in subsection 3 of
Section 5 of this act .
( 3 ) ( a ) If and when said Fiscal Court and
said legislative body authorize the Commission to
approve or disapprove any such proposed construc-
tion or authorization , the Commission shall hold
at least one public hearing thereon , adequate notice
of which shall be given by the Commission not less
than one week prior to such hearing .
(b ) Within thirty days after such hearing , the
Commission shall either approve or disapprove
such proposed construction or authorization . If the
Commission approves the proposed construction for
authorization , it shall so notify all parties of record
and the proposer may proceed to carry out the pro-
posal unless an appeal is taken as granted in para-
graph (d ) of this subsection 3 of this Section 7 of
this act . If the Commission disapproves such pro-
posal , it shall set forth in writing the reasons for its
disapproval and notify all parties of record .
(c) In approving or disapproving any proposed
construction or authorization , the Commission shall
be guided and governed by the purposes and pro-
visions of this act , to the end that said purposes and

728 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
a coordinated and harmonious development of the
incorporated and unincorporated areas may be ac-
complished and attained .
( d ) Any party of record to such proceeding ,
claiming to be injured or aggrieved by such ap-
proval or disapproval , shall have the right to ap-
peal therefrom in the same manner as provided in
paragraphs ( d ) and ( c ) of subsection 6 of Section
6 of this act with reference to appeals from actions
or decisions of the Commission . But the foregoing
remedy shall not be exclusive . An appeal shall stay
all action by all parties of record in the matter ap-
pealed from .
( e ) If the Commission's approval is appealed
from and is affirmed or remanded with directions ,
such proposal as approved or as revised in accord-
ance wtih the court's directions may be carried out .
If the Commission's approval appealed from is
reversed and the court disapproves same , such pro-
posal shall not be carried out . If reversed with
directions, then and in that event the proposer may
proceed to carry out the proposal as revised in
accordance with the court's decision .
(f ) If the Commission's disapproval is not ap-
pealed from , or if appealed from is affirmed , such
proposal shall not be carried out . If the Commis-
sion's disapproval is appealed from and is reversed
or remanded with directions , such proposal as sub-
mitted or as revised in accordance with the court's
decision may be carried out .
(4 ) The approval or disapproval of any pro-
posed dedication , widening, removal , extension , re-
location , narrowing , vacation , abandonment , use of
or change of use of, or acquisition or sale of land
for, any public street , public road , public parkway ,
public ground , public place , public building or struc-
ture , or public utility , publicly or privately owned ,

CHAPTER 176 729
shall be subject to the same procedure provided , in
this Section 7 of this act , for the approval or dis-
approval of proposed constructions or authori-
zations .
§ 8. Zoning Plan and Regulations —Noncon-
forming Uses—Conflicts —Setback Lines on
Streets .
( 1 ) The original zoning plan , which is a part of
the comprehensive plan , shall show the boundaries
of the various districts and include appropriate
regulations and restrictions to be enforced therein.
to avoid congestion in streets , to secure safety from
fire , panic and other dangers , to provide adequate
light and air , to prevent the over -crowding of land ,
to avoid undue concentration of population , to
facilitate adequate provision for traffic , transporta-
tion , water , sewerage , schools , parks and other public
requirements , to encourage the most appropriate
use of land throughout such city and county with
reasonable consideration to be given to the char-
acter of any district and its peculiar suitability for
particular uses , and with a view to conserving the
value of land, buildings and other structures ; and
shall be prepared , adopted and recorded in the
manner provided in Section 5 of this act , and ad-
justed in the manner provided in Section 6 of this
act , for the preparation , adoption and adjustment
of the master plan .
( 2 ) For the aforesaid purposes , the legislative
body of such city and the Fiscal Court of such coun-
ty , respectively , are hereby empowered to regulate ,
within their respective jurisdictions , the location ,
height , bulk , number of stories , and size of buildings
and other structures , the percentage of lot area
which may be occupied by buildings or structures ,
the size of yards , courts and other open spaces ,
building setback lines on streets and highways , and

730 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the future location and width of streets and high-
ways , the density of population , the erection , use
and occupancy of buildings or structures , and the
use of land, for trade , industry or residence , or other
purposes . Except as to said setback lines and the
future location and width of streets and highways ,
the foregoing enumeration of powers shall not apply
to land used solely for agricultural , farming , dairy-
ing , stock -raising or similar purposes , nor buildings
nor structures thereon , but if any portion of any
such land or any building or structure on such land
is used for commercial or business purposes then
that portion of the land and any such building or
structure shall be subject to all of the above regu-
lations .
Such county and such city may be divided into dis-
tricts , which may be of such number , shape and area
as is deemed best suited to carry out the purposes
of this act . Regulations shall be uniform for each
class or kind of building throughout each district ,
but the regulations in one district may differ from
those in other districts .
(3 ) Except as otherwise provided herein the
lawful use of land for trade, industry or residence ,
existing at the time of adoption of any zoning regu-
lation or restriction , or at the time of the adjust-
ment or revision thereof , or amendment thereto , al-
though such use does not conform to the provisions
of such new regulations or restrictions , may be con-
tinued . If such non -conforming use is discontinued
for a period of one year , without the owner thereof
making a reasonable effort to resume such non-
conforming use or to lease such property for the
same or a more restricted use , any future use of
such land shall be in conformity with the provisions
of such then existing zoning regulations or restric-

CHAPTER 176 731
tions , and other restrictions running with or per-
taining to such land , whether by deed, contract , or
otherwise .
( 4) Except as otherwise provided herein , the
use of a building or structure existing at the time
of the adoption of any zoning regulation or restric-
tion , or at the time of any adjustment or revision
thereof or amendment thereto , although such use
does not conform to the provisions of such new
regulations or restrictions , may be continued , if no
structural alterations or additions are made thereto
except those required by some law or ordinance
other than zoning regulations or restrictions . If no
structural alterations or additions are made , a non-
conforming use of a building or structure may be
changed to another non -conforming use of the same
or more restricted classification . If such non -con-
forming building or structure is removed , damaged ,
or destroyed to the extent of more than 75 % of its
reasonable value , or if such non -conforming use is
discontinued for a period of one year, without the
owner thereof making a reasonable effort to re-
sume such non - conforming use or to lease such
premises for the same or a more restricted use ,
every future use of such premises shall be in con-
formity with the then existing zoning regulations
adopted in accordance with the provisions of this
act , and with all other restrictions running with
or pertaining to such premises , whether by deed,
contract , or otherwise . Nothing herein shall be
deemed to prevent the restoration of a building or
structure damaged or destroyed to the extent of not
more than 75 % of its reasonable value , by fire , ex-
plosion or other casualty , or the public enemy , or
prevent the continued occupancy or use of such
building or structure, or part thereof, remaining .
(5) Said zoning regulations and restrictions

732 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
may contain reasonable provisions for the eventual
termination of non -conforming uses either by speci-
fying the period or periods in which non - conform-
ing uses shall be required to cease , or by providing
a formula or formulae whereby the compulsory
termination of non -conforming uses may be so
reasonably fixed as to allow for the amortization of
the then investment in the non -conforming property
or use . The foregoing provisions shall also apply to
non -conforming uses created by subsequent changes
in district boundaries .
(6 ) Whenever said zoning regulations and re-
strictions require a greater width or size of yards ,
courts , or other open space , or requires a greater
percentage of lot to be left unoccupied , or imposes
other higher standards than are required in or
under any other restriction or covenant , the pro-
visions of such zoning regulations or restrictions
shall govern . Whenever the provisions of any other
restriction or covenant require a greater width or
size of yards , courts , or other open space , or require
a greater percentage of lot to be left unoccupied , or
impose other higher standards than are required
by said zoning regulations and restrictions , the
provisions of such other restriction or covenant
shall govern.
(7) If the owner of a lot does not own any parcel
or tract of land immediately adjacent thereto , and if
the deed or instrument under which such owner
acquired title to such lot was of record in the office
of the clerk of the county court of such county prior
to the adoption of any subdivision control and
zoning regulations and restrictions , adopted pur-
suant to this act , and if such lot does not conform
to the requirements of such regulations and restric-
tions as to width of lots and lot area per family ,
nothing herein contained or in such adopted regu-

CHAPTER 176 733
lations and restrictions shall prevent the owner
thereof from erecting or making improvements on
said lot which improvements conform in all other
respects to such regulations and restrictions .
9. Enforcement of Zoning Regulations .
( 1 ) The Commission is authorized to appoint
and fix the compensation of one or more officers
or it may designate one or more officials of such
city or county , to be called " Zoning Enforcement
Officer ' , to administer and enforce zoning regu-
lations and restrictions , and to issue zoning per-
mits and certificates of occupancy . The Commis-
sion may fix a reasonable schedule of fees for the
issuance of such permits and certificates .
"
( 2 ) It shall be unlawful to erect , construct , re-
construct , alter , or change the use of any building
or structure used for trade , industry , or residence ,
or change the use of land , within the respective ter-
ritorial jurisdictions of such city or county , without
first obtaining a zoning permit or certificate of
occupancy from the Zoning Enforcing Officer .
Such officer shall not issue any such permit or
certificate unless the plans of and for the proposed
erection , construction , reconstruction , alteration ,
repair or use of structures or buildings and the pro-
posed use of land fully conform with all zoning regu-
lations or restrictions in effect in the location .
(3 ) No such permit for the erection of any
building or structure on private property shall be
issued unless such private property fronts on an
existing or proposed public street , public highway
or public road shown on the master plan and map ,
which gives access to the land upon which said pro-
posed structure is to be erected , or unless the ap-
plicant for such permit establishes to the satisfac-
tion of the Zoning Enforcement Officer or the
Board , that there is of record in the County an

734 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
easement providing for a right of ingress and
egress to the private property on which applicant
proposes to erect improvements , or that by pre-
scription , such applicant has such right of ingress
and egress to said property .
(4) It shall be unlawful to erect any building or
structure, or improvements or additions thereto , on
property lying within a building set back area on
any street or highway , existing or proposed , made a
part of the master plan . Upon the application to
the Zoning Enforcement Officer for zoning permit
to erect such building , structure , improvement , or
additions thereto within said building set back area ,
it shall be the duty of such officer to refuse such
permit . But if such restriction prevents the yield.
of a fair return on such property , or where the
enforcement of such regulations and restrictions
would result in peculiar and exceptional practical
difficulties , or exceptional hardship upon the owners
of such property , the applicant for such a permit
may appeal from such refusal to the Board of
Zoning Adjustment and Appeals for relief in the
same manner as provided in Section 10 of this act .
Such Board shall hold a hearing on such appeal and
in a specific case , where the facts and circumstances
warrant , may grant a zoning permit for such build-
ing or structure , or improvement or addition
thereto , which will as little as practicable increase
the cost of widening , establishing or constructing
such street or highway, or tend to cause a change in
the master plan ; and may impose reasonable re-
quirements as a condition of granting such a per-
mit , which requirements shall inure to the benefit
of such county or such city in which such building ,
structure, improvement or addition is proposed to
be located . The hearing by the Board shall be
public and upon notice , as in zoning cases , and any

CHAPTER 176 735
decision made by the Board shall be subject to
review in the same manner as provided in Section
10 of this act .
(5 ) It shall be unlawful to construct in any
street or highway any building , structure , improve-
ment , sewer or utility , publicly or privately owned ,
until and after such street or highway has been
made a part of the master plan and until a permit
therefor has been duly issued by the proper
authority . But the granting or issuing of such
permit shall not affect the power of any govern-
mental agency , whose duty it is to make , repair or
maintain an existing street , road or highway , or
whose duty it may be or will be to construct , repair
or maintain the proposed street , road or highway ,
if and when constructed , ( a ) to require reimburse-
ment from such permittee for any expenditures made
by such agency due to the exercise of such permit ,
or (b) to determine where and how such sewers ,
pipes , lines , poles , conduits or other type or charac-
ter of construction are to be placed in said existing ,
projected or proposed street , road or highway , or
( c ) to require that all work and installations shall
be done under the supervision of such governmental
agency , or any one designated by such govern-
mental agency . The granting or issuing of any
such permit shall not in any way affect the right , if
any , of any owner of private property to demand
and receive compensation from the permittee for
damage to such property caused by such use ,
whether the right to such use as distinguished from
such permit , be acquired by purchase , lease , con-
demnation , or otherwise .
(6 ) Sections 7 , 8, and 9 of this act shall not be
effective or operative in any part of the incorpo
rated or unincorporated areas until the master plan
or part or parts thereof and the zoning plans and

736 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
regulations provided for in Section 4 of this act
shall have been approved and adopted either in
whole or in part as provided for in Section 5 of this
act . When said master plan and maps have been
approved and adopted as herein provided , then the
provisions of Sections 7 , 8 , and 9 shall be applicable
only to the area covered and included in the said
master plan, zoning plan , and maps , or part or
parts of such plans or maps and shall not be effec-
tive or operative in any section or area until such
section or area is included in and covered by such
plans and maps whether approved and adopted as
a whole or in part or parts as provided in Section
5. However , the provisions of Section 11 of this
act shall be effective from and after the Commis
sion has been appointed and organized .
Section 10. Appeals to and from Board of Zon-
ing Adjustment and Appeals-powers and duties of
board- rights of parties and other persons .
"
( 1 ) Appeals to the Board may be taken by any
person , firm , corporation , organization , or any gov-
ernmental official , board, or department , being a
party of record , hereinafter called " Appellant ”
claiming to be injuriously affected or aggrieved by
any official action or decision of any Zoning En-
forcement Officer . Such appeal shall be taken
within thirty ( 30 ) days after the Appellant or his
agent receives actual notice of the action appealed
from , by filing with said officer and with the Board
a notice of appeal specifying the grounds thereof,
and giving notice of such appeal to any and all
parties of record . Said officer shall forthwith trans-
mit to the Board all the papers constituting the
record upon which the action appealed from was
taken and shall be treated as and be the respondent
in such further proceedings . At any hearing by
the Board any interested person may appear and

CHAPTER 176 737
enter his appearance , and all parties of record may
appear in person or by agent or attorney , and shall
be given an opportunity to be heard .
(2 ) An appeal shall stay all action by all parties
of record in the matter appealed from , including
the Zoning Enforcement Officer , unless said officer ,
or a party of record , certifies to the Board reasons
and facts showing that a stay would cause imminent
peril to life or property , or irreparable damage to
property . In which event , action shall not be
stayed except by an order of the Board or of the
Circuit Court of such county , on application or
motion and upon good cause shown by any person
claiming to be injured or aggrieved by such official
action or decision , for a stay order and , in either
instance , upon at least twenty -four ( 24 ) hours
written notice to all parties of record , including the
Zoning Enforcement Officer , of such application or
motion . The said court or Board , as the case may
be , shall fix a reasonable time for the hearing of
such application or motion , and shall decide same
within a reasonable time .
(3 ) The Board shall have the following powers
and duties :
(a ) to hold hearings on and hear and decide
appeals taken pursuant to subsection ( 1 ) of this
Section 10 of this act ; to affirm or reverse , in whole
or in part , or modify the order , requirement , de-
cision or determination of the Zoning Enforcement .
Officer , from which the appeal is taken ; and to make
and enforce , by all lawful means , such order , re-
quirement , decision or determination , with respect
to the matter in question , as the Board feels may
be just, and to that end the Board , in addition to its
other powers , shall have the same powers as the
Zoning Enforcement Officer .
Acts- 24

738 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(b) To receive and hold hearings on and decide
petitions for special exceptions to the requirements
of zoning regulations or restrictions , or for the in-
terpretation of zoning district maps , or for de-
cisions upon other special zoning questions upon
which the Board is authorized to pass .
( c ) To hold hearings on petitions for and to
authorize variances from a strict application of any
zoning regulation or restriction so as to relieve
difficulties or hardships in cases when and where ,
by reason of exceptional narrowness , shallowness ,
or shape of a specific piece of property at the time
of the enactment of such regulation or restriction ,
or by reason of exceptional topographical condi-
tions or other extraordinary and exceptional situa-
tions or conditions of such piece of property , the
strict application of such regulation or restriction
would result in peculiar and exceptional practical
difficulties to , or exceptional hardship upon , the
owner of such property . Such grant of variance
shall comply as nearly as possible in every respect
with the spirit , intent and purpose of the zoning
plan ; it being the purpose of this provision to
authorize the granting of variations only for
reasons of demonstrable and exceptional hardship
as distinguished from variations sought by appli-
cants for purposes or reasons of convenience , profit
or caprice .
(d ) To hold hearings and , in specific and proper
cases , to grant relief , as provided for in subsec-
tion (4 ) of Section 9 of this act , in appeals from
refusals of the Zoning Enforcement Officer to grant
zoning permits to erect buildings and structures , or
improvements or additions thereto , on property
lying within any building setback area , or within
the boundaries of any proposed street or highway ,
as the case may be.

CHAPTER 176 739
( e ) All meetings and hearings of the Board
shall be open to the public . The concurring vote of
a majority of the entire membership of the Board
shall be necessary in making any decision . The
Board in the exercise and performance of its
powers and duties is authorized to subpoena and
examine witnesses , and certify official acts and issue
appropriate orders . The members of the Board are
authorized to administer oaths in relation to mat-
ters pending before the Board .
(f ) In case of a failure on the part of any person
or persons , firm or corporation , or governmental
agency , to comply with any lawful order of the
Board or with process , or in case of refusal of any
witness to testify concerning any matter on which
he may be lawfully interrogated , the circuit court.
or a judge thereof having jurisdiction in such
county , may upon application by the Board or any
member of the Board , compel obedience to such
order or process by proceedings for contempt as in
the case of a disobedience of the requirements of a
subpoena issued from such court , or the refusal to
testify therein .
(g ) It shall be the duty of the Board to decide
promptly , consistent with justice , all appeals or
petitions filed with the Board . The Board shall
immediately notify in writing all parties of record
of its decision and on demand shall furnish them
with copies thereof .
(4 ) Any party or parties of record , jointly or
severally , claiming to be injured or aggrieved by
any decision , order or ruling of said Board may
appeal from such decision , order or ruling , to the
circuit court of such county , and jurisdiction is
hereby given to such circuit court to hear and de-
termine all questions and issues properly brought
before it on such appeal . Such appeals shall be

740 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
taken to said circuit court within 30 days after
notice of such decision , order or ruling has been
given as required , by giving notice of the appeal to
said Board and all other parties of record , and by
filing in the office of the clerk of said circuit court a
statement of appeal , setting forth the decision , or
order , or ruling appealed from , the date thereof ,
and the reason and grounds for said appeal , and
attaching thereto as a part thereof a certified copy
of said decision , order , or ruling .
After the appeal is taken the procedure therein
shall be the same as provided in paragraphs ( d )
and ( e ) of subsection 6 of Section 6 of this act with
reference to appeals from actions or decisions of
the Commission , and the Board and other parties of
record shall have the same rights as given therein
to the Commission and other parties of record .
(5 ) No decision , order or ruling either of the
Zoning Enforcement Officer or of the Board shall be
attacked or questioned collaterally or otherwise in
any court in such city or such county inferior to the
circuit court of such county except by an appeal
from such decision , order or ruling , as provided for
in this Section 10 of this act .
(6 ) Nothing contained in this act shall or shall
be construed to deprive any person , firm or cor-
poration , taxpayer , or governmental official or
agency , claiming to be injured or aggrieved by any
action or decision of either the Commission , or the
Board , or the Zoning Enforcement Officer , of any
right , or to curtail or limit such right , which he or
it may have , to proceed by injunction , mandamus ,
or other appropriate proceeding either in equity or
law .
Section 11. Subdivision control regulations
subdivision plats and approval thereof.
( 1 ) The original subdivision control regulations ,

CHAPTER 176 741
which constitute a part of the comprehensive plan ,
shall provide for : ( a ) The manner in which sub-
division plats shall be submitted to the Commission
for approval and how same shall be approved and
recorded ; ( b ) the proper arrangement of streets ,
roads and highways in relation to other existing or
planned streets or highways ; ( c ) adequate and con-
venient open spaces for traffic , utilities , recreation ,
light and air, and access for firefighting apparatus ;
(d ) the avoidance of congestion of population by
specifying minimum widths and areas of lots ; ( e )
the extent to which streets and other ways shall be
graded and improved and the extent to which water ,
sewer and other utility mains , piping or other facili-
ties shall be installed as a condition precedent to
the approval by the Commission of any subdivision
plat ; and (f ) provisions for the approval of the
plat prior to the completion of such improvements
and utilities . Said regulations shall be prepared
and adopted in the manner provided in Section 5 of
this act , and adjusted in the manner provided in
Section 6 of this act , for the preparation , adoption
and adjustment of the master plan .
( 2) When the Commission has been appointed
and organized , pursuant to the provisions of this
act, any and all subdivision plats shall be filed with
and submitted to the Commission for its approval
or disapproval . Each subdivision plat shall contain
the name and address of the person or persons mak-
ing the subdivision and applying for approval
thereof, and the names and addresses of all owners
of property adjoining or adjacent to the property
to be subdivided . Any agreement between parties
affected by such subdivision in the nature of a re-
striction or covenant affecting or applicable to the
land in the area within or bordering upon such sub-
division shall be filed with and referred to upon the

742 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
plat, and any restrictions or covenants in prior
deeds to such lands shall likewise be referred to
upon said plat . Within thirty days after any such
application the Commission shall hold a public
hearing thereon , and shall, not less than five days
before the date fixed for such hearing , send written
notices thereof to all persons whose names and
addresses are stated on said plat , unless the appli-
cant for such approval and all such persons waive
such notice . The Commission shall have the power
to fix , or to agree with the applicant and the ad-
joining and adjacent property owners upon the use ,
height , area or bulk requirements or restrictions
governing buildings and premises within the sub-
divsion , provided such requirements or restrictions
do not authorize the violation of any then existing
zoning plan , regulation or restriction . Within
thirty ( 30 ) days after such hearing the Commission
shall approve or disapprove such plat , unless addi-
tional time is necessary to enable the Commission
to make a thorough investigation of the proposed
plat . In which event the Commission may extend
such period , but such extension shall not exceed
sixty days .
The Commission's approval or disapproval , as
the case may be , of any subdivision plat shall be
endorsed thereon , and written notice thereof shall
be given by the Commission to all parties of record .
Any person , firm or corporation , being a party of
record to such hearing or matter , claiming to be
injuriously affected or aggrieved by such action of
the Commission may appeal therefrom to the cir-
cuit court of such county . Such appeal and the
procedure thereafter shall be taken in accordance
with and pursuant to the provisions of paragraphs
(d ) and ( e ) of subsection 6 of Section 6 of this act
for appeals from decisions of the Commission .

CHAPTER 176 743
If the Commission approves a subdivision plat
and no appeal is taken , a copy thereof with the en-
dorsement thereon shall be made a part of the Com-
mission's records ; and the Commission shall certify
said plat with the approval thereof and transmit
same to the applicant subdivider who shall have
same recorded as provided in subsection 3 of this
Section 11 of this act . If the Commission disap-
proves a subdivision plat and no appeal is taken ,
said plat shall not be recorded and shall have no
force or effect .
If the Commission approves a subdivision plat
and an appeal is taken and said action is affirmed ,
or is reversed and remanded with directions , then
said plat as affirmed , or as modified and revised in
accordance with the court's decision , as the case
may be , shall be endorsed by the Commission and
made a part of the Commission's records ; and the
Commission shall certify said plat , endorsed as so
approved or so modified and revised , as the case
may be , and transmit same to the applicant sub-
divider who shall have same recorded as herein
provided . If said action is reversed and the court
disapproves said plat , said plat shall not be re-
corded and shall have no force or effect .
If the Commission disapproves a subdivision plat
and an appeal is taken and said action is affirmed ,
said subdivision plat shall not be recorded and shall
have no force or effect . If such action is reversed
and the court approves said subdivision plat , or
remands and directs that said plat be modified or
revised , then said plat so approved , or as so modi-
fied and revised in accordance with the court's de-
cision , as the case may be , shall be made a part of
the Commission's records ; and the Commission
shall certify said plat , endorsed as so approved or
so modified or revised , as the case may be , and

744 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
transmit same to the applicant subdivider who shall
have same recorded as herein provided .
( 3 ) Within ninety days after the approval and
certification of a subdivision plat as herein pro-
vided it shall be the duty of the applicant sub-
divider , at his or its own expense , to file said ap-
proved plat in the office of the clerk of the county
court of such county and cause same to be recorded .
The clerk of the county court shall neither receive
nor record any such plat more than ninety days
after its approval and certification , and shall
neither receive nor record any plat which has not
been approved and certified as provided in this act ,
nor shall any clerk of the county court either receive
or record any deed or mortgage in which the prop-
erty therein described and conveyed is referred to
or dscribed as being a part or parcel of or shown by
any plat or map of a subdivision unless said plat or
map is duly and properly recorded in said clerk's
office . Any clerk violating this provision shall be
guilty of a misedemeanor and upon conviction.
thereof, shall be fined not less than one hundred
(100.00 ) dollars and not more than five hundred
($500.00 ) dollars , and the court in which said clerk
is tried and the fine imposed shall by its judgment
order the then clerk of the county court to mark
and print in large letters on and across the face of
such recorded plat or map the words " VOID by
order of (name of the court
making the order and the date of said order ) . "
(4 ) Every subdivision plat approved , certified
and recorded as provided herein shall , by virtue of
such approval and recording , be deemed to be and
be a part of the master plan .
Section 12. Violations and penalties :
( 1 ) Except as otherwise herein specifically pro-
vided , any person , firm , or corporation violating

CHAPTER 176 745
any provisions of this act or of any ordinance , reso-
lution or order , or adjustment or amendment
thereto , passed , adopted or approved under au-
thority of this act , or any lawful order of the Com-
mission or the Board or the Zoning Enforcement
Officer , shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and , upon
conviction thereof , shall be fined not less than ten
( $ 10.00 ) dollars and not more than one hundred
($ 100.00 ) dollars , or imprisoned for not more than
twenty days , or both so fined and imprisoned . Each
and every day during which such violation continues
shall be deemed a separate offense .
(2 ) In case any building or structure is or is
proposed to be erected , constructed , reconstructed ,
altered , maintained or used , or any land is or is
proposed to be used , in violation of any provision
of this act , the County Attorney of such county or
the City Attorney of such city, as the case may be ,
or any property owner or occupant who would be
damaged by such violation , may, in addition to
other remedies provided by law , institute injunction ,
mandamus , abatement or other appropriate action.
to prevent , enjoin , abate or remove such unlawful
erection , construction , reconstruction , alteration ,
maintenance or use.
(3) (a) After the effective date of this act if
the applicant subdivider, owning land composing a
subdivision , or his agent , transfers or sells or agrees
to sell any lot or parcel of land located within such
subdivision by reference to , or exhibition of , or by
any other use of a plat of such subdivision , before
such plat has been approved , filed and recorded , as
provided in Section 11 of this act , such owner or
agent shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and , upon
conviction thereof , shall be fined not less than one
hundred ($ 100.00 ) dollars nor more than three
hundred ($ 300.00 ) dollars for each lot or parcel so

746 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
transferred or sold or agreed to be sold ; and any
such instrument of transfer , sale or contract shall
be void and shall not be subject to be recorded , but
any and all rights of such purchaser to damages , if
any , are hereby preserved .
(b) The description of such lot or parcel by
metes and bounds in any contract or instrument of
transfer or other document used in the process of
selling or transferring same shall not exempt such
owner or agent from the penalties provided for in
paragraph ( a ) , supra , or deprive the purchaser of
any rights or remedies he may otherwise have .
(c) Such county or such city, or any munici-
pality in such county , or any tax payer of such
county , affected thereby , as the case may be , may
file a petition in the circuit court of such county to
enjoin such transfer or sale or agreement , and
jurisdiction is hereby given to such circuit court to
hear and determine all questions and issues properly
brought before it in such proceeding .
(d) Nothing in this subsection ( 3 ) of this Sec-
tion 12 of this act shall prohibit an applicant sub-
divider , if he has not sold or contracted to sell any
lot or parcel of land located in the proposed sub-
division , from selling and transferring , prior to
final approval thereof by the Commission , all of his
or its rights , title and interest in and to such sub-
division plan and plat and said land . If such sale
and transfer is made , the applicant subdivider shall
forthwith give the Commission written notice of
such sale and transfer , stating the date of such sale
and the name and address of the purchaser . There-
upon , with reference to said land and subdivision
plan and plat , and the proceedings for approval
thereof, the said purchaser shall in all respects be
substituted for and be treated as the original ap-

CHAPTER 176 747
plicant subdivider and shall be and become subject
to all of the provisions of this act relating to sub-
division control .
(e ) If, prior to final approval of a subdivision
plan and plat , the applicant subdivider has sold or
contracted to sell any lot or parcel of land , located
within any proposed subdivision , in violation of the
provisions of this act , and desires to sell and trans-
fer all of his or its then rights , title and interest in
and to such subdivision plan and plat and the land
embraced therein, he or it may do so by and with
the consent of the Commission and upon such
reasonable and lawful conditions as the Commis-
sion may impose . The purchaser of said rights ,
title and interests shall be substituted for and be
treated as the original applicant subdivider and

(f) Nothing in this subsection ( 3 ) of this Sec-
tion 12 of this act shall be construed as prohibiting ,
preventing or restraining the applicant subdivider ,
at any time prior to the recording of such proposed
subdivision and plat and after notice in writing to
the Commission , from withdrawing his application
for approval and abandoning such proposed sub-
division plan and plat . Thereafter his rights , title
and interest in said land shall be the same as though
said land had not been proposed to be subdivided .
Section 13. Duties of city and county attorneys .
In the performance of their duties as herein pro-
vided for , the Commission and the Board may
request the advice and services of the county
attorney of such county or the city attorney of such
city , and such services shall be rendered without
compensation from the Commission or Board .
Section 14. Power and authority of city and
county in case either withdrawals from agreement .

748 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
If, at any time after such county and such city
execute an agreement to create and establish such
Commission and such Board , as provided for in
Section 1 of this act , either such city, through or by
action of its legislative body , or such county through
or by action of its Fiscal Court , withdraws from
and cancels such agreement , then and in that event
in order to carry out the purposes of this act :
( a) The Fiscal Court of such county is hereby
authorized and empowered , by appropriate order
or orders , ( 1 ) to create , for the purposes set forth
in this act as they relate to the unincorported area
of such county , a county planning and zoning com-
mission and a county board of zoning adjustment
and appeals , the functions , powers and duties of
which shall be as nearly as possible comparable to
and with the functions , powers and duties of the
Commission and Board herein provided for ; and
( 2 ) to provide for the personnel and staff of such
county commission and board, including an enforce-
ment officer , and the appointment , organization and
financing thereof, and for appropriate rules , regula-
tions and procedure .
(b ) The legislative body of such city is hereby
authorized and empowered by appropriate ordi-
nance or ordinances , ( 1 ) to create , for the purposes
set forth in this act as they relate to such city , a city
planning and zoning commission and a city board
of adjustment and appeals , the functions , powers
and duties of which shall be as nearly as possible
comparable to and with the functions , powers , and
duties of the Commission and Board herein pro-
vided for ; and ( 2 ) to provide for the personnel and
staff of such city commission and board, including
an enforcement officer , and the appointment , or-
ganization and financing thereof , and for appro-
priate rules , regulations and procedure .

CHAPTER 176 749
(c) If and so long as the Fiscal Court fails to
create a county planning and zoning commission
and county board of zoning adjustment and appeals
as provided for in paragraph (a ) , of this Section 14
of this act , then and in that event , any master plan.
submitted by the city planning and zoning commis-

14 of this act , for the physical development of such

boundaries , including any city of a lower class than
first class within any such areas , which , in such
commission's judgment , bear relation to the plan-
ning of such city ; and the territorial jurisdiction of
such city planning and zoning commission , with
reference to the control of subdivision of land and
the uses thereof , shall include all land located in
said city and all land lying within five miles of the
corporate limits of said city and not located in any
other municipality of the first class , except that , in
the case of any such non -municipal land lying within
five miles of more than one municipality having a
planning commission under this act , the jurisdiction
of each such municipal planning commission shall
terminate at a boundary line equidistant from the
respective corporate limits of such municipalities .
Section 15. Severability . If any phrase , clause ,
sentence, provision , paragraph , section or part of
this act shall be judicially declared to be invalid or
unconstitutional , the remaining phrases , clauses ,
sentences , provisions , paragraphs , sections , or parts
thereof shall not be affected thereby , but shall re-
main in full force and effect .
Section 16. Repeal . When a city and county
planning and zoning commission and a city and
county board of zoning adjustment and appeals are
created and established as provided for in this act ,
