CHAPTER 178
(S. B. 110 )
AN ACT called "State Agency Regulations Filing Act " , relat-
ing to the making , filing , compilation , codification and pub-
lication of the rules and regulations of State officers , boards ,
authorities , agencies and commissions , and creating a Codi-
fication Board .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. DEFINITIONS AND CONSTRUCTION .
-As used in this Act :

758 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(1 ) " State Agency " includes any officer , de-
partment , bureau , division , board, authority ,
agency , commission or institution of this State ex-
cept the judicial and the legislative branches .
(2 ) " Regulation " includes every rule or regula-
tion made by any State Agency , except a rule , reg-
ulation or order which :
(a) Relates only to the organization or inter-
nal management of the State Agency ;
(b) Establishes or fixes rates or tariffs ;
( c) Is directed to a specifically named person
or to a group of persons , and does not apply gen-
erally throughout the State ;
(d ) Is duly served by the State agency in the
manner authorized by law upon the person or per-
sons designated therein as the party or parties
legally affected ; or
(e ) Relates to the use of public works , includ-.
ing streets and highways , under the judisdiction
of any State Agency when the effect of such order
is indicated to the public by means of signs or
signals .
(3 ) Nothing in this Act shall prevent any State
Agency , in its discretion and subject to the ap-
proval of the Codification Board , from filing , and
the Codification Board from publishing in such
manner as it believes proper , rates , tariffs , and
other documents not required by this Act to be
filed and published .
(4) The filing of a regulation as required by
this Act does not dispense with the requirements
of any other law necessary to make the regulation
effective .
§ 2. ( 1 ) REGULATIONS MUST BE FILED .
-No regulation hereafter made by a State Agency
shall become effective until an original and two

CHAPTER 178 759
duplicate copies of the regulation are filed in the
office of the Secretary of State . Each regulation
so filed shall include a citation of the authority
pursuant to which it , or any part thereof , was
adopted .
(2) The Secretary of State shall endorse on
the original and duplicates of each regulation filed
the time and date of the filing thereof and shall
maintain a file of such regulations for public in-
spection .
—$ 3. CODIFICATION BOARD . There is
hereby created a Codification Board composed of
the Commissioner of Revenue , the Commissioner
of Business Regulation , the Commissioner of In-
dustrial Relations , and the Revisor of the Statutes
Revision Commission . Each member may in writ-
ing designate one of its assistants to act in his
place as a member of the board . The Codification
Board shall select one of its members as Chairman
and one as Secretary .
$ 4. FUNCTIONS OF THE BOARD .-The
Codification Board shall prescribe regulations for
carrying out the provisions of this Act . Among
other things , such regulations shall provide for :
(1) The periodic publication of all regulations
filed with the Secretary of State under this Act ,
in a publication to be known as " Kentucky's Ad-
ministrative Register " ;
(2 ) The compilation , publication , recompila-
tion , and republication of all administrative regu-
lations in a publication to be known as " Kentucky's
Administrative Code " ;
(3) The manner and form in which regulations ,
compilations , and codifications shall be prepared ,
printed , reprinted , compiled , recompiled and in-
dexed , to the end that all regulations , compliations

760 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and codifications shall be prepared and published
in a uniform manner and at the earliest practica-
ble date ; and
(4 ) The pro -rating of the cost of printing of
publications , provided for herein , to be paid out of
appropriations to various state agencies accord-
ing to the volume of regulations made by each state
agency and compiled in such publications .
$ 5. EFFECT TO BE GIVEN PUBLICA-
TION AND FILING OF REGULATIONS .-The
filing or publication of a regulation shall raise a
rebuttable presumption that :
( 1 ) The regulation was duly adopted , issued
or promulgated ;
(2 ) The regulation was duly filed with the Ser
retary of State and made available for public in-
spection at the day and hour endorsed on it ;
(3 ) The copy of the regulation printed is a
true and correct copy of the original regulation ;
and
(4 ) All requirements of this Act and the regu-
lations prescribed hereunder relative to such reg-
ulations have been complied with .
$ 6. JUDICIAL NOTICE . The courts shall
take judicial notice of any regulation duly filed or
published under the provisions of this Act or the
regulations prescribed hereunder .
$ 7. STATE AGENCIES TO FILE PRES .
ENT REGULATIONS .-Within three months
after the effective date of this Act, every State
Agency shall prepare and file with the Secretary
of State a complete compilation or codification of
all regulations which have been made prior to the
effective date of this Act and which are still in
force and effect and relied upon by the State
Agency as authority for, or invoked or used by it

CHAPTER 179 761
in the discharge of, any of its functions or activi-
ties , together with a citation of the authority pur-
suant to which each such regulation or any part
thereof was adopted .
$ 8. Any regulation made by any State Agency
prior to the effective date of this Act and not filed
in the office of the Secretary of State within three
months after the effective date of this Act , as re-
quired by section 7 , shall be void and of no effect .
§ 9. If the Secretary of State fails to properly
perform his duties under subsection ( 2 ) of section
two , or fails to comply with the regulations of the
codification board made pursuant to section four ,
he shall be fined not less than five hundred dol-
lars and not more than one thousand dollars .
Approved 11th March , 1942 .
CHAPTER 179
( S. B. 78)
AN ACT relating to county finance repealing , amending and
re -enacting Section 1 , Section 4 , Section 6 , Section 8 and
Section 9 of Chapter 31 , Acts of the General Assembly ,
1938 , First Extraordinary Session, and declaring an
emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1
Section 1 of Chapter 31 , Acts of the General As-
sembly , 1938 , First Extraordinary Session , is here-
by repealed , amended and re -enacted so that when
amended and re -enacted it shall read as follows :
Section 1. Definitions .-Whenever used in this
Act , unless the context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) " Commission " means the County Debt
Commission as hereinafter established .

762 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(2 ) "State Local Finance Officer " means the
Commissioner of Revenue or his agent designated
in writing with the approval of the Governor .
(3 ) " Fiscal Court " means the judge of the
county court and the justices of the peace or the
judge of the county court and the commissioners
of the county .
(4) "Funding bonds " or "funded indebted-
ness " means bonds issued to fund floating in-
debtedness , or reissues thereof.
(5 ) " Road and bridge bonds " means county
road and bridge bonds issued under the authoriza-
tion of Section 157a of the Constitution of Ken-
tucky, or reissues thereof .
(6) "Refunding bonds " means bonds issued for
the purpose of reissuing or renewing outstanding
bonds .
SECTION 2
Section 4 of Chapter 31 , Acts of the General As-
sembly , 1938 , First Extraordinary Session , is here-
by repealed , amended and reenacted so that when
amended and reenacted it shall read as follows :
Section 4. Approval of county bonds .- No
county may contract an indebtedness which, to-
gether with all other indebtedness of the county ,
is in excess of one-half of one percent (0.5 % ) of the
value of the taxable property therein, as determined
by the next preceding complete assessment , and
issue valid bonds therefor , without having first se-
cured the written approval of the State Local Fi-
nance Officer . Any other bonds to be issued or re-
issued by any county may be submitted for ap-
proval as hereinafter provided . When the fiscal
court of any county has petitioned the State Local
Finance Officer under § 983q - 5 for assistance in
formulating a plan for reorganizing its debt struc-
ture , or has received the approval of any issue or

CHAPTER 179 763
reissue of county bonds voluntarily as provided in
this section , all bonds thereafter issued or reissued
by the county must be approved as provided in this
section .
.
The State Local Finance Officer shall hold a hear-
ing for the purpose of determining whether any
issue or reissue of bonds submitted to him for ap-
proval should be approved or disapproved . The
State Local Finance Officer shall withhold his ap-
proval if he believes the financial condition and
prospects of the county do not warrant a reason-
able expectation that interest and principal maturi-
ties can be met when due without seriously restrict-
ing other expenditures of the county ; if, in his dis-
cretion , the issue or reissue of bonds will not serve
the best interests of both the county issuing same
and a majority of its creditors ; or if it appears that
the bonds or the issuance thereof will be invalid .
If the State Local Finance Officer is petitioned
by any county to approve the issuance of bonds to
refund outstanding county bonds , and if the State
Local Finance Officer is unable to find that the
bonds sought to be refunded were in their entirety
validly issued , he shall nevertheless find that bonds
may be issued validly for the purpose of refunding
said bonds , in equivalent or lesser par principal
amount , provided that the interest rate to be borne
by said refunding bonds shall be such as to make
possible their liquidation within their life at no
greater average annual cost to the county than
would be required to liquidate , within the same num-
ber of years , the portion of the outstanding indebt-
edness found to be valid at the interest rate borne
by it before refunding .
The State Local Finance Officer shall give at least
two weeks ' notice of the hearing hereinabove re-
quired to the county judge of the county proposing

764 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to issue such bonds , and the county judge shall
cause a copy of said notice to be posted at a conspic-
uous place near the front door of the courthouse
at least ten (10 ) days , exclusive of Sundays and
holidays , prior to the date set for the hearing . In
the case of bonds proposed to be issued for the pur-
pose of refunding currently outstanding bonds , the
State Local Finance Officer shall in addition cause
a notice of said hearing to be published at least ten
(10 ) days , exclusive of Sundays and holidays , prior
to the date set for the hearing in a financial news .
paper or periodical having general circulation
among bondholders and bond dealers and said notice
shall be paid for by the Department of Revenue
out of its appropriation . Any party having a ma-
terial interest shall have an opportunity to be heard
and to present evidence . The State Local Finance
Officer shall make a record of the proceedings of
such hearing and shall prepare a written decision ·
approving or disapproving the issuance of the pro-
posed bonds . Any interested party or taxpayer
may within fifteen ( 15 ) days , exclusive of Sundays
and holidays , appeal to the Commission from the
decision of the State Local Finance Officer . The
Commission , upon the basis of the record prepared
by the State Local Finance Officer , and of any ad-
ditional evidence which may be introduced , shall
pass upon the decision of the State Local Finance
Officer .
Within thirty ( 30 ) days , exclusive of Sundays
and holidays , after a decision by the Commission
any interested party or taxpayer of the county ,
upon filing bond for costs , may appeal to the Frank-
lin Circuit Court or , in the case only of bonds pro-
posed to be issued for the purpose of funding float-
ing indebtedness , to either the Franklin Circuit
Court or to the circuit court of the county propos-

CHAPTER 179 765
ing to issue said bonds . Appeal shall be taken by
filing with the clerk of the court a copy of the record
of the proceedings and decision of the State Local
Finance Officer and the decision of the Commis-
sion . The Commission , the fiscal court , the taxpay-
ers of the county , and , in the case of funding or re-
funding bonds , the creditors whose claims or bonds
are proposed to be funded or refunded , shall be
made parties to the appeal . Summons shall be
served and class representatives designated as pro-
vided in the Civil Code of Practice . To facilitate
adjudication the State Local Finance Officer is
hereby required , on payment of a reasonable fee
therefor to be fixed by the State Local Finance Of-
ficer , to supply a certified copy of such records to
any party . The case shall be advanced on the
docket of the circuit court and shall be heard and
decided as in the case of a declaratory judgment ,
except that all findings of fact of the State Local
Finance Officer and of the Commission shall be final
if supported by any substantial evidence ; provided ,
however , that in the case only where the question
of the validity of the indebtedness proposed to be
funded or refunded is in issue , additional evidence
relating to the validity of such indebtedness may be
presented . An appeal may be taken from the
circuit court to the Court of Appeals in the same
manner as the law now permits appeals in declara-
tory judgment cases , except that such appeal must
be taken within ( 30 ) days , exclusive of Sundays and
holidays , from the entry of the judgment . In case
no appeal is taken from the approval of a bond issue
by the State Local Finance Officer or the Commis-
sion as provided in this section , said decision as
to the legality of such bonds shall be res adjudicata
in any subsequent case or cases raising such ques-
tion of legality . When bonds are approved as pro-

766 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
vided in this Act, they shall not be required to be
approved as provided in Sections 186c -6 and 186c -7
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition .
Record of the approval of bonds as provided in
this section shall be made in the minutes of the
next meeting of the fiscal court of the county issu-
ing the bonds so approved , and copies of all deci-
sions of the State Local Finance Officer and of the
Commission shall be filed with the Secretary of
State.
SECTION 3
Section 6 of Chapter 31 , Acts of the General As-
sembly , 1938 , First Extraordinary Session , is here-
by repealed , amended and re -enacted so that when
amended and re -enacted it shall read as follows :
Section 6. Sale of County Bonds -The order
of the fiscal court of any county providing for the
issue or reissue and sale of any bonds shall specify :
(a) the maximum amount to be issued , ( b ) the de-
nominations in which the bonds shall be issued , ( c )
the maximum interest rate to be paid on the bonds ,
(d ) the term of the bonds ( if serial , a fully de-
tailed principal maturity schedule shall be shown ) ,
and such other information as the Commission may
by regulation require . A certified copy of such
order shall be submitted to the State Local Finance
Officer , who shall expedite approval or disapproval
if requested or if required by this Act .
All county bonds offered for sale shall be adver-
tised for sealed competitive bids at least once dur-
ing each of two consecutive weeks in a newspaper
of general bona fide circulation in Kentucky and in
the county offering the bonds for sale , and at least
once in a periodical having general circulation
among bond buyers , and shall be sold by the fiscal
court to the bidder at such sale offering the price
and interest rate most advantageous to the county .

CHAPTER 179 767
Nothing herein shall prohibit a county from ex-
changing reissues of bonds by private arrange .
ments with the bondholders , subject to the approval
of the State Local Finance Officer , but such bonds
received at exchange must be taken in at par value
or less .
The costs of advertising , printing , and any other
necessary expenses incident to the issue and sale
of county bonds , excepting and excluding those in-
curred by the State Local Finance Officer or any
member of his staff and the Commission , shall be
borne by the county issuing the bonds , and may be
deducted from any premium received on the sale of
the bonds .
SECTION 4
Section 8 of Chapter 31 , Acts of the General As-
sembly , 1938 , First Extraordinary Session , is here-
by repealed , amended and re -enacted so that when
amended and re - enacted it shall read as follows :
Section 8. County Sinking Funds .-On or be-
fore the fifteenth day of July of each year , the
county judge of each county in this Common wealth
shall , for the fiscal year immediately preceding , de-
liver to the State Local Finance Officer a report
showing for the county the total amounts of each
and every issue of outstanding bonds and the
amount of unfunded debt , if any , together with the
interest rate or rates of all indebtedness , sinking
fund balances , sinking fund investments or deposits
and earnings , payments of debt principal and inter-
est, and such other information respecting indebt-
edness , whether or not herein enumerated , as the
Commission may by regulation require .
A county may , by order of its fiscal court , sur-
render to the State Local Finance Officer all money
in hand , notes , bonds , accounts or other credits

768 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
representing assets available , and such other sums
which may hereafter become available from any
and all sources for paying the principal and in-
terest of any bonded debt of the county ; provided ,
however , that if a county surrenders the sinking
fund for any bond issue payable either from the
tax levy authorized by Section 157 or by Section
157a of the Constitution of Kentucky or from any
special tax levy authorized by law , it must surren-
der also the sinking funds for all other bonds pay-
able from the same tax levy as herein defined . Such
surrender shall be irrevocable on the part of the
county . Provided , further , that any county which
has a bond issue approved under the provisions of
this Act is required to comply with the provisions
of this paragraph with respect to the sinking funds
for the bonds thus approved and for any other bonds
payable from the same tax levy as herein defined .
The county judge of any county complying with the
provisions of this paragraph is hereby relieved of
the requirement to submit the annual reports here-
inabove specified in this section insofar as these
apply to bonded debts , the sinking funds for which
have been surrendered to the State Local Finance
Officer .
All cash so received by the State Local Finance
Officer shall be deposited with the State Treasurer
to the credit of a fund designated the " County Sink-
ing Fund." All assets other than cash shall be de-
posited with the State Treasurer and shall be liqui-
dated , upon authorization of the Commission , with-
in a reasonable time .
The county treasurer of any county complying
with the provisions of this section shall remit
monthly to the State Local Finance Officer all mon-
ies received from any tax levy made for the ex-
clusive purpose of paying principal and interest on

CHAPTER 179 769
any bonds . Any monies appropriated in the county
budget from any other source or any monies re-
quired by law to be used for the same purpose shall
be remitted as required for paying any principal
and/or interest maturities or meeting sinking fund
requirements . The State Finance Officer is author-
ized to institute actions in the Franklin Circuit
Court to enforce the provisions of this paragraph
or to recover any such funds as may have been mis-
applied .
Accounts showing the County Sinking Fund re-
ceipts and disbursements shall be kept by the State
Local Finance Officer for each bond issue of each
county for which deposits are made in said fund .
As of the close of the county fiscal year the State
Local Finance Officer shall , within thirty ( 30 ) days
thereafter , render to the judge of each county hav-
ing deposits in said fund a statement thereof for
each bond issue of that county . On or about the
first day of May of each year , the State Local Fi-
nance Officer shall deliver to the county judge , as
Chairman of the County Budget Commission , an
estimate of the principal and interest require-
ments of outstanding bonds issued by said county
or of the proportionate annual amount which should
be deposited in a sinking fund .
Disbursements from the County Sinking Fund
shall , when authorized by the State Local Finance
Officer , be made in the same manner as other claims
on the Commonwealth are paid . Disbursements may
be made only for ( a ) the payment of principal or
interest , or both , of the bonds for which the deposit
was made , and (b) the investment of such funds as
is hereinafter authorized .
All coupons and bonds for the payment of which
deposits are made in the County Sinking Fund shall
be paid either directly by the State Local Finance
Acts- 25

770 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Officer or by the bank designated as paying agent .
Such bank may be paid a reasonable fee for such
services by the Department of Revenue out of its
appropriation . All paid bonds and coupons shall
be surrendered to the State Local Finance Officer
and canceled , and shall be delivered to the judge of
each county along with the annual statement pro-
vided for in this section .
SECTION 5
Section 9 of Chapter 31 , Acts of the General As-
sembly , 1938 , First Extraordinary Session , is here-
by repealed , amended and re-enacted so that when
amended and re -enacted it shall read as follows :
Section 9. Investment of County Sinking Fund .
-The State Local Finance Officer , with the ap-
proval of the Commission , shall have authority to
invest any funds accrued to the County Sinking
Fund and the fiscal court shall have authority to
invest any funds accrued to and maintained in a
county sinking fund which are not needed to meet
interest and principal maturities on county debts
within the next succeeding twelve ( 12 ) months in
obligations secured by the credit of the United
States Government , or in warrants of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , or in road and bridge bonds
of any county of the Commonwealth which have
been approved by the Commission for such invest-
ment , provided that the securities thus purchased
shall become payable before the money with which
they were purchased shall be required for meeting
obligations of the sinking fund . Nothing in this
section shall prevent a county or the State Local
Finance Officer from purchasing any bonds , at a
price which will result in a savinng to the county ,
prior to their maturity date with sinking fund
monies created for their redemption ; provided that

CHAPTER 179 771
such monies are not required for the redemption
of bonds maturing prior to those purchased , and
provided further that bonds so purchased shall be
canceled and interest thereon shall cease .
Any earnings or losses of the County Sinking
Fund shall be charged or credited as of July 1 of
each year among the counties maintaining deposits
therein in the ratio which the largest amount in
any sinking fund account continuously on deposit
for a period of six (6) months during the twelve
(12) months preceding allocation bears to the sum
of such accounts for all sinking fund accounts .

utes , 1936 Edition " in section two of this Act ; the

"$ 938q -5" in section two of this Act ; the numbers
" KRS 66.290 to 66.390 " shall be substituted for
" this Act" in sections 1 , 3 and 4 of this Act ; and
KRS 68.210 and 66.210 shall remain unaffected by
this Act .

The distressed condition of several Kentucky
counties resulting from unavoidable delay in ob-

CHAPTER 180
(H. B. 341 )
AN ACT relating to the employment and compensation of
officers and employees in certain counties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The following sections of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , are hereby re-
pealed , to -wit : 136a - 2 , 136a -6, 907b -4 , 911 , 1020b - 2 ,
1061a -4 , 1083a -4, 1851b -3.
Section 2. The following sections of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Kentucky Statutes
Service , 1938 Edition , are hereby repealed , to -wit :
136b -2 and 2237a - 6 .
Section 3. Section 1 of Chapter 146 of the Acts
of 1940 , Section 2 of Chapter 55 of the Acts of 1940
and Section 2 of Chapter 135 of the Acts of 1940 ,
compiled as Sections 331e -3, 1055a - 1 and 1851b - 12
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Ken-
tucky Statutes Service , 1940 Edition , are hereby
repealed .
Section 4. Each of the foregoing Statute sec-
tions referred to in Sections 1 , 2 and 3 of this Act
are hereby amended and reenacted so that when
thus amended and reenacted , they shall read as fol-
lows :
Sec . 136a -2-Appointment ; term ; compensation
The first assistant County Attorney shall be ap-

CHAPTER 180 773
pointed by the County Attorney for a term of four
years , but shall be subject to removal at any time
by the County . Attorney . He shall continued to re-
ceive the salary now fixed by law and paid out of
the County levy ; provided , however , that such sal-
ary may be increased or decreased to the amount
fixed by the County Attorney by written recom-
mendation to the Fiscal Court of the County , which
recommendation shall become effective upon its ap-
proval by resolution of the Fiscal Court .
Sec . 136a -6-Appointment ; term ; compensation
-The second Assistant County Attorney shall be
appointed by the County Attorney for a term of
four years , but shall be subject to removal at any
time by the County Attorney . He shall continue
to receive the salary now fixed by law and paid out
of the County levy ; provided , however , that such
salary may be increased or decreased to the amount
fixed by the County Attorney by written recom-
mendation to the Fiscal Court of the County , which
recommendation shall become effective upon its ap-
proval by resolution of the Fiscal Court .
Sec . 136b -2- Salary-Said stenographer shall
continue to receive the salary now fixed by law and
paid out of the county levy ; provided , however , that
such salary may be increased or decreased to the
amount fixed by the County Attorney by written.
recommendation to the Fiscal Court of the County ,
which recommendation shall become effective upon
its approval by resolution of the Fiscal Court .
Sec . 331e -3- Probation Officers ; compensation-
( 1 ) The County Courts of the several counties of
this state may appoint or designate one or more
discreet persons of good moral character to serve
as volunteer probation officers during the pleasure
of the Court without any compensation whatsoever .
In counties having a city of the second class , or a

774 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
city of a population of more than 20,000 and not
exceeding 100,000 as determined by the last pre-
ceding federal census , the County Court may ap-
point one probation officer , who shall be designated
as Chief Probation Officer , and who shall receive a
salary of not exceeding twenty -four hundred dol-
lars per annum , to be fixed by the County Judge ;
one or more assistant probation officers , one of
whom shall receive a salary of not exceeding
eighteen hundred dollars per annum , and all other
assistants so appointed shall receive a salary not
exceeding one thousand dollars per annum , such
salaries to be fixed by the Judge of the County
Court .
( 2 ) In counties having a city of the first class ,
the County Court may appoint one probation of-
ficer , who shall be designated as Chief Probation
Officer and one or more assistant probation officers ,
each of whom shall continue to receive salaries now
fixed by law and paid out of the County levy ; pro-
vided , that the salaries of such officers may be in-
creased or decreased to the amounts fixed by the
County Judge by written recommendation to the
Fiscal Court of the County , which recommendation
shall become effective upon its approval by resolu-
tion of the Fiscal Court .
(3 ) The salaried probation officers appointed
pursuant to this section shall be allowed their actual
and necessary expenses incurred in the performance
of their duties ; said salaries and expenses to be paid
as provided herein . All such salaried probation of-
ficers shall have all the powers of sheriffs and
county patrolmen in making arrests .
Sec . 907b -4- Salary- Said purchasing agent shall
be paid for his services a salary to be fixed by the
Fiscal Court of the county and to be paid in equal
monthly installments out of the county levy .
11

CHAPTER 180 775
Sec . 911- Salary of Indexer ; appointment of as-
sistants ; salaries -The official indexer , appointed
as aforesaid , shall continue to receive for his serv-
ices the salary now fixed by law and paid out of the
County levy ; provided , however , that such salary
may be increased or decreased to the amount fixed
by the judges appointing and confirming him by
written recommendation to the Fiscal Court of the
County , which recommendation shall become effec-
tive upon its approval by resolution of the Fiscal
Court ; and said indexer may appoint his own as-
sistants subject to the approval of the County
Judge , but their compensation shall be fixed by the
Fiscal Court of the County and such compensation
shall be paid in equal semi -monthly installments out
of the County levy.
Sec . 1020b -2- Chancery Branch judges ' clerical
assistants ; salary ; The Circuit Courts in Counties
having a population of 200,000 or more , comprising
a separate Judicial District , and having six Judges ,
or more , may by order in General Term , any four
of said Judges concurring , appoint a clerical as-
sistant to each of the Judges of the respective
Divisions of the Chancery Branch of said Court ,
whose duties shall be to perform such stenographic
and other clerical services for said respective
Judges thereof as may be assigned or directed by
said Judges , in the performance of their judicial
duties or functions ; each of such clerical assistants
shall continue to receive the salaries now fixed by
law and paid out of the County levy ; provided ,
however , that such salaries may be increased or
decreased to the amount fixed by the General Term
of said Circuit Court by written recommendation
to the Fiscal Court of the County , which recom-
mendation shall become effective upon its approval
by resolution of the Fiscal Court .

776 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 1055a - 1- Clerks in counties of 250,000 ; ap-
pointment ; term ; salary ; powers ; fees-In Counties
having a population of 250,000 or more the County
Judge elected at a regular election for said office ,
shall , on the first Monday in July succeeding his
election , appoint for a period of four years a Clerk
and five Deputy Clerks of the Quarterly Court , who
shall have the power to file papers and issue all
processes , and for their services the Clerk and each
of the Deputy Clerks shall continue to be paid
salaries now fixed by law and paid out of the County
levy ; provided , however , that such salaries may be
increased or decreased to the amount fixed by the
County Judge by written recommendation to the
Fiscal Court of the County, which recommendation
shall become effective upon its approval by resolu-
tion of the Fiscal Court . Each clerk and deputy ,
before entering upon the discharge of his duties ,
shall execute bond before the county court , with
security approved by the court . Fees collected from
the Quarterly shall be paid into the Treasury of the
County. Said collections shall be made by the Clerk ,
who shall monthly settle with the County Judge and
pay the amount found to be due by such settlement
in his hands immediately into the County Treasury .
Sec . 1061a -4-Salaries-Said stenographer , or
clerk , shall continue to receive the salary now fixed
by law and paid out of the County levy ; provided ,
however , that such salary may be increased or de-
creased to the amount fixed by the County Judge
by written recommendation to the Fiscal Court of
the County , which recommendation shall become
effective upon its approval by resolution of the
Fiscal Court , and in counties other than those con-
taining a city of the first class shall not exceed
$ 1,500 per annum ; provided , however , no such

CHAPTER 180 777
stenographer shall be appointed except when au-
thorized by the Fiscal court of the County .
Sec . 1083a -4-Recorders and Deputy Recorders ;
employment of ; term ; compensation ; bonds- In
each justice's district of a county having a popula-
tion of over 250,000 the justice of the peace shall
employ one clerical assistant to be known as the
recorder , whose employment shall continue for the
period during which the justice of the peace making
the appointment holds office , but not beyond the
current term of office of said justice . Each justice
of the peace may employ not exceeding three clerical
assistants known as deputy recorders . Each re-
corder and each deputy recorder shall continue to
be compensated for his services by salaries now
fixed by law and paid out of the County levy ; pro-
vided, however , that such salaries may be increased
or decreased to the amount fixed by the justice of
the peace by written recommendation to the Fiscal
Court of the County , which recommendation shall
become effective upon its approval by resolution
of the Fiscal Court . The said salaries shall be in
lieu of all other compensation . The Fiscal Court
of the county shall require bonds , with good and
sufficient sureties , of recorders and deputy record-
ers , for the faithful performance of their duties .
The employment of each recorder and each deputy
recorder shall be noted of record by the justice of
the peace by the entry of a suitable order upon the
records of the court, and the employment of any
recorder or deputy recorder may be terminated at
any time , for cause , by order of record by the justice
of the peace holding office within the district . Any
of said employees shall be eligible to serve in any
justice's district in the county .
Sec . 1851b -3- Court shall appoint Clerk , deputy
Clerk , stenographer ; duties ; terms ; compensation

778 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
-The Fiscal Court of each county in this Com-
monwealth in which there is located a city of the
first class shall have a clerk , and may have a deputy
clerk and may employ a stenographer , all of whom
shall attend its sessions and keep a full and correct
record of all the proceedings of the court , together
with a complete index , and who in addition shall
perform such duties as may be required of them by
the Court . The Fiscal Court shall , on or before
July every four years , beginning with 1920 , appoint
a clerk who shall hold his office for a term of four
years , commencing on July 1. The Clerk shall exe-
cute bond to be approved by the Fiscal Court . He
shall receive an annual salary which shall be fixed
by the Court and pain in monthly installments out
of the county levy . The Court may appoint a deputy
and a stenographer who shall qualify by taking the
constitutional oath and who shall serve at the will
of the Court . The salary of the Deputy Clerk and
the stenographer shall be fixed by the Court and
paid in monthly installments out of the County levy.
Sec . 1851b - 12- Auditor , Assistant Auditor ; ap-
pointment ; term ; compensation ; bond-The Audi-
tor and Assistant Auditor shall be appointed by the
Fiscal Court and shall hold office at the pleasure of
said Court . The Auditor and Assistant Auditor
each shall receive an annual salary , which shall be
fixed by the Fiscal Court and paid in monthly in-
stallments out of the county levy. Before said
Auditor and Assistant Auditor enter on the duties
of their respective offices , each shall execute bond
with an incorporated surety company , authorized
and qualified to become surety on bonds in this
State , or with at least two solvent and responsible
individuals as surety , approved by the Fiscal Court ,
that he well, truly and faithfully perform and dis-
charge all and every duty of said office . Said Court

CHAPTER 180 779
shall fix the amount of the bond to be executed by
said Auditor in the sum of not less than Three
Thousand Dollars ( $3,000.00 ) and the amount of
the bond to be executed by said Assistant Auditor
in a sum of not less than Two Thousand Dollars
( $2,000.00 ) .
Said PhysicianSec . 2237a -6-Compensation.
shall continue to receive as compensation for his
services , the salary now fixed by the law and paid
out of the county levy ; provided , however , that such
salary may be increased or decreased to the amount
fixed by the jailer by written recommendation to the
Fiscal Court of the County , which recommendation
shall become effective upon its approval by resolu-
tion of the Fiscal Court . In counties containing
cities having a separate government , the County
may require the City government to pay its prorata
of such compensation .
Section 5. In all Counties containing cities of the
first class , the County Treasurer shall receive for
his services such compensation as may be fixed by
the Fiscal Court , payable monthly out of the county
levy . So much of Section 934 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , as is in conflict of the pro-
visions hereof, is hereby expressly repealed .
Section 6. In all counties having a population of
200,00 or more , the Official Court Reporters of the
Civil and Criminal branches of the Circuit Court
shall continue to receive salaries now fixed by law ;
provided , however , that such salaries may be in-
creased or decreased to the amounts fixed by a ma-
jority of the members of the General Term of the
Circuit Court by written recommendation to the
Fiscal Court of the County , which recommendation
shall become effective upon its approval by resolu-
tion of the Fiscal Court ; provided , further , how-
ever , that the Official Reporter of the Criminal

780 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Branch of the Circuit Court shall receive , as com-
pensation for his services , when acting in that capac-
ity, only the salary so allowed him and shall re-
ceive no further compensation from the County for
his services ; provided , further , that no part of the
salary or any fees of said reporter shall be paid by
the Commonwealth . The fees of said reporters ,
when serving in Civil cases in any of said Courts or
Divisions , shall be fixed by the presiding Judge
thereof , not to exceed $5.00 per day for taking
stenographic reports and fifteen cents per 100 words
for the transcript thereof , which shall be paid forth-
with by the party or parties in whose behalf said
report or transcript is ordered , and shall be taxed
as a part of the costs of the suit or proceedings .
Said reporter shall not be required to file any of
said transcripts without the payment therefor by
the party , or parties in whose behalf the same is
ordered , except upon motion of any party suing in
forma pauperis upon order of the Court .
The stenographic reporters of the Common Pleas
Branches of Circuit Courts in counties having a
population of 200,000 or more shall be paid a salary
to be fixed by the Fiscal Court of the County and
paid in equal monthly installments out of the county
levy , which salaries are to be in addition to the fees
allowable by the Court to said reporters in the
of $5.00 per day for taking stenographic reports and
fifteen cents per 100 words for the transcript there-
of, to be paid forthwith by the party or parties in
whose behalf said report or transcript is ordered
and taxed as a part of the costs of the suit or pro-
ceedings . Said reporter shall not be required to file
any of said transcripts without payment therefor by
the party or parties in whose behalf the same is
ordered , except upon the motion of any party suing
in forma pauperis upon order of the court .

CHAPTER 180 781
The Official Reporters of the Chancery Branch of
Circuit Courts in counties having a population of
200,000 or more shall be paid , in the discretion of
the Fiscal Court , either a fixed annual salary , to be
paid in monthly installments out of the county levy ,
or shall be paid a guaranteed annual amount , pay-
able in equal monthly installments out of the county
levy . In the event that the Fiscal Court elects to
pay a fixed annual salary to said reporters , said
reporters shall not be required to repay to the
county said salary or any part of the fees collected
by them . In the event that the Fiscal Court elects
to pay such reporters a guaranteed annual amount
in lieu of a fixed salary, said reporters shall settle
monthly with the Fiscal Court of the County for
fees collected by such reporters and shall pay the
amount found to be due on said settlement im-
mediately into the County Treasury until they have
paid into the County Treasury during any calendar
year an amount equal to the guaranteed annual
amount paid to them by the Fiscal Court ; provided ,
however , that said reporters shall in no event be
required to reimburse the County for sums received
by them beyond the amount which said reporters
shall have collected from the County in their
respective offices during said calendar year , and
they may retain any said excess without accounting
to the County therefor . If any of said reporters of
the Chancery Branch are employed for less than one
year , they shall be required to account to the
County only for so much of the fees collected by
them during that time as shall equal the sums paid
to them by the County for that time . If such
reporters collect less than the amounts they are
authorized to receive , then they shall reimburse the
County for only such sums as have actually been
received by them. In computing the fees of the

782 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
reporters of the Chancery Branch , fees for the price
of transcripts and depositions at the rate of fifteen
cents per 100 words which they make in their capac-
ities as such reporters , and which are filed , shall be
included .
So much of Section 4642 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , as may conflict with the pro-
visions hereof is , to the extent of said conflict , here-
by expressly repealed .

wishing to appeal , is unable to pay for a transcript ,
the court may direct the reporter to make a tran-
script for the defendant , and the fee may be
recovered at any future time from the defendant .
" (2) ( a ) Reporters of the common pleas
branches of the court shall continue to be paid the
salaries now provided by law in addition to the
fees above prescribed ; provided , however , that such
salaries may be increased or decreased to the
amount fixed by a majority of the members of the

786 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
General Term of the Circuit Court by written
recommendation to the Fiscal Court of the County ,
which recommendation shall become effective upon
its approval by resolution of the Fiscal Court ".
66
' ( 2 ) ( b ) Reporters of the chancery branches of
the court shall be paid , in the discretion of the fiscal
court, either a fixed annual salary , to be paid in
equal monthly installments out of the county levy ,
or a guaranteed annual amount , payable in equal
monthly installments out of the county levy . If the
fiscal court elects to pay a fixed annual salary , the
reporters shall not be required to pay to the county
any part of the fees collected by them . If the fiscal
court elects to pay the reporters a guaranteed
annual amount in lieu of a fixed salary , the re-
porters shall settle monthly with the fiscal court for
the fees collected by them and shall pay the amount
found to be due on the settlement immediately into
the county treasury until they have paid into the
county treasury during the calendar year an amount
equal to the guaranteed annual amount paid to them
by the fiscal court ; they shall in no event be required
to reimburse the county for sums received by them
beyond the amount which they have collected from
the county during the calendar year , and may
retain any such excess without account to the county
therefor . Reporters of the chancery branches em-
ployed less than a year on a guaranteed amount
basis shall be required to account to the county only
for so much of the fees collected by them during
that time as shall equal the sums paid to them by
the county for that time , and if they collect during
that time less than the sums so received from the
county , they shall pay to the county only the sums.
actually received by them . "
Section 8. That part of § 1083a -9 of Baldwin's
1938 Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes

CHAPTER 180 787
relating to the compensation of the constable and
deputy constables , and consisting of the first gram-
matical paragraph , is amended to read as follows :
" (1) In counties containing a population of
over 250,000 , for the performance of the duties of
his office , each constable shall be exclusively com-
pensated by a salary of $ 2,400 per annum , to be paid
in equal monthly installments out of the county
treasury .
66
' ( 2 ) In counties containing a population of over
250,000 , each deputy constable appointed by the con-
stable shall be exclusively compensated for the per-
formance of the duties of his office , by a salary
which shall continue as now fixed by law and paid
in equal monthly installments out of the county
treasurer , but the amount paid to each deputy con-
stable shall in no event exceed the amount paid to
the county by him, pursuant to § 1083a - 2 , during the
preceding month ; provided , however , that such sal-
ary may be increased or decreased to the amount
fixed by the Constable by written recommendation
to the Fiscal Court of the county , which recom-
mendation shall become effective upon its approval
by resolution of the Fiscal Court . "
Section 9. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , then , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , subsection ( 1 ) of section eight of
this Act shall be substituted in place of subsection
( 1 ) of KRS 64.200 ; subsection ( 2 ) of section eight
of this Act shall be substituted in place of subsection
(3 ) of KRS 70.320 , and the number " KRS 64.210 "
shall be substituted in place of the number " section
1083a - 2 " in subsection ( 2 ) of section eight of this
Act .
Section 10. WHEREAS , the salaries of numer-
ous officers appointed by fiscal courts in counties

788 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
containing cities of the first class and in counties
having a population of 150,000 or more are now fixed
by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky ; and
WHEREAS , said salaries are not fixed in accord-
ance with any general scale or classification and it
is deemed advisable that such fiscal courts be given
authority to fix general scales of salaries for the
aforesaid officers , in accordance with the character
of the work being done by them ; and
WHEREAS , this Act will prevent confusion and
uncertainty, and will permit the application of an
orderly schedule of salaries ;
NOW , THEREFORE , an emergency is now de-
clared to exist , and this Act shall take effect and be
in force from and after its passage and approval by
the Governor .
Approved March 11 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 181
(H. B. 253 )
AN ACT relating to elections, with particular relation to vot-
ing machines .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Voting machines may be used in regular ,
special and primary elections under the terms and
conditions set forth in this Act .
§ 2. The fiscal court of any county may pur-
chase or lease , from available funds or from the
proceeds of bonds which may be issued for that pur-
pose , voting machines , including extra or reserve
machines , for use in regular , special and primary
elections in such precincts of the county as are des-

CHAPTER 181 789
ignated by the fiscal court by an order entered on
record . Such machines may be purchased or leased
at any time , but no order directing the use of voting
machines in any precinct shall be made by the fiscal
court less than sixty days prior to the election at
which the use is to be made , nor unless the machines
are available for use and have been placed in the
custody of the county clerk at the time the order is
made . The precincts in which voting machines are
to be used may be changed from time to time by
order of the fiscal court, subject to the same time
limit set forth in the preceding sentence .
"Section 3. The fiscal court of any county may
select in its discretion type and make of voting
machines that complies with the specifications and
requirements of this Act . The fiscal court may em-
ploy engineers and other skilled persons to advise
and aid in the selection of the machines and in
determining the specifications thereof. "
§ 4. No make of voting machine shall be ap-
proved for use unless it is so constructed that :
(1 ) It will insure secrecy to the voter in the act
of voting .
(2 ) It provides facilities that will permit votes
to be cast for any candidate entitled to have his
name printed upon the ballots at any regular , spe-
cial or primary election , and for or against any pub-
lic question entitled to be placed upon the ballots .
(3 ) It will , except at primary elections , permit
a voter to vote for all the candidates of one party or
for one or more candidates of every party having
candidates entitled to be voted for , or for one or
more independent candidates .
(4) It will permit a voter to vote for as many
persons for an office as he is lawfully entitled to
vote for , and no more .

790 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 5 ) It will prevent a voter from voting for the
same person , or for or against the same question ,
more than once .
( 6 ) It will permit a voter to vote for or against
any question he may have the right to vote on , but
no other.
(7) It may be adjusted for use in primary elec-
tions so that a voter may not vote for any person
except those seeking nomination as candidates of his
party .
( 8 ) It will correctly register and accurately
count all votes cast for each person , and for or
against each public question .
( 9) It can be determined whether the machine
has been unlocked and operated after once being
locked .
( 10 ) It will show at all times during an election
how many persons have voted by a device herein-
after referred to as a public counter .
(11 ) The counter indicating the number of votes
cast for each person and for or against each public
question cannot be seen or tampered with without
unlocking a covering device that cannot be un-
locked by a key that unlocks any other part of the
machine . When such counters are so exposed the
machine can no longer be placed into condition for
operation without the use of a special key , which
key shall not have been in the possession of the
election officers at the polling places ; but if this re-
quirement has the effect of eliminating from con-
sideration any other make of machine such require-
ment shall not apply .
( 12 ) The operating device and operating mech-
anism may be locked before the time for opening
the polls and after the time for closing the polls .

CHAPTER 181 791
( 13 ) It is accompanied by a mechanical model
illustrating the manner of voting on the machine ,
suitable for the instruction of voters .
( 14 ) It will permit a voter to vote for all the
candidates for presidential electors of any party by
one operation .
( 15 ) It will permit a voter to vote , in any regu-
lar or special election , for any person desired to be
voted for whose name does not appear upon the
voting machine .
(16 ) It bears a number that will distinguish it
from any other machine .
(17 ) The frames in which ballot labels are
placed shall be constructed with transparent pro-
tective devices , in order that the names thereon can-
not be mutilated or altered .
§ 5. When voting machines are acquired by any
county , they shall be immediately placed in the cus-
tody of the county clerk, and shall remain in his
custody at all times except when in use at an elec-
tion or when in the custody of a court or court
officer during contest proceedings . The clerk shall
see that the machines are properly protected and
preserved from damage or unnecessary deteriora-
tion , and shall not permit any unauthorized person
to tamper with the machines .
§ 6. At the same time the county clerks are re-
quired by law to cause the ballots for any regular ,
special or primary election to be printed and bound ,
the county clerk of each county that has acquired
voting machines shall cause to be printed and ready
for use , in the precincts that have been designated
by the fiscal court as the ones in which voting ma-
chines are to be used , ballot labels for each candi-
date who , and each question which , is entitled to be
printed upon the ballots for such election . The bal-
lot labels shall be printed on clear white paper or

792 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
other material which shall be furnished by the
printer . They shall be printed in black ink , in plain ,
clear type clearly legible to a person with normal
vision , and shall be of such size as will fit the ballot
frames . The labels shall include the necessary party
designations . Wherever a statute requires that a
public question or the name of a candidate be
printed upon the ballots , it shall be considered to
have been complied with , in precincts where voting
machines are used , when appropriate ballot labels
have been prepared and inserted in the proper ballot
frames . Any provision of the statutes requiring the
names of candidates to be rearranged or rotated
shall not apply to the arrangement of ballot labels
upon voting machines .
§ 7. Upon receiving the printed ballot labels , the
county clerk shall place them in ballot frames upon
the machines , in such a manner as will most nearly
conform to the plan of arrangement of the paper
ballots , and as will clearly indicate the party desig-
nation or the symbol under which each person to be
voted for is a candidate , and as will enable the ma-
chine to be operated according to the principles set
forth in section 4 of this Act . He shall then see that
the counters referred to in subsections ( 10 ) and ( 11 )
of section 4 are set at zero , and shall lock the operat-
ing device and mechanism and the devices protect-
ing the counters and ballot labels . He shall then
enter in an appropriate book , opposite the number
of each precinct in which a machine is to be used , the
distinguishing number of the machine to be used in
that precinct .
$ 8. ( 1 ) Upon completing the preparation of
the machines in accordance with the provisions of
§ 7 of this Act , and not later than three days before
the day of the election , the county clerk shall notify
the members of the county board of election com-

CHAPTER 181 793
missioners that the machines are ready for use . The
board shall thereupon convene at the office of the
county clerk, not later than two days before the day
of the election , and examine the machines to deter-
mine whether the requirements of Section 7 have
been met . If found in proper order , the members of
the county board of election commissioners shall en-
dorse their approval in the book in which the county
clerk has entered the numbers of the machines op-
posite the numbers of the precincts . The clerk shall
then deliver all of the keys to the machines to the
county board of election commissioners who shall
give a receipt for the keys which shall contain iden-
tification of such keys . Not later than one hour
before the time set for the opening of the polls , the
board shall deliver the key to the device covering
the registering counters and such other keys as may
he necessary for the operation of the machine in
registering votes , to the election officers of the pre-
cinct in which the machine is being used , who shall
give the commission receipt therefor containing
identification of such keys . The master key and all
other keys shall remain in the possession of the
county board of election commissioners .
(2 ) Where a vacancy in a candidacy occasioned
by the death , removal or resignation of a candidate
before the election and after the ballot labels have
been prepared is filled by the chairman of the state ,
district or county political organization as provided
by law , the chairman shall furnish to the county
clerk a sufficient number of printed ballot labels
bearing the name of the new candidate to replace
those bearing the name of the candidate with re-
spect to which the vacancy has occurred . If such
ballot labels are furnished to the clerk before the
machines are delivered to the election precincts , the
clerk shall cause them to be inserted in the proper

794 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ballot frames . If they are furnished after the ma-
chines have been delivered to the election precincts ,
the county clerk shall cause the ballot labels to be
delivered to the clerk of each precinct , with direc-
tions as to insertion in the proper ballot frames .
(3) Any candidate , and one representative of
each political party having candidates , to be voted
for at the election , may be present when the exami-
nation of the machines is made by the county board
of election commissioners .
§ 9. The county clerk shall also , with the county
attorney , prepare a sufficient number of instruction
cards containing a diagram showing the front of the
voting machine as it will appear on the day of the
election , and instructions as to the proper method
of voting by the use of the machine . The cards shall
be examined and approved by the county board of
election commissioners at the time the machines are
examined and approved . These cards shall be fur-
nished and posted as other instruction cards under
the general election laws .
§ 10. Not less than five days before the day of
any election in which voting machines are to be
used , the county board of election commissioners
shall call together the officers of election of each
precinct in which voting machines are to be used ,
and instruct them in the use of the machines . No
election officer , upon being notified to appear for in-
struction , shall fail without just cause so to do . If
any officer does so fail to appear , the board shall ap-
point and instruct some other qualified person , from
the same list from which the defaulting officer was
appointed , to act in place of the defaulting officer .
§ 11. At least one hour prior to the opening of
the polls , the county clerk shall deliver each ma-
chine , with the operating device and mechanism and
the device covering the registering counters securely

CHAPTER 181 795
locked , to the clerk of the precinct in which it is to
be used , and shall take a receipt therefor indicating
the distinguishing number of the machine . The
clerk of the precinct shall cause the machine to be
arranged in the voting place in such manner that the
front of the machine , on which appear the ballot la-
bels and the operating devices , will not be visible ,
when being operated , to any person other than the
voter . The sheriff of the county , in preparing the
voting places , shall so prepare them as to permit the
machines to be so arranged, and shall provide a cur-
tain , screen or hood that will enable concealment of
the voter and his actions during the act of voting ,
unless such a curtain , screen or hood is furnished
with the machine .
§ 12. Before permitting any person to vote on
the day of the election , in a precinct where a voting
machine is used , the election officers shall examine
the machine to ascertain whether it has been op-
erated since the counters referred to in subsections
( 10 ) and ( 11 ) of section 4 were set at zero and to
ascertain whether the ballot labels are arranged as
specified on the printed instruction cards . If the
machine indicates that it has been operated or if the
ballot labels are not so arranged , the officers shall
not unlock the operating device or mechanism , but
shall immediately secure the attendance of one or
both of the members of the county board of election
commissioners , who shall reset said counters at zero
and then relock the device covering the counters , or
properly arrange the ballot labels , as the case may
be , in the presence of the election officers . If the
attendance of such members of the board of election
commissioners cannot be obtained before the open-
ing of the polls or within one hour thereafter , the
election officers shall notify the county clerk of the
foregoing facts and obtain from the county clerk a

796 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
reserve voting machine , and thereafter proceed to
conduct the election . Any reserve machine so used ,
shall be prepared for use at the election by the
county board of election commissioners and the
election officers in such precinct in the same manner
as the original machine was prepared for the elec-
tion by the county clerk . The machine found to have
been so operated shall be returned immediately to
the custody of the county clerk whose duty it shall
be to promptly repair same in order that it may be
used as a reserve machine in the election if needed .
§ 13. If , during the conduct of an election , a ma-
chine becomes in a state of disrepair so that it can-
not be operated in a manner that will comply with
the provisions of this Act , the election officers shall
lock or seal the machine in such a manner as to pre-
vent further voting thereon and record the numbers
shown by the public counter . Then the election
officers shall secure from the county clerk a reserve
voting machine , which shall be repaired and made
ready for use as provided in section 12 , and there-
upon proceed to conduct the election . When the polls
are closed both the original and reserve voting ma-
chines shall be examined and the votes thereon reg-
istered shall be counted as provided in section 15
and the aggregate number of votes cast on both ma-
chines for each candidate and on each question shall
be certified as the result of the election in that pre-
cinct .
$ 14. The voter shall then be instructed by the
officers of election , with the aid of the instruction
cards and the mechanical model , in the use of the
machine .
( 2 ) No voter shall be permitted to receive any
assistance in voting unless he makes and signs an
oath that , by reason of inability to read English , or
by reason of blindness or other physical disability

CHAPTER 181 797
he is unable to vote without assistance . Upon mak-
ing and filing with the judges such oath , the voter
shall retire to the voting machine , with the judges ,
and one of the judges shall then , in the presence of
the other and the voter , operate the machine as the
voter directs . No voter shall be assisted under this
subsection unless the judges and the sheriff of elec-
tion are satisfied of the truth of the facts stated in
the oath . The voter shall state in his oath the spe-
cific physical disability that requires him to receive
assistance , and the judges of election shall enter in
writing , on a record of assisted voters , the voter's
name and the specific physical disability which re-
quired him to receive assistance .
( 3) No voter shall be permitted to occupy the
voting machine more than two minutes if other
voters are waiting to use it.
(4) In primary elections , before a voter is per-
mitted to use the voting machine , a judge of the
election shall adjust the machine so that the voter
will only be able to vote for the persons who are
seeking nomination as candidates of the party with
which the voter is affiliated .
(5 ) If the machine is so constructed as to re-
quire adjustment after one person has voted before
another person may vote , the judges of election shall
so adjust it after each person has voted .
(6 ) The election officers shall constantly main-
tain a watch in order to prevent any person from
voting more than once .
§ 15. ( 1 ) At the count of the votes in any such
precinct, any candidate and any representatives to
witness and check the count of the votes therein , who
are hereby authorized to be appointed by the same
persons and in the same manner as is provided in

798 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 1482 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , shall be ad-
mitted and be permitted to be present and witness
the count .
( 2 ) As soon as the polls are closed , and the
last voter has voted , the judges shall immediately
lock and seal the operating lever or mechanism of
the machine so that the voting and counting mech-
anism will be prevented from operation , and they
shall then sign a certificate stating : ( 1 ) that the ma-
chine has been locked against voting and sealed ;
( 2 ) the number of voters , as shown on the public
counters ; ( 3 ) the number registered on the protec-
tive or accumulative counter or device , if any ; and
(4) the number or other designation of the voting
machine , which certificate shall be returned by the
judges of election to the officials authorized by law
to receive the same . The judges shall then compare
the number of voters , as shown by the counter of the
machine , with the number of those who have voted
as shown by the protective or accumulative counter
or device .
(3 ) The judges , in the presence of the representa-
tives mentioned in subsection ( 1 ) , if any , and of all
other persons who may be lawfully within the poll-
ing place , shall then make visible the registering
counters , and , for that purpose , shall unlock and
open the doors , or other covering concealing the
same , giving full view of all the counter numbers .
The judges shall , under the scrutiny of such repre-
sentatives , and in the order of the offices as their
titles are arranged on the machine , read and an-
nounce , in distinct tones , the results as shown by the
counter numbers for each candidate and for and
against each question voted on . The counters shall
not be read consecutively along the party rows or
columns , but shall always be read along the office
columns or rows , completing the canvass for each

CHAPTER 181 799
office or question before proceeding to the next . The
vote as registered shall be entered by the judges , in
ink , on triplicate return sheets , and also on a general
return sheet and statement , all of which , after the
canvass is completed , shall be signed by the judges .
The election officers in precincts where voting ma-
chines are used shall consist of the same officers as
in precincts where ballots are used under the general
election laws . The total vote cast for each candidate ,
and for and against each question , shall then be com-
puted and entered on the general and triplicate re-turn sheets and statement . There shall also be en-
tered on the general return sheet and statement the
number of voters who have voted , as shown by the
lists of voter, and the number who have voted on
each machine , as shown by the public counters , and
also the number registered on the protective or ac-
cumulative counter or device on each machine im-
mediately prior to the opening of the polls and im-
mediately after the closing thereof and sealing of
the machine . The number or other designation of
each machine used shall also be entered thereon . In
the case of primary elections , triplicate return
sheets shall be prepared for each party . The regis-
tering counters of the voting machines shall remain
exposed to view until the said returns and all other
reports have been fully completed .
(4) The proclamation of the result of the votes.
cast shall be announced distinctly and audibly by
one of the judges of election , who shall read the
name of and vote cast for each candidate , and the
vote cast for and against each question submitted .
During such proclamation ample opportunity shall
be given to any person lawfully present to compare
the results so announced with the counter dials of
the machine , and any necessary corrections shall
then and there be made by the judges, after which

800 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the doors or other cover of the voting machine shall
be closed and locked and the return sheet shall be
signed by each of the judges of election . If any
judge shall decline to sign such return , he shall state
his reason therefor in writing , and a copy thereof ,
signed by him , shall be enclosed with such return .
Each of the return sheets shall be enclosed in an
envelope , which shall be securely sealed with seal-
ing wax, or other sealing material , and each of the
judges shall write his name across the fold of the
envelope . One of the triplicate return sheets shall
be directed to the County Board of Election Com-
missioners of the county in which the election is
being held , one to the local governing body of each
of the two dominant political parties , and the gen-
eral return sheet and statement shall be directed and
immediately delivered to the clerk of the county
court of the county in which said precinct is located .
Said envelope shall have endorsed thereon a cer-
tificate of the election officers , stating the number
of the machine , the precinct where it has been used ,
the number on the seal , and the number on the pro-
tective or accumulative counter or device at the close
of the polls .
( 5 ) As soon as possible after the completion of
the count , the election officers shall return to the
County Board of Election Commissioners the keys.
to the voting machine received and receipted for by
them , and the clerk of the county court in which the
precinct is located shall have the voting machine
properly boxed or securely covered and removed.
to a proper and secure place of storage .
§ 16. The general return sheet , triplicate return
sheets, and statement , shall be printed to conform
with the make of voting machine used . The desig-
nating number and letter , if any , on the counter for
each candidate shall be printed thereon opposite the

CHAPTER 181 801
candidate's name . Immediately after the vote has
been ascertained , the statement thereof shall be
posted on the door of the polling place . Thereupon ,
each of the judges shall take into his possession two
of the above mentioned return sheets , sealed up in
its envelope as aforesaid , and shall forthwith deliver
the said return sheets to the respective officers and
persons to whom they are addressed , as aforesaid ,
and shall take receipts therefor . The judges repre-
senting each of the two dominant political parties
shall take the envelope addressed to the local gov-
erning authority of the party with which said re-
spective judges are affiliated , also , one of the judges
shall take and deliver the envelope addressed to the
board and the other judge shall take and deliver the
envelope addressed to the county clerk .
17. ( 1 ) For a period of fifteen days follow-
ing any primary election , and for a period of thirty
days following any general or special election , the
voting machine shall remain locked against voting ,
except that it may be opened and all the data and
figures therein examined , upon the order of any
court of competent jurisdiction , or judge thereof ,
or by direction of any legislative committee au-
thorized and empowered to investigate and report
upon contested elections affected by the use of such
machine , and such data and such figures shall be
examined by such court, judge or committee in the
presence of the officer having the custody of such
machine . In the event of a contest of election the
court in which such contest is pending or the com-
mittee before which said contest is being heard may
upon motion of any party to said contest issue an
order requiring that said voting machines shall re-
main continuously locked for such further time as
may be reasonable or necessary , with due regard for
the preparation of said machines for a succeeding
Acts- 26

802 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
primary , general or special election , but in no event
shall such order compel that said machines remain
locked to a time within thirty days next preceding
any such approaching primary , general or special
election .
(2 ) During the period when such machine is re-
quired to be kept locked the keys thereto shall re-
main in the possession of the County Board of
Election Commissioners . After such period it shall
be the duty of the County Board of Election Com-
missioners to return such keys to the custody of the
county clerk.
§ 18. The provisions of the general election laws
as to the number of voters to be contained in each
election precinct shall not apply to precincts in
which voting machines are designated to be used ,
and the county court , subject to the other provisions
of the general election laws as to changing the
boundaries of voting precincts , may change the
boundaries of precincts or consolidate precincts so
that a precinct in which a voting machine is to be
used may contain any number of registered voters
not exceeding seven hundred .
"Section 19. Whenever it appears that there is a
discrepancy in the returns of any precinct , or it is
alleged in a petition of three voters of any precinct ,
verified by affidavit , that an error , although not ap-
parent on the face of the returns , has been commit-
ted therein , the county board of election commission-
ers shall , at any time prior to the completion of the
computation of all of the returns for the election ,
summon the officers of election of the precinct , and
said officers , in the presence of the board, shall make
a record of the number of the seal upon the voting
machine , and, without unlocking the machine
against voting , shall recanvass the vote cast there-
on . Before causing such recanvass , the board shall

CHAPTER 181 803
give notice in writing to the county clerk and to each
candidate , and to the chairman of the local commit-
tee of each party affected by the canvass , and each
such candidate may be present in person or by
attorney , and each of such parties may send two
representatives to be present at such recanvass . If ,
upon such recanvass , it is found that the original
canvass of the returns has been correctly made from
the machine , and that the discrepancy still remains
unaccounted for , the board, with the assistance of
the county clerk , in the presence of the judges of
election and the authorized candidates and repre-
sentatives , shall unlock the voting and counting
mechanism of the machine , and shall proceed thor-
oughly to examine and test the machine to deter-
mine and reveal the true cause , if any , of the dis-
crepancy in returns from such machine . Each
registering counter shall be reset at zero before it
is tested , after which it shall be operated at least
one hundred times . After the completion of such
examination and test , the county clerk shall then
and there prepare a statement , in writing , giving in
detail the result of the examination and test , and
such statement shall be witnessed by the persons
present , and shall be filed with the board . If , upon
such recanvass , it appears that the original canvass
of the returns by the election officers was incorrect ,
the returns and all papers being prepared by the
board shall be corrected accordingly , except that
there shall be no change in the vote of any candidate
from that originally certified unless such candidate
was actually notified ."
§ 20. Except as modified by this Act , the gen-
eral laws applying to regular , special and primary
elections shall apply to elections conducted with the
use of voting machines , and all provisions of such

804 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
laws with respect to the custody of ballot boxes
shall apply , as far as applicable , to the custody of
voting machines .
§ 21. Any person who violates any provision of
this Act , or who willfully does any act that will
accomplish a result intended to be prevented , or that
will prevent from being accomplished a result in-
tended to be permitted , by § 4 of this Act , shall be
fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than one thousand dollars , or imprisoned in the
county jail for not more than one year , or both .
Approved by Governor , March 11 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 182
(H. B. 247)

743a -8 . Kinds of insurance not to be written ,-

to transact any of the kinds of insurance described

ditional surplus , also insure against loss or damage
by fire of or to any automobile .

806 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

(c) To transact the kind of insurance described

(g ) To transact more than one of the kinds of

(h ) To transact the business of Workmen's

CHAPTER 182 807

(i ) To transact both the kinds of insurance de-

(3 ) The maximum single risk assumed by any

(4 ) No company that has been issued a license

Section 3. Section 743a - 21 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as fol-
lows :
743a -21 . Foreign companies , admission of .
(1 ) When by the laws of any other state , district
or territory any company is authorized to engage
in the business of insurance , other than life insur-

808 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ance, on the mutual plan in accordance with the

not less than fifty thousand dollars if authorized to

less than three hundred thousand dollars if author-
ized to engage in the business of insurance other
than life or fire , it shall be admitted to do the kinds
of insurance permitted to like mutual insurance
companies organized under the laws of this Com-
monwealth when it shall have complied with the fol-
lowing requirements , to wit :
(a ) Filed with the Director a duly certified copy
of the charter and articles of association .
(b) Paid the Director a fee of twenty-five dol-
lars .
( c ) Filed with the Director a copy of its bylaws
certified to by its secretary .
(d ) Appointed the Director as a person upon
whom process may be served , which when so served
shall be of the same force and effect as if served
upon the company . This authority shall continue
in force so long as liability remains outstanding in
this Commonwealth .
( e ) Filed a certificate of the person charged
with the administration of the insurance laws in the
state in which the company is organized certifying

such business in that state .
(f) Filed a financial statement under oath , in
