( 2) Upon compliance by any foreign company
with the provisions of this section , its application

CHAPTER 182 809
to do business in this Commonwealth shall be ap-
proved by the Director and such officer shall issue
to such company a permit , in writing , authorizingit to do business within the Commonwealth .
(3) When legal process against a foreign com-pany is served upon the Director , he shall imme-
diately notify the company of such service by regis-
tered letter, prepaid and directed to its home office ,
or to such officer or authorized representative as thecompany may direct, and enclose copy of the process
served on him. The Director shall keep a record of
all processes served upon him, which record shallshow the day and hour when such service was made .
(4 ) Nothing in this section shall apply to or be
so construed as to prevent the admission or licensing
or readmission or relicensing of any foreign insur-
ance company now authorized to transact business
in this state , provided , however , that such com-panies shall at all times maintain a surplus of atleast $ 50,000.00 .
Section 4. Section 743a -7 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , is amended to read as fol-lows :
Section 743a -7 . Business which may be trans-
acted . Any corporation organized under the pro-
visions of this section is empowered and authorizedto make contracts of insurance or to reinsure or
accept reinsurance on any portion thereof for thekinds of insurance as follows :
(a) Fire insurance .-Against loss or damage toproperty and loss of use and occupancy by fire ,

(b) Disability insurance .-Against bodily inury

( d ) Fidelity insurance . To guaranty the fidel-
ity of persons in positions of trust , public or private ,

(e ) Title insurance .-Against loss or damage on

(h ) Burglary insurance .-Against loss or dam-
age by burglary , theft or housebreaking .
(i ) Livestock insurance .-Against loss or dam-
age by theft , injury, sickness or death of animals ,
and to furnish veterinary services .
(j ) Automobile insurance .-Against loss , ex-
pense and liability resulting from the ownership ,

vehicle .
(k) Use and occupance insurance .-Against loss
from interruption of trade or business which may

CHAPTER 183
(H. B. 361 )
AN ACT amending section 2554b - 169 of Baldwin's 1938 Sup-
plement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 2554b - 169 of Baldwin's 1938 Sup-
plement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Labeling-Use of Word "Kentucky . " No whis-
key produced in Kentucky , except whiskey, the bar-
rel containing which is branded " Corn Whiskey , "
under the Internal Revenue laws , shall be bottled in

814 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Kentucky or removed from this State unless such
whiskey has been aged in charred , new oak barrels
for a period of not less than one full year ; provided ,
however , that whiskey aged less than one year may
be removed from the state and bottled , or bottled in
Kentucky , if the word " Kentucky " or any word or
phrase implying Kentucky origin does not appear
on the front label or elsewhere on the retail con-
tainer or package except in the name and address
of the distiller as required by Federal regulation .
For violations of this section , the Department shall
revoke the permit of the licensee from whose ware-
house or premises such whiskey shall have been re-
moved or in which such whiskey shall have been
bottled .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 244.370 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 244.370
rather than an amendment to § 2554b - 169 of Car-
roll's Statutes .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 184
( S. B. 116 )
AN ACT for the regulation of real estate brokers and real
estate salesmen in cities of the first , second and third class ,
and within five miles from the corporate limits thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 3990e - 1 of Baldwin's 1939
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :

CHAPTER 184 815
On and after the effective date of this Act it shall
be unlawful for any person , copartnership , associa-
tion or corporation , to act as a real estate broker or
real estste salesman or to advertise or assume to
act as such real estate broker or real estate sales-
man in any city of the first , second and third class ,
and within five miles from the corporate limits
thereof without a license issued by the Kentucky
State Real Estate Commission .
No copartnership , association , or corporation
shall be granted a license unless every member or
officer of such copartnership , association or cor-
poration , who actively participates in the brokerage
business of such copartnership , association or cor-poration shall hold a license as a real estate broker ,
and unless every employee who acts as a salesman
for such copartnership , association or corporation
shall hold a license as a real estate salesman .
Section 2. Section 3990e -3 of Baldwin's 1939
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
There is hereby created the State Real Estate
Commission . The governor shall appoint three per-
sons , each of whom , immediately prior to the date
of his appointment , has been a resident of the State
for five years and whose vocation for a period of at
least ten years shall have been that of a real estate
broker , or a real estate salesman ; one member shall
be appointed for a term of one year ; one member
shall be appointed for a term of two years ; one
member shall be appointed for a term of three
years ; and until their successors are appointed and
qualify , thereafter the term of the members of said
Commission shall be for three years and until their
successors are appointed and qualify . Members to
fill vacancies shall be appointed for the unexpired
term . The Commission immediately upon qualifica-

816 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion of the member appointed in each year , shall
organize by selecting from its members a chairman ,
and may do all things necessary and convenient for
the carrying into effect the provisions of this Act
and may from time to time promulgate necessary
rules and regulations, but in no event shall the Com-
mission promulgate any rule or regulation which in
any wise fixes prices or commissions .
Each member of the Commission shall receive as
full compensation for each day actually spent on
the work of said Commission the sum of $ 10.00 per
day and his actual and necessary expenses incurred
in the performance of duties pertaining to his
office .
The Commission shall employ , and at its pleasure
discharge , a secretary , and such clerks and assist-
ants as shall be deemed necessary to discharge the
duties imposed by the provisions of this Act , and
shall outline their duties and fix their compensation
subject to the general laws of the State . The Com-
mission shall obtain such office space , furniture ,
stationery , fuel , light , and other proper conveniences
as shall be reasonably necessary for carrying out
the provisions of this Act .
The Commission shall adopt a seal with such
design as the Commission may prescribe engraved
thereon , by which it shall authenticate its proceed-
ings . Copies of all records and papers in the office
of the Commission , duly certified and authenticated
by the seal of said Commission , shall be received.
in evidence in all courts equally and with like effect
as the original . All records kept in the office of the
Commission under the authority of this Act shall be
open to public inspection under , such rules and
regulations as shall be prescribed by the Com-
mission .
All fees and charges collected by the Commission

CHAPTER 184 817
under the provisions of this Act shall be paid into
the general fund in the State Treasury . All ex-
penses incurred by the Commission under the pro-
visions of this Act , including compensation to mem-
bers , secretaries , clerks , and assistants , shall be
paid out of the General fund in the State Treasury
upon warrants of the Auditor of Public Accounts
and/or Director of Finance as warrants generally
are required to be drawn by the Statutes govern-
ing such respective offices from time to time , when
vouchers therefor are exhibited and approved by
the Commission ; Provided , that the total expense
for every purpose incurred shall not exceed the total
fees , charges , fines , and penalties imposed under
the provisions of this Act, and paid into the State
Treasury .
Section 3. Section 3990e - 5 of Baldwin's 1939
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
The Commission shall issue to each licensee a
license in such form and size as shall be prescribed
by the Commission . This license shall show the
name and address of the licensee and in case of a
real estate salesman's license , shall show the name
of the real estate broker by whom he is employed .
Each license shall have printed thereon the seal of
the Commission , and in addition to the foregoing ,
shall contain such matter as shall be prescribed by
the Commission . The License of each real estate
salesman shall be delivered or mailed to the real
estate broker by whom such real estate salesman is
employed and shall be kept in the custody and con-
trol of such broker . It shall be the duty of each real
estate broker to conspicuously display his license in
his place of business .
The Commission shall prepare and deliver to
each licensee a pocket card , which card among other

818 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
things shall contain an imprint of the seal of the
Commission and shall certify that the person whose
name appears thereon is a licensed real estate
broker or real estate salesman , as the case
may be , and if it is a real estate salesman's card it
shall contain the name and address of his employer .
The matter to be printed on such pocket card , except
as above set forth , shall be prescribed by the Com-
mission.
The original fee for each real estate broker's
license shall be Ten Dollars ( $ 10.00 ) and for the
annual renewal fee shall be Five Dollars ( $5.00 ) .
The original fee for each real estate salesman's
license shall be Two Dollars ( $2.00 ) and for the
annual renewal fee shall be One Dollar ( $ 1.00 ) .
Provided that when a copartnership , association or
corporation shall have paid an original fee of Ten
Dollars ( $ 10.00 ) or a renewal fee of Five Dollars
($ 5.00 ) and shall have designated one of its mem-
bers or officers as hereinafter provided in this sec-
tion , the fees payable by any other member or officer
actively engaged in the real estate business of such
copartnership , association , or corporation shall be
Two Dollars ($2.00 ) for the first registration fee
and One Dollar ( $ 1.00 ) for a renewal fee , for which
a salesman's license shall be issued , but any such
member or officer shall be entitled to a broker's
license upon the payment of the usual fee therefor .
When a real estate broker's license is granted to
any copartnership or association , consisting of more
than one person , or to any corporation , this shall
entitle the copartnership , association , or corporation
to designate one of its members or officers , who upon
compliance with the terms of this Act shall , without
payment of any further fee, upon issuance of said
broker's license , be entitled to perform all of the
acts of a real estate salesman contemplated by this

CHAPTER 184 819
Act . The person so designated , however , must make
application for a salesman's license , which applica-
tion shall accompany the application of the real
estate broker and be filed with the Commission , at
the same time . If , in any case the person so desig-
nated by a real estate broker shall be refused a
license by the Commission , or in case such persons
cease to be connected with such real estate broker ,
said broker shall have the right to designate another
person who shall make application as in the first
instance .
Each real estate broker's license which may be
granted to an individual shall entitle such indi-
vidual to perform all of the acts contemplated by
this act without any application upon his part and
without payment of any fee other than the real
estate broker's annual fee .
Every license shall expire on the thirtieth day of
June of each year . The Commission shall issue a
new license for each ensuing year , in the absence of
any reason or condition which might warrant the
refusal of the granting of the license , upon receipt
of the written request of the applicant and the an-
nual fee therefor , as herein required . The revoca-
tion of a broker's license shall automatically suspend
every real estate salesman's license granted to any
person by virtue of his employment by the broker
whose license has been revoked , pending a change of
employer and the issuance of a new license . Such
new license shall be issued without charge , if granted
during the same year in which original license was
granted.
No person , copartnership , or corporation en-
gaged in the business of acting in the capacity of a
real estate broker or a real estate salesman , in any
city of the first , second and third class , and within
five miles of the corporate limits thereof of this

820 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
State shall bring or maintain any action in the
Courts of this State for the collection of compensa-
tion for any services performed as a real estate
broker or salesman without alleging and providing
that such person , copartnership , or corporation was
duly licensed real estate broker or real estate sales-
man at the time the alleged cause of action arose .
Notice in writing shall be given to the Commission
by each licensee of any change of principal business
location whereupon the Commission shall issue a
new license for the unexpired period without charge .
The change of business location without notification
to the Commission shall automatically cancel the
license theretofore issued .
When any real estate salesman shall be discharged
or shall terminate his employment with the real
estate broker by whom he is employed , it shall be the
duty of such real estate broker to immediately de-
liver or mail by registered mail to the Commission
such real estate salesman's license . The real estate
broker shall at the time of mailing such real estate
salesman's license to the Commission , address a
communication to the last known residence address
of such real estate salesman , which communication
shall advise such real estate salesman that his
license has been delivered or mailed to the Commis-
sion . A copy of such communication to the real
estate salesman shall accompany the license when
mailed or delivered to the Commission . It shall be
unlawful for any real estate salesman to perform
any of the acts contemplated by this Act either
directly or indirectly under authority of said license
from and after the date of receipt of the said license
from said broker , by the Commission ; Provided that
another license shall not be issued to such real estate
salesman until he shall return his former pocket
card to the Commission or shall satisfactorily

CHAPTER 184 821
-
account to it for the same . Provided , further , that
not more than one license shall be issued to any real
estate salesman for the same period of time .
Section 4. Section 3990e - 7 of Baldwin's 1939
Supplement of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
The Commission shall before denying an applica-
tion for license or before suspending or revoking
any license set the matter down for a hearing and
at least twenty days prior to the date set for the
hearing it shall notify the applicant or licensee in
writing , which said notice shall contain an exact
statement of the charges made and the date and
place of the hearing . The applicant or licensee in
all hearings shall have the opportunity to be heard
in person and by counsel in reference thereto . Such
written notice may be served by delivery of same
personally to the applicant, or licensee or by mail-
ing same by registered mail to the last known busi-
ness address of such applicant or licensee . If such
applicant or licensee be a salesman the Commission
shall also notify the broker employing him or in
whose employ he is about to enter by mailing notice
by registered mail to the broker's last known busi-
ness address . The hearing on such charges shall be
at such time and place as the Commission shall pre-
scribe .
In the preparation and conduct of hearings the
Commission shall have power to require by sub-
poena the attendance and testimony of witnesses
and the production of papers , and any member of
the Commission may sign subpoenas , administer
oaths and affirmations , examine witnesses and re-
ceive evidence , the fees and mileage shall be the
same as prescribed by law in judicial procedure in
the Courts of this State in civil cases . Any party to
any hearing before the Commission shall have the

822 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
right to the attendance of witnesses in his behalf at
such hearing upon making a request thereof to the
Commission and designating the person or persons
sought to be subpoenaed .
In cases of disobedience to the subpoena any mem-
ber of the Commission may invoke the aid of any
Court of competent jurisdiction in requiring the at-
tendance and testimony of witnesses and the pro-
duction of papers ; and such court may issue an order
requiring the persons to appear before the Commis-
sion and give evidence and to produce papers as the
case may be ; and any failure to obey such order of
the Court may be punished by the Court as contempt
thereof .
Testimony may be taken by deposition as in civil
cases , and any person may be compelled to appear
and depose in the same manner as witnesses may be
compelled to appear and testify as hereinbefore
provided .
Any person who shall neglect or refuse to attend
and testify or to answer any lawful inquiry or to
produce documentary evidence if in his power to do
so in obedience to a subpoena or lawful requirement
by such Commission or member thereof shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction thereof
by a Court of competent jurisdiction shall be pun-
ished as provided in this Act .
If the Commission shall determine that any ap
plicant is not entitled to receive a license , a license
shall not be granted to such applicant , and if the
Commission shall determine that any licensee is
guilty of a violation of any of the provisions of this
Act, the license shall be suspended or revoked . The
Commission upon request of the applicant or
licensee , shall furnish said applicant or licenasee
with a definite statement of its findings of facts and
its reason or reasons for refusing to grant the

CHAPTER 184 823
license or for suspension of the rights of the licensee ,
or for the revocation of the license , as the case may
be . The findings of fact made by the Commission
acting within its powers shall , in the absence of
fraud , be conclusive , but any person aggrieved shall
have the right of an appeal from the findings of the
Commission as provided by this Act .
Any party aggrieved by the action of the Commis-
sion in refusing to grant a license or in suspending
or revoking a license may , within ten days after the
entry of such order or refusal , revocation or su-
spension , file in the office of the Clerk of the Circuit
Court of the County in which the action of the Com-
mission is applicable , an attested copy of the pro-
ceedings before the Commission provided he shall
first post a bond to secure the costs of the action in
such sum as may be approved by the Clerk of the
Circuit Court , with good and solvent surety . The
Commission shall be a necessary party to all such
appeals . The Circuit Court Clerk shall thereupon
docket the case as though it were a petition in equity
and shall immediately issue a summons for such
Commission , and said summons shall be returned in
the same manner as are summonses in equity cases .
No formal pleadings shall be required in such ap-
peals but the case shall be set down by the Court for
as early a date as possible for a hearing, and after
such hearing the Court shall enter a judgment
sustaining or setting aside the order of the Commis-
sion . Either party may appeal to the Court of
Appeals from the judgment of the Circuit Court in
the same manner as an appeal can be taken from a
judgment in equity cases .
Section 5. Section 3990e - 8 of Baldwin's 1939
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :

824 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
A non -resident of this State may become a real
estate broker or a real estate salesman by conform-
ing to all the conditions of this paragraph and this
Act .
In its discretion the Commission may recognize in
lieu of the statements required to accompany an ap-
plication for license , the license issued to a non -resi
dent broker or salesman in such other state , upon
payment of the license fee and the filing by the ap-
plicant with the Commission of a certified copy of
applicant's license issued by such other state .
( a) Provided that such applicant , if a broker
shall maintain an active place of business in the
State by which he is originally licensed ; and
(b ) Provided further that every non - resident
applicant shall file an irrevocable consent that suits
and actions may be commenced against such ap-
plicant in the proper court of any county of this
State in which a cause of action may arise in which
the plaintiff may reside , by the service of any pro-
cess or pleading authorized by the laws of this State
on the Secretary of the Commission , said consent
stipulating and agreeing that such service of such
process or pleadings on said Secretary shall be
taken and held in all Courts to be as valid and bind--
ing as if due service had been made upon said ap-
plicant in the State of Kentucky . Said instrument
containing such consent shall be authenticated by
the seal thereof , if a corporation , or by the acknowl-
edged signature of a member or officer thereof , if
otherwise . All such applications , except from in-
dividuals , shall be accompanied by the duly certified
copy of the resolution of the proper officers or
managing board, authorizing the proper officers to
execute the same . In case any process or pleadings
mentioned in the case are served upon the Secretary
of the Commission , it shall be by duplicate copies ,

CHAPTER 184 825
one of which shall be filed in the office of the Com-
mission and the other immediately forwarded by
registered mail to the main office of the applicant
against which said process or pleadings are directed .
( c ) Provided further , however , that every non-
resident of this State shall file a bond in form and
content the same as is required of applicants under
Section 4 , in this Act .
Section 6. Section 3990e -9 of Baldwin's 1939
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
The Commission shall at least semi -annually pub-
lish a list of the names and addresses of all licensees
licensed by it under the provisions of this Act , and
of all persons whose license has been suspended or
revoked within one year ; together with such other
information relative to the enforcement of the pro-
visions of this Act as it may deem of interest to the
public . One of such lists shall be mailed to the
County Clerk of each County of the State , and shall
be held by the County Clerk as a public record .
Such lists shall also be mailed by the Commission
to any person in this State upon request .
Section 7. Section 3990e -12 of Baldwin's 1939
Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Nothing contained in this Act shall affect the
power of municipalities to tax , license , and regulate
real estate brokers . The requirements hereof shall
be in addition to the requirements of any existing or
future ordinances of any municipality so taxing ,
licensing, or regulating real estate brokers .

CHAPTER 185
(H. B. 371 )
AN ACT relating to motor carriers , repealing all existing laws
in conflict therewith , and providing for the substitution of
this Act in lieu of Chapter 281 , Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Chapter 281 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is repealed and , in lieu thereof , there is
enacted the following , to read and be numbered as
follows :

828 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
DEFINITIONS AND APPLICATION
281.010 ( 2739j - 1 ; 2739j -42 ; 2739j -94 ; 2739L -9 ;
27390-1 ) Definitions . As used in this chapter , unless
the context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) " Certificate " means a certificate of public
convenience and necessity authorized to be issued
under KRS 281.090 .
(2 ) "City bus " means any motor vehicle used
for the transportation of persons for hire exclus-
ively within the limits of a city or within ten miles
of its limits , over a regular route .
( 3) " Common carrier " means any operator of
a motor vehicle for hire in common carriage other
than the operator of a taxicab or city bus , except
that the operator of a city bus who obtains a certifi-
cate under subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 281.040 shall
thereupon become a common carrier as to that por-
tion of its operation covered by such certificate .
(4) "Contract carrier " means any person who ,
under individual contracts or agreements , engages
in the transportation ( other than as a common car-
rier ) by motor vehicle of passengers or property
for hire .
(5 ) " Director " means the Director of the Di-
vision of Motor Transportation .
(6 ) " Division " means the Division of Motor
Transportation in the Department of Business Reg-
ulation .
(7) " Motor Vehicle " means any motor -propelled
vehicle used for the transportation of persons or
property on the public highways , including any
such vehicle operated as a unit in combination with
other vehicles , but not including vehicles propelled
by electric power obtained from overhead wires ,
and not including vehicles usually operated over
or on rails .

CHAPTER 185 829
( 8 ) " Operator of a motor vehicle for hire ”
means any person who owns , controls , operates or
manages any motor vehicle for hire for the trans-
portation of persons or property on any public
highway , or any person who engages in the business
of leasing motor vehicles for a compensation for
the transportation of persons or property upon the
public highways, but does not include any person
who transports only his own property .
(9) "Permit" means a permit to operate a
motor vehicle as a contract carrier authorized to be
issued under KRS 281.220 .
(10 ) " Public highway " means any public street ,
alley , road or highway in this State , whether within
or without the corporate limits of any city .
( 11 ) "Taxicab " means any motor vehicle de-
signed or constructed to accommodate or transport
passengers , not more than six in number exclusive
of the driver , the principal operation of which is
confined to the corporate limits and suburban area
of cities of this State , and not operating over any
definite route , and the destination of which is des--
ignated by the passenger or passengers at the time
of such transportation .
( 12 ) " Suburban area" means territory wholly
within the State of Kentucky which extends not
more than one mile beyond the corporate limits of
any municipality in this State and one mile addi-
tional for each fifty thousand population or major
fraction thereof .
( 13 ) "Occasional or casual trips " means the
occasional transportation of passengers or the prop-
erty of others for hire by one who does not regu-
larly engage in the transportation business in this
State nor operate under a certificate between fixed
termini or over a regular route .
281.020 ( 2739j -42 ; 2739j - 94 ) Exemption of trol-

830 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ley busses , city trucks , school busses and trucks
used by cooperatives or to haul agricultural prod-
ucts . The provisions of this chapter do not apply
to :
( 1 ) Motor vehicles used for the transportation
of property for hire operating exclusively within
the limits of a city or within its suburban area .
( 2 ) Motor vehicles used as a school bus and
while engaged in the transportation of students ,
under the supervision and control, and at the di-
rection , of school authorities .
(3 ) Motor vehicles being used exclusively for
the transportation of property belonging to a non-
profit cooperative association or its members , where
the vehicle is owned or leased exclusively by the
association .
(4 ) Motor vehicles being used exclusively for
the transportation of agricultural and dairy prod-
ucts from the farm , market , gin , warehouse , dairy
or mill , regardless of the ownership of the vehicle ,
so long as the title of the goods transported re-
mains in the producer . As used in this subsection ,
the word " producer " includes a landlord where
the relation of landlord and tenant or landlord and
cropper is involved , and the phrase " agricultural
products " includes fruit , livestock , meats , fertil-
izer , wood , lumber , cotton , naval stores , products
of grove or orchard , poultry and eggs .
(5 ) Motor vehicles used as a church bus and
while engaged in the transportation of persons to
and from a church or place of worship or for other
religious work under the supervision and control ,
and at the direction , of church authorities .
( 6 ) Trucks engaged exclusively in hauling ma-
terial and supplies used in the construction of high-
ways .
(7) Motor vehicles used for the exclusive trans-

CHAPTER 185 831
portation of members of the armed forces on edu-
cational or recreational trips under the supervis-
ion and control of and under the exclusive direc-
tion of military authorities . Such vehicles , how-
ever , shall be insured as other vehicles under this
act . This exclusion shall be limited to the duration
of the present national emergency .
281.030 ( 2739j - 95 ; 2739k - 1 ; 2739k -2 ) Regula-
tion of interstate commerce . ( 1 ) The provisions
of this chapter shall apply to interstate commerce ,
except in so far as such application is in conflict
with any provision of the constitution of the United
States or the Acts of Congress .
( 2 ) The director of the Division of Motor Trans-
portation may grant a certificate of convenience and
necessity for the transportation of persons or prop-
erty for compensation in interstate commerce , and
may regulate such interstate commerce under the
authority of, and in accordance with , the pro-
visions of any Act of Congress vesting in or dele-
gating to the Director such authority , as an agency
of the United States Government . The Director
may notify the proper department of the Federal
Government of his assent to the requirements and
conditions of such Act of Congress in regard to
interstate commerce by motor vehicle .
COMMON CARRIERS ; CERTIFICATES ,
RATES AND SERVICE
281.040 ( 2739j -42 ; 2739j -45 ; 2739j - 94 ) Common
carrier must obtain certificate ; carrier of passen-
gers between city and surrounding territory may
obtain certificate . ( 1 ) No person shall act as a
common carrier without having obtained a certifi-
cate of public convenience and necessity from the
Division of Motor Transportation .

832 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 2 ) Any person who owns , controls , operates or
manages any city bus between a city and a point
or points within ten miles of the limits of the city ,
and over a regular route or between fixed termini ,
may apply to the division for a certificate , as any
other common carrier . Any person who owns , con-
trols , manages or desires to operate any city bus
between a city and a point or points within its
suburban area may apply to the division for a cer-
tificate , in which latter instance the comprehensive
nature and extent of the proposed service and con-
necting service , the cost to the public thereof , and
the public convenience , if any , which will result from
the extension of urban service into the city's sub-
urban area , shall be the basis for the determina-
tion as to whether or not such certificate shall be
granted or refused , provided , however , that this
provision shall not be applicable on any highway
now being served by a certificated carrier except
upon inter -county - seat highways and within two
miles of the limits of such city on such highways .
After the issuance of such a certificate , no operator
of a motor vehicle for the transportation of passen-
gers for hire , not holding a certificate , shall operate
in competition with the holder of such certificate ,
over the route or any part of the route covered by
the certificate .
281.050 ( 2739j -46 ; 2739j -69 ; 2739j -82 ) Applica-
tion for certificate , fee . ( 1 ) Every application for
a certificate shall be made to the Division of Motor
Transportation in such form , and shall contain such
matters , as the division prescribes , and shall be
signed and sworn to by the applicant or , if the ap-
plicant is not a natural person, by a person having
knowledge of the matters therein set forth and duly
designated for that purpose by the applicant . Each
application for a certificate to operate a motor

CHAPTER 185 833
vehicle for hire designed primarily for the trans-
portation of persons and having a seating capacity
of seven persons or less shall be accompanied by a
fee of ten dollars . Every other application for a
certificate shall be accompanied by a fee of twenty-
five dollars .
Registration fee for trucks , KRS 47.020 , 186.050 .
281.060 ( 2739j - 47 ; 2739j -48 ) Hearing on appli-
cation , notice to interested persons . ( 1 ) Upon the
filing of an application for a certificate , accompan-
ied by the fee , the division shall , within a reason-
able time , fix the time and place for a hearing on
the application . At least ten days before the hear-
ing , the division shall mail written notice of the
hearing , and of the right to file a protest in accord-
ance with the requirements of the division , to the
applicant , to each common carrier , including steam
and electric railway companies , serving any part
of the route to be served by the applicant , to the
Commissioner of Highways , to the county attorney
and the county judge of each county in which the
applicant proposes to render service , and to any
other person , who , in the opinion of the director ,
may be interested in or affected by the issuance of
the certificate .
( 2 ) At the time fixed in the notice , or at such
time thereafter as the division determines , the di-
vision shall hold a public hearing on the applica-
tion , except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of KRS
281.090 . Any person having an interest in the sub-
ject matter may , in accordance with the rules pre-
scribed therefor by the division , file a protest to the
granting , in whole or in part , of the application ,
make representations , and introduce evidence in
support of the protest .
281.070 ( 2739j -50 ; 2739j -51 ) Basis for granting
or refusing certificate . In granting or refusing to
Acts- 27

834 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
grant a certificate , the division shall take into con-
sideration the effect that the proposed operation
may have upon public transportation business and
facilities of every character within the territory
sought to be served by the applicant , whether ex-
isting transportation service is adequate , the pub-
lic need for the service the applicant proposes to
render , the ability of the applicant efficiently to
perform the service , and the probable effect upon
the highways and upon the safety of the public using
the highways .
281.080 ( 2739j -76 ) Bond , insurance policy or de-
posit to protect patrons and public and to insure
compliance with law must be filed before certificate
is issued . ( 1 ) No certificate shall be issued until
there has been filed with and accepted by the Di-
rector of the Division of Motor Transportation a
good and sufficient indemnity bond or insurance
policy issued by some surety or insurance company
or other insurance carrier authorized to transact
such business in this State . The bond or policy
shall contain such terms and shall be in such penal
sum or maximum amount as the director deems
necessary for the reasonable protection of the pub-
lic and the patrons of the operator of the motor
vehicle in the collection of damages for which the
operator may be liable by reason of the operation
of any motor vehicle for hire , except that the bond
or insurance policy required of the operator of a
motor vehicle for hire engaged solely in interstate
commerce shall not be for the protection of the
patrons of the operator .
( 2 ) In addition to the requirements of subsec-
tion ( 1 ) of this section , no certificate authorizing
the transportation of passengers for hire shall be
issued until there has been filed with the Director
of the Division of Motor Transportation a bond

CHAPTER 185 835
in the penal sum of one thousand dollars payable
to the Commonwealth , executed by some surety
company authorized to transact such business in
this State , and conditioned that the applicant will
pay all fees , taxes or penalties that may be due
under the provisions of this chapter and will com-
ply faithfully with all lawful decisions , orders , rules ,
demands and requirements of the Director of the
Division of Motor Transportation made under the
provisions of this chapter . If the director deems
it necessary , he may require that the amount of
the bond shall be increased to not more than five
thousand dollars .
( 3 ) In lieu of the bond provided for by subsection
( 2 ) , the applicant for a certificate may deposit with
the director a certified check payable to the State
Treasurer in an amount to be fixed by the director .
The check shall be held by the director as a guar-
antee for the payment to the State of any fees or
taxes due under this chapter . If the person de-
positing the check fails to pay any such fees or
taxes within fifteen days after they become due , the
director may direct the State Treasurer to cash
the check and deduct therefrom any fees or taxes
due the state , and return any balance to the person
who deposited the check , in which event the certifi-
cate issued to such person shall be canceled unless
he files with the director a new certified check or
bond in compliance with this section .
(4) No bond or insurance policy provided for in
this section may be canceled or terminated at any
time prior to its expiration unless the surety or in-
demnity company has filed a notice to that effect
with the director at least fifteen days prior to the
date of the termination or cancellation . This pro-
vision shall be deemed to be a part of every such

836 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
bond or insurance policy , and no other provision
thereof, and no agreement between the parties
thereto , shall invalidate it.
Common -law liability , common carriers may not
contract for relief from , Const . 196
Motor vehicle financial responsibility law, Ch . 187
281.090 ( 2739j -49 ; 2739j - 51 ) Granting of certifi-
cate . ( 1 ) After the hearing , if the applicant has
established to the satisfaction of the division , upon
consideration of the matters mentioned in KRS
281.070 or KRS 281.040 , that the privilege sought by
the applicant is convenient and necessary in the pub-
lic interest, and the applicant has complied with the
provisions of KRS 281.080 , the division may issue to
the applicant a certificate declaring that the public
convenience and necessity require the operation for
which the application was made , or may refuse to
issue the certificate , or may issue it for the partial
exercise only of the privilege sought , and may attach
to the exercise of the rights granted by the cer-
tificate such terms and conditions as , in its judg-
ment , the public convenience and interest require .
The certificate shall be in a form prescribed by the
division .
(2 ) If no protest against the granting of the cer-
tificate is filed with the division prior to the date
fixed for the hearing, and the division is satisfied
that the privilege sought by the applicant is con-
venient and necessary in the public interest , the
division may grant the certificate without a public
hearing .
281.100 ( 2739j - 52 ) New application when cer-
tificate denied . When any application for a cer-
tificate has been heard and denied , the division shall
not entertain another application from the same ap-

CHAPTER 185 837
plicant covering the same or similar routes , sched-
ules or service until the expiration of six months
from the date of the denial.
281.110 ( 2739j -71 ) Amendment of certificate
transfer . (1 ) Applications for amendments to cer-
tificates may be made in the same manner as an ap-
plication for an original certificate , and may be
granted in the same manner as provided for an
original certificate .
(2 ) Any certificate owned , held or obtained by
any carrier may be sold , assigned , leased , be-
queathed or transferred as other personal property
upon approval by the Division of Motor Transporta-
tion , which may inquire into the responsibility of the
person obtaining or seeking to obtain ownership or
control of any certificate , and if it finds such person
to be irresponsible or unable to render satisfactory
and adequate service under said certificate it may
enter an order denying the transfer ; provided , how-
ever , that no certificate may be sold , assigned , or
leased , bequeathed or transferred except after a
public hearing and after notice as required for other
hearings .
281.120 ( 2739j - 72 ; 2739j -82 ) Duration of certifi-
cate ; renewal ; cancellation . No operating rights
under a certificate shall be effective after January 1
of any year unless extended for the subsequent year
by the payment to the division of the fees prescribed
for original applications . Evidence of the extension
of operating rights and a receipt for the fees thereof
shall be issued in a form prescribed by the division .
It may refuse to accept extension fees for any cer-
tificate only for just cause and may cancel any cer-
tificate for any just cause other than those named in
KRS 281.130 but only after a hearing , thirty days '
notice of which shall be given to the holder of the
certificate .

838 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
281.130 ( 2739j -89 ) Revocation , suspension or
alteration of certificate . Whenever any common
carrier is found to be violating the provisions of this
chapter , or any of the rules and regulations pre-
scribed by the division pursuant thereto , or any of
the laws of this State touching on such carrier's
operation upon the public highways , the division
may , upon complaint or upon its own motion , issue
its order to the carrier notifying him to appear be-
fore the division at a fixed time and place , at which
time and place the division shall investigate the
violation . If the division is satisfied , after a hear-
ing , that the carrier has violated or refused to ob-
serve such laws , rules or regulations , the division.
may suspend , revoke , alter or amend any certificate
issued to such carrier .
Franchises subject to revocation , alteration or
amendment , Const. 3
281.140 ( 2739j -56 ; 2739j - 57 ) Rates to be reason-
able ; service to be adequate . Every rate demanded
or received by any common carrier shall be just and
reasonable , and every such carrier shall furnish
adequate , efficient , safe and reasonable service .
281.150 ( 2739j - 58 ) Filing and public inspection
of rate and service scheduled . Under rules and
regulations prescribed by the division , every com-
mon carrier shall maintain on file with the division
a schedule of the rates , fares , charges and class-
ifications , and a time schedule , if any , of all motor
vehicles operated under a certificate , and shall keep
open for public inspection at designated offices so
much of such schedules as the division deems neces-
sary for public information .
281.160 (2739j -59 ) Adherence toto schedules ;
preference ; discrimination . No common carrier
shall charge , demand , collect or receive a greater ,
less or different compensation for the transporta-

CHAPTER 185 839
tion of persons or property , or for any service in
connection therewith , than the rates , fares and
charges specified in its tariffs and classifications
filed with and approved by the division and in effect
at the time ; nor shall any common carrier refund
or remit any part of the rates , fares or charges so
specified , or make or give any unreasonable prefer-
ence or advantage to any person , or subject any per-
son to any unreasonable prejudice or discrimination .
Discrimination forbidden , Const. 196
Free passes and reduced rates , when forbidden ,
Const. 197 ; KRS 276.240
Greater charge for short haul than for long haul
forbidden , Const . 218
281.170 (2739j - 60 ) Changes in rates how made .
(1 ) Unless the division otherwise orders , no com-
mon carrier shall make any change in any rate that
has been duly established under this chapter except
after thirty days ' notice to the division , stating
plainly the changes proposed to be made and the
time when the changed rates will go into offect . The
carrier shall also give such notice of the proposed
change to other interested persons , and in such
manner , as the division directs . All proposed
changes shall be shown by filing new schedules , or
shall be plainly indicated on schedules filed and in
force at the time and kept open for public inspec-
tion . For good cause shown and under such condi-
tions as the division may prescribe , the division may
allow changes in rates , without requiring the thirty
days ' notice , and the carrier shall immediately
indicate all such changes upon his schedules .
( 2 ) Whenever any schedule stating a new rate
is filed with it , the division may , upon complaint or
upon its own initiative , and upon reasonable notice ,
enter upon a hearing concerning the lawfulness of
the rate . Pending the hearing and decision thereon ,

840 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the division may suspend the operation of the new
rate for a period of not more than six months . If ,
after the hearing , the division finds any such rate to
be unjust , unreasonable or unjustly discriminatory ,
or in violation of the law , the division shall deter-
mine the just and reasonable rate to be charged and
shall fix the same by order .
281.180 ( 2739j -54 ; 2739j -61 ) Powers of Division
of Motor Transportation to regulate rates and
service . ( 1 ) The Division of Motor Transporta-
tion has authority to fix or approve the rates , fares ,
charges , classifications , rules and regulations of
each common carrier , and to regulate operating
schedules so as to insure adequate and convenient
transportation service .
( 2 ) If the division , after a hearing held upon
reasonable notice upon its own motion or upon com-
plaint , finds any existing rate to be excessive , un-
reasonable or unjustly discriminatory , or finds the
services rendered or facilities employed by any
common carrier to be unsafe , inadequate , incon-
venient or in violation of law or of the rules and
regulations of the division , it may determine the
just and reasonable rates to be charged thereafter ,
or the reasonable , safe , adequate and convenient
service to be thereafter furnished , and shall fix the
same by order . The division need not fix the same
rates as are or may be fixed by other authorities for
carriers engaged in other methods of transporta-
tion .
or281.190 (2739j -5 ; 2739j - 55 ) Abandonment
change of route of service ; forfeiture of certificate .
(1 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) , no com-
mon carrier shall abandon or change any route or
service without an order of the division that public
convenience and necessity permit such abandon-
ment or change . Applications for any such

CHAPTER 185 841
abandonment or change shall be made in accordance
with the requirements of the division , and the pro-
cedure on an application for an abandonment or
change of route shall be the same as for the issuance
of a certificate .
( 2 ) If it becomes necessary on account of the
condition of the roads or other emergency , a com-
mon carrier may make temporary changes in route ,
service or schedule , subject to the approval of the
Division of Motor Transportation .
( 3 ) If any person holding a certificate over any
public highway in this State between fixed termini
or over a regular route fails to begin operation
under the certificate on said line for a period of
sixty days , or abandons operation thereunder , or
fails to pay the renewal fees therefor for a period
of thirty days after the due date thereof ; or fails
to keep the required insurance on file and in force ,
such certificate shall be and become null and void ,
and an order to that effect shall forthwith be
entered on the records of the Division of Motor
Transportation .
CONTRACTS CARRIERS ; PERMITS , RATES
AND SERVICE
281.200 ( 2739j - 63 ) Contract carrier must obtain
permit . No person shall act as a contract carrier
without having obtained a permit from the Division
of Motor Transportation .
281.210 ( 2739j -64 ; 2739j -69 ; 2739j - 82 ) Applica-
tion for permit ; fee ; hearing . ( 1 ) Applications
for permits shall be made to the division in the
manner and form provided for in the regulations of
the division . Each application for a permit to
operate a motor vehicle for hire designed primarily
for the transportation of persons and having a seat-

842 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ing capacity of seven persons or less shall be
accompanied by a fee of ten dollars . Every other
application for a permit shall be accompanied by a
fee of twenty dollars .
( 2) The division may , if it deems it advisable ,
hold a public hearing on the application , in which
event the division shall give written notice thereof,
at least ten days prior to the time fixed for the hear-
ing , to all persons who may , in the opinion of the
division , be interested in or affected by the issuance
of the permit .
Registration fee for trucks , KRS 47.020 , 186.050
281.220 ( 2739j - 65 ; 2739j -76 ) Granting of per-
mit; basis. Upon the filing of a proper application ,
accompanied by the prescribed fee , and upon com-
pliance by the applicant with the provisions of KRS
281.080 , as made applicable to contract carriers by
KRS 281.230 , and upon the applicant establishing
to the satisfaction of the division that the privilege
sought will not endanger the safety of the public ,
unreasonably impair the efficient public service of
any authorized common carrier then adequately
serving the same territory , or interfere with the
use of the highways by the public or impair the
condition or maintenance of the highways , the
division may issue to the applicant , in a form pre-
scribed by the division , a permit authorizing the
operation for which the application is made .
281.230 ( 2739j - 71 ; 2739j -72 ; 2739j -76 ; 2739j -89 )
Filing of bonds , insurance policy or deposit ; amend-
ment , transfer , duration , renewal , cancellation , re-
vocation, suspension or alteration of permit . The
provisions of KRS 281.080 , 281.110 and 281.130 shall
apply to contract carriers , except that the bond or
insurance policy required by subsection (1 ) of KRS.
281.080 shall not be for the protection of the patrons
of a contract carrier . When applied to contract

CHAPTER 185 843
carriers , KRS 281.080 , 281.110 and 281.130 shall be
read as if " permit " were substituted for " certifi-
cate " and " contract carrier " for " common
carrier . " Each permit shall be renewed and the
fees therefor paid annually .
281.240 (2739j -66 ) Unfair competition with
common carriers ; statement of charges and other
information to be filed . ( 1 ) No contract carrier
shall give or cause any undue or unreasonable
advantage or preference to those whom he serves as
compared with the patrons of any common carrier ,
or subject the patrons of any common carrier to any
undue or unreasonable discrimination or dis-
advantage , or by unfair competition destroy or im-
pair the service or business of any common carrier ,
or the integrity of state regulation of any such
service or business . In order that the division may
enforce these provisions , each contract carrier shall
maintain on file with the division a statement of his
charges and of such other matters as the division
requires .
(2) "Common carrier , " as used in this section ,
means common carriers as defined in KRS 281.010
and also common carriers by other methods of
transportation .
Discrimination forbidden , Const . 196
281.250 ( 2739j -67 ; 2739j - 68 ) Powers of Division
of Motor Transportation to regulate rates and
business . (1 ) Whenever, upon complaint or upon
its own motion , and after hearing conducted in
accordance with its general orders , the division.
finds that any rate , fare , charge , classification , rule
or practice of any contract carrier causes any viola-
tion of KRS 281.240 , the division shall prescribe the
maximum or minimum , or maximum and minimum ,
rate, fare or charge thereafter to be charged , and
the classification , rule or practice thereafter to be

844 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
observed by the contract carrier , in such manner as ,
in its judgment , will remove the violation . The
division need not fix the same rates as are or may be
fixed by other authorities for carriers engaged in
other methods of transportation .
( 2 ) The division may , after such general
investigation and hearing as it deems advisable ,
issue such general and special orders regulating
and controlling the business , rates and practices of
contract carriers as it deems reasonably necessary
to prevent violation of KRS 281.240 .
REGULATIONS AS TO OPERATIONS BY
COMMON CARRIERS AND CONTRACT
CARRIERS
281.260 ( 2739j -54 ) General regulations as to
common carriers ; accounts ; reports . The Division
of Motor Transportation may, by general order or
otherwise , prescribe rules and regulations govern-
ing common carriers , and may prescribe a uniform
system and classification of accounts to be used ,
require the filing of annual and other reports , and
supervise and regulate common carriers in all other
matters in which the public interest is involved .
281.270 (2739j - 77 ) Nonresident carrier to have
agent for service of process . Any common carrier or
contract carrier who is a nonresident of this State ,
other than a corporation required to have an agent
under KRS 271.090 , shall , prior to operating any
motor vehicle for hire in this State , appoint a per-
son residing in this State as agent upon whom
process may be served , and shall maintain such an
agent so long as the carrier operates a motor
vehicle for hire in this State . The appointment
shall be made by filing in the office of the Division of
Motor Transportation a statement giving the name
and residence of the agent . If the carrier is an

CHAPTER 185 845
individual , the statement shall be subscribed by
him ; if the carrier is a partnership or an unincorpo-
rated association or company , the statement shall
be subscribed by a partner or by an officer . When
any change is made in the agent , the carrier shall
immediately file with the Division of Motor Trans-
portation a statement of the change , and the former
agent shall remain agent for the purpose of service
until the statement of appointment of the new agent
is filed .
Service of process on nonresident motorists , Ch .
188
281.280 (2739j - 37 ) Common carriers of pas-
sengers to furnish free transportation to director
and assistants . All common carriers of passengers
shall furnish to the director and his assistants free
transportation over their lines at all times .
Free passes and reduced rates , when forbidden ,
Const, 197 ; KRS 276.240
281.290 ( 2739j -70 ) Distinguishing plates for
vehicles . The division shall prescribe and furnish
a distinguishing plate or plates , which shall at all
times be displayed on each motor vehicle author-
ized to be operated under a certificate or permit .
No person shall transfer such plates from one
vehicle to another except by the authority and with
the consent of the division .
Certifica-281.300 ( 2739j - 73 ; 2739j -74 ; 2739j - 82 )
tion of drivers and chauffeurs . ( 1 ) No operator
of a motor vehicle for hire shall permit such vehicle
to be operated upon the public highways unless the
driver or chauffeur thereof has obtained , and from
time to time has renewed , in accordance with the
requirements of the division , a certificate of the
division to the effect that the driver or chauffeur
has met its requirements with respect to his health ,
vision , sobriety , intelligence , care , ability and

846 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
experience . At the time of the original issuance of
the certificate , and at the time of each renewal , a
fee of two dollars shall be paid to the division . The
holder of such a certificate , at all times when
operating a motor vehicle for hire shall have the
certificate in his possession , and shall wear a badge ,
to be issued by the division , indicating that he is a
certified driver or chauffeur .
( 2) The division may , for good cause , upon com-
plaint or upon its own motion , revoke or suspend
any such certificate , and the certificate and badge
shall thereupon be returned to the division .
Age limit for drivers of busses , KRS 186.600
Drivers ' licenses generally , Ch . 186
281.310 ( 2739j -75 ) Working hours of drivers
and chauffeurs . No common carrier or contract
carrier shall require or permit any driver or
chauffeur operating a motor vehicle for hire under
a certificate or permit to remain continuously on
duty for a longer period than twelve hours , and
when any such driver or chauffeur has been con-
tinously on duty for twelve hours he shall have at
least eight consecutive hours off duty . No common
carrier or contract carrier shall require or permit
any such driver or chauffeur to remain on duty for
a longer period than sixteen hours in the aggregate
in any twenty -four hour period , and when a driver
or chauffeur has been on duty sixteen hours in the
aggregate in any twenty-four hour period he shall
have at least ten consecutive hours off duty . The
periods of release from duty required by this section
shall be given at such places and under such circum-
stances that rest and relaxation from the strain of
the duties of the employment may be obtained . No
period off duty shall be deemed to break the con-
tinuity of service unless it is for at least three con-
secutive hours and is given at such a place and

CHAPTER 185 847
under such circumstances that rest and relaxation
from the strain of the duties of the employment may
be obtained . In case of an unforeseen emergency
not resulting from the negligence of the carrier or
his agents , servants or employes , the driver or
chauffeur may complete his run or tour of duty , if
the run or tour of duty, but for the delay caused by
the emergency , could reasonably have been com-
pleted without a violation of this section . The
division may require such reports as it deems neces-
sary for the enforcement of this section .
281.320 ( 2739j - 22 ) Overcrowding of passenger
vehicles ; maximum width . ( 1 ) No common carrier
or contract carrier shall permit any motor vehicle
operated by him for the transportation of passen-
gers for hire to carry a greater number of passen-
gers than twenty -five per cent over its seating
capacity or number specified in the certificate or
permit issued by the division , or permit any pas-
senger to ride upon the steps or running board of
any such motor vehicle . No common carrier or
contract carrier shall permit any passenger to ride
on the top of any such motor vehicle unless the top
has been designed and equipped with seats con-
structed for such use , and provided with protecting
railing or protective enclosure on all four sides of
the top of the vehicle , and unless such use has been
authorized by a certificate issued by the division .
( 2 ) No common carrier shall operate , for the
transportation of persons for hire , any motor
vehicle with an extreme width exceeding ninety - six
inches .
Number of passengers in ordinary vehicles , pas-
sengers not to interfere with driver , KRS 189.470
Width of truck or trailer not to exceed ninety - six
inches , KRS 189.180

848 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
381.330 ( 2739j - 23 ) Accident reports . All com-
mon carriers and contract carriers of passengers
shall give to the division immediate notice , in
writing , of any accident resulting in loss of life or
serious injury .
Duty of operator of any motor vehicle to report
accidents , KRS 189.580
281.340 ( 2739j -33 ) Passenger vehicles required
to stop at railroad crossings. The chauffeur of
every motor vehicle used in the common carriage of
passengers for hire shall stop the vehicle before
crossing at grade the main track of any railroad or
interurban electric railway , except where the cross-
ing is a guarded crossing protected by gates or a
flag controlled or operated by an employee of the
railroad or interurban company . The stop shall be
made at not less than ten feet nor more than thirty
feet from the nearest track to be crossed . After
making the stop , the chauffeur shall look carefully
in each direction for approaching cars or trains ,
and shall not start his vehicle until he has as-
certained that no cars or trains are approaching ineither direction .
All motor vehicles to stop at railroad crossings on
signal , KRS 189.560
281.350 (2739j -25 ) Matters to be displayed on
passenger vehicles . All common carriers and con-
tract carriers shall at all times have displayed on
each side of every vehicle used in the transportation
of passengers , the name and office address of the
owner , the number of the vehicle corresponding to
the number on the distinguishing plates furnishedby the division , and the place of its starting termi-
nal , and shall have the name of the chauffeur posted
in a conspicuous place in the vehicle .
281.360 ( 2739j - 26 ) Inspection of passenger vehi
cles . The director and representatives of the

CHAPTER 185 849
Division of Motor Transportation may , at any time.
or place , make inspection under the provisions of
this chapter that relate to passenger motor vehicles
operated by common carriers and contract carriers
and shall be provided with appropriate badges for
identification . They may enter into and upon any
such motor vehicles for the purpose of ascertaining
whether or not any provision of this chapter or
rule or regulation of the division relating to such
vehicles has been violated . Wilful refusal to stop
any such vehicle when ordered to do so by any
representative of the division , or to permit the
representative to enter into or upon the vehicle for
the purpose of inspection , shall be sufficient ground.
for the revocation or suspension of the certificate or
permit of the carrier .
281.370 ( 2739g -96 ) Enforcement ; arrest ; bail
bond . Any peace officer , including sheriffs and
their deputies , constables and their deputies , police
officers and marshals of cities or incorporated
towns , county police or patrols , and special officers
appointed by any agency of the commonwealth of
Kentucky for the enforcement of its laws relating
to motor vehicles , now existing or hereafter
enacted , shall be authorized , and it is hereby made
the duty of each of them , to enforce the provisions
of this chapter and to make arrests for any viola-
tion or violations thereof , and for violations of any
other law relating to motor vehicles , without war-
rant if the offense be committed in his presence , and
with warrant or summons if he does not observe the
commission of the offense . When in pursuit of an
offender for any offense committed within his
jurisdiction , any such officer may follow and effect
an arrest beyond the limits of his jurisdiction . If
the arrest be made without warrant , the accused
may elect to be immediately taken before the nearest

850 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
court having jurisdiction , whereupon it shall be the
duty of the officer to so take him . If the accused
elect not to be so taken , then it shall be the duty of
the officer to require of the accused a bail - bond in a
sum not less than five hundred dollars , conditioned
that the accused binds himself to appear in the
court of jurisdiction at the time fixed in the bond ,
not however in any case later than six days from
the day of arrest . In case the arrested person fails
to appear on the day fixed , the bond shall be for-
feited in the manner as is provided for the for-
feiture of bonds in other cases . No officer shall
be permitted to take a cash bond . The officer mak-
ing the arrest and taking the bond shall report the
same to the court having jurisdiction within
eighteen hours after taking such bond .
281.380 ( 2739g - 97 ) Commissioning employees
as highway patrols ; powers . In addition to all
other enforcement agencies , the Division of Motor
Transportation and the Department of Revenue are
hereby authorized to issue commissions to their
employees , or any of them , as highway patrols , and
such employees , when so commissioned , shall have
all the powers of peace officers in respect of the
enforcement of this chapter , and of all the laws
relating to motor vehicles , and particularly the
powers hereinabove in KRS 281.370 granted to
other officers .
HEARINGS , APPEALS AND INJUNCTIONS AS
TO COMMON CARRIERS AND CONTRACT
CARRIERS
281.390 ( 2739j -43 ) Investigations , inquiries , and
hearings; how conducted . The Division of Motor
Transportation may conduct any investigation ,
inquiry , or hearing that it deems proper in con-

CHAPTER 185 851
nection with the performance of its duties under
this chapter with respect to common carriers and
contract carriers . The investigation , inquiry or
hearing may be conducted by or before the director
or any representative of the division designated by
him . Any decision or order made by a person
other than the director shall, when approved and
confirmed by the director and entered on the records
of the division , be treated for all purposes as the
decision or order of the division .
Heads of administrative departments and their
representatives may administer oaths , KRS 12.120
281.400 ( 2739j - 85 ) Report of hearing . Upon
the completion of any hearing , the division shall
render a written report of its findings of fact and
conclusions of law , and shall include a statement of
any other matters pertinent to the question at issue .
This report shall be filed with the records of the
proceedings , and a copy thereof shall be im-
mediately sent to all parties to the proceedings .
281.410 ( 2739j -72 ; 2739j - 86 ; 2739j -89 ) Right of
appeal , how taken . (1 ) An appeal may be taken
to the Franklin circuit court from the action of the
division in the following cases by the following
persons :
(a) When an application for a certificate or
permit has been refused , in whole or in part , by the
applicant .
(b ) When an application for a certificate or
permit has been granted , in whole or in part , by any
person who has filed a protest to the granting of the
application .
(c ) When the division , under the provisions of
KRS 281.120 has refused to renew or has under-
taken to cancel a certificate or permit in whole or
in part , by the holder of the certificate or permit .
(d ) When the division , under the provisions of

852 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
KRS 281.130 , has suspended , revoked , altered , or
amended or refused to amend a certificate or
permit , by the holder of the certificate or permit , or
by anyone who has filed a protest .
( 2 ) The appeal may be taken by filing a petition
of appeal with the clerk of the Franklin circuit court
within twenty days after the rendition of the order
of the division . The petition shall state completely
the grounds upon which the review is sought , shall
assign all errors relied on , and shall be accompanied
by a copy of the record , certified by the director , or
an abstract thereof if agreed to by all parties to
the hearing . The appellant shall furnish a copy of
the petition to each person who was a party to the
hearing before the division . Summons shall be
issued upon the petition , directing the adverse
party or parties to file answer with fifteen days
after service of the summons .
( 3 ) In the case of appeals under paragraph ( c )
of subsection ( 1 ) of this section , the certificate or
permit shall remain in effect pending the appeal in
the circuit court and in the Court of Appeals , not to
exceed however a period of eighteen months from
the date of the order appealed from .
281.420 ( 2739j -72 ; 2739j - 87 ; 2739j - 88 ; 2739j -89 )
Procedure on appeal ; powers of circuit court ; judg-
ment ; appeal to Court of Appeals . ( 1 ) The
division and each party may appear in the pro-
ceedings in circuit court on an appeal taken under
KRS 281.410 .
(2 ) In the case of appeals under paragraph
( a ) , (b ) , or ( d ) of subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 281.410 ,
no new or additional evidence may be introduced in
the circuit court except as to fraud or misconduct
of some person engaged in the administration of
this chapter and affecting the order ; the court shall
hear the case upon the certified record or abstract

CHAPTER 185 853
thereof , and shall dispose of the case in a summary
manner , its review being limited to determining
whether or not the division acted without or in
excess of its power , the order was procured by
fraud , the order is in conformity to the provisions
of this chapter , and the finding of facts in issue is
supported by any substantial evidence . On such
appeals , the court may , in advance of judgment and
upon a sufficient showing of facts , remand the case
to the division .
(3 ) In the case of appeals under paragraph ( c )
of subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 281.410 , all parties may
be permitted to introduce new evidence , and the
circuit court shall try the matter de novo .
(4) The court shall enter judgment confirming ,
modifying or setting aside the order , or , in its
discretion , remanding the case to the division for
proceedings in conformity with the directions of
the court .
(5 ) Any party may appeal from the circuit
court to the Court of Appeals , and such appeals
shall have precedence of other cases pending in the
Court of Appeals .
281.430 ( 2739j - 92 ) Injunctions .Injunctions . At the instance
of the Division of Motor Transportation , or of any
person having an interest in the subject matter , the
courts of this state may enjoin any person from
violating any of the provisions of this chapter
relating to common carriers or contract carriers , or
any order , rule, regulation or requirement of the
division relating to such carriers .
281.440 ( 2739j - 93 ) Public attorneys to repre-
sent Division of Motor Transportation . Upon the
request of the Division of Motor Transportation ,
the Attorney General , or any Commonwealth's at-
torney or county attorney , shall represent the
division within his jurisdiction and without addi-

854 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tional compensation in any action or proceeding , in
which the division is a party , arising under the pro-
visions of this chapter relating to common carriers
or contract carriers .
Attorney General , duties as to state litigation ,
KRS 15.020
Police powers of officers and employes of
Division of Motor Transportation , KRS 189.640
TAXICABS AND CITY BUSSES
281.450 ( 2739L - 10 ) Taxicabs and city busses
must obtain permit ; list of drivers to be filed . No
person shall transport passengers for hire upon any
public highway who is not qualified so to do under
KRS 281.040 or 281.200 , without having procured
from the Division of Motor Transportation an
authorization for such operation . Before the
division shall issue an authorization the person
seeking it shall file with the division an application ,
in such form as the division requires , setting forth ,
among other matters , the name and residence of the
applicant, the name of the chief officer if the appli-
cant is a corporation , and the name of the members
if the applicant is a partnership , the number and
description of the vehicles to be used , the place where
the applicant proposes to operate , and a list of all
drivers employed or to be employed . If any
changes are made in the drivers or any additional
drivers are employed , the operator shall furnish a
list thereof to the division . Authorizations shall be
renewed at such times , in such manner and upon
such conditions as the division prescribes .
281.460 ( 2739L - 11 ) Financial responsibility of
operators of taxicabs and city busses . ( 1 ) Before
any authorization for the operation of a taxicab or
city bus is granted by the division under KRS
281.450 , the applicant shall file with the director a

CHAPTER 185 855
good and sufficient bond with adequate corporate
surety, payable to the Commonwealth , binding the
obligor to pay any final judgment rendered against
him arising out of the death of or injury to any pas-
senger , or loss of or damage to property while in
transit , or death of or injury to other persons or
damage to their property , or any act or omission
connected with the operation of motor vehicles by
the applicant . The bond shall be in the penal sum
of five thousand dollars for each motor vehicle
operated , for death of or injury to persons , and in
the further sum of one thousand dollars for each
motor vehicle operated , for damage to property .
( 2) In lieu of the bond , the director may accept
a policy of insurance , or other written contract
deemed by the director to be adequate , issued by
any insurance company or insurance carrier
authorized to do business in this State , by which the
insurance company or insurance carrier shall
assume the liability prescribed by subsection ( 1 ) of
this section .
(3 ) The director may exempt in whole or in part
from the requirements of this section any person
who applies for such exemption and shows to the
satisfaction of the director that , by reason of the
financial ability of the person applying , there is due
assurance of the payment of all damages for which
he may become liable as a result of the operation of
any vehicles owned by him .
(4 ) If any applicant files with the director an
affidavit as to his inability to obtain a bond or policy
of insurance with corporate surety , the director may
accept a bond with personal surety, in lieu of the
bond or policy required by subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 )
of this section . Before accepting a bond with
personal surety, the director may require the
applicant to file his affidavit , and the affidavit of the

856 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
surety , as to the property qualifications of both
principal and surety, and the director shall be the
judge of the sufficiency of both principal and surety .
(5 ) The exemptions provided for in subsection
(4) of this section shall be made only by written
order of the director . The director may from time
to time require statements of the financial condition
of the person so exempted , and may for cause , upon
ten days ' notice in writing , revoke the order grant-
ing the exemption , in which event the person whose
exemption is revoked shall immediately comply
with the other requirements of this section .
( 6) No insurance company or insurance carrier
issuing any policy filed with the director , and no
surety or other obligor on any bond or contract filed
with the director shall be relieved from liability
under the policy , bond or contract until after the
expiration of fifteen days ' notice to the director of
an intention to cancel the policy , bond or contract .
The acceptance of such notice and the cancellation.
of the policy , bond or contract by the director shall
not relieve the insurance company , insurance
carrier , surety or obligor of any liability that
accrued prior to the effective date of the cancella-
tion . The director may require all bonds or insur-
ance policies to expire and be renewed upon a given
day.
(7) If, after notice and hearing, the director
finds that any person holding an authorization
under KRS 281.450 has violated any of the pro-
visions of KRS 281.450 to 281.480 , the director shall
cancel the authorization issued to such person .
After cancellation , such person may not ask for a
renewal of this authorization for a period of six
months . Any person whose authorization is can-
celed by the director may appeal to the Franklin
circuit court , where the case shall be tried de novo .

CHAPTER 185 857
Motor vehicle financial responsibility law , Ch . 187
School busses , liability insurance for KRS
160.310
281.470 ( 2739j - 21 ; 2739j - 21a ; 2739j - 15 ) Opera-
tion in competition with common carrier . No per-
son shall operate a taxicab in competition with a
common carrier over the route or any part of the
route covered by the certificate of the common
carrier , except for casual trips , and on such casual
trips the operator of the taxicab shall not pick up
any passengers along the route and shall not on the
return trip carry any passengers other than those
carried on the original trip .
TAXES
281.480 ( 2739j -78 ) Weight tax on trucks oper-
ated by common carriers and contract carriers .
On or before January 1 of each year , and as a condi-
tion precedent to the use of the public highways ,
every common carrier and every contract carrier
primarily engaged in transporting property for
hire shall pay to the Division of Motor Transporta-
tion or its agent at Frankfort for the privilege of
using the highways , an annual excise tax equal to
one dollar for each hundred pounds or fraction
thereof , of weight of each motor vehicle operated by
him for the transportation of property for hire .
The taxable weight shall be the weight of the
vehicle at the time of the application for use of the
highways , empty , and ready for operation . The tax
shall be increased fifty percent on each motor
vehicle not equipped wholly with pneumatic tires .
( 2 ) The weight tax imposed by this section does
not apply to motor vehicles operated by a common
carrier for hire under a certificate issued it by the
Division of Motor Transportation where such motor
vehicle does not enter the State of Kentucky a

858 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
distance of more than fifteen miles from its point of
entry into this State , but such motor vehicles shall
be registered annually with the Division of Motor
Transportation and an annual registration fee of
$10.00 paid therefor . The Division of Motor
Transportation shall thereupon issue an evidence of
such registration which shall at all times be promi-
nently displayed on such vehicle while in this State .

281.510 -Tax on casual or occasional trips . ( 1 )

(3 ) For every motor vehicle engaged in the

860 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

281.520 ( 27390-1 ; 27390-2 ) Mileage , tag and seat
taxes on passenger vehicles operated by common
carriers and contract carriers ; taxicabs . ( 1 )
Except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of this section ,
every common carrier and every contract carrier
shall pay to the Director of the Division of Motor
Transportation , for each motor vehicle operated by
