thirty -sixth of a cent for each regular seat in the
vehicle multiplied by the total number of miles
traveled, and , in addition , a tag tax equal to eight
dollars for each regular seat for the first sixteen
seats , fifteen dollars for each regular seat for the
next eight seats , and twenty -five dollars for each
regular seat for all remaining seats .
( 2 ) The mileage tax and tag tax imposed by sub-
section ( 1 ) of this section do not apply to taxicabs ,
city busses and U-drive -its , nor to vehicles operat-
ing under a certificate in interstate commerce where
such vehicle does not enter the State of Kentucky a
distance of more than ten miles from the point of
entry into this State , but a tax of one dollar and
fifty cents for each regular seat in such vehicles
shall be paid .
(3 ) The term " regular seat , " as used in this
section and in KRS 281.530 , means a seat ordinarily
and customarily used by one passenger , but does
not include folding or collapsible emergency aisle
seats , provided that the number of such folding or
collapsible emergency aisle seats shall not be

between the rear seat and the driver's seat . Where
any seats in the vehicle are not distinguished by

for each eighteen -inch space on such seats , or
major portion thereof .

tag or certificate of such vehicle issued by the

(3 ) A penalty of twenty per cent shall be added

CHAPTER 186 .
(H. B. 349 )
AN ACT relating to Alcohol Control .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 2554c -20 of Baldwin's 1936 Edition of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended to read as
follows :
Unlawful to Transport Intoxicating Liquors into
Local Option Territory . It shall be unlawful for
any person or public or private carrier to bring
into , transfer to another , deliver or distribute in any
local option territory any intoxicating liquor , re-
gardless of the name by which it may be called .
Each package of such liquor so brought , trans-
ferred or delivered in such territory shall constitute
a separate offense . Provided , however , that nothing
herein shall be construed to prevent any distiller or
manufacturer or any authorized agent of a distil-
ler , manufacturer or wholesale dealer from trans-
porting or causing to be transported by a licensed
carrier any intoxicating liquors to their distilleries ,
breweries , wineries or warehouses where the sale of
such liquors may be lawful , either in or out of the
State .
2. Section 2554c - 21 of Baldwin's 1938 Supple-
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended to
read as follows :
Receiving or Accepting Liquor . It shall be un-
lawful for any person of such territory to receive
Acts- 28

866 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or accept any intoxicating liquor from a common
carrier or from any person who has transported
such liquor in or into such territory for compensa-
tion, hire or profit of any kind whatsoever either
directly or indirectly . Each and every package of
said intoxicating liquor so received or accepted shall
constitute a separate offense . Provided , however ,
that nothing herein shall be construed to prevent
any distiller or manufacturer of intoxicating liquors
or any authorized agent of a distiller or manu-
facturer or wholesale dealer from receiving or ac-
cepting any intoxicating liquors which are to be sold
in a territory where the sale of such liquors may be
lawful either in or out of the State .
3. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are enacted
at the present session of the General Assembly ,
sections one and two of this Act shall , upon the
taking effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
substituted , respectively , in place of KRS 242.260
and 242.280 , and shall thereafter be treated as an
amendment to KRS 242.260 and 242.280 rather than
an amendment to §§ 2554c -20 and 2554c - 21 of Car-
roll's Statutes .
Not approved .

AN ACT relating to finance and revenue of counties and cities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 4281u-5 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,

Section 1. Where the special object or purpose
for which a tax was levied and collected has been

868 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

pose similar to that for which the fund was origi-
nally accumulated and may invest the same in in-
vestments in which other funds of the county or
city may lawfully be invested until such time as it
is necessary to expend the fund for such purpose .

sembly , section one of this Act shall stand repealed

and has not been commingled with other funds in

an object or purpose similar to that for which the

the same in investments in which other funds of the
county may lawfully be invested until such time as
it is necessary to expend the fund for such pur-
pose , " and subsection ( 4 ) of KRS 92.360 shall be
treated as amended to read as follows :

CHAPTER 189 .
(H. B. 261 )
AN ACT relating to drainage districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 2380b - 12 of Baldwin's 1939 Supple-
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes is amended to
read as follows :
(1) The county judge shall divide the district
into three precincts as nearly equal in area as prac-
ticable , following owners ' property lines , and im-
mediately upon such division he shall appoint a
temporary secretary to act for the district board .
The secretary for the board shall record the result
of the division of the district into precincts in the
drainage record .

870 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 2 ) The secretary shall give twenty days ' notice
in a newspaper published in each county where any
part of the drainage district lies and by posting
three notices in each precinct of the district , inform-
ing the owners of the district that they may vote
for a drainage commissioner for each precinct and
a secretary for the board and the district , within a
stated time . The vote shall be taken by the owners
filing a written designation of choice , together with
the number of acres the voter owns in the district ,
or if benefits have been confirmed the amount of
benefits assessed against the owner's land , with the
county judge . Each acre owned and assessed in the
district shall count one vote , or if benefits have been
confirmed each one hundred dollars or fraction
thereof in benefits assessed against the voter's land
shall count one vote for secretary and drainage com-
missioner . The votes of the owners , when cast and
filed with the county judge , shall be recorded by the
secretary of the board in the drainage record im-
mediately after the expiration of the time for cast-
ing the vote . Votes shall be filed with the county
judge by the first Monday in April for drainage
commissioner and for secretary , for a term of one
year beginning on the first Monday of May follow-
ing . If there is a tie vote in any contest the judge
of the county where the district has been established
shall cast the deciding vote .
(3 ) The board shall consist of three members ,
being the persons who have received the largest
number of votes in each of the three precincts . The
secretary shall be the party who received the largest
number of votes from the district at large for that
office . The board shall elect some responsible resi-
dent of the district , who is not a member of or secre-
tary to the board, as treasurer . The treasurer shall
be paid whatever salary the board deems proper ,

CHAPTER 189 871
and shall execute bond as the board directs . One
month after the advertised election is completed ,
or on the first Monday in May if not an advertised
election , the elected officers shall take charge of the
drainage record and all records , maps , papers and
property belonging to the district , except money in
the hands of the treasurer .
(4 ) No one except an owner of land in the pre-
cinct for which he acts shall be eligible for drainage
commissioner . Each commissioner shall be a free-
holder and over twenty -one years of age . The board
shall have control of all improvements in the dis-
trict , except as otherwise provided . Vacancies in
the office of board member or secretary shall be
filled by a special election after due advertising .
Each drainage commissioner when elected shall
execute bond for the faithful performance of his
duties in the sum of $2500 and shall be sworn to
perform the duties of his office to the best of his
ability .
(5 ) Immediately upon election and qualifica-
tion , the board shall become a body corporate , under
the name and style of the " Board of Drainage Com-
missioners of District , " with all
the powers of a corporation or as necessary to carry
on its work . It shall use a corporate seal which it
may change at pleasure . The board shall elect from
among its members a president , and a vice
president .
(6 ) The board shall furnish its secretary with
the necessary office room , furniture , fixtures , sta-
tionery , maps , plats , typewriter and postage . The
board shall meet as often as necessary for the
proper discharge of duties at whatever places these
duties require within the county in which any part
of the district lies . The board shall keep complete

8.72 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
records of its proceedings , which shall be at all
times open to the inspection of the public .
(7) The treasurer of the board shall, by May 1
of each year , make a complete and itemized report
of its accounts and doings during the previous year ,
showing the amount of money received and from
what source and the amount paid out and for what
purposes . This report shall be spread upon the
records and open for public inspection . He shall
also , by May 1 of each year , have published in the
county seat newspaper of the county where most of
the acreage of the district lies , a complete and
itemized statement of the previous year showing the
accounts received , the date of receipt and from what
source , the amounts paid out , the date of disburse-
ment and for what purposes .
( 8 ) All expenses incurred by the board shall be
taken out of drainage funds and not from any
general funds of the county . The commissioners
shall be paid not more than three dollars per day
while actually engaged in the performance of their
duties as well as all traveling expenses incurred in
attending any meeting of the board or in the per-
formance of their duties , to be paid out of the funds
of the district .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly, then , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , subsections ( 1 ) to ( 5 ) of section
one of this Act shall be substituted in place of KRS
268.160 , and subsections ( 6 ) to ( 8 ) of section one
of this Act shall be substituted in place of KRS
268.170 , and this Act shall thereafter be treated as
an amendment to KRS 268.160 and 268.170 rather
than an amendment to § 2380b - 12 of Carroll's
Statutes .

CHAPTER 190 873
$ 3. Whereas , it is deemed important by the
General Assembly that landholders in drainage dis-
tricts organized under the above laws be permitted
at once to elect their own officers , an emergency is
hereby declared to exist , and this Act as amended
shall become effective immediately upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 190.
(H. B. 237)
AN ACT relating to persons who solicit or sell insurance
other than life .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The name and address of employees ,
solicitors , salesmen or agents of mutual companies
other than life , and reciprocal or interinsurance ex-
changes engaged in the solicitation or sale of insur-
ance , shall be forwarded on or before November 1
of each year by such mutual company or inter-
insurance exchange to the Director of Insurance of
the State of Kentucky together with a fee of three
dollars for each such employee , solicitor or agent ,
and , upon receipt thereof , the Director of Insurance
shall issue to each such person a license certifying
that such person is the authorized representative of
the company or exchange supplying him with the
name of such person , and no such representative
shall solicit or sell insurance in this state until pos-
sessed of such license which shall expire on the first
day of November in each year ; provided , that such
license may be refused or revoked by the Director of
Insurance in such manner and to the extent pro-
vided in Section 762a - 14d and Section 762a - 14e ;

assessment or co -operative associations or com-
panies or their agents , servants or employees or
representatives .
The penalty for failure to comply with this Act
shall be the same as provided in section 762a -20c .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , the numbers " subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
298.150 and subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 298.990 " shall be
substituted for " section 762a -20c " in section one of
this Act, and the number of " KRS 298.220 " shall
be substituted for " Section 762a - 14d and Section
762a -14e " in Section ( 1 ) of this Act .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 192.
(H. B. 126 )
AN ACT amending and re- enacting Section 1358 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , relating to the conceal-
ment of personal property subject to mortgage or statutory
lien .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
SECTION ONE : That Section 1358 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , be amended and
re -enacted , so when amended and re -enacted it shall
read as follows :
If any person shall fraudulently sell , conceal , dis-
pose of or knowingly remove from this state any

876 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
personal property on which there is at the time a
duly executed and acknowledged mortgage whether
the same be of record at said time or not , or any lien
given under the statutory laws of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky with intent to prevent or hinder
the enforcement of the lien thereon or the fore-
closure of the mortgage or lien and sale of the
property , he shall be deemed guilty of the larceny
of the property , and if the value thereof be twenty
dollars or more , shall be punished by confinement

CHAPTER 196 .
(H. B. 58)

utes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
DUTIES ; OF BOARD ; ENFORCE LAW ;
ADOPT REGULATIONS ; INVESTIGATIONS ;
LITERATURE ; QUARANTINES ; SANITARY
MEASURES ; EMPLOY NECESSARY ASSIST-
ANTS AND FIX THEIR SALARIES ; ENTRY
ON PRIVATE PREMISES .-That the Board is

tion of biological , chemical or other products , sub-

em-

CHAPTER 197 .
( S. B. 192 )
AN ACT relating to the apportionment of school taxes where
the boundary of any school district is changed .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

884 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Any property assessable for school purposes in
one school district is annexed by or transferred to
another school district , the recipient district shall
assume a part of the indebtedness , if any , of the
other school district incurred for school buildings
and grounds in the proportion the assessed valua-
tion of property taxable for school purposes trans-
ferred bears to the total assessed valuation of

CHAPTER 198.
(S. B. 170 )
AN ACT relating to distilled spirits .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That any person or persons owning any distilled
spirits which were manufactured prior to National
Prohibition and upon which all Federal taxes have
been paid shall have the right to withdraw same for
his own personal use without the payment of the
Kentucky Consumer's Tax or any other excise
taxes , and the proprietor of any free warehouse or
of any distillery shall deliver such distilled spirits
to the owner thereof.
Any person so owning such distilled spirits shall
before the removal of same from any distillery or
free warehouse execute an affidavit and file it with
the Administrator of the Distilled Spirits Unit in
the Department of Revenue to the effect that such
distilled spirits will be used only for personal con-
sumption and are not to be withdrawn for purposes
of re - sale and will not be placed in the ordinary
channels of trade.

CHAPTER 199 885
Upon the execution and filing of this affidavit the
Administrator of the Distilled Spirits Unit shall
issue a permit for the withdrawal of the distilled
spirits and the owner of such distilled spirits shall
be entitled to the immediate possession thereof and
the proprietor of any bonded warehouse or distil-
lery shall deliver same directly to the owner .
If after withdrawing such distilled spirits any
person sells same , or any part thereof , or places
same in the ordinary channels of trade he shall be
fined not less than Five Hundred ( $ 500.00 ) Dollars
nor more than Two Thousand ( $ 2,000.00 ) Dollars
or imprisoned for not less than thirty days nor more
than sixty days , or both .
Not approved .

Section 2. Unless another penalty is provided by
law , any person who violates any of the provisions
of this Act shall be fined not less than two nor more
than five hundred dollars or imprisoned for not

CHAPTER 200 .
(S. B. 168)

Practice , and repealing §§ 99 , 100 and 101 of the Civil Code
of Practice .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 89 of the Civil Code of Practice is
amended to read as follows :
Pleadings allowed . The pleadings allowed are-
1. Petitions , answers and replies . Petitions , an-
swers and replies .

CHAPTER 200 887
2. Demurrers .
2.
Demurrers .
Section 98 of the Civil Code of Practice is
amended to read as follows :
Reply may contain ; what . A reply may con-
tain-
1. Traverse . A traverse .
2. Facts constituting estoppel or avoidance . A
statement of facts which constitute an estoppel
against , or avoidance of , a set -off, counter - claim or
defense stated in the answer .
All affirmative allegations in a reply shall stand
traversed of record .
§ 3. Section 111 of the Civil Code of Practice is
amended to read as follows :
Pleading that may be made cross -petition ; set-
off ; counter - claim . No pleading, except an answer
to an original petition , shall be made a cross-
petition , or present a set -off or counter -claim .
§ 4. Paragraph 7 of Section 113 of the Civil
Code of Practice is amended to read as follows :
Traverse defined ; denial of knowledge or infor-
mation. A traverse is a denial , by a party , of the
facts alleged in an adverse pleading , if they be pre-
sumptively within his knowledge ; or a denial of
them , or a denial that he has sufficient knowledge
or information to form a belief concerning them , if
they be not presumptively within his knowledge . It
shall be a sufficient traverse to state that all the
affirmative allegations of the adverse pleading are
specifically denied , except those specifically admit-
ted in the pleading containing the traverse .
5. Section 126 of the Civil Code of Practice is
amended to read as follows :
Allegations not denied admitted ; exceptions .
Every material allegation of a pleading must , for

888 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the purpose of the action , be taken as true , unless
traversed , as provided in paragraph 7 of § 113 , ex-
cept these , which must be proved though not tra-
versed :
1. Allegations against defendant under disabil-
ity. Allegations of a petition , or cross -petition ,
against a defendant who is under any disability
except coverture ..
2. Allegations of answer ; seeking recovery
against new party. Allegations of an answer , or
reply , so far as it states a set-off or counter - claim
against a new party who is under any disability
except coverture .
3. Against defendant constructively summoned .
Allegations against a defendant constructively sum-
moned , who has not appeared in the action .
4. Concerning value or amount of damages .
Allegations concerning value or amount of damage ,
not accompanied by an allegation of an express
promise , or by a statement of facts showing an im-
plied promise , to pay such value or damage ; such
allegations , so accompanied , need not be proved
unless traversed .
§ 6. Sections 99 , 100 and 101 of the Civil Code
of Practice are repealed .
Approved by Governor , March 13 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 201 .
(S. B. 47 )
AN ACT relating to the State Board of Education .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 201 889
§ 1. Section 4377-8 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition , is amended to read as follows :
A majority of the board shall constitute a quorum
for the transaction of business . The board shall
meet every three months and at such other times as
it may be called as provided in this section . Special
meetings of the board may be called by the chair-
man , and upon written request of three members
the chairman shall call a special meeting of the
board . The chairman shall give notice through the
secretary by registered mail to each member of the
board at least ten days prior to the time of any
meeting , unless all members of the board waive
notice in writing . The offices of the board shall be
at the seat of government in the Department of
Education and shall be provided by the state . Mem-
bers of the board may receive a per diem of ten
dollars for each regular or special meeting attended
and actual expenses for attending meetings , and
may be reimbursed for other actual and necessary
expenditures incurred in the state in the per-
formance of their duties authorized by the board.
The per diem and expenses shall be paid out of the
appropriation for the State Board of Education .
However no member of said Board shall receive
more than $ 100.00 per annum for per diem under
the provisions of this act .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall , upon the taking
effect of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be sub-
stituted in place of KRS 156.060 , and shall there-
after be treated as an amendment to KRS 156.060
rather than an amendment to § 4377-8 of Carroll's
Statutes .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 202 .
(H. B. 321 )
AN ACT relating to automobiles and motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 2739g - 13 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1938 Supplement be amended and re-
enacted so that said section as amended and re-
enacted shall read as follows :
Section 2739g - 13 . Whenever an automobile that
has been previously registered changes ownership ,
the registration plates shall remain upon such auto-
mobile as a part thereof until the expiration of the
registration year , subject , however , to the following
provisions :
1. No person acting for himself or another shall
offer for sale or trade any such automobile without
then and there having in his actual physical posses-
sion the clerk's receipt for the registration fee for
the current year .
2. No person shall sell or trade any such auto-
mobile without transferring by endorsement of the
owner and delivering to the purchaser the clerk's
receipt for the registration fee for the current year .
3. No person acting for himself or another shall
buy or trade for any such automobile without re-
ceiving the clerk's receipts for registration fee for
the current year , endorsed as provided for above .
4. Upon such change of ownership the pur-
chaser , as the application for transfer of registra-
tion , shall present to the clerk the endorsed receipt
of the original owner together with a copy of bill of
sale , both of which the clerk shall retain , and the
purchaser shall accompany same with a transfer
fee of one dollar ( 1.00 ) for which the clerk shall

CHAPTER 203 891
issue a receipt bearing the same data and informa-
tion as is required on the original receipt except the
change in name or address . For his services here-
under the purchaser shall pay the clerk a fee of fifty
cents (504 ) . Said purchaser shall have in his pos-
session a receipt showing such transfer before said
automobile shall be operated upon any public high-
way in this Commonwealth . Provided , if the pur-
chaser junks or otherwise renders the automobile
unfit for future use as such he shall be relieved of
the duty of transferring registration by the im-
mediate delivery of registration plates , endorsed
receipt and second copy of bill of sale to the clerk ,
whereupon the clerk shall immediately return said
plates , receipt and copy of bill of sale to the com-
mission, and for these services the purchaser shall
pay the clerk the sum of fifty cents ( 504 ) : provided
further , however , that no licensed automobile dealer
shall be required to pay the fees provided by this
section .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 203 .
(H. B. 72 )
AN ACT relating to trained nurses .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That § 3727a -5 Kentucky Statutes , Bald-
win's 1936 Edition , be amended and re -enacted so
that when so amended and re -enacted the same shall
read as follows :-
It shall be the duty of the board to meet for the
purpose of holding examinations not less than once
in each year at such time and such place as they may
determine , and the board may adopt rules for its

892 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
government and examination of applicants for
registration in accordance with the provisions of
this act . Notices of the meetings of said board shall
be published in two newspapers of general circula-
tion and in at least one journal devoted to the
interests of professional nursing , and after applica-
tions are received , notices by mail to every applicant
and to every reputable training school in this state
at least thirty days prior to the meeting . At such
meetings it shall be the duty of said board to ex-
amine all applicants for registration under the
provisions of this act as are required to be ex-
amined , and to issue to each duly qualified applicant
which shall have complied with the provisions of
this act and passed such examination a certificate of
registration . Any person to whom a certificate of
registration shall be issued shall , within thirty days
thereafter , cause the same to be recorded with the
county court clerk of the county in which such per-
son resides at the time of application , and such per-
son shall be prepared , whenever requested , to ex-
hibit such certificate or certified copy thereof .
Registered nurses changing residence in this State
must present a certificate of registration to the
county clerk of their new residence within thirty
days of the time of establishing such new residence .
All applicants for registration shall furnish satis-
factory evidence that he or she is at least 21 years
of age , of good moral character , and has been
graduated from a school for nurses connected with
a special hospital affiliated with an infirmary or
general hospital , or infirmary or general hospital
approved by said board, where a systematic course
of at least three years ' instruction is given and such
applicant shall produce satisfactory evidence that
he or she had at least one year's training in a high
school or some school equivalent to a high school ,

CHAPTER 203 893
except in cases hereinafter provided for , and all
persons registered under the provisions of this act
shall pay the treasurer of said board a registration
fee of ten dollars ( $ 10.00 ) which shall accompany
the application , and shall annually thereafter pay
to said treasurer a renewal fee of two dollars
( $ 2.00 ) . The failure to pay the renewal fee of $ 2.00
for a period of one year shall forfeit such registra-
tion certificate . Any nurse possessing a registra-
tion certificate under the provisions of this act but
who has failed to pay the annual renewal fees prior
to the enactment hereof , but not subsequent thereto ,
and who has continuously performed the duties of
a nurse under the supervision of a practicing phy-
sician , shall be reinstated without examination upon
furnishing sworn affidavit signed by one or more
physicians who supervised her nursing duties , and
upon the payment of the fees in arrears , in no case ,
however , in the aggregate to exceed the total sum
of ten dollars ( $ 10.00 ) .
Any nurse who has paid her dues or who shall
have forfeited her registration certificate by non-
payment of dues and who has not engaged in
nursing for a period of two years , all or a substan-
tial part of said time , shall be reinstated without
examination by the payment of dues in arrears not
to exceed the total sum of $ 10.00 and by attending
a nurses school maintained at an accredited hos-
pital for a period of not less than four weeks .
The said board shall have full discretion to deter-
mine whether any hospital or infirmary , with which
applicants must have been connected , meets the re-
quirements of this act , and if said board is of the
opinion that any such hospital or infirmary does not
furnish the proper training or instruction to ap-
plicants having been connected therewith , the board
may refuse to register such applicants .

894 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Any certificate , issued in another state under the
laws of such state requiring registration which may
be obtained in such state under like or similar
standard to that required in this state shall be
recognized by said board as valid in this state : Pro-
vided the certificates of registration issued in this
state are recognized in such other state , and any
nurse holding such certificate from another state
may be registered in this state without examination
upon the payment of a fee of Ten ( $ 10.00 ) Dollars .
§ 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are en-
acted at the present session of the General Assem-
bly , section one of this Act shall stand repealed as
of the effective date of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , and the following shall take effect in lieu
thereof :
"A license fee of ten dollars , payable to the
secretary -treasurer of the board , shall accompany
every application for a license . Every licensee
shall pay to the secretary -treasurer a renewal fee of
two dollars annually thereafter . Failure to pay the
renewal fee within one year after its due date shall
work a forfeiture of the license . A license so for-
feited may be renewed at any time without examina-
tion upon the payment of all renewal fees in arrears ,
but in no case shall more than ten dollars be re-
quired for the renewal of such license . " The pro-
vision so substituted by this section shall thereafter
be treated as an amendment to KRS 314.060 rather
than an amendment to § 3727a -5 of Carroll's Stat-
utes , and KRS 314.030 , 314.040 , 314.100 , 314.150 and
314.180 shall remain in full force and effect and
shall not be treated as affected by this Act .
Not approved .

the provisions of this Act shall be deemed to take
place to the person or persons receiving such prop-
erty as a result of such omission or failure to the

subject to taxation if it has passed under the will
of the donee of such power . Provided that in the
case of such power of appointment , the transfer
shall be deemed to take place , for the purpose of
taxation , at the time of the death of the donor and
the assessment be made at that time against the
life interest of the donee and the remainder against
the corpus and collection thereof shall be made pur-
suant to section 4281a -49 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition . In the event this provision
should operate to provide an exemption for any
beneficiary of a donee not authorized by section

added to and made a part of the distributable share

Sec . 3. That Section 4281a - 18 of Carroll's Ken-

All transfers to educational , religious or other

transfers to cities and towns or public institutions

poration or association shall be entitled to such

Sec . 9. It is hereby declared to be the legislative
policy that Kentucky shall not be a party to inter-
state double taxation under the terms of the Ken-
tucky inheritance and estate tax laws . Pursuant
to this policy , the Commossioner of Revenue is
hereby authorized to omit from the property sub-
ject to tax under those laws , any intangible per-
sonal property of a nonresident decedent ( having
a domicile in the United States ) held in trust by
a Kentucky trustee if the jurisdiction ( state , terri-
tory or District of Columbia ) is which the deced-
ent was domiciled grants similar immunity to res-
idents of Kentucky , but only in the event the per-
sonal representative shall present evidence that the

tucky trustee , then the Commissioner of Revenue

subject to tax under the Kentucky inheritance and
estate tax laws , the intangible personal property of
a Kentucky resident held in trust in that jurisdic-
tion but only in the event the personal representa-
tive shall present evidence that the tax has been or
will be paid to the other jurisdiction .
It is expressly provided , however , in view of the
uncertainty now prevailing with respect to the cor-

states , that the provisions of this section shall be
inoperative under the second alternative until and
unless an agreement , approved as to legality by the

have been executed and an original copy thereof
filed with the Kentucky Department of Revenue .
This section is intended to apply retroactively to
all estates of decedents on or after April 25 , 1936 ,
which are subject to Kentucky inheritance tax
laws .

CHAPTER 205
(H. B. 372 )
AN ACT to repeal section seventeen hundred sixty -three of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes Baldwin's 1936 revision and
reenact in lieu thereof the following , which relates to sal-
aries in the offices of county clerk , circuit court clerk and
sheriffs in counties having a population of two hundred
thousand or over .

904 . ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Section seventeen hundred sixty-
three of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's
1936 Revision , be repealed and that the following
new matter be enacted in lieu thereof .
( 1 ) In counties having a population of two hun-
dred thousand ( 200,000 ) or over the salary of the
chief deputy sheriff shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding three thousand dollars
($3,000.00 ) a year . The salary of one deputy sheriff
who shall act as process clerk shall be fixed at a
reasonable amount not to exceed twenty-five hun-
dred dollars ( $2,500.00 ) a year and the salary of
each of the other deputy sheriffs shall be fixed at
a reasonable amount not exceeding two thousand
dollars ( $2,000.00 ) a year .
( 2 ) In counties having a population of two hun-
dred thousand ( 200,000 ) or over the salary of the
chief deputy circuit court clerk shall be fixed at a
reasonable amount not exceeding three thousand
dollars ($3,000.00 ) a year , the salary of one deputy
circuit court clerk who shall act as cashier shall be
fixed at a reasonable amount not exceeding twenty-
five hundred dollars ( $2,500.00 ) a year , and the sal-
ary of each of the other deputies in the office of the
circuit court clerk shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding two thousand dollars
($2,000.00 ) a year .
( 3 ) In counties having a population of two hun-
dred thousand ( 200,000 ) or over the salaries of the
first four deputy county court clerks shall be fixed
at a reasonable amount not exceeding three thou-
sand dollars ( $3,000.00 ) a year and the salary of
each of the other deputies in the office of the county
court clerk shall be fixed at a reasonable amount not
exceeding two thousand dollars ( $2,000.00 ) a year .

CHAPTER 205 905
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , then , upon the taking effect of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 28.300
shall be treated as amended to read as follows :
In counties containing a population of 75,000 or
more and up to 200,000 , the salary of the chief dep-
uty circuit court clerk shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding twenty -five hundred ( 2,500.00 )
dollars per annum , and the salaries of the other
deputy circuit clerks shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding two thousand ( $2,000.00 ) dol-
lars per annum . In counties having a population
of two hundred thousand ( 200,000 ) or over the sal-
ary of the chief deputy circuit court clerk shall be
fixed at a reasonable amount not exceeding three
thousand dollars ( $3,000.00 ) a year , the salary of
one deputy circuit court clerk who shall act as cash-
ier shall be fixed at a reasonable amount not ex-
ceeding twenty -five hundred ( $2,500.00 ) a year , and
the salary of each of the other deputies in the office
of the circuit court clerk shall be fixed at a reason-
able amount not exceeding two thousand dollars
($2,000.00 ) a year .
Subsection ( 3 ) of KRS 28.300 shall be treated as
amended to read as follows :
"In counties containing a population of 75,000 or
more and up to 200,000 , the salaries of the first four
deputy county court clerks shall be fixed at reason-
able amounts , which need not be the same , and none
of which shall exceed $ 2,500.00 per annum . The sal-
aries of each of the other deputy county court clerks
in such counties shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding $2,000 per annum . "
Subsection ( 3 ) of section one of this Act shall be
substituted in place of subsection ( 4 ) of KRS

906 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
28.300 ; subsection ( 3 ) of KRS 70.030 shall be treated
as amended to read as follows :
" In each county containing a population of

A certified copy of the order , and of any subsequent

num ."

CHAPTER 206
(S. B. 121 )
AN ACT to amend and reenact Section 2542 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 edition , relating to limitation of actions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 207 907
Section 1. That Section 2542 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 edition , relating to limitation
of actions , is hereby amended and reenacted and ,
as amended and reenacted , said section shall read
as follows :
When a cause of action has arisen in another
state or coutry , and by the laws of this state or
country where the cause of action accrued the time
for the commencement of an action thereon is lim-
ited to a shorter period of time than the period of
limitation prescribed by the laws of this state for
a like cause of action , then said action shall be barred
in this state at the expiration of said shorter period .
Section 2. If the Kentucky Revised Statutes are
enacted at the present session of the General As-
sembly , section one of this Act shall , upon the tak-
ing effect of the Kentucky Statutes , be substituted
in place of KRS 413.320 , and thereafter be treated
as an amendment to KRS 413.320 rather than an
amendment to Section 2542 of Carroll's Statutes .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 207
(S. B. 156 )
AN ACT relating to the employment and compensation of
Clerk and Deputies and providing for the payment of certain
fees into the County Treasury in counties having a popula-
tion of less than seventy -five thousand and an assessed
valuation of one hundred million and containing a city of
the second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. In each county in this state having a
population of less than seventy -five thousand and
an assessed valuation of more than one hundred

908 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
million and containing a city of the second class , the
clerk of the county court shall have the right to
appoint such deputies as may be necessary for the
proper conduct of his office . The number appointed
shall be approved by the fiscal court . The salaries
of said deputy county clerks shall be fixed by the
fiscal court of the county and shall be payable out of
the county levies in equal monthly instalments . The
salary of the clerk shall be paid in the same man-
ner .
§ 2. The fiscal court in such counties may in its
discretion direct the county clerk to have made in
books proper for that purpose general indexes of
all records in the office of the county clerk accord-
ing to system approved by the fiscal court . When
the general indexes are thus completed they shall
constitute the official indexes and the clerk of the
county shall keep the indexes up to date by index-
ing therein the records of all property within one
month from the date they are lodged for record ,
and when they are so indexed , the alphabetical cross
index of such instruments need no longer be pre-
served and when such records or any part of them
become defaced or injured , the clerk shall tran-
scribe the defaced or injured records into new books ,
and they shall be as valid in law as the original
record . The fiscal court may in its discretion re-
quire the county clerk and his deputies to make such
indexes as provided by this act without additional
compensation or may allow the said clerk for his
services rendered by him and his deputies reason-
able compensation .
Section 3. All fees collected by the clerk shall
be paid to the Treasurer of the county at the end
of each month and when such payments are made
he shall file with the fiscal court an itemized list
of the amounts collected by him during each month .

CHAPTER 208 909
Section 4. In all such counties , if the county
judge appoints the clerk of the county court , or
any other person , to act as clerk of the quarterly
court, then the clerk or person thus appointed , shall
receive the fees now prescribed by law in full com-
pensation for his services rendered as such clerk
provided , however , that if the clerk of the county
court is appointed , then the fees shall be paid into
the county treasurer in the same manner as the
other fees are paid under this act . Provided that
the salary of the Chief Deputy Clerk shall not ex-
ceed $3,600.00 per annum . The second and third
deputies shall not exceed $ 3,000.00 per annum and
the salary of no other deputy shall exceed $ 1,800.00
per annum .
CHAPTER 208
(H. B. 271 )
Not approved .
AN ACT revising the statute laws of the Commonwealth , en-
acting the revised statutes as the law of the Common-
wealth , repealing all prior statute laws of a general and
public nature , and prescribing the effective date of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The following statute laws are hereby
enacted as the laws of the Commonwealth , to be
known , designated and cited as the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes :
(Here follow the 455 chapters of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes which have been printed in a
separate volume designated " Bill To Revise the
Kentucky Statutes 1942. " )
Section 2. All statute laws of the Commonwealth
of a general and public nature enacted prior to the
present session of the General Assembly , except the

910 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Codes of Practice and such statute laws as are con-
tinued in force by express provision in the body of
sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , are
hereby repealed .
Section 3. Any Act passed at the present session
of the General Assembly amending or repealing any
provision of the statute laws by reference to a sec-
tion number of the Session Acts or of Carroll's
Statutes shall be considered as amending or repeal-
ing , as the case may be , so much of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes as is indicated in the Kentucky
Revised Statutes as having been derived from the
section of the Session Acts or of Carroll's Statutes
so amended or repealed , and shall not be considered
as reviving any part of Carroll's Statutes or of any
Session Acts so amended other than the part set
forth at length in the amending Act .
Section 4. This Act shall not become effective
until October 1 , 1942 .
Approved March 5 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 209
(S. R. 7)
JOINT RESOLUTION for the benefit of the Bank of Blaine ,
a banking corporation , of Blaine , Kentucky , for refund of
money erroneously paid for taxes .
WHEREAS the Board of Equalization of Lawrence County ,
Kentucky , did assess against the Bank of Blaine all real
estate mortgages held by it as of July 1, 1938 , in the sum
of $30,443.00 , and
WHEREAS no notice of said assessment was given to the
Bank of Blaine , and
WHEREAS the said mortgages were reported by the Bank
of Blaine as provided by Kentucky Statutes 4109a - 10 , and
WHEREAS the value of said mortgages was included in ar-
riving at the value of the shares of stock of said Bank of
Blaine for taxation for the year 1939 , and

CHAPTER 210 911
WHEREAS in addition to said values of shares of stock the
Bank of Blaine was charged with taxes on said $30,443.00
worth of mortgages , and a tax bill was prepared by Wm .
W. Moore , County Court Clerk of Lawrence County , for
said amount and exhibited to the Bank of Blaine by Proctor
Fyffe , Sheriff of Lawrence County , and collection of same
required , and
WHEREAS payment of tax on said $30,443.00 , the value of
the mortgages , at the rate of fifty cents per one hundred
dollar valuation , to -wit , the sum of $ 152.22 , less only the
discount of two per cent , making a net total of $ 149.18 , was
collected from the Bank of Blaine for said taxes , and
WHEREAS the same to said extent of $ 149.18 is a duplication
of taxes ; a duplication of assessments and a duplication of
collection against said Bank of Blaine for said year of 1939 ,
therefore
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Bank of Blaine , a banking corporation ,
of Blaine , Kentucky , is entitled to a refund in the
sum of $149.18 , because said sum was erroneously
assessed and collected from it for taxes as afore-
said . Immediately upon passage of this resolution
the Commissioner of Finance is directed to draw
a warrant in favor of the Bank of Blaine , a corpora-
tion , in the aforesaid amount of $ 149.18 , and im-
mediately transmit it to the State Treasurer who
shall issue a check in favor of the Bank of Blaine ,
a corporation , in the amount of $ 149.18 from the
general expenditure fund .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 210
(S. R. 11 )
A RESOLUTION authorizing Billy Ray Guess and Lucy Guess
to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State High-
way Commission , either or both .
WHEREAS , Billy Ray Guess and Lucy Guess on March 16 ,
1941 were injured and the automobile of Billy Ray Guess

912 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
destroyed by reason of the fact that while the above men-
tioned persons were driving an automobile along the Knob
Lick Pike in Lincoln County , Kentucky , they ran over an
abutment of a bridge which had been removed and the
Highway Department had carelessly and negligently failed
to erect signs notifying the above named persons and other
persons using said road of the fact that the bridge had been
removed ;
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Billy Ray Guess and Lucy Guess are hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commissioner ,
both or either of them , in the Circuit Court of Lin-
coln County, Kentucky , for such damages as they
or either of them suffered by reason of personal
injury or injuries or property damage , brought
about through the carelessness and negligence of
the State Highway Commissioner , the Rural High-
way Commissioner , its or their agents , or em-
ployees . In the event any judgment is recovered
by Billy Ray Guess or Lucy Guess in any such suit
or suits for the personal injuries or property dam-
ages or the same is compromised or settled , the
same shall be paid by the Commissioner of Finance
by warrant or warrants drawn on the State Treas-
urer and paid out of the General Fund . Either
party to said suit may appeal from any such judg
ment which may be rendered as in any other civil
suit and the case or cases may be settled , compro-
mised or adjusted with the consent and approval
of the Attorney -General of Kentucky in the same
way or manner of any other civil suit .
Anything contained in this Resolution shall not be
considered by either the Court or the jury as sub-
stantive evidence on the merits of the alleged clai n
and the court shall instruct the jury that the reso-
lution authorizing this suit or suits shall not be

CHAPTER 211 913
considered as an admission by the Commonwealth
of Kentucky or any of its agencies of any liability
on the part of the Commonwealth of Kentucky or
any of its agencies , nor an admission that any ver-
dict of any kind whatsoever shall be rendered for
the plaintiff or plaintiffs .
Provided that the amount sued on , for personal
injuries shall not exceed the sum of two hundred

Not approved .
A RESOLUTION for the benefit of persons who were County
Tax Commissioners of Henderson , Harlan , Pike , Bourbon ,
Boyd , Clark , Daviess , Franklin , Madison , Mason , Warren ,
Christian , and McCracken Counties , between the years of
1936-1940 , both inclusive .
WHEREAS , the persons holding the office of County Tax
Commissioner in the above named counties from 1936-1940 ,
both inclusive , were not paid the minimum net salary of
$ 4,000 . per annum for rendering the services required of
them under the law, and ,
WHEREAS , the Attorney General of the Commonwealth in
construing and interpreting the Statutes relating to the
compensation of such tax commissioners in such counties
has given an opinion that such officers are entitled to a
minimum of $4,000 . per annum where the valuation of prop-
erty of such counties permit , based upon the regular statu-
tory fees for assessing such property , and,
WHEREAS , the Commissioner of Revenue is in agreement
with this interpretation of the Statutes involved , and ,
WHEREAS , in a few instances , the full amount due one or
more of the above named tax commissioners has been fully
paid by the Department of Revenue for one or more years
involved ,
Now therefore be it Resolved by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CUAPTER 213 915
That the Commissioner of Revenue is hereby di-
rected to ascertain from the records relating to the
assessment of property in the above named coun-
ties the amount due and owing the above named
officials for any particular year or years between
the above named dates and to certify same to the
Commissioner of Finance ; and the Commissioner
of Finance is hereby directed to draw his warrant
upon the State Treasurer of Kentucky in favor of
the named county tax commissioner for the amount
due and owing each of said county tax commis-
sioners ; and there is hereby appropriated out of
the general fund a sum not otherwise appropriated
sufficient to cover the amount of warrants so di-
rected to be issued to said officials of said counties ,
not to exceed the sum of $ 14,000.00 .

A RESOLUTION authorizing W. G. Tate and Wanda Tate
to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and / or the State

certain tract of land abutting on the south side of U. S.
Highway No. 60 , just outside the city limits of Frankfort ,
Franklin County , Kentucky ; and ,
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth of Kentucky and /or the
State Highway Commission has caused to be erected in
front of the property of the owners herein named , a new
grade of U. S. Highway No. 60 , resulting in a fill 11½ feet
high at the west end of said property and tapering down
to grade at the east end of said property , and which high-
way is used for purposes of travel ; and now , therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That W. G. Tate and Wanda Tate be and
they are hereby empowered and authorized to file
and prosecute appropriate action against the Com-

932 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
monwealth of Kentucky and/or State Highway
Commission , or either or both of them in the Frank-
lin Circuit Court for the purpose of determining
the extent , if any , of the damages resulting to the
property of the owners caused by reason of the
construction of the new road and fill aforesaid ,
if any there be.
2. Said suit shall be for a sum not to exceed
$2000.00 .
3. In the event any judgment is recovered by the
said W. G. Tate and Wanda Tate or any com-
promise agreement or settlement is made , same
shall be paid by the Commissioner of Finance by
warrant drawn on the State Treasurer of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky and paid out of the general
fund .
4. Either party to such suit may appeal from
any judgment which may be entered in said action
as in any other civil suit, and such case may be
settled , compromised or adjusted with the consent
and approval of the Attorney General of Ken-
tucky in the same manner as any other suit .
Nothing contained in this Resolution shall be
considered by either the Court or jury as sub-
stantive evidence on the merits of the alleged dam-
ages , and the Court shall instruct the jury that the
Resolution authorizing this suit shall not be con-
sidered as an admission by the Commonwealth of
Kentucky or any of its agencies , of any damage
done on the part of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky or any of its agencies by reason of the con-
struction of said road and fill , nor as an admission
that any verdict of any kind whatsoever shall be
rendered for the plaintiff .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 236
( S. R. 51 )
RESOLUTION for the benefit of officers and employees of
the Senate and House .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. That there is hereby appropriated from
the Legislative Session Fund an additional compen-
sation for the following employees and officers of
the Senate and House of Representatives , viz :
Assistant Clerk , Enrolling Clerks , Sergeant -at-
Arms , Doorkeepers , Cloakroom Keepers , Janitors
and Pages , to that compensation provided in Chap-
ter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1936 ,
the sum of $5894.00 .
Sec . 2. That the Chief Clerk of the Senate shall
requisition and the Commissioner of Finance shall
draw a warrant for the sum of $2986.00 on the State
Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in
favor of the Chief Clerk of the Senate . Such Chief
Clerk shall forthwith distribute to the employees and
officers of the Senate such funds on the basis of the
number of days that such employees and officers
served during the regular session of the General
Assembly in the year 1942 , the rate per day to be
computed by said Chief Clerk and the presiding
officer of the Senate on a pro rata basis of the
amounts authorized to be paid them by Chapter 88
of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1936 .
Sec . 3. The Chief Clerk of the House of Repre-
sentatives shall requisition and the Commissioner

940 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of Finance shall draw a warrant , for the sum of
$2908.00 , on the State Treasurer of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky in favor of the Chief Clerk of
the House of Representatives . Such Chief Clerk
shall forthwith distribute to the employees and
officers of the House of Representatives such funds
on the basis of the number of days that such
employees and officers served during the regular
session of the General Assembly in 1942. The rate
per day to be computed by the said Chief Clerk and
the presiding officer of the House of Representatives
on a pro rata basis of the amounts authorized to be
paid them by Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of 1936 .
All Acts or parts of Acts in conflict herewith to
the extent of such conflict are hereby repealed .
Whereas , the General Assembly will within a short
time adjourn and the employees and officers will
depart for various sections of the State , an emerg-
ency is hereby declared to exist , and this Resolution
shall be effective from and after its passage and
approval by the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky .
Approved 5th March , 1942

CHAPTER 238
(S. R. 55 )
A RESOLUTION waiving any claim or right of recovery
which the Commonwealth of Kentucky has or may have
against any person who has heretofore received compensa-
tion in excess of $ 5,000.00 per annum as President , or Dean ,
or Professor , or Teacher , employed or appointed by the
Board of Trustees of the University of Kentucky or by the
Board of Regents of either of the State Teachers Colleges .
WHEREAS , successive Attorneys General of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky have ruled that the Presidents of the
University of Kentucky , the Presidents of the State
Teachers Colleges , the Deans , Professors , and Teachers of
said institutions were not public officers and were not
subject to the limitations of Section 246 of the Constitu-
tion of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , the Board of Trustees of the University of Ken-
tucky and the Board of Regents of the respective State
Teachers Colleges , acting upon such legal advice and legal
advice of other lawyers did not believe that Section 246 of

942 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the Constitution of Kentucky applied and limited the
compensation of such Presidents , Deans , Professors , and
Teachers , and
WHEREAS , in reliance upon that construction of the law and
of the Constitution of Kentucky and in the absence of any
decision of the Court of Appeals to the contrary , various
schools , colleges , and universities of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky procured the services of trained experts and
experienced educators as Presidents , Deans , Professors ,
and Teachers , and
WHEREAS , said universities , schools , and colleges could not
have procured the services of such trained educators and
experts at the salaries and compensation within the limita-
tion of $ 5,000.00 per annum , and
WHEREAS , said universities , schools , and colleges , and the
people of the Commonwealth of Kentucky have received
the full benefits of the services of said Presidents , Deans ,
Professors , and Teachers , who have been paid salaries and
compensation which was and is reasonable , in keeping with
earning power of such educators of other states in the
United States , although in some instances said salaries or
compensation exceeded $ 5,000.00 per annum , and
WHEREAS , the Court of Appeals of Kentucky did no
February 24 , 1942 , in the case of J. Dan Talbott , Commis-
sioner of Finance , Appellant , vs. Public Service Commis-
sion et al ., Appellees , hold and adjudge that the salaries of
said Presidents , Deans , Professors , and Teachers , come
within the limitation imposed by Section 246 of the Con-
stitution of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , it is deemed by the General Assembly of Ken-
tucky that it would be unjust and inequitable that said
Presidents , Deans , Professors , and Teachers , who were
employed and paid in good faith and have rendered the
services for which they were employed , be required to
repay or refund the amount which they have been paid
and have received in excess of $5,000.00 per annum , now ,
therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
All claims and demands and causes of action
which the Commonwealth of Kentucky has or may
have to recover from any President , or former
President , Dean or former Dean , Professor or for-

CHAPTER 239 943
mer Professor , Teacher or former Teacher , of the
University of Kentucky or of either of the State
Teachers Colleges in Kentucky , to recover or require
to be refunded or paid back to the Commonwealth
of Kentucky may* amount which they or either of
them has received in excess of $5,000.00 per annum ,
should be and the same hereby is waived , cancelled ,
and released , and the Commonwealth of Kentucky
hereby declares the principle of equitable estoppel
should be and hereby is made applicable to all such
claims and demands for such recovery or any part
thereof .
* Any .
Not approved .
CHAPTER 239
(J. R. 74 )
A RESOLUTION designating June 4 as Jack Jouett Day .
WHEREAS , one of the heroes of the Revolutionary War was
Captain Jack Jouett , who saved Thomas Jefferson and the
members of the General Assembly of Virginia from cap-
ture by the British by a cross - country dash on horseback to
warn them of a secret plan to surround the members of the
General Assembly , and
WHEREAS , Captain Jouett later moved to Kentucky and
became one of the outstanding leaders of this state , and it
is right and proper that his memory should be honored ,
now therefore
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the fourth day of June of each year be
designated and observed as Jack Jouett Day in
Kentucky , but shall not be regarded as a legal
holiday .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 241
(H. R. 2 )
RESOLUTION providing for the purchase of "The Legislative
Digest ."
WHEREAS , it is a matter of importance and convenience that
each Representative and each Senator be advised as to the
various and detailed steps of legislation ; and
WHEREAS , the General Assembly for each of the past ses-
sions since 1912 has approved and adopted as its official

CHAPTER 241 945
publication "The Legislative Digest " , published under the
management of competent and experienced persons , the
purpose of which is to fully advise from day to day the
officials and public of the Commonwealth as to matters
affecting them in an intelligent and prompt manner ; now ,
therefore
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the Senate
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky concurring,
that :
§ 1. The C. T. Deering Company hereby is
authorized , requested and directed to furnish two
hundred ( 200 ) copies of " The Legislative Digest "
daily for delivery and distribution as hereinafter
provided . The C. T. Deering Company will be paid
the sum of two thousand and five hundred
($2,500.00 ) dollars for furnishing two hundred
copies of " The Legislative Digest " daily as pro-
vided in this Resolution , which amount hereby is
fixed , determined , approved and allowed as a con-
tingent expense of the 1940 regular session of the
General Assembly of Kentucky . Said amount will
be paid on or before March 30 , 1942 , out of the ap-
propriation made by Chapter 1 of the Acts of 1940
for legislative sessions . The C. T. Deering Com .
pany will present to the Speaker of the House and
to the President of the Senate its bill for two thou-
sand and five hundred ($ 2,500.00 ) dollars and the
Speaker of the House and the President of the
Senate hereby are authorized and directed to ap-
prove said bill for payment out of the appropriation
hereinabove referred to ; and when said bill is so
approved the Department of Finance shall issue
his warrant upon the State Treasurer in payment
thereof, and the State Treasurer shall issue and
deliver a check upon the State Treasury for the
payment thereof .

946 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 2. The C. T. Deering Company hereby is
directed to furnish and deliver daily to the Clerk
of the House one hundred and ten ( 110 ) copies of
" The Legislative Digest " for distribution to the
Members of the House and to such officers of the
House as the Speaker may direct ; to furnish and
deliver daily to the Clerk of the Senate forty -five
(45 ) copies of " The Legislative Digest " for dis-
tribution to the members of the Senate and to such
officers of the Senate as the President of the Senate
may direct ; and to furnish and deliver daily the re-
maining forty - five ( 45 ) copies of " The Legislative
Digest" to the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and to such departments , officers and em-
ployees of the Commonwealth as the Governor of
the Commonwealth , the President of the Senate and
the Speaker of the House may direct . The Speaker
of the House , the President of the Senate and the
Governor of the Commonwealth hereby are re-
quested to furnish to the C. T. Deering Company
a list of the departments , officers and employees to
whom said copies are to be furnished as herein-
above provided .
The word " daily " as hereinabove used shall be
construed to mean each day that the House or
Senate is in session .
$ 3. " The Legislative Digest " hereby is adopted
and approved as the official publication of the
General Assembly of Kentucky .
§ 4. The Clerk of the House of Representatives ,
on the passage of this Resolution and the concur-
rence therein by the Senate , hereby is directed to
furnish and deliver to the Department of Finance
an attested or certified copy of this Resolution ;
and the amount hereinabove provided to be paid

CHAPTER 242 947
shall be noted and entered on the books of the De-
partment of Finance as a charge against the appro-
priation hereinabove referred to .
Approved by the Governor , January 23 , 1942 .

CHAPTER 248
(H. R. 9 )
WHEREAS , the poet would make the whole world a demo-
cracy , the essence of poetry being the heart of man and its
aim to proclaim the dignity of all mankind , and
WHEREAS , the poets of Kentucky have done and still do
much to elevate the thought of mankind , and
WHEREAS , some of their writings have immortalized Ken-
tucky throughout the civilized world , and
WHEREAS , the essential purpose of Kentucky Poetry Day is
to honor and give recognition to Kentucky poets , to promote
the study and appreciation of poetry , and to encourage
creative talent in this field , and
WHEREAS , Kentuckians at all times are delighted to honor
their men and women who have achieved distinction in any
calling or endeavor , now therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Governor of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky is hereby authorized to designate the seventh
day of April to be set apart and to be observed an-
nually as Kentucky Poetry Day , to honor and pre-
serve the memory of the poets of Kentucky . This
day shall be observed with suitable exercises by the
citizens of the Commonwealth and by the study and
special observance of poetry in the public schools
of the State according to such program as may be
outlined by the Superintendent of Public Instruc-
tion .
Approved by the Governor , January 23 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 249
(H. R. 10 )
RESOLUTION to pay Mrs. Louis B. Dugan the per diem al-
lowance of her deceased husband , the late Louis B.
Dugan .

CHAPTER 249 955
WHEREAS , the late Louis B. Dugan was elected to the House
of Representatives of this General Assembly at the general
election in November , 1941 , from the 56th legislative dis-
trict, being in Jefferson County , and
WHEREAS , the said Louis B. Dugan was elected for a term
of two years and before being able to take the oath of
office died suddenly on December 18 , 1941 , and
WHEREAS , the said Louis B. Dugan had gone through a
long , heated election , and
WHEREAS , if he had been permitted to take the oath of
office and serve during this term of the General Assembly
he would have received as per diem or salary the sum of
Seven Hundred Fifty ( $ 750.00 ) Dollars as a member of the
General Assembly , and
WHEREAS , it has heretofore been the custom and precedent
set by the General Assembly to allow the widow or the
dependents of the deceased member of the General
Assembly his per diem or salary that he would have
received had he attended the session , and
WHEREAS , the deceased spent considerable time and money
in his compaign for nomination and election as aforesaid ,
and
WHEREAS , his widow , Mrs. Louis B. Dugan , is now obligated
for and will be compelled to pay certain debts and obliga-
tions created by the said Louis B. Dugan , much of
which was created while and due to his making the race
for member of the General Assembly as aforesaid , and
WHEREAS , the said widow , Mrs. Louis B. Dugan , needs an
allowance for her support and maintenance .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That there is hereby appropriated out of the leg-
islative session fund the sum of Seven Hundred
Fifty ($750.00 ) Dollars to be paid to Mrs. Louis B.
Dugan, widow of Louis B. Dugan, and the Commis-
sioner of Finance is hereby directed to draw his
warrant upon the Treasurer in favor of the said
Mrs. Louis B. Dugan for the sum of Seven Hun-
dred Fifty ( $ 750.00 ) Dollars .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 251
(H. R. 12 )
RESOLUTION authorizing Carl K. Giles to file suit against
the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS on December 5th , 1941 at the Central State Hos-
pital at Lakeland , Kentucky Carl K. Giles , an employee of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , was severely injured due
to the alleged carelessness and negligence of the agents
and/or employees of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in
furnishing to the said Carl K. Giles old and defective equip-
Iment with which to work.
NOW in order that the said Carl K. Giles may have his day
in Court to determine by judicial action the question of
negligence , if any , causing said injuries aforesaid .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky ;
That Carl K. Giles , or in the event of his death
before or during the prosecution of this action to
its final conclusion , his executor or administrator ,
be and he is hereby empowered and authorized to
file and prosecute appropriate actions against the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , for the purpose of
determining the liability of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky for such injury , if any there be ; and in the
event any judgment in the action is recovered by
said Carl K. Giles , or same be compromised or set-
tled , same shall be paid by the Commissioner of
Finance by warrant drawn on the State Treasurer ,
and paid out of the general fund . Either party to
any suit may appeal from any judgment which may
be entered therein as in any other civil suit , and the
case may be settled and adjusted with the consent
and approval of the Attorney General of Kentucky
in the same way as any other civil case .
Such action may be brought in any county of
Kentucky having jurisdiction of the parties and
subject matter .
Nothing contained in this Resolution shall be con-

958 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sidered by either the Court or jury as substantive
evidence on the merits of the alleged claim, and the
Court shall instruct the jury that the Resolution
authorizing this suit shall not be considered as an
admission by the Commonwealth of Kentucky of
any liability on the part of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , nor an admission that any verdict of any
kind whatsoever shall be rendered for the Plain-
tiff .
In the event that any suit is filed under this reso-
lution the amount of recovery is limited to the sum
of Three Thousand Dollars .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 254
(H. R. 15 )
A RESOLUTION appropriating and authorizing the payment
to Joe Holcomb of the city of Jenkins , Letcher County ,
Kentucky , the sum of three hundred forty-three dollars
and twenty cents for indemnity for cattle destroyed by
order of the State Live Stock Sanitary Board .
WHEREAS , Joe Holcomb of the city of Jenkins , Letcher
County , Kentucky , was the owner of certain cattle during
the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1939 , which cattle were
destroyed by order of the State Live Stock Sanitary Board
during said fiscal year , under the provisions of section 63c- 7
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , and
WHEREAS , the State Live Stock Sanitary Board awarded
indemnity to the said Joe Holcomb to be paid by the Com-
monwealth in the sum of three hundred forty -three dollars
and twenty cents , and
WHEREAS , the said Joe Holcomb has not been paid the sum
awarded to him as indemnity because the sums appro-
priated by the General Assembly for that purpose during
that fiscal year were insufficient and sums appropriated for
subsequent fiscal years could not lawfully be expended for
that purpose , and
WHEREAS , the said Joe Holcomb has been forced to borrow
money to replace the cattle so destroyed and the lender is
demanding payment .

CHAPTER 255 961
Now , therefore , be it Resolved by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That there is appropriated out of the General
Fund of the Commonwealth the sum of three hun-
dred forty -three dollars and twenty cents for the
purpose of paying the indemnity due to Joe Hol-
comb , of the city of Jenkins , Letcher County , Ken-
tucky , and the Department of Finance is directed
to draw a warrant on the Treasurer , payable to the
said Joe Holcomb , for that purpose .
Not approved .

WHEREAS , on November 24 , 1940 , George Allen Abernathy ,
a fugitive from justice wanted in Earlington , Kentucky , for
murder , was arrested in St. Louis , Missouri , and there
detained , by the authorities of St. Louis , Missouri , and

traveled to St. Louis , Missouri , and conveyed George Allen
Abernathy back to Earlington , Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , Floyd C. Wilkey personally incurred expenses
totalling forty - four dollars and eighty cents ( $44.80 ) , and
WHEREAS , the claim of Floyd C. Wilkey is a just claim for
services rendered on behalf of the State , therefore
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
There is hereby appropriated from the General
Fund of the state , the sum of forty - four dollars and
eighty cents ($44.80 ) , and the Department of Fi-
nance shall draw its warrant upon the Treasurer for
that amount , in favor of Floyd C. Wilkey .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 265
(H. R. 27)
WHEREAS , Harold W. Cain , of Somerset, Pulaski County ,
Kentucky , was , during the years 1939 and 1940 , the duly
commissioned and appointed Captain of Company B of the
149th Infantry of the National Guards of the State of Ken-
tucky , and
WHEREAS , in the year 1939 the said Captain Cain and his
Company were ordered by the Governor of the Common-
wealth to Harlan county for active duty during a Labor
dispute there , and
WHEREAS , in the performance of such duties Captain Cain
and the enlisted men of Company B of the 149th Infantry
were required to wear their uniforms constantly , by reason
of which the uniforms became soiled and dirty so that it
became necessary to have same laundred , and
WHEREAS , Captain Cain in his official capacity employed
the Somerset Laundry and Cleaners to wash the uniforms
and equipment of this Company soiled while on active
duty , and
WHEREAS , the cost of such cleaning and laundering
amounted to the sum of $281.42 , no part of which has ever
been paid , and
WHEREAS , said claim is a lawful claim against the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky ,
Now therefore be it Resolved by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
