CHAPTER 267
( H. R. 30 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION ratifying and approving the inter-
state compact to conserve oil and gas as herein set out ,
authorizing the Governor of this State to execute an agree-
ment to enable this State to become a member thereof ,
prescribing the form of the agreement , designating the
Governor of this State as the authorized representative
upon the interstate oil compact commission , authorizing
and enabling him to appoint an assistant representative ,
prescribing procedure for the withdrawal from the com-
pact , and declaring an emergency.
WHEREAS on the 16th day of February , 1935 , the States of
Colorado , Illinois , Kansas , New Mexico , Oklahoma and
Texas entered into an interstate agreement known as the
Interstate Compact to Conserve Oil and Gas , which agree-
ment signed by the proper officials of these States is now
on deposit with the Department of State of The United
States in Washington , D. C. , said agreement , unless ex-
tended , terminating on September 1 , 1937 ;
.
AND WHEREAS by agreements duly entered into by said
States pursuant to legislative authority said Compact has
been extended from time to time and is now in full force
and effect .
AND WHEREAS said Interstate Compact to Conserve Oil and
Gas provides as follows : "Any oil-producing State may
become a party hereto by affixing its signature to a
counterpart to be similarly deposited , certified and ratified ."
AND WHEREAS it is deemed advisable that the Common-
wealth of Kentucky join and become a member of the
Interstate Compact to Conserve Oil and Gas as now
constituted :

974 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Now , therefore , be it Resolved by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky that :
Section 1. The Interstate Compact to Conserve
Oil and Gas entered into by the Governors of the
States of Colorado , Illinois , Kansas , New Mexico ,
Oklahoma and Texas , and the representatives of
certain other oil producing States at Dallas , Texas ,
on the 16th day of February , 1935 , and extended as
aforesaid , is hereby ratified , approved and con-
firmed by the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the
Governor of this Commonwealth is hereby author-
ized , empowered and directed to affix its signature
to a counterpart of said Interstate Compact to Con-
serve Oil and Gas and have a certified copy of same
deposited with the Department of State of the
United States in Washington , D. C. , as provided
by Article VIII of said Compact .
Section 2. The Interstate Compact to Conserve
Oil and Gas referred to in the above section and
which was ratified , approved and confirmed by the
legislature of the several states of Colorado ,
Illinois , Kansas , New Mexico , Oklahoma and Texas ,
and which was extended by agreement subject to
the approval of Congress , reads as follows :
" AN INTERSTATE COMPACT TO CONSERVE
OIL AND GAS
" ARTICLE I.
"This agreement may become effective within
any Compacting State at any time as prescribed by
that State , and shall become effective within those
States ratifying it whenever any three of the States
of Texas , Oklahoma , California , Kansas , and New
Mexico have ratified and Congress has given its
consent . Any oil -producing State may become a
party hereto as hereinafter provided .

CHAPTER 267 975
"ARTICLE II .
"The purpose of this compact is to conserve oil
and gas by the prevention of physical waste thereof
from any cause .
"ARTICLE III .
" Each State bound hereby agrees that within a
reasonable time it will enact laws , or if any laws
have been enacted , then it agrees to continue the
same in force , to accomplish within reasonable
limits the prevention of :
"(a) The operation of any oil well with an in-
efficient gas -oil ratio .
" "
(b) The drowning with water of any stratum
capable of producing oil or gas , or both oil and gas
in paying quantities .
"(c) The avoidable escape into the open air or
the wasteful burning of gas from a natural gas well .
" (d) The creation of unnecessary fire hazards .
"(e) The drilling , equipping , locating , spacing
or operating of a well or wells so as to bring about
physical waste of oil or gas or loss in the ultimate.
recovery thereof .
" (f) The inefficient , excessive or improper use
of the reservoir energy in producing any well .
" The enumeration of the foregoing subjects shall
not limit the scope of the authority of any State .
"ARTICLE IV.
" Each State bound hereby agrees that it will ,
within a reasonable time , enact statutes , or if such
statutes have been enacted , then it will continue the
same in force , providing in effect that the oil pro-
duced in violation of its valid oil and/or gas con-
servation statutes or any valid rule , order or

976 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
regulation promulgated thereunder , shall be denied
access to commerce ; and providing for stringent
penalties for the waste of either oil or gas .
“ ARTICLE V.
"It is not the purpose of this compact to author-
ize the States joining herein to limit the production
of oil or gas for the purpose of establishing or fix-
ing the price thereof, or create or perpetuate
monopoly , or to promote regimentation , but is
limited to the purpose of conserving oil and gas and
preventing the avoidable waste thereof within
reasonable limitations .
"ARTICLE VI.
" Each State joining herein shall appoint a repre-
sentative to a commission hereby constituted and
designated as THE INTERSTATE OIL COM-
PACT COMMISSION , the duty of which commis-
sion shall be to make inquiry and ascertain from
time to time such methods , practices , circumstances
and conditions as may be disclosed for bringing
about conservation and the prevention of physical
waste of oil and gas , and at such intervals as said
commission deems beneficial it shall report its find-
ings and recommendations to the several States for
adoption or rejection .
" The commission shall have power to recom-
mend the coordination of the exercise of the police
powers of the several states within their several
jurisdictions to promote the maximum ultimate
recovery from the petroleum reserves of said
States , and to recommend measures for the maxi-
mum ultimate recovery of oil and gas . Said Com-
mission shall organize and adopt suitable rules and
regulations for the conduct of its business .

CHAPTER 267 977
"No action shall be taken by the Commission
except : ( 1 ) by the affirmative votes of the majority
of the whole number of the compacting States ,
represented at any meeting , and ( 2 ) by a concurring
vote of a majority in interest of the compacting
States at said meeting , such interest to be deter-
mined as follows : Such vote of each State shall be
in the decimal proportion fixed by the ratio of its
daily average production during the preceding
calendar half -year to the daily average production
of the compacting States during said period .
"ARTICLE VII .
"No State by joining herein shall become
financially obligated to any other State , nor shall
the breach of the terms hereof by any State subject
such State to financial responsibility to the other
States joining herein .
"ARTICLE VIII.
"This compact shall expire September 1 , 1937 .
But any State joining herein may , upon sixty ( 60 )
days ' notice , withdraw herefrom.
"The representative of the signatory States have
signed this agreement in a single original which
shall be deposited in the archives of the Department
of State of the United States , and a duly certified
copy shall be forwarded to the Governor of each
of the signatory States .
"This compact shall become effective when
ratified and approved as provided in Article I.
Any oil - producing State may become a party hereto
by affixing its signature to a counterpart to be
similarly deposited , certified and ratified .

978 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
" Done in the City of Dallas , Texas , this Six-
teenth day of February , 1935 .
" E. W. MARLAND
The Governor of the State of Oklahoma
JAMES V. ALLRED
The Governor of the State of Texas
R. L. PATTERSON
For the State of California
FRANK VESELY
E. H. WELLS
HUGH BURCH
HIRAM M. DOW
For the State of New Mexico
" The following representatives recommend to
their respective Governors and Legislatures the
ratification of the foregoing agreement :
" JOHN W. OLVEY
of Arkansas
WARWICK M. DOWNING
of Colorado
WILLIAM BELL
of Illinois
GORDEN F. VAN EENANAAM
GERALD COTTER
of Michigan
RALPH J. PRYOR
E. B. SHAWVER
T. C. JOHNSON
of Kansas . ”
Section 3. The Governor is hereby designated
as the official representative of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky on " The Interstate Oil Compact Com-
pact Commission " provided for in the compact here-
in ratified , approved and confirmed , or as extended .
The Governor shall exercise and perform for the

CHAPTER 267 979
Commonwealth of Kentucky all the powers and
duties imposed by the aforesaid compact upon a
representative to " The Interstate Oil Compact
Commission " ; provided , that he shall have the
authority to appoint an assistant representative
who shall act as the official representative of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky on " The Interstate Oil
Compact Commission " when the authority to so act
is delegated to him by the Governor . In addition ,
said assistant representative shall perform such
other duties as the Governor may designate which
are necessary to enable the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky to fully cooperate in accomplishing the objects
of said Compact . Said assistant representative
shall take the oath of office prescribed by the Consti-
tution , which shall be filed with the Secretary of
State.
Section 4. The Governor of Kentucky is author-
ized and empowered for and on behalf of this State
to determine if and when it shall be for the best
interest of this State to withdraw from said Com-
pact upon sixty ( 60 ) days ' notice as prescribed by
the terms of the Compact . In the event he shall
determine that this State should withdraw from
said Compact , he shall have full power and author-
ity to give the necessary notice and to take any and
all steps necessary and proper to effect the with-
drawal of the Commonwealth of Kentucky from
said Compact .
Section 5. It being immediately necessary for
the preservation of the public peace , health and
safety , an emergency is hereby declared to exist ,
by reason whereof this resolution shall take effect
and be in full force from and after its passage and
approval .
Approved March 2 , 1942 .

the part of the state for the return of a fugitive from
justice .
WHEREAS , T. R. Sullivan , former Deputy Sheriff of Russell
County was on June 4, 1938 made the agent of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky to go to the State of North Caro-
lina for the purpose of demanding and receiving from the
proper authorities of that state one Alva Morgan , a fugitive
from justice from the State of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , the said T. R. Sullivan went to the State of North
Carolina for the purpose of returning the fugitive , and
WHEREAS , the Governor of the State of North Carolina
refused to honor the requisition for the return of said
fugitive , and
WHEREAS , the expenses incurred by T. R. Sullivan in going
to and returning from the State of North Carolina as agent
for the Commonwealth amounted to the sum of $ 72.00 , and
said expenses have not been paid to the said T. R. Sullivan
because of the refusal of the Governor of the State of
North Carolina to honor the requisition of the Governor of
this Commonwealth , NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That there is hereby appropriated out of the
general fund to T. R. Sullivan the sum of $72.00 and
the Department of Finance is hereby directed to
draw its warrant upon the Treasurer in favor of
T. R. Sullivan for said amount .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 295 .
(H. R. 64 )
Not approved .
RESOLUTION authorizing Mattie E. Combest and Mabel
Wesley , or either of them , to sue the State Highway Com-
mission and the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or either .
WHEREAS , The State Highway Commission , during the
years 1928 and 1929 , reconstructed the state highway lead-
ing from Liberty in Casey County to Dunville in Casey
County , and being part of state highway No. 35 , and
WHEREAS , In the reconstruction of same along by , and
through , the farm of Mattie E. Combest and Mabel Wesley ,
said Commission by its agents , servants and employees ,
raised the grade of said highway and changed and deepened
the drainage ditches , thereby changing and diverting the
natural flow of waters , and
WHEREAS , In the reconstruction of said highway as afore-
said, it removed a culvert or bridge that carried the water
of a branch across and under said highway and replaced
same with metal pipes of insufficient capacity to carry said
water , and
WHEREAS , And by reason of the raising of said grade ,
deepening the drainage ditches , changing and diverting the
natural flow of said waters , and the removal of said bridge
and culvert and replacing same with metal pipes of insuf-
ficient capacity to carry the waters during rains , all of
which caused the waters to back up in the yard and under
* Aforesaid

1008 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the dwelling house of said Combest and Wesley , and stand
for days therein and thereunder , causing the sills , and
sleepers and floors to rot and decay and forcing the occu-
pants of said house to wade the waters going to and from
same , and further , causing the waters to flow over the said
highway on the garden and other tillable lands and
destroying the crops planted thereon and carrying stone
and gravel from said highway on said lands materially
damaging same , and
WHEREAS , The said damage resulting to said dwelling house
was caused during the years 1930 , 1931 , 1932 , 1933 , 1934 ,
and 1935 , and said garden and crops were damaged and
destroyed during the years 1930 , 1931 , 1932 , 1933 , 1934 , and
1935 , which damages amount to several hundreds of dollars ,
all of which have resulted from the carelessness and negli-
gence of said Highway Commission as foresaid , in the re-
construction of said highway , and
WHEREAS , Said State Highway Commission , by its agents
and employees has repeatedly , and from year to year ,
promised and agreed to remedy the situations above com-
plained of but failed to do so : Therefore ,
-Said resolution reads as follows :
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Mattie E. Combest and Mabel
Wesley , or either of them , jointly or separately , are
permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the State Highway Commission , or either , in
the Circuit Court of Casey County , the county
wherein said land is situated , for such damages as
they or either may have sustained , if any , by reason
of the reconstruction of said highway as aforesaid ,
resulting to the dwelling house , garden and grow-
ing crops and injury to said lands during the years
1930 , 1931 , 1932 , 1933 , 1934 , and 1935 , and such as
may result before the trial in 1936. Suit may be for
sum not in excess of $ 500.00 .
Section 2. Either party to said suit may appeal
from any judgment that may be rendered in said
suit and the same procedure shall be followed as is

CHAPTER 296 1009
provided in the Civil Code for other suits , and the
case may be settled and compromised with the con-
sent and approval of the Attorney - General of Ken-
tucky in the same way as other civil cases .
Section 3. Any amount recovered in said suit or
by settlement with the Attorney -General shall be
paid out of the General Fund upon warrant drawn
by the Commissioner of Finance upon the State
Treasurer .
Not approved .

CHAPTER 297 .
( H. R. 66 )
RESOLUTION authorizing Richard Cox and W. F. Masden ,
to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and Fish and Game
Commission of the Commonwealth or either .
WHEREAS the estate of Isaac Bernheim has purchased about
15,000 acres of mountainous land located , partly in Bullitt
County and partly in Nelson County , in this Common-
wealth and said land is held by said estate for the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky and is known as the Bernheim
Foundation for the rearing and protection of wild animals
and wild turkeys of all kind , and whereas the Common-
wealth has , through its Fish and Game Commission , placed
on said land large numbers of deer and same have been
prolific and increased to a very large number , and are not
confined on the above said land , but are permitted to run
in large droves over land adoining and surrounding this
said 15,000 acres and destroy the crops of the farmers in
the surrounding country , and said deer are by sections
1938a - 16 and 1938a - 17 and amendments thereto of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revision , protected from
being killed or chased by dogs .
WHEREAS , Richard Cox and W. F. Masden are farmers in
the surrounding territory of the above said 15,000 acres of
land and were such farmers during the years 1937 , 1938 ,
1939 , 1940 and 1941 and during the above said years the
deer as above said have gone upon the land cultivated by
Richard Cox and destroyed his crops and damaged him in
the sum of $ 3,360.00 and have gone upon the land culti-
vated by W. F. Masden during said years and destroyed
his crops and damaged him in the sum of $ 2,100.00 .
NOW, in order that Richard Cox and W. F. Masden and each
of them may have their " Day in Court " to determine by
judicial action the extent of damages , if any , sustained by
them , and the liability therefor .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That Richard Cox and W. F. Masden , and
each of them , in their own right and name , be and
they are respectively and severally authorized to
sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and the State

CHAPTER 298 1011
Game and Fish Commission , or either for the pur-
pose of determining the liability of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and the State Game and Fish
Commission , or either for such damages sustained ,
if any therebe . If any judgment in either action is
recovered by Richard Cox or W. F. Masden , or
either of them , or same are compromised or settled ,
same shall be paid by the Department of Finance
by warrant drawn on the State Treasurer and paid
out of the general fund .
2. Either party to any suit may appeal from
any judgment which may be entered therein as in
other civil suits and the case may be settled and
adjusted with and by the consent and approval of
the Attorney General of Kentucky in the same way
as any other civil suit .
3. Said action may be brought in Bullitt County ,
the county where the damage was done , in the Cir-
cuit court of said county . The limit of liability as
to Richard Cox is $ 3,360.00 ( three thousand three
hundred and sixty dollars ) ; as to W. F. Masden is
$2,100.00 (two thousand one hundred dollars ) .
Not approved .

A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing the personal representa-
tive of Alvis Tincher to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky :
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 300 1013
That the personal representative of Alvis
Tincher is authorized to sue the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in Jackson Circuit Court for damages
resulting from personal injury (or property
damage ) caused by alleged negligence on the part
of an employee of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
while said employee was in the line of duty as an
employee of this Commonwealth .
Said suit shall be for a sum not in excess of
$ 10,000 .
The Attorney General of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky may investigate the claim (or claims )
against the Commonwealth herein and settle said.
claim ( or claims ) before or after suit is filed , if he
deems it advisable . Any amount recovered in said
suit or by settlement with the Attorney General
shall be paid out of the General Fund upon warrant
drawn by the Commissioner of Finance upon the
State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered
by the Circuit Court , either party may appeal to the
Court of Appeals of Kentucky .
No statement in this resolution shall be con-
sidered by either the Court or jury as an admission
of negligence upon the part of the Commonwealth
and this resolution shall not be introduced as evi-
dence , nor commented upon by counsel in argument
to the jury .
Not approved .

Section 2. Section 2000 of Baldwin's Kentucky
Statute Service , now known as Section 6,010 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes , is hereby repealed .
Provided , however , that in the event of an extraor-
dinary session of the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth prior to the regular session of the
General Assembly in the year 1944 , the Senators
now elected shall continue to represent the Sena-
torial Districts defined in Section 2000 of Bald-

win's Kentucky Statute Service , now known as Sec-
tion 6.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .

resign or die prior to the regular session of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth in 1944 ,
the vacancy shall be filled by an election for the
unexpired term , as now provided by law , in the
Senatorial District as defined in Section 2000 of
Baldwin's Kentucky Statute Service , now known
as Section 6.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Approved April 8, 1942 .

Unless the context requires otherwise , any ref-

CHAPTER 3
( S. Res . 4. )
RESOLUTION appropriating the sum of Seven Hundred Fifty
Dollars ( $ 750.00 ) for a contingent fund for the Clerk of the
Senate , and appropriating the sum of Seven Hundred Fifty
Dollars ( $ 750.00 ) for a contingent fund for the Clerk of the
House .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That for the purpose of paying such expenses as
the Chief Clerk of the Senate of Kentucky is
directed to incur by the Senate of Kentucky there is
set aside out of the appropriation made by Chapter
1 of the Acts of 1940 for legislative sessions as a
contingent fund , the sum of seven hundred fifty
dollars ( $750.00 ) , and for the purpose of paying the
expenses incurred by the Chief Clerk of the House
of Representatives , under the direction of the
House of Representatives of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , there is hereby set aside out of the ap-
propriation made by Chapter 1 of the Acts of 1940

CHAPTER 4 1051
for legislative sessions as a contingent fund , the
sum of seven hundred fifty dollars ( $750.00 ) . Said
respective funds shall be paid out on warrant of the
Department of Finance on requisition of the Chief
Clerk of the Senate and of the House of
Representatives .
Approved April 2 , 1942 .
CHAPTER 4
( S. Res. 10. )
A JOINT RESOLUTION appropriating money for the 1942
Special Session of the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. There is hereby appropriated out of
the General Expenditure Fund for the payment of
salaries and other necessary expenses of the 1942
Special Session of the General Assembly the sum of
Fifty Thousand ( $ 50,000 ) Dollars .
Section 2. Whereas , the funds heretofore ap-
propriated for the 1942 General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky are exhausted , and
Whereas , the work of the 1942 Special Session
of the General Assembly is not completed , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this resolution shall
become effective immediately upon its passage and
aproval by the Governor .
Approved April 2 , 1942 .
